En: Don't 'ya dare to call' er...
Zh: “你敢打给她……”

En: *whimpers*
Zh: *呜咽*

En: Abby? Don't call Abigail!
Zh: “艾比？不要打给阿比盖尔！”

En: Just please don't call 'er.
Zh: “请不要打给她。”

En: Abigail!
Zh: “阿比盖尔！”

En: Don't call Abigail.
Zh: “不要打给阿比盖尔。”

En: Shit, Abigail... please don't call 'er.
Zh: “该死，阿比盖尔……请不要打给她。”

En: Ar'ya... callin' Abigail?
Zh: “啊……打给阿比盖尔？”

En: You're not going to get anything from this guy. He's too drunk.
Zh: 你从这家伙口里问不出什么的。他醉得太厉害。

En: Never thought you'd see such a thing in your life, but this guy's a little *too* drunk.
Zh: 从没想过你这辈子还会见到这种事情，但是这家伙醉得有些*太*厉害。

En: Abigail is his wife or girlfriend. Chances are, she's gone. Calling her wouldn't make it any better or worse.
Zh: 阿比盖尔是他的妻子，或者是女朋友。有可能她离开了。打给她也是于事无补。

En: He draws out a disgusting snort, then mumbles, waving a finger in your general direction: "Ugh... Abigail... don't ya... fuckin'... call Abigail."
Zh: 他发出一阵令人厌恶的哼声，然后举起一只手指朝你的方向挥了挥，嘟囔到：“呃……阿比盖尔……不要……他妈的……打给阿比盖尔。”

En: The dozing man mumbles something inaudible.
Zh: 假寐的男人无声地嘟囔着什么。

En: Don't call Abigail, grumbles an unshaven man with a ruddy nose. He narrows his eyes at you as if in recognition, then turns his head away.
Zh: “别打给阿比盖尔，”一个胡子凌乱的红鼻子男人嘟囔着。他眯起眼睛看着你，似乎在辨认你是谁，然后又把头转到了一边。

En: Tell me about your friends. (Point to the others.)
Zh: “跟我说说你的朋友吧。”（指向其他人。）

En: Who are you? What's your name?
Zh: “你是谁？你叫什么名字？”

En: Why shouldn't I call Abigail?
Zh: “为什么我不能打给阿比盖尔？”

En: Where am I? What is this place?
Zh: “我在哪？这是什么地方？”

En: The noxious odour emanating from the drunken man is strong and familiar.
Zh: 从醉汉身上传出的有毒气味强烈又熟悉。

En: Walk away. [Leave.]
Zh: 走开。[离开。]

En: Don't... call Abigail... he whimpers softly, his voice trailing off into nothingness.
Zh: “别……打开阿比盖尔……”他轻声抱怨着，声音逐渐消失在一片虚无之中。

En: I'll call whoever I want, don't tell me what to do.
Zh: “我想打给谁就打给谁，不用你告诉我怎么做。”

En: I am the law.
Zh: “我就是法律。”

En: I am the law.
Zh: “我就是法律。”

En: Who is Abigail?
Zh: “阿比盖尔是谁？”

En: This is making me uncomfortable. [Leave.]
Zh: “我感觉有点不太舒服。”[离开。]

En: Right, you might have found the one person who doesn't know you are the law, given how drunk he is.
Zh: “没错，你可能找到了不知道你是执法人员的人，考虑到他有多醉的话。”

En: Abigail... oh, Abigail... He mutters while trying to brush something off his extremely dirty trousers. His movements are slow and awkward.
Zh: “阿比盖尔……哦，阿比盖尔……”他一边嘟囔，一边试图把什么东西从那条脏的要命的裤子上拍掉。他的动作缓慢而笨拙。

En: The lieutenant sighs. "You really don't have to... Besides, he will understand you anyway."
Zh: 警督叹了口气。“你真不用……再说了，他会理解你的。”

En: Have you seen any women around here lately?
Zh: “你最近有没有在附近见过一个女人？”

En: Abigail! He grunts. "Don't... don't call her." He waves a finger in your general direction.
Zh: “阿比盖尔！”他咕哝到。“不要……打给她。”他伸出一只手指，朝你的方向挥舞着。

En: Hey, I'm on an important official investigation, I demand you answer my questions.
Zh: “喂，我正在进行一项重要的官方调查，我要求你回答我的问题。”

En: There's no use in yelling at drunks, the lieutenant interjects. "He's barely holding it together."
Zh: “朝醉鬼大声嚷嚷是没用的，”警督插了一嘴。“他勉强才能撑下来的。”

En: The drunk man starts coughing, a really disgusting hacking cough.
Zh: 醉汉开始咳嗽，非常恶心的干咳。

En: Abigail? His voice perks up, then trails into a string of random babbling. "Abigail, where are you?"
Zh: “阿比盖尔？”他的声音兴奋了起来，然后变成一连串胡乱的呓语。“阿比盖尔，你在哪？”

En: He glares at you. "Don't ya fuckin' call her, hear me." His voice trembles with every word, becoming ever weaker. "Abigail," he whimpers in the end.
Zh: 他怒视着你。“别他妈打给她，听到我的话了吗。”他说出的每一个词都在颤抖，变得越发虚弱了。“阿比盖尔，”最后话语变成了啜泣。

En: The man hiccups, then mumbles something unintelligible.
Zh: 男人打了个嗝，接着开始不知所云的喃喃自语。

En: His eyes move around erratically, the look in them hazy and unfocused. "Don't call Abigail," he says.
Zh: 他的眼睛游移不定，眼神朦胧而涣散。“别打给阿比盖斯，”他说道。

En: He snorts and beckons you to lean in closer.
Zh: 他哼了一声，召唤你靠的更近一些。

En: Don't ya call her, ya hear. Don't call Abigail...
Zh: “别打给她，你听见没。别打给阿比盖尔……”

En: Don'... don'... the man stammers, "don't call..." Slowly, his head nods off to the side and he passes out, tongue dangling from his mouth.
Zh: “不……别……”男人结巴了，“别打给……”慢慢地，他的脑袋歪向一边，晕了过去，舌头还悬在嘴里。

En: There was little chance he'd be a reliable witness, anyway.
Zh: “不管怎么说，他基本上不可能成为一个可靠的证人。”

En: Lean in.
Zh: 靠近些。

En: Don't lean in.
Zh: 不要靠近。

En: He waves his hand, as if shooing you away, all the while murmuring something indecipherable.
Zh: 他摆摆手，似乎要把你赶走，嘴里还嘟囔着某些难以理解的话。

En: His breath smells harsh like a toxic swamp as he whispers to you: "Don't call Abigail. Don't call Abigail!" He then waves his hand, as if shooing you away.
Zh: 他对着你耳语，浓烈的口气闻起来就像有毒的沼泽：“别打给阿比盖尔。别打给阿比盖尔！”接着他摆摆手，似乎要把你赶走。

En: He does not sound well at all.
Zh: 他听起来一点也不好。

En: Don't call Abigail. The drunk man looks at you with his sad, swollen eyes.
Zh: “别打给阿比盖尔。”醉汉看着你，眼睛悲伤又肿胀。

En: Don't call... don't call her... The drunk man speaks in his sleep. Drool dribbles from the left side of his mouth and down his jacket lapel.
Zh: “别打……别打给她……”醉汉在睡梦中呓语。口水从左边的嘴角滴落下来，流到了夹克的翻领上。

En: There's no waking him from his stupor. His mind is elsewhere.
Zh: 唤醒恍惚的他是不可能的。他的思绪已经不在这里了。

En: Hey, wake up!
Zh: “喂，醒醒！”

En: Let him sleep it off. [Leave.]
Zh: 让他睡去吧。[离开。]

En: He's too banged up himself -- to talk torque with you.
Zh: 他伤的太严重——没办法跟你讨论扭矩。

En: Yeah. *Sad* rock and roll. Tune it to Sad FM.
Zh: 是啊。*悲伤*的摇滚乐。调到悲伤FM电台。

En: Very cool. Now tune it to Sad FM. You know you want to.
Zh: 太酷了。现在调到悲伤FM电台。你知道你想的。

En: Fine. He gives you resigned shrug. "Let's *blast* Sad FM then."
Zh: “好吧。”他顺从地耸耸肩。“那就*来点*猛的，悲伤FM。”

En: Sad FM is a radio station specializing in sad, slow rock songs. You seem to know its frequency by heart.
Zh: 悲伤FM是一个无线电台，专门播放悲伤的慢摇滚歌曲。你似乎已经把那个频率记在心里了。

En: I mapped the place out and uh... it looks like both of us aren't gonna make it. This is death island. The hell maw. He wipes snot from his nose.
Zh: “我在地图上把那个地方标出来了，还有……看来我们俩都撑不过去的。这是死亡岛。地狱的咽喉。”他擦掉了鼻子里的鼻涕。

En: Hell island? Finally.
Zh: “地狱岛？终于找到你了。”

En: I don't know. He throws his hands in the air.
Zh: “我不知道。”他举起双手。

En:
What the hell are you talking about?	“你到底在说什么鬼啊？”
0x0100000700008B73	Dialogue Text	VILLAGE / BOAT	Cuno	Yes... finally." He says with some reservations.
Zh: “是的……终于到了。”他有些保留地说道。

En: This isn't death island. Is it?
Zh: “这不是死亡岛，对吧？”

En: Maybe Cuno's just pessimistic because of the massive speed hangover. I did scout the place out. I can fill you in.
Zh: “也许坤诺嗑了很多思必得还没清醒，所以有点悲观吧。不过我确实侦察过那个地方。我可以跟你说说。”

En: Good. You made it. Cuno stands with his arms akimbo, looking at the shadow of the ruined flak tower looming overhead.
Zh: “很好。你做到了。”坤诺站在那里，双手叉腰，看着头顶那尊废弃高射炮投下的阴影。

En: The kid turns away from you, cautiously looking to his left and right. He's on the edge.
Zh: 男孩转过身去，小心地左右张望着。他有些紧张。

En: He hasn't been here before either.
Zh: 他之前也没来过这里。

En: Remember what the Net Picker said: It's a small tank. You won't be going back and forth on this.
Zh: 要记得拾网人说的话：油箱很小。乘着它往返是不可能的。

En: Have you made all the necessary preparations? Closed all your accounts?
Zh: 你已经做好所有必要的准备了吗？所有报告都结束了吗？

En: Nothing to do here without the lieutenant... You must return here with him.
Zh: 警督不在的话，这里没什么能做的——你必须跟他一起回到这里来。

En: The time has come.
Zh: 时间到了。

En: The skiff bobs in the calm waters. It has nothing more to do.
Zh: 小艇在平静的水面上摆动。没什么别的事可以做了。

En: A skiff with a small steering engine in the back floats on the calm mirror of the sea. Its two seats are empty.
Zh: 一艘尾部装有小舵机引擎的小艇正漂浮在平滑如镜的海面上。里面有两个空座位。

En: SRV-made, this machine represents humanitarian aid from a workers' state on the Samaran isola. The *ROO* logo on the engine gives it away -- that and the white five-pointed star.
Zh: SRV制造，这台机器代表了萨马拉洲一个工人国家的人道主义援助。引擎上的*ROO*标志暴露了它的身份——除此之外，还有那颗白色的五角星。

En: This banged up old thing... it's a ROO A72.
Zh: “这个破旧的老东西……是ROOA72。”

En: You take the engine, Kim. I'll hold the boombox.
Zh: “你负责引擎，金。我拿手提音响。”

En: (I'm not ready yet.) [Leave.]
Zh: （我还没准备好。）[离开。]

En: It'll float.
Zh: “它能浮起来的。”

En: The boat's belly is a shiny yellow colour, industrial paint over fresh tar. You see it reflect off the water, along with the factory number: A72.
Zh: 船身是亮黄色的，新刷的柏油上面涂了一层工业油漆。你看着它倒映在水面上，还有出厂编号：A72。

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: What 'what'? How else do we blast *Sad FM* on our way to the island?
Zh: “什么‘什么’？不然我们怎么能在上岛的路上播*悲伤FM*呢？”

En: You heard me. Don't make me repeat myself.
Zh: “你已经听见了。别让我再重复一遍。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Fine. Why not? He shrugs.
Zh: “好吧。为什么不呢。”他耸耸肩。

En: First -- tie your porta-reel boombox to the breasthook to keep you company.
Zh: 首先——把你的手提音响绑在船首肘板上陪伴你。

En: There. This is now a *Rock and roll* skiff.
Zh: 好了。现在它是一艘*摇滚*小艇了。

En: Cool.
Zh: 酷啊。

En: What did I just do? Why did I do that?
Zh: 我刚刚做了什么？我为什么要这么做？

En: It'll keep you company on this lonely voyage.
Zh: 这样它就能陪伴你度过这段孤独的旅程。

En: Why? Because *Rock and Roll*.
Zh: 为什么？因为*摇滚*啊。

En: The boat comes to a slow stop. The lieutenant turns the engine off -- then there's silence.
Zh: 船慢慢停了下来。警督关掉引擎——接着，周围一片寂静。

En: The boat comes to a slow stop. You turn the engine off -- then there's silence.
Zh: 船慢慢停了下来。你关掉引擎——接着，周围一片寂静。

En: Get in and ride to the island.
Zh: 上船吧，朝小岛进发。

En: Let's go. He whispers.
Zh: “我们走吧。”他低语到。

En: [Climb out.]
Zh: [爬出船来。]

En: Once you get in, that's it -- one pull of the starter handle and you're off into the bay. A strange trepidation comes over you: are you sure you want to go *now*?
Zh: 一旦爬进去，没错——拉一下启动杆，就可以驶入海湾。一种奇怪的恐惧朝你袭来：你确定想*现在*过去吗？

En: In the silence -- a sputter of wings. A flock of quails takes off in the distance...
Zh: 在一片沉默之中——传来一阵翅膀的扑扇声。远方，一群鹌鹑起飞了……

En: There is very little wind here today. The ghost is standing still. You look at your arms, then the cliffs above you...
Zh: 今天这里的风很小。那个幽灵一动不动。你看着自己的胳膊，然后是头上的悬崖……

En: The skiff floats on the calm mirror of the sea -- its two seats are empty.
Zh: 小艇漂浮在平滑如镜的海面上——里面的两个座位是空的。

En: [Climb out.]
Zh: [爬出船来。]

En: [Climb out.]
Zh: [爬出船来。]

En: Gotta ride this one out.
Zh: “我得挺过去。”

En: Not sensing a lot of concept in this.
Zh: “没在里面感觉到太多概念。”

En: Where's my lighter?! Oh, holding it again.
Zh: “我的打火机去哪了？！哦，手里拿着呢。”

En: But are you *really* cornering the market is the question?
Zh: “但问题是，你*真的*操纵市场了吗？”

En: I should definitely start thinking about jogging again.
Zh: “我绝对应该考虑重新开始慢跑。”

En: Don't sit on it. Keep the hard stuff moving.
Zh: “别坐在那上面。让最难的东西动起来。”

En: It's a jungle out there... and in here.
Zh: “外面是危险的丛林……这里面也是。”

En: Pass it on, Rosie.
Zh: “传下去，罗西。”

En: Okay, here's your jacket. Fresh washed!
Zh: “好吧，这就是你的夹克。刚刚洗过的！”

En: I don't have any on me right now. (Lie.)
Zh: “现在我身上没有。”（撒谎。）

En: You've done a great service to the community, to the RCM and to Revachol.
Zh: “你对社区、RCM还有瑞瓦肖做出了巨大的贡献。”

En: That's it then. The deed is done.
Zh: “那就这样吧。大功告成。”

En: Mailbox, plaza -- got it! Bye! [Leave.]
Zh: “邮筒，广场——明白了！再见！”[离开。]

En: Thank you for your help. He turns to you. "Should we go and mail this? I think I saw a mailbox on the plaza."
Zh: “感谢你的服务。”他转向你。“我们把它寄出去吧？我好像看见广场上有邮筒。”

En: Hey, guys -- I'm a hero! He hands you the envelope, and waves his hand erratically at his companions.
Zh: “喂，伙计们——我是个英雄！”他把信封给你，然后朝着自己的同伴胡乱地挥了挥手。

En: I wanna ask these guys a couple of things first.
Zh: “我想先问这些家伙一些问题。”

En: Glad to have been of assistance! He gives you a thumbs up, and the envelope also. "After all this time, I feel like I've made a difference..."
Zh: “很高兴能帮到你！”他朝你竖起了大拇指，还有信封。“经过这么长时间以后，我觉得自己终于有所作为了……”

En: Hell yes! The red-nosed drunk brings his hand up to his head for a salute.
Zh: “是的！”红鼻子酒鬼把手举到头顶，行了一个礼。

En: Anything else this merry band of adventurers can do for you, or do you need to go and mail that serious looking document of yours? His face lightens up. "There's a mailbox on the plaza."
Zh: “这帮快活的冒险家还能为你做些什么吗，或者你需要把这份看起来很严肃的文件寄出去？”他的脸亮了起来。“我好像看见广场附近有个邮筒。”

En: The incapacitated drunk lays there snoring like an ancient rusting tractor.
Zh: 不能正常活动的醉汉躺在那里，像一台生锈的老拖拉机一样打着鼾。

En: Don't call Abigail! The drunk in the pipe snorts and takes a swig from his bottle. The bottle is empty.
Zh: “不要打给阿比盖尔！”管道里的醉汉哼了一声，拿起瓶子喝了一口。瓶子已经空了。

En: Okay, what's on your mind?
Zh: “好吧，你在想什么？”

En: My favourite was the gun he lost.
Zh: 我最喜欢的是他弄丢的枪。

En: My favourite is the gun.
Zh: 我最喜欢枪。

En: Don't know if I've mentioned it, but I used to be a businessman and *as* a businessman I am going to keep the pen for my trouble. He nods confidently.
Zh: “不知道我有没有提过，不过我曾经是一个非常成功的商人，而且，*作为*一个商人，我要留下这支笔来解决我遇到的麻烦。”他自信地点点头。

En: Uh, can I have the pen back too?
Zh: “呃，笔也可以还给我吗？”

En: Don't know if I've mentioned it, but I used to be a businessman and *as* a businessman I am going to keep the pen for my trouble. He nods confidently.
Zh: “不知道我有没有提过，不过我曾经是一个非常成功的商人，而且，*作为*一个商人，我要留下这支笔来解决我遇到的麻烦。”他自信地点点头。

En: Nooooo! That was my favourite thing, of all the things you have. This had better be worth losing the pen for.
Zh: 不！！！所有那些东西里面，它可是我最喜欢的一个。这最好比损失一支笔要更值得。

En: I don't think you're ready for that *grade* of reality yet. It'd be ill-advised for me to tell you. You gotta go do the *intro* yourself -- come back when you've been to the motor carriage, Tequila. He sips his beer.
Zh: 他喝了一口啤酒。“我觉得你还没准备好接受那种*级别*的现实。如果告诉你的话，我就太不明智了。你必须先了解*简介*——等你去汽車那边看过之后再回来吧，龙舌兰。”

En: Idiot Doom Spiral... I am ready now. I've seen it. Tell me the story of Tequila Sunset.
Zh: “白痴厄运漩涡……现在我已经准备好了。我见过了。跟我说说龙舌兰日落的故事吧。”

En: So, uh -- can you tell me the Tequila Sunset story now?
Zh: “所以——现在你能告诉我龙舌兰日落的故事了吗？”

En: Didn't I already say, I don't wanna be the one who breaks it to you? You have to get acquainted with *the event* first! It'd be irresponsible... The man shakes his head. His gray eyes look into yours.
Zh: 男人摇摇头。用灰色的眼睛凝视着你。“我不是说过了吗，我不想成为那个捅破它的人？你必须先熟悉一下那个*大事件*！不然就太不负责了……”

En: He points toward the waterline with his bottle.
Zh: 他用酒瓶指向水线的方向。

En: ...and also scary to tell you.
Zh: ……而且很害怕告诉你。

En: You need to face up to it. Go to the sea, inspect the machine.
Zh: 你必须面对。去大海那边，检查那台机器。

En: Please... please don't call... don't call... begs the man in the pipe.
Zh: “拜托……请不要打……不要打给……”管道里的男人祈求到。

En: Okay, we're drinking. We're drinking alcohol -- that's what we're doing. I *tried* to save the world once, a long time ago, with enterprise, creativity and willpower, but that didn't work out.
Zh: “好吧，我们在喝东西。在喝酒——我们就在做那个。我*试过*拯救世界，很久以前，用事业心、创造力和意志力，不过并没有成功。”

En: So now it's a pirate's life for me.
Zh: “所以现在，我过着海盗般的生活。”

En: Mhm. Let me take a sip to moisten up my cords... He takes a big sip, then begins: "Tequila Sunset rolled into Martinaise last Friday. And by *Tequila Sunset* I mean *you* -- the man, the myth."
Zh: “嗯。让我先小酌一口润润嗓子……”他灌了一大口酒，然后开始说道：“龙舌兰日落上周五来到马丁内斯。我说的*龙舌兰日落*其实指的就是*你*——那个男人，那个神话。”

En: It is.
Zh: 是的。

En: Your hands cramp on the steering levers.
Zh: 你放在操纵杆上的手在抽筋。

En: It's not meant as a joke. He's sorry for the Hermit Cop.
Zh: 这不是在开玩笑。他为隐士警探感到抱歉。

En: You also kept pausing to knock the heel of your hand against your temples, saying 'Boring, boring, boring.'
Zh: “你还会不断地停下来，一边用手指关节敲打自己的太阳穴，一边说‘无聊，无聊，无聊’。”

En: All kinds sir. Beer *and* wine.
Zh: “各种各样的都有，先生。啤酒*和*葡萄酒。”

En: What happened?
Zh: “发生什么事了？”

En: God bless them, though. I'd be alone without them. Anyway, that was all the story one bottle gets you. He turns his eyes to the bottle. "Almost empty this one..."
Zh: “不过，愿神明保佑他们。如果没有他们，我就真的是孤身一人了。总之，一瓶酒只能换来这么多故事。”他看向酒瓶。“这瓶都快空了……”

En: I'm almost homeless myself. Doesn't seem so bad.
Zh: “我基本上也算无家可归了。好像也没有很糟糕。”

En: He's right. It's incredibly annoying.
Zh: 他说得对。真的非常烦人。

En: You of all people should empathize with this. Perhaps this lost jacket is something you could help with...
Zh: 在所有人当中，最有共鸣的应该是你。也许你可以帮他找到那件丢失的夹克……

En: A lost jacket? Sounds like a mystery you could look into...
Zh: 一件丢失的夹克？听起来像是可以调查的谜题……

En: That's a lot of places.
Zh: “地方还不少啊。”

En: He really has no idea who this Ruby is, sire.
Zh: 他是真不知道露比是谁，大人。

En: I'm calling it -- it's neurological.
Zh: 我叫它——神经学。

En: ...the pause is long and dramatic...
Zh: ……这个停顿又长又富有戏剧性……

En: It's the *Headless FALN Rider*. A headless man wearing a FALN tracksuit. Riding a horse. In search for the FALN cap that went missing -- when he lost his head.
Zh: “是*无头FALN骑士*。一个穿着FALN运动服的无头人。骑着一匹马。在寻找他丢失的FALN帽子——是他失去头颅的时候不见的。”

En: A shovel hits the sand somewhere behind the reeds, near an abandoned construction yard. The young men look over their shoulders, suspiciously. The sound of their digging seems loud in the sudden silence.
Zh: 废弃的施工场地附近有一片芦苇丛，在那后面，一把铁锹铲进了沙地。年轻男子们怀疑地回过头去。在这片突如其来的寂静里，他们的挖掘声似乎格外响亮。

En: You know...
Zh: 你知道的……

En: I'm talking about...
Zh: 我说的是……

En: I'm not surprised. Many men find it hard reaching that final level of genius without blow.
Zh: “我并不惊讶。很多人发现自己很难达到天才的终极境界，如果没有白粉的话。”

En: All day, baby...
Zh: 我能吸上一整天，宝贝……

En: Wow.
Zh: “哇哦。”

En: No, he corrects you: "WOW!!"
Zh: “不，”他纠正你到：“是哇哦！！！”

En: And why wouldn't they, ey? Sounds like right strong stuff.
Zh: “为什么不可以呢？听起来像是很有力量的东西。”

En: I'm pretty sure they all just want to snort it, though.
Zh: “不过，我很肯定他们只是想吸一点……”

En: Bullshit. They just want to snort it. But you could beat them to it, Harry. *You* could snort the magic skull cocaine instead...
Zh: 胡说。他们只是想吸一点。但你可以赶在他们前面，哈里。*你*可以吸食魔力骷髅里的可卡因……

En: What do these expeditions plan to do with it?
Zh: “这些探险队打算用它做什么呢？”

En: The archaeologists say they want to put it in a museum. The gangsters say they want to sell it on the black market. And the ad agency guys say they're seeking inspiration...
Zh: “考古学家说想把它放进博物馆里。帮派分子说想拿到黑市上去卖。还有广告公司的人说他们在寻找灵感……”

En: And why do people think the skull is here, in Martinaise?
Zh: “为什么这些人认为骷髅会在这里，在马丁内斯呢？”

En: Here's the kicker -- this designer, this *lead designer* of a world famous design studio -- was *born* in Martinaise. A local boy. Martin Martinaise.
Zh: “这里就有意思了——这位设计师，这位世界知名设计工作室的*首席设计师*——*出生*在马丁内斯。一个本地的小伙子。马丁·马丁内斯。”

En: That's why he brought his studio here -- back to where it all began. And that's why  they buried him here too -- perhaps right under Abs' pipe there... he points to the pipe.
Zh: “所以他把工作室搬到了这里——回到了一切开始的地方。而且，这就是他们把他埋葬在这里的原因——也许就在阿比躺着的管道下面……”他指向管道。

En: Or probably further down the coast? Or in some yard in Martinaise proper. A hidden mausoleum? No one knows exactly.
Zh: “或者是在远处的海岸线上？或者是马丁内斯的某个院子里？一座隐藏的陵墓？没有人能确切地知道……”

En: No, my grandma' always told me his grave lays somewhere on the islets on the bay...
Zh: “不，我外婆总是说他的坟墓在海湾附近的小岛上……”

En: This is ludicrous. The lieutenant turns to you. "And physically impossible. Sinuses can't contain that amount of anything."
Zh: “这也太荒唐了。”警督转向你。“而且从身体结构上讲是不可能的。鼻窦容纳不了那么多东西。”

En: Now, now detective... always the sceptic.
Zh: 现在的警探啊……永远都是怀疑论者。

En: My only question is -- where does one get a *shovel*?
Zh: 我唯一的问题是——该去哪里弄*铁锹*呢？

En: In a pyramid? Now that *would* be something...
Zh: 在一座金字塔里？*那*就有点意思了……

En: He says it like it's obviously your name. Like you call someone Billy Brunuel or 'Leader of the Fourth Street Gang'.
Zh: 他说话的口气，好像它显然就是你的名字。就像你叫别人比利·布鲁尼尔或者‘四街帮头子’一样。

En: Good, these people know your *true* name. Looks like it has preceded you, Mr. Sunset. More on that later.
Zh: 很好，这些人知道你的*真名*。看来它已经领先你了，日落先生。之后再多了解一点。

En: I like this guy. You should, too. He *respects* you by calling you your *true name*.
Zh: 我喜欢这家伙。你也应该喜欢。他叫出了你的*真名*，以此表达对你的*尊重*。

En: This guy's your buddy-buddy. You feel it immediately: you belong to an organization. A fraternity. Of *drunks*.
Zh: 这家伙是你的好兄弟。你立马感觉到了：你属于一个组织。一个兄弟会。*醉鬼*兄弟会。

En: *Idiot Doom Spiral*, huh? This is bound to be a good, high-concept conversation. At last!
Zh: *白痴厄运漩涡*，嗯？这一定会是一场愉快的高概念对话。总算来了！

En: It's a touchy topic. He hasn't gotten laid in *ages*.
Zh: 这是个很敏感的话题。他已经*好多年*没有性生活了。

En: No-no. You need the *wisdom*.
Zh: 不不不。你需要这种*智慧*。

En: Oh yes, you do, *bratushka*. The only thing better than that is pushing the pedal to the metal after you kiss the tie. And off we go...
Zh: 是的，你懂，*兄弟*。唯一更棒的只有在亲吻领带之后，踩满油门，全速前进。我们准备出发……

En: No one's fucked me. *I* do the fucking around here.
Zh: “没人搞我。*我*自己在这周围游手好闲。”

En: Abigail...
Zh: “阿比盖尔……”

En: It's more like you were cramping theirs.
Zh: 更像是你在束缚他们。

En: Thank you for your services, gentlemen. He turns to you. "Should we go and mail this? I think I saw a mailbox on the plaza."
Zh: “感谢你们的服务，先生们。”他转向你。“我们把它寄出去吧？我好像在广场上看见过一个邮筒。”

En: Anything else this merry band of adventurers can do for you, or do you need to go and mail that serious looking document of yours? His face lightens up. "I think I saw a mailbox near the plaza."
Zh: “这帮快活的冒险家还能为你做些什么吗，或者你需要把这份看起来很严肃的文件寄出去？”他的脸亮了起来。“我好像看见广场附近有个邮筒。”

En: A few more questions first.
Zh: “先问几个问题再说。”

En: Mailbox... plaza... got it! [Leave.]
Zh: “邮筒……广场……明白了！”[离开。]

En: Okay, what's on your mind?
Zh: “好吧，你在想什么？”

En: The man mumbles incoherently.
Zh: 男人语无伦次地咕哝着。

En: Not exactly an expert forgery. Don't Call Abigail would have signed something more like *Domwtclall Abheqoeel*.
Zh: 并不是伪造方面的专家。不要打给阿比盖尔签的更像是*尔盖比阿给打要不*。

En: Nooooo! That was my favourite thing, of all the things you have.
Zh: 不！你所有那些东西里面，这可是我最喜欢的一个。

En: I got a freebie, when I purchased a whole carton of cigarettes. He produces a white pen with brown logos. "Smoke cigarettes, get a free pen. Deals don't come any sweeter."
Zh: “我买下一整箱香烟的时候，得到了一个免费赠品。”他拿出一只带棕色商标的白色钢笔。“抽烟，还能免费得到一支笔。没有比这更划算的交易了。”

En: The writing under the tiny crown logo reads *Royal Extra*.
Zh: 小皇冠商标下的文字是*精装皇家*。

En: My favourite is the gun.
Zh: 我最喜欢的枪。

En: My favourite is the gun he lost.
Zh: 我最喜欢的是他弄丢的枪。

En: Tequila... Tequila Sunset... Something ominous there.
Zh: 龙舌兰……龙舌兰日落……有种不祥的预兆。

En: Alright! He grabs the pen and paper and carefully, scribbles on the dotted line -- next to Lilienne's signature -- 'Idiot Doom Spiral'.
Zh: “好吧！”他小心地接过纸和笔，然后在虚线上，‘莉莉恩·卡特’的名字旁边，写下：‘白痴厄运漩涡’。

En: Thanks, Rosie. He grabs the pen and paper and carefully, scribbles on the dotted line -- next to Lilienne's signature -- 'Idiot Doom Spiral'.
Zh: “谢谢，罗西。”他小心地接过纸和笔，然后在虚线上，‘莉莉恩·卡特’的名字旁边，写下：‘白痴厄运漩涡’。

En: Do you know any Personal Brand Strategists? I need to start building my image. (Show him the certificate.)
Zh: “你认识个人品牌战略师吗？我得开始树立形象了。”（给他看看股票。）

En: He scans the document and lets out a long, low whistle. "Things have really turned around for you, haven't they? You've got *big money* problems now." He looks back at you, eyes gleaming.
Zh: 他看了看你手中的东西，然后用低沉的声音吹了个长长的口哨。“看来你真是变了不少啊，嗯？你现在可是有*大钱*了。”他回头看向你，眼中闪烁着光芒。

En: What do you need to know? He leans forward and takes another sip from his bottle, brows knitted together in mock seriousness.
Zh: “你想知道些什么？”他探身又抿了一口瓶子里的酒，紧锁着眉头，扮出一副正经的样子。

En: What you truly want to know is -- can you really trust your entire fortune to a drunk who lost his house keys while out for a jog?
Zh: 你真正想知道的是——真的能把自己的全副身家都交给一个出个门能把家里钥匙都弄丢的醉汉吗？

En: He doesn't exactly come across as responsible. Are you sure you can trust him with your *entire fortune*?
Zh: 他一看就不像是有责任感的人。你真的可以把*全副身家*都交付给他吗？

En: Let's talk about improving my personal brand again...
Zh: “我们再聊聊提升我个人形象的事情吧……”

En: Right on. He swigs his beer. "Have you made your mind up?"
Zh: “好啊。”他喝了一大口啤酒。“你决定好了吗？”

En: So I checked out the horseback monument...
Zh: “所以我去看了一下马背纪念碑……”

En: No one does it better than me, eh? He claps his hands in delight. "I was able to get it all together -- and *so fast* too. No wonder I used to be an industry leader..."
Zh: “没人比我做得更好了吧？”他高兴得拍起手来。“我什么都能搞定——而且速度还*相当快*。难怪我曾经是行业领军者……”

En: You want my opinion on the re-conceptualization?
Zh: “你想听听我对二次概念化的看法吗？”

En: Yes, show them *real* art, then they'll be able to appreciate the majesty of my finances.
Zh: “是啊，让他们看看*真正的*艺术，他们就会见识到我的财力有多雄厚。”

En: Here he is! The man, the reality, the *financial czar*. He presents you to the group as if on a stage. "You seen it yet?"
Zh: “他来了！这位真正的*金融之王*。”他如同站在舞台上一般介绍着你。“你们看见过吗？”

En: Yes, Tequila?
Zh: “嗯，龙舌兰？”

En: What can I do for you, boss-man?
Zh: “有什么吩咐，老板？”

En: Oh right. I had a quick look but I haven't really taken it in properly.
Zh: “哦，我就随便看了一下，不过没太上心。”

En: The re-conceptualization? I had a quick look but I haven't really taken it in properly.
Zh: “重新定义？我就随便看了一下，不过没太上心。”

En: Seen what?
Zh: “看见什么？”

En: Oh right. I've been busy with other things.
Zh: “哦，我刚刚一直在忙别的事情。”

En: The re-conceptualization? I've been busy with other things.
Zh: “二次概念化？我刚刚一直在忙别的事情。”

En: Oh, I see what you're doing. Keeping us waiting for the verdict, drawing it out -- I like it. Really builds up the suspense. He looks to his drinking partner. "Forever the showman, this one."
Zh: “哦，我知道你正在做什么了。让我们等着你宣布结果嘛——我喜欢这样。这样能增加悬念。”他望向自己的酒友。“这位是真正的表演者啊。”

En: He's covering up any nerves with the tried and true method -- excessive drinking.
Zh: 他在用惯用的方式掩饰自己酗酒。

En: He's struggling to cover up his nerves now that he's having *one* less beer a day.
Zh: 为了努力掩饰自己的紧张情绪，他现在每天少喝*一瓶*啤酒。

En: Well, no rush, but it'd be good to know what you make of it when you get a chance.
Zh: “好吧，不着急的。但有机会的话我还是想知道你的看法。”

En: The re-conceptualization of the monument? Your new reality situation? His words are stale with alcohol and utter disbelief.
Zh: “纪念碑的二次概念化？还是你现在的现实状况？”他的话语因酒精和缺乏信任而有些含糊。

En: *Other* things? We're talking about your big moment here, your time to shine, your ascension into a household name.
Zh: “*别的*事情？我们现在谈论的是你的重要时刻啊，让你一举成名的闪耀时刻啊。”

En: Oh right. Yeah, I've seen it.
Zh: “哦，嗯，我见过。”

En: The re-conceptualization? Yeah, I've seen it.
Zh: “二次概念化？哦，我见过。”

En: Great! He scans your face for any telltale signs of a verdict. "Great."
Zh: “太好了！”他打量着你的脸，观察你是否在认真回答“很好。”

En: Yeah, two secs. He slurps from his bottle then wipes droplets from his beard with a sleeve. Satisfied, his stomach sends a muted eruption to his throat, where it turns into a damp growl.
Zh: “对，两秒。”他啜了一口酒，然后用袖子擦去胡须上的酒滴。心满意足之后，一股气流从胃里直冲嗓门，他打了个嗝。

En: Right, let's hear you then. I put a lot of work into that project, Tequila, a *lot of work*. Had to bring back my hustle game and call in some old partners to pull it off. So, c'mon, tell me what I want to hear.
Zh: “好吧，那我们就来听听你的看法吧。我在那个项目上可下了不少功夫呢，龙舌兰，*不少功夫*啊。我得重拾旧业，还要去找老朋友帮忙。行了，说些我想听的话吧。”

En: It's good but the timing is unfortunate.
Zh: “没问题，但现在不是时候。”

En: It doesn't quite meet my expectations.
Zh: “这跟我期望中完全不一样。”

En: It's gonna have to wait. I have important business to attend to first. (Put it off indefinitely.)
Zh: “那你得等等了。我还有更重要的事情要去处理。”（无限期推迟。）

En: What important business? I'm your *Personal Brand Strategist*, Tequila -- you're supposed to run all business matters by me. That's how this works.
Zh: “什么重要的事情？我是你的*个人品牌战略师*啊，龙舌兰——你应该把所有重要的事情都交给我去处理，这样才对。”

En: It's police business.
Zh: “我说的是警务工作。”

En: This is different...
Zh: “这个事情不一样……”

En: They finally caught up to you, have they?
Zh: “他们最后还是找上你了？”

En: Something like that...
Zh: “差不多吧……”

En: Yes, they have, the lieutenant says, unamused.
Zh: “是的，没错，”警督一脸不快地说道。

En: Alright, I understand. We'll do this some other time, when you can focus on yourself and not this *inconvenience*.
Zh: “好吧，我明白了。我们改天再谈吧。希望那时候你能把注意力放在自己身上，而不是*麻烦*上面。”

En: Painful images replay in the lieutenant's mind as he lies still in his sick bed: gun fumes, the flash of metal, and occasionally a clumsy stack of scaffolding...
Zh: 当警督静静躺在病床上时，一些痛苦的画面在他的脑海中重现：火药的浓烟、金属的光泽，不时还会浮现一堆碍手碍脚的脚手架……

En: You hear the lieutenant suppress an almighty scoff.
Zh: 你听见警督正强忍着笑。

En: Different *how*? Business is business. Doesn't matter if it's small or large, I handle it for you now -- that's what you hired me to do.
Zh: “*怎么*不一样了？大小生意都是生意。我现在替你办事——你雇我来就是做这个的。”

En: (Applaud.) "I love it. Pure, naked brilliance."
Zh: （鼓掌。）“我很喜欢。纯粹，简直精彩。”

En: It's an all-round disaster. Just awful.
Zh: “简直就是灾难，糟糕透了。”

En: You love it? Shit, Tequila, that's just made my day. His face lights up as his drinking buddy gives him a congratulatory pat on the back.
Zh: 他喜笑颜开。“你喜欢吗？哎呀，龙舌兰，我太高兴了。”他的酒友拍了拍他的背，表示祝贺。

En: What do you mean? The timing couldn't be *better*. The traffic through town will return now that the strike's over. Don't worry, I've *thought* about this.
Zh: “你这话什么意思？现在的时机*再好不过*了。随着罢工结束，城里的交通也会恢复正常。不用担心，这些我都已经*考虑过*了。”

En: That's... disappointing. But, okay, I can work with that. I'm used to dealing with fussy clients and retrogressing from my highest concepts for them. Can't expect everyone to be on... He points at himself with both thumbs. "...*this* level."
Zh: “真是……让人失望啊。不过没事，我可以处理的。我已经习惯和那些挑剔的客人打交道，并为他们从最高级的概念上做出让步了。我不能指望所有人都……”他用拇指指着自己。“……达到*这个*水平。”

En: You're kidding me? This is a joke? He hangs a confused look off you. "You're  being serious? Alright..." He takes a gulp of beer.
Zh: “你在和我开玩笑吗？这是玩笑吗？”他迷惑地看着你。“你是认真的？好吧……”他喝下一大口啤酒。

En: He dares to sit there in disbelief when you confront him about his own *scam*!
Zh: 在你质疑他的*骗局*时，他居然敢坐在那儿一脸怀疑！

En: Be *professional*. He probably doesn't even know what happened to you, why the strike is over...
Zh: *专业*一点吧。他甚至都不知道你身上发生了什么事情，还有为什么罢工会结束……

En: It's bad PR and therefore bad for business. Tell him you don't want anything to do with it -- or him.
Zh: 这种公关很糟糕，也会对生意不利。告诉他你不想和这种东西——也不想和他扯上关系。

En: Plastic bags heavy with tins and deformed vegetables swing from the fists of a young mother crossing a roundabout. Her mouth falls open as she looks up at the central structure. 'C'mere, Luc. Over here now, please,' she says to her curious toddler. The bags beat steady bruises into the mother's calves as she hurries him away.
Zh: 一位年轻的母亲路过环岛，手中提着大大小小的塑料袋，里面装着一堆罐头和奇形怪状的蔬菜。她看着眼前的东西，嘴巴张得老大。‘来，卢克，到这儿来，’她对她好奇的孩子说道。她匆忙催促孩子赶紧离开这儿，手中的口袋将她的腿拍得青一块紫一块。

En: So where does this leave us? Do you still want me as your Personal Brand Strategist or what?
Zh: “那我们怎么办？你还想让我做你的个人品牌战略师吗？”

En: The utter *genius* of its design -- conveyed beautifully through his commitment to a singular aesthetic. He's gone above and beyond!
Zh: 这种设计真是*天才*——正是因为他对独特审美的执着才能如此优美地传达出来。他真是太棒了！

En: What more could you want? He got your brand out there while giving back to the community. He hit the brief perfectly.
Zh: 你还想怎样？他在回报社区的同时也帮你打出了名声。他做得非常好。

En: It's an image of you that will stand the test of time. People will remember this for an age.
Zh: 你的形象能经受住时间的考验。人们会永远铭记它。

En: It's like he knows *nothing* about you. Stinks of a man doing what's best for him, not his clientele.
Zh: 他好像对你*一无所知*。他总是为自己考虑，完全不管他的客户。

En: Picking the right praise to give is just as important as picking the right critique. People shape themselves according to any and all feedback they get.
Zh: 选择合适的赞美言辞和选择合适的批判言辞同样重要。人们可以根据反馈获得提高。

En:
I've never seen marketing as inspired as that. It really is *high concept*!	“我从没见过这样优秀的营销。这可真是*高端概念*啊！”
0x0100000F00016032	Dialogue Text	VILLAGE / IDIOT DOOM SPIRAL	You	This is an event. It's *history* -- people will remember this."
Zh: “这将成为*历史性*事件——人们会永远铭记的。”

En: I'm amazed you were able to do it so well and for so cheap.
Zh: “用这么少的钱就能完成得这么好，真厉害。”

En: It's exactly the brand image I want to present to the public.
Zh: “这就是我想向大众展示的品牌形象。”

En: It's a load of shit! I knew you'd rip me off.
Zh: “这就是坨狗屎！我就知道你在骗我的钱。”

En: I don't want anything to do with it. You have to take it down.
Zh: “我不想和这玩意儿扯上关系，你快去把它撤掉。”

En: Your timing is awful! People died over there, then you go and do *that*.
Zh: “你选的时机太糟糕了！那里正在有人死去，结果你过去做了*那些事*。”

En: That isn't the right image of me to present to the public.
Zh: “我不想向公众展现这样的形象。”

En: 'Awful' is very strong, Tequila. You're gonna have to explain how I missed the mark by *so much* if that's the case. What don't you like about it?
Zh: “说‘糟糕’就太严重了，龙舌兰。既然这样，你就得和我说说我究竟为什么*那么*不合你意。你为什么不喜欢它？”

En: Gimme more, gimme more. What do you like most about it? He's giddy -- both from the drink and the anticipation.
Zh: “多说些，多和我说些。你最喜欢它什么地方？”在酒精和期待的双重作用下，他兴奋不已。

En: Let your Personal Brand Strategist handle the timing, alright? You just focus on telling me what you liked about my work up there.
Zh: “让你的个人品牌战略师来把控时机好吗？你只要告诉我你对什么地方满意就行。”

En: Bet you're glad you hired me as your Personal Brand Strategist now? So, what's next, boss?
Zh: “你现在肯定很高兴雇佣了我来做你的个人品牌战略师吧？下一步怎么做，老板？”

En: The wooden clack of bilboquet comes to a halt before the central structure of a roundabout. Awe whistles through the air as the onlooker takes in every angle. Eager eyes fill nearby windows, brought to the glass by the sound of the toy, but now also taking in the sight of the striking new installation.
Zh: 杯球发出的木质咔哒声在中央建筑面前倏然停止。四面八方的观众们充满惊叹的口哨声在空中回响。人们被玩具的声响吸引至窗前，附近的窗户上贴满了一双双渴望的眼睛，与此同时，他们也见到了那惊人的新装置艺术。

En: Go on then, what exactly isn't doing it for you? He wraps both hands around his bottle and leans forward, ready to listen.
Zh: 说完，他双手握紧酒瓶，倾身向前想要听你的回复。“继续说吧，你究竟觉得哪方面不合适呢？”

En: I knew it! That's what you get when you hire me -- customer satisfaction guaranteed. Woo!
Zh: “我就知道会这样！既然你雇佣了我，我就必须让你满意。喔哦！”

En: Didn't I tell you? He turns to his drinking buddy. "I told him, didn't I? Several times. This is what I *do*."
Zh: “我没和你说过吗？”他转向酒友。“我还和你说过很多次，对吧？我就是这么*做事*的。”

En: Fuck yeah they will. Tequila Sunset is on the timeline. They'll be talking about you for years. The one and only.
Zh: “他们当然会的。龙舌兰日落是要写进历史的。你会是他们经久不衰的谈论对象。”

En: That's how I roll. Best results at a low cost. Isn't that what the business game is all about? Let me tell you -- it is! I'm its biggest and best player. He winks at you.
Zh: 他朝你眨眨眼。“这就是我的行事风格。以最低成本获取最好结果。这才叫商业游戏啊，对吧？我来告诉你吧，这就是！我是这场游戏中最优秀的玩家。”

En: Take it down? Is it really that bad? He drags a hand down his face. "This is *fucking me up*, Tequila. I thought you'd love it -- honestly."
Zh: “撤掉？真有那么差劲吗？”他把一只手从脸上放下。“这就让我*很糟心*了啊，龙舌兰。说实话，我以为你会喜欢呢。”

En: What are you talking about? Who died? I think I would know if anyone had died over there. Would have heard *something* about it. There's no need to make up little stories, Tequila.
Zh: “你说什么呢？谁死了？如果有人死在那儿我肯定会知道，也肯定会*听说*吧。没必要编故事啊，龙舌兰。”

En: It isn't? I thought that's what you were all about. He drags a hand down his face. "This is *fucking me up*, Tequila. I thought you'd love it -- honestly."
Zh: “不合你意吗？我以为你就是这意思呢。”他把一只手从脸上放下。“这就让我*很糟心*了啊，龙舌兰。说实话，我以为你会喜欢呢。”

En: What are you talking about? *Rip you off*? Stop with this bullshit. Just say you don't like it. There's no need to drag my name through the mud like this.
Zh: “你说什么呢？*骗你的钱*？别胡说八道了。你直接说不喜欢就得了，没必要还给我安个污名啊。”

En: Sure am! Not sure what's next yet but I know you'll nail it.
Zh: “当然了！虽然不知道下一步该怎么做，但是我知道你一定会大获成功的。”

En: Actually, I think you've done enough for me. I no longer need you.
Zh: “其实你已经为我做了很多事情了，我不需要你了。”

En: I do. I'm willing to give you another chance.
Zh: “嗯，我愿意再给你一次机会。”

En: Nope. I'm going to have to let you go.
Zh: “不了，我不需要你了。”

En: You bet your ass I will. Tequila Sunset is just getting started. No one's ready for the shit we're going to pull. He beats his chest a couple of times. "Feels good to be back."
Zh: “那绝对的。龙舌兰日落才刚刚起步呢。大家都还没准备好迎接我们准备做的事呢。”他拍了拍胸脯。“回归的感觉真棒。”

En: Good one, boss. He laughs weakly but stops when he realises you're being serious. "What -- really? That's it? You can't cut it short *now*. We're just getting started. At least give me an explanation."
Zh: “真有你的，老板。”他有气无力地笑了笑，但笑容在他发现你是认真的后便消失了。“什么——真的吗？就这样？你不能*现在*结束啊。我们才刚刚开始呢。你至少得给我一个解释吧。”

En: Phew! Alright, you had me going there. I thought that was it for me -- back to rock bottom I go! He lets out a nervous laugh.
Zh: “哦！好吧，话既然已经说到这个份上。那就这样吧——我又跌回人生的低谷了！”他局促不安地笑起来。

En: Fuck... He lets his head drop between his arms. "Well, that's that. At least I tried." A long and heavy breath out, a few sloppy sucks on his bottle.
Zh: “干……”他把头埋在手臂间。“好吧，就这样吧。至少我努力过了。”他长叹一声，然后胡乱地喝了几口酒。

En: Thanks for sticking with me. I'll make sure I do better in the future. He exhales with huge relief. "Let me make it up to you. Anything I can do right now?"
Zh: “谢谢你愿意继续和我合作。以后我一定会做得更好。”他松了一大口气。“让我来补偿你吧，需要我为你做什么吗？什么都可以。”

En: Thanks for the opportunity. Gutted that I fucked it up... but what have I really lost? He tries to shrug into a more carefree existence.
Zh: “谢谢你给我这个机会，虽然我把事情搞砸了……但是我又失去了什么呢？”他耸了耸肩，摆出一副悠闲的姿态。

En: Fine. You know what? Fuck it. You'll be back, Tequila. You wanna go -- you go. This isn't the end. I'm taking you all the way to the top.
Zh: “行吧。我告诉你吧，妈的，你肯定会回来的，龙舌兰。你想走——就走吧。事情还没结束。我会让你走上人生巅峰的。”

En: Thanks for this, boss. Just what I needed. Is there anything else I can do for you right now?
Zh: “谢谢你，老板。正合我意。现在还有什么需要我做的吗？”

En: You wince as your injured leg throbs with fresh pain. The drunk doesn't notice.
Zh: 你受伤的腿又痛了起来，你的脸部抽搐着，可这醉汉根本没注意到。

En: Pretty sure you could dial up the melodrama to max and he still wouldn't figure it out.
Zh: 就算你把这场闹剧推动到极致，他依然不会明白。

En: The market moves fast and with extreme casualty.
Zh: “市场变化太快，损失惨重。”

En: He better not let you down again. Remember, this is *your* money and *your* reputation he's playing with.
Zh: 他最好别让你再失望一次。你得记住，他玩弄的是*你的*钱和*你的*声誉。

En: Calm down! I'm only yanking your leg. Of course I'm not firing you.
Zh: “冷静点！我只是开个玩笑而已。当然不会解雇你。”

En: He freezes in his seat, motionless except his pupils, which quickly flick from side to side.
Zh: 他一动不动地坐在位子上，眼神不停闪烁着。

En: Fucking hell, Tequila. Don't do that to me. I appreciate being kept on my toes, but *man*! He shakes his head to straighten himself out.
Zh: “操，龙舌兰。别这样对我。我很感谢你能让我忙起来，但是*哥们啊*！”他摇摇脑袋，想让自己清醒起来。

En: Is he reading a book about his life so far to figure out which reality he's in?
Zh: 他是靠阅读自己的人生传记来搞清楚自己处境的吗？

En: He doesn't know whether to expunge or consume. What will pacify the wrenching feeling in his gut?
Zh: 他不知道是该放弃还是挥霍。什么才能平复他内心的痛苦？

En: He cracks open a bottle and sucks the bitter foam welling at the top. "Do you want anything else or can I concentrate on my drinking?"
Zh: 他打开一瓶酒，吸着瓶口冒出的苦涩泡沫。“你还想要什么吗？我能专心喝酒了吗？”

En: Business buddies, huh? This guy might be just what we're looking for. Ask him if he knows any Personal Brand Strategists who can help you out.
Zh: 商业合作伙伴？这家伙可能就是我们想找的人。问问他是不是认识能帮到你的个人品牌战略师吧。

En: What are you doing?!
Zh: “你这是干嘛？！”

En: It's Idiot Doom Spiral -- he's *back*. Like a beast made of jaundice and fetid polypropylene, he returns locked in stiff combat and with a most fearsome foe...
Zh: 是白痴厄运漩涡——他*回来了*。他像是一只由黄疸和恶臭的聚丙烯组成的怪兽，回来迎战最可怕的对手……

En: Let's just move on. Anything else you want from me?
Zh: “我们继续吧。还有什么需要我帮忙的吗？”

En: Do you want anything else? If not, I've got some serious drinking to do...
Zh: “你还有什么需要的？没有的话，我就要好好喝酒了……”

En: Wait, on the second thought -- I've heard enough.
Zh: “等等，再仔细一想——我已经听够了。”

En: So you *don't* want to just hear the same story again? Okay, okay. Huh. He looks taken aback.
Zh: “所以你*不想*把同样的故事再听一遍？好吧，好吧。嗯。”他看起来有些惊讶。

En: Don't be shy, Tequila. Of course I'll do it. He ploughs a thumb into his chest. "I'm your man."
Zh: “别害羞，龙舌兰。我当然会这么做了。”他扬起大拇指戳了戳胸膛。“我可是你的人。”

En: What do you mean you're 'my man'?
Zh: “你说你是‘我的人’是什么意思？”

En: Sorry, I'm already taken. (Nod at Kim.)
Zh: “抱歉，我名草有主了。”（对金点点头。）

En: No, you've misunderstood me -- I want you to *recommend* a Personal Brand Strategist, not become one.
Zh: “不，你误会了——我是让你给我*推荐*一位个人品牌战略师，不是让你当策划师。”

En: Tequila, Tequila... You don't know what you're missing out on. Those other guys? They'll just steal your money while providing meagre service.
Zh: “龙舌兰啊龙舌兰……你都不知道你错过了什么好东西。就那些家伙？他们骗走你的钱却给不了你什么服务。”

En: Now *me*, on the other hand? I only provide *very* high-concept ideas. And as I already understand *your* needs I can marry them with your brand easily. I'll help you make your mark.
Zh: “可是*我呢*？我只为你提供*极*高质量的点子。因为我了解*你的*需求，可以轻而易举把它们和你的个人形象结合起来，帮你一举成名。”

En: The lieutenant is about to say something, but gets cut off...
Zh: 警督本想说点什么，可是却被打断……

En: But I've got something the others don't. I'm on *your* level. And as I already understand *your* needs I can marry my high-concept ideas with your brand easily. I'll help you make your mark.
Zh: “别人可没有我的本事。我能配得上*你的*水准。而且我了解*你的*需求，我可以轻而易举把它们和你的个人形象结合起来，帮你一举成名。”

En: Your Personal Brand Strategist man. Now I know I'm not your 'typical' choice. I don't wear a suit, I'm not a hundred years old, I'm not about to die tomorrow...
Zh: “你的个人品牌战略师啊。现在的我并不是你的*最优*选择。我又不是西装革履的人，没活到一百岁，也不会明天就死了……”

En: Not so sure about the last part, with all that drinking...
Zh: 最后一点还真说不准，毕竟他喝了那么多酒……

En: Listen, I'm not about to come between you two. I know my place, eh? I'll just be your Personal Brand Strategist man. Your high-concept idea generator. Yeah?
Zh: “我不想妨碍你们，我也知道自己的身份。我只是想当你的个人品牌战略师，当你优秀的智囊而已，可以吗？”

En: *Great* can-do attitude! A billion reál mindset. Clinically *insane* energy! Hire him on the spot!!
Zh: 这种积极乐观的态度*太棒了*！如此价值连城的心态，近乎*疯狂*的活力！你应该立即雇佣他！！

En: That's it, you're *hired*!
Zh: “好了，你被*雇佣*了！”

En: This voice in my head tells me to hire you, but I'm not so sure...
Zh: “我脑海中有个声音让我雇佣你，可是我不确定……”

En: No, Tequila, no... He sighs heavily. "I'm a *professional*, you see. I insist that you give me a formal interview. It wouldn't be fair on the other candidates if you didn't."
Zh: “不，龙舌兰，不……”他深深叹了口气。“我是一名*专业人士*，你明白吗。我希望你能给我安排一场正式的面试。否则对其他人来说不公平。”

En: Relevant experience?
Zh: “你有相关经验吗？”

En: I have reservations about your... personal responsibility.
Zh: “我对你的个人责任感……持保留意见。”

En: How does your drinking impact your work performance?
Zh: “酗酒会影响你的工作表现吗？”

En: Alright, give me your best pitch.
Zh: “好了，亮出你的真本事吧。”

En: Sure, my man, let's circle back whenever you want. He hiccups. "Take your time. I'll be here."
Zh: “行吧，伙计，你可以随时回来和我谈谈。”他打着嗝说道。“请便吧，反正我一直在这儿。”

En: Let's come back to this later.
Zh: “以后再说吧。”

En: Grill him a bit, see how he performs under pressure.
Zh: 严厉盘问他，看他在压力之下表现如何。

En: Like what -- any examples?
Zh: “比如呢？举个例子？”

En: I used to be a moderately-high-net-worth individual myself, if I can say so. I've *managed* big money before, played the game, hustled the movers of the marketing world...
Zh: “我曾经也是一个挥金如土的高端人士。我过去也*赚过*大钱，经常和营销界的大人物们打交道……”

En: Remember when Maria Kapaleva caused an uproar by walking the red carpet in those slippers -- the ones with the stick-on jiggly tits?
Zh: “你还记得玛利亚·卡帕列娃曾经穿着一双粘有大奶子的拖鞋走红毯而引起轰动的事情吗？”

En: Numbers? How about fifteen fucking years of jamming *art* into people's heads? That's hundreds of high-concept ideas developed *and* executed for those fancy corp boys in their suits. The result? He leans back, ready for his mic-drop...
Zh: “数据？十五年里用*艺术*给别人洗脑算不算？我帮那些纨绔子弟们想出*并*执行了数百个优秀的点子。结果呢？”他靠在椅背上，准备结束演讲……

En: I need numbers -- years of service, P&L statements...
Zh: “我需要了解数据——你的任职时间、损益报告……”

En: A subtle smile lights up his puffy face, like a ray of sun breaking through the stratosphere. He likes reminiscing.
Zh: 他浮肿的脸上露出一抹淡淡的笑容，如同一缕穿过云层的阳光。他喜欢追忆。

En: At the Boom Boom Room I directed a team of *talent architects*. People so high concept their names were nothing but a single letter. We had *manic* ideas, fuelled by copious amounts of coffee-flavoured whey powder. We did stuff no one had ever seen before -- or *since*!
Zh: “我曾经在砰砰砰带领过一支*天才建筑师*团队。大家走的都是高端概念路线，所以他们的名字都只用一个字母代替。在大量咖啡味乳清粉的催动下，我们有过许多*疯狂的*想法。我们做出过许多前无古人，*后无来者*的事情！”

En: We're going to need examples here -- just going by one of your initials doesn't cut it.
Zh: 我们需要的是例子——说得那么粗略是不够的。

En: You're after measurable performance, not tales of happy memories. Numbers, please! How many years did he work, what profits did he earn?
Zh: 你要听的是他那些可以用来评估的表现，而不是聆听他快乐的回忆。我们需要的是数据！比如他工作了多少年，赚了多少钱？

En: That was *me*. I did that. He looks up at the sky, victorious and content like an old cat. "We sold a lot of slippers that year..."
Zh: “那是*我*的杰作。”他满意地抬头望向天空，如同一只老猫。“那一年我们卖出去了好多拖鞋……”

En: Yes, yes we *do* remember that...
Zh: 是啊，是啊，我们*当然*记得……

En: No, we don't?
Zh: 不，好像不记得？

En: But why don't we remember that??!
Zh: 可为什么我们不记得呢？？！

En: It's because it's not *cool* or *stylish*. It's lame and *out* -- even Ms. Kapaleva couldn't pull it off.
Zh: 正是因为这个点子一点都不*酷*，也不*有型*。蹩脚又*过时*——即使是Kapaleva小姐也没能靠它出彩。

En: Alright, here we go -- Ms. Kapaleva (née Olga Grebennikova) was once known as East Revachol's premier vedette and sex symbol. Her role in director Do Do Chakan's sexploitation motion picture 'Psychotronic Stripper' launched her into stardom. She's been voted Most Captivating Woman by La Fayette *three years* running.
Zh: 是这样的——卡帕列娃小姐（又名奥尔加·格雷苯尼科娃）曾被称为东瑞瓦肖首屈一指的美女兼性感女神。她在咚咚恰卡导演的三级片《灵子脱衣舞女》中的角色让她一举成名。她连续*三年*被拉法叶评为最迷人的女性。

En: Three years, you hear that? Three years... how hot did she have to be?!
Zh: 三年，你听到没？三年哎……她得多性感才能有这样的成就啊？！

En: *Millions* of reál generated for clients. I've made my name in this world.
Zh: “我从客户那儿赚来的钱有*好几百万*。我在这个领域已经很出名了。”

En: And then he lost it all by going for a jog... not exactly *inspiring*.
Zh: 然后他就因为一次慢跑，失去了这一切……真是*无趣*。

En: Tequila, Tequila... Whatever's going on in my reality situation doesn't have any bearings on yours. This is all about *you* and *your* future. Ask me about *that*.
Zh: “龙舌兰啊，龙舌兰……我遭遇的事情和你一点关系都没有。我们现在聊的是*你*和*你的*未来。请问我有关*那些*的事情吧。”

En: Okay, okay, let me get a sip first... He manages to down half a bottle, then sits up straight, a hazy but oddly determined stare in his eyes. His finger shoots up.
Zh: “好了，好了，先让我喝口酒……”他一下子喝了半瓶酒，然后身子坐得笔直。

En: You see, net worth means *nothing* to the people of Martinaise. Nada. Null. For them to see your wealth, to really *respect* that, you need to convert it into something real, something *tangible*. The bigger the better. He peers into his bottle for inspiration.
Zh: “身份对马丁内斯的人来说*不算什么*。什么都不是。为了让人们看到你的财富，真正*尊重*你，你就需要把这些东西转变成一些实实在在的*有形之物*，而且这东西越大越好。”他紧盯着酒瓶，仿佛在寻找启发。

En: The horseback monument! He snaps his fingers. "That's the centre of the district -- they'd *have* to pay attention to that. We could get your name up there in lights. Damn thing's due for a *re-conceptualization* anyway."
Zh: “马背纪念碑！”他打了个响指。“就在这片区域的中心——人们*必定*会留意到它。我们可以把你名字用灯光打到上面去。反正那破东西该*重新定义*一下了。”

En: What are you talking about? I'm the hottest shit in town. I'm as *real* as it gets.
Zh: “你说什么呢？我才是城里最性感、最*真实*的人。”

En: There's nothing more real than the *End of Everything*.
Zh: “没有什么比*万物终结*更真实了。”

En: Yeah, okay. Real like paying your bills or eating malt fibre in the morning.
Zh: “是的，好吧。就像你的账单和早上吃的高纤麦片一样真实。”

En: To never even *grasp* the idea of economic nonentities... I feel sorry for them.
Zh: “绝不去*理解*小人物的想法……我为他们感到抱歉。”

En: You're aware that monument is *supposed* to look like it's exploding? It doesn't need renovating.
Zh: “你也注意到那座纪念碑表现的*就是*爆炸的瞬间吧？根本没必要翻新。”

En: I didn't say renovating, I said *re-conceptualization*. It's medium-concept shit is what it is, he says, spitting out each word.
Zh: “我没说翻新，我说的是*重新定义*。这是一种媒介概念，”他一字一句说道。

En: Hmm... the level of conceptualization *could* always be higher, yes.
Zh: 嗯……概念化的层次*总是*高一些的。

En: Drinking is the fuel to my fire. Moistens me up to ensure the high concepts have a smooth landing. He tries taking another sip from the bottle, but misses and pours some of its contents inside his collar.
Zh: “喝酒是在给我添柴加火，酒精可以滋润我的大脑，让我能构思出更高端的概念。”他本想再喝一口酒，可是没喝上，还把一点酒洒进了衣领。

En: But what would it cost?
Zh: 那这要花多少钱呢？

En: Ah, shit...
He could definitely work more on his Hand/Eye Coordination...
Zh: 他真应该好好磨练下自己的眼明手巧技能……

En: How much is this going to cost me?
Zh: “这要花我多少钱？”

En: We really haven't got time for this, the lieutenant says, getting impatient. "In case you've forgotten, we're supposed to be investigating a murder."
Zh: “我们真没时间讨论这种事情，”警督不耐烦地说道。“别忘了我们是来调查一起谋杀案的。”

En: Nothing up front. That's 'cause I always put the client first. My cut'll come out of the back-end, *after* we've made you a sensation....
Zh: “你不用提前支付。因为我永远把客户放在首位。在我帮你造成轰动*之后*，我再提成就行……”

En: And forget about contractors and the like. This job I'm handling *personally*, concept and execution, he continues. "Any unexpected expenses we'll take care of later. Can't let price compromise the work. Easy."
Zh: “别去考虑那些合约商了。从构思概念和执行都会是我*一手操办*，”他继续说道。“那些计划之外的花销我们晚点再考虑吧。不能让价格影响工作，就这么简单。”

En: Okay, let's do it, you're hired! (Accept his offer.)
Zh: “好，那就这么办，你被雇佣了！”（接受他的提议。）

En: You should hire him and ask him to stop drinking. Everyone deserves a second (or third) chance.
Zh: 你应该雇佣他，并让他不再酗酒。每个人都应该拥有第二次（甚至第三次）机会。

En: That's it, I'm *BACK*! He takes a celebratory swig -- a *big* one. Too big. The brown liquid cascades down his face and floods his nostrils. Once he's finished choking he spits on the ground.
Zh: “很好，我*回归*了！”他猛喝了*一大口*酒表示庆祝。这口酒太大，以至于啤酒顺着他的脸流进他的鼻孔里。恢复过来后，他往地上啐了一口。

En: Sorry about that, my friend. This isn't about me, this is about *you*, of course. A chance for you to give back to the community. To get your brand out there. To teach them your *name*. He spreads his hands across the sky...
Zh: “不好意思啊，朋友。这不是我的事情，而是*你的*事情。这是你回报社会、让你的形象脱颖而出，让大家都认识*你*的机会。”他将双手伸向天空……

En: I can already see it -- Tequila Sunset -- spelled out in cathode rays. Luminous against the evening sky. There won't be a single kid in this town who doesn't know who you are.
Zh: “我已经看到了用阴极射线拼出来的字——龙舌兰日落，在夜空中闪耀光芒。城里所有小孩都会知道你是谁。”

En: This is a good moment to bring up his drinking... He'll get nothing done if he continues like that.
Zh: 正好趁这机会和他谈谈酗酒的问题…如果他继续酗酒，将一事无成。

En: One more thing -- you're going to have to cut back on the booze.
Zh: “还有一件事——你得少喝酒了。”

En: Whoa there! The man looks taken aback, hands high in the air. Tiny droplets of beer flicker in his beard like miniscule pearls.
Zh: “哇啊！”他双手举到半空，一脸惊讶。啤酒在他的胡须上闪烁着，如同小小的珍珠。

En: Yeah, whoa there! What is this, the fun police?!
Zh: 哈，哇啊！什么情况，要扮有趣的警察吗？！

En: No need to worry about *that*, he says, his inebriated mouth twisting into odd shapes, "I've got it *all* under control. Besides, it helps with my creativity. Gets the business juices flowing, y'know?" He finishes it off with a wink.
Zh: “你不需要担心*这个*，”他喝了酒的嘴摆出奇怪的形状，“*一切*都在我的掌控之中。而且喝酒还能提高我的创造力。让我的想法源源不断，你懂吗？”说完他还眨了眨眼。

En: Good, when can you start work?
Zh: “好吧，你什么时候可以开始工作？”

En: Give me half an hour, my friend. In fact, he raises his grubby finger again, "I demand you to *sit down here* and *not move* until I'm back. Open a beer, chat with your old pals..."
Zh: “再给我半个小时吧，朋友。其实吧，”他再次举起他肮脏的手指，“我希望要你*坐在这儿**别动*，直到我回来。开瓶小酒，和你的老朋友聊聊天什么的……”

En: The lieutenant massages his temples and looks down at the ground.
Zh: 警督揉了揉太阳穴，望向地面。

En: *Relax*. I see you both running around like maniacs, just back and forth, back and forth. Gotta live a little, enjoy the sun.
Zh: “*放松点*。我见你们两人像疯子一样忙前忙后跑来跑去的。你们应该享受生活，享受阳光。”他转头将臃肿的鼻子朝向太阳，双颊被寒风吹得红通通的。

En: There is little time for this...
Zh: 时间不多了……

En: *Relax*. I see you both running around like maniacs, just back and forth, back and forth. Gotta live a little, enjoy the day.
Zh: “*放松点*。我见你们两人像疯子一样忙前忙后跑来跑去的。你们应该享受生活，享受阳光。”他转头将臃肿的脸埋在雪里，胡子上的啤酒滴结成了冰。

En: *Relax*. I see you both running around like maniacs, just back and forth, back and forth. Gotta live a little, enjoy the day.
Zh: “*放松点*。我见你们两人像疯子一样忙前忙后跑来跑去的。你们应该享受生活，享受阳光。”他臃肿的脸迎着风，冰冷的雨水拍打在他的脸颊上。

En: *Relax*. I see you running around like a maniac, just back and forth, back and forth. Gotta live a little, enjoy the sun.
Zh: “*放松点*。我见你像疯子一样忙前忙后跑来跑去的。你们应该享受生活，享受阳光。”他转头将臃肿的鼻子朝向太阳，双颊被寒风吹得红通通的。

En: *Relax*. I see you running around like a maniac, just back and forth, back and forth. Gotta live a little, enjoy the day.
Zh: “*放松点*。我见你像疯子一样忙前忙后跑来跑去的。你们应该享受生活，享受阳光。”他转头将臃肿的脸埋在雪里，胡子上的啤酒滴结成了冰。

En: *Relax*. I see you running around like a maniac, just back and forth, back and forth. Gotta live a little, enjoy the day.
Zh: “*放松点*。我见你像疯子一样忙前忙后跑来跑去的。你们应该享受生活，享受阳光。”他臃肿的脸迎着风，冰冷的雨水拍打在他的脸颊上。

En: I don't think I have time to just sit and wait...
Zh: “我没时间坐下来等你……”

En: Sure, sounds like a much-needed break from all the work.
Zh: “行啊，工作之余也可以休息下。”

En: No, Tequila, let me take care of it.
Zh: “不，龙舌兰，交给我吧。”你还没来得及反应，他就放下酒瓶，起身离开了。几分钟过去，天空中云卷云舒……

En: After what feels like ages, you hear the fence creaking as if under a large weight. Look at him. Just look at him.
Zh: 过了很久之后，你听到栅栏受到重压发出的嘎嘎声。看，他回来了。

En: You chat a bit with old Rosemary...
Zh: 你和老罗斯马雷聊了一小会……

En: Then try to strike up a conversation with Don't Call Abigail about something other than Abigail (to no avail)... It's all about Abigail.
Zh: 然后和不要打给阿比盖尔聊了一会无关阿比盖尔的事情（并没有用）……

En: Exactly! Just give me a minute or two...
Zh: “是啊！给我一两分钟就行……”他放下酒瓶，站起身离开。几分钟过去，天空中云卷云舒……

En: The lieutenant sits silent the entire time, preferring to focus on his notebook.
Zh: 警督一直保持着沉默，专注地看着他的笔记本。

En: Here he is -- the man of the hour!
Zh: “风云人物来啦！”

En: All finished?
Zh: “都搞定了吗？”

En: We've pissed off the lieutenant! (Look at Kim.)
Zh: “我们把警督惹毛啦！”（看向金。）

En: Clearly we have better things to do than sit down and chat with a bunch of drunks!
Zh: 很显然，比起坐下来和一群醉鬼聊天，我们还有更重要的事情要做！

En: I know, I know. Ran into some problems there -- material shortages and the like -- but don't worry, genius *thrives* under constraints! He points at himself, proud and happy. "That's why you pay for the best..."
Zh: “我知道，我知道。我只是遇到了些麻烦——资源短缺之类的问题——但是不用担心，天才哪怕在限制之下也能*发挥*！”他指了指自己，仿佛比之前醉得更厉害了。“所以你才会花钱请个最好的……”

En: What an athlete! There should be a new word for drunk moves like that -- like alco-lete or gin-nast!
Zh: 他简直像个运动员一样！我们应该为这种醉酒的姿势造个新词，比如醉动员，或者酒育家！

En: He can't even walk straight. How did he write your name in cathode rays?
Zh: 他连走个直线都很困难，还怎么能把你的名字用阴极射线拼出来呢？

En: You seem a bit drunk. You sure you're happy with your work?
Zh: “你好像喝醉了。你真的对你的工作满意吗？”

En: Good, good. Can I go and take a look at the monument now?
Zh: “很好，很好。我现在可以去看看那座纪念碑吗？”

En: The lieutenant closes his notebook and nods at you, ready to go.
Zh: 警督合上笔记本，朝你点点头，准备离开。

En: Just head on over to the horseback monument. You'll see. World's gonna *know* -- about you, me, and the shiny numbers in our bank account.
Zh: “直接去马背纪念碑吧。全世界都会*知道的*——知道你，知道我，还有我们银行账户上闪闪发光的数字。”

En: Of course I am. When you're ready -- and I don't think you ever will be... He pauses to pick up his bottle.
Zh: “当然了。等你准备好了……虽然我觉得你永远也准备不好……”他止住话语，拿起酒瓶。

En: But if there's anything else your old friend can do for you, let me know. He takes a mouthful of beer, swishing it around in his mouth like some kind of sommelier.
Zh: “但要是你的老朋友还能为你做些什么的话，一定要告诉我。”他喝了一大口酒，如同品酒师一般将酒在嘴里转来转去。

En: Yes, whenever you're ready -- and I don't think you ever will be... He pauses to pick up his bottle.
Zh: “好吧，等你准备好了——虽然我觉得你永远也准备不好……”他止住话语，拿起酒瓶。

En: If I can do it, so can you.
Zh: “如果我能做到，那你也可以。”

En: Well, I guess if it helps with your work...
Zh: “好吧，如果这对你的工作有帮助的话……”

En: I know what you mean, I become a *superpower* when I'm drunk.
Zh: “我懂你的意思，我喝醉的时候也觉得自己是个*超能力者*。”

En: I don't think *I* can do it. Sorry. He stares at the bottle in his hand, then lets out a deep sigh...
Zh: “我觉得*我*不行。抱歉。”他盯着手中的酒瓶，然后深深叹了口气……

En: No, you're right. I've *got* to do this. I *need* this. He pushes a balled fist into a thigh. "Alright, you know what, Tequila, you old rat? I'll consider your request."
Zh: “不，你说得对。我*得*这么做。我*需要*这个。”他捏起拳头捶在腿上。“好了，龙舌兰，我会考虑你的请求。”

En: Some complex calculations are taking place in that intoxicated mind of his...
Zh: 他那喝醉的脑子里在盘算着什么……

En: Suddenly you feel sad, looking at this man and his bottle... somehow he looks *ancient*, these thoughts having chased him for years.
Zh: 你看着这个男人和他的酒瓶，莫名觉得有些难过……他看起来是那么*苍老*，这些想法已经萦绕他心头多年。

En: No, you have to stop drinking. I insist.
Zh: “不，我还是觉得你必须戒酒。”

En: Exactly! We're partners, you and I -- partners in drinking, partners in business... He chugs in celebration.
Zh: “对嘛！我们是伙伴啊——是喝酒的伙伴，也是商业上的伙伴……”他喝了一大口酒表示庆祝。

En: No can do. Sorry. He stares at the bottle in his hand, then lets out a deep sigh...
Zh: “不行，抱歉。”他盯着手中的酒瓶，然后深深叹了口气……

En: Exactly! I just know I wouldn't be able to deliver the hot shit you're after without it. He has a quick swig from his bottle.
Zh: “对嘛！我只知道如果不喝酒，就没法给你想出好主意来。”他飞快地喝了一口手中的酒。

En: Listen, I can feel it in my guts -- a bright future awaits you, the one and only *Tequila Sunset*! You're already building great brand equity. No doubt the profits will come rolling in shortly.
Zh: “我能从内心深处感受到——光明的未来正等着你，独一无二的*龙舌兰日落*！你已经打造出相当不错的品牌价值了。不用怀疑，财源很快会滚滚而来。”

En: This little weasel still isn't owning up to *ripping you off*!
Zh: 这只黄鼠狼竟然还不承认*敲诈你*！

En: That's it? It's just a bunch of scaffolding!
Zh: “就这？这就是一堆脚手架而已啊！”

En: It was extraordinary. Truly a feat of the century to get that scaffolding up so fast.
Zh: “太棒了，脚手架能搭建得这么快真是一项世纪壮举啊。”

En: Listen, I know you're trying to *rip me off*... (Take a threatening step forward.)
Zh: “我知道你想*敲诈我*……”（充满威胁地向前走去。）

En: Of course not. Your trademark will be all over it as soon as the workers can get to it. The strike's holding them up. Not much I can do about that. He shrugs, then takes a swig from the bottle.
Zh: “当然不是了。等工人来了，这儿到处都会有你的商标。只不过最近他们罢工了。我也没有办法啊。”他耸耸肩，喝了一口酒。

En: Ya hear that, Rosie? I'm back -- *big time*. He reaches over to his buddy to clink the bottles, then turns back to you.
Zh: “你听见了吗，罗西？我回归了——这是多么*辉煌的时刻*啊。”他走过去和他的伙伴碰了杯，然后转身看你。

En: Whoa! Rip you off? What is this, a radio novella on Channel 8? Friends don't let friends start ripping each other off! No, that's not how we operate here.
Zh: “哇！敲诈你？什么啊，你是在说8频道播的那种有声小说吗？我们是朋友，我怎么会敲诈你呢！我们可不是在干这个。”

En: Listen, I know it might look a little *lacking* right now, but you gotta trust the process. I've got it all under control. Once the strike is over, the workers will be all over the monument...
Zh: “我知道自己现在看起来好像能力*不足*的样子，但是你要相信一切都需要一个过程。事情都在我的掌控之下。只要罢工结束，工人们就会去纪念碑那儿……”

En: And that's only the start. Wait until the strike's over and the workers can finally deliver the product. I *guarantee* the papers will pick it up. You'll be the talk of the town -- -- investing into the community, creating value, giving back...
Zh: “这只是开始。等罢工结束，工人们就能顺利交付。我*保证*这件事会登上报纸。城里的人都会谈论你——为社区投资、创造价值、回馈社会之类的……”

En: *Papers*? Maybe she'll read the papers and see your name... Maybe it will bring her back...
Zh: *报纸*？或许她会在报纸上看到你的名字……然后回心转意……

En: I *guarantee* the papers will pick it up once it's all finished, with photos and everything. You'll be the talk of the town -- investing into the community, creating value, giving back...
Zh: “我*保证*完工后我们的照片和事迹的都会登上报纸。城里的人都会谈论你——为社区投资、创造价值、回馈社会之类的……”

En: I *guarantee* the papers will pick it up, with photos and everything. You'll be the talk of the town -- investing into the community, creating value, giving back...
Zh: “我*保证*我们的照片之类的都会登上报纸。城里的人都会谈论你——为社区投资、创造价值、回馈社会之类的……”

En: Hey, your new Personal Brand Strategist still hasn't laid off the drink -- even though he promised.
Zh: 嘿，你新的个人品牌战略师还没戒酒呢——虽然他已经答应你了。

En: A woman is sitting by the kitchen table, eating buttered toast and drinking herbal tea. She opens the Salzneben Report, a quarterly business magazine. There, in shiny tricolour print -- *him*, triumphant and victorious, like an apparition from the past...
Zh: 一个女人正坐在餐桌旁，吃着黄油面包，喝着花草茶。她打开了商业季刊《萨勒本报告》。杂志上用闪亮的彩色油墨印着*他*得意洋洋的模样，如同幻影……

En: Weren't you going to drink less while on the job?
Zh: “你工作的时候就不能少喝点酒吗？”

En: I wonder what the newspapers might say...
Zh: “我想知道报纸上会怎么报道……”

En: Alright, I trust you to deliver the final final product. (Proceed with the quest.)
Zh: “好吧，我相信你能拿出最后的成果。”（继续任务。）

En: I'm pulling the project. I don't have confidence it will ever get finished. [Cancel the entire task.]
Zh: “我要撤销这个项目，我觉得恐怕永远都没法完成。”[取消整个任务。]

En: What would you like them to say?
Zh: “你想让他们报道什么？”

En: The strike might take forever... Maybe you should just cut your losses while you can.
Zh: 罢工可能会永远持续下去……或许你现在就应该及时止损，减少损失。

En: Here's a man who knows how to play the media -- by starting with the right question.
Zh: 眼前这个人懂得如何从关键问题切入，将媒体玩弄于股掌。

En: Very nice. You could be your own PR agent, Tequila. He nods.
Zh: “很好，你当自己的公关代理去吧，龙舌兰。”他点点头。

En: Tequila... you know it isn't that easy yourself. I promise you I'm *working on it*. He winks, his fingers tightening around the bottle.
Zh: “龙舌兰……你自己也知道这件事做起来没那么容易。我保证我*已经在安排*了。”他紧握着酒瓶，眨了眨眼。

En: He isn't 'working on it' -- he'll never stop drinking.
Zh: 他根本没有‘安排’——他的酒瓶子就没放下来过。

En: He sighs. "It isn't that easy... But I'm working on it. I really am. I even signed up for this evening group in Jamrock."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“这件事不容易啊……但是我已经在安排了，真的。我甚至还报名了这次的加姆洛克晚会。”

En: Sire, he's telling the truth.
Zh: 是啊，他说的是实话。

En: But Tequila... He hangs his head in despair. "For what? I really tried to make it work this time -- for you, for me, for the triumph of *high-concept* ideas..."
Zh: “可是龙舌兰……”他失望地低下头。“我这样做是为了什么呢？这次我真的很努力了——不仅是为了你，还为了我自己，也为了*高端概念*……”

En: Don't spend a single brain cell worrying about it, just leave it all to me... the final outcome will be *spectacular*. Just takes a little time...
Zh: “你连一个脑细胞都不用担心这件事，都交给我就好了……最终的结果一定*万众瞩目*。这只是时间问题……”

En: Nightclub owner reinvigorates youth culture, makes a fortune!
Zh: “夜总会老板复兴青年文化，发大财！”

En: Local bankruptcy curse lifted by stock market mogul!
Zh: “股市大亨破除当地的破产诅咒！”

En: Philanthropist rescues local kid from violent father, town pays tribute!
Zh: “慈善家从当地家暴的父亲手中救出小孩，向他致敬！”

En: Local hobo solves murder while making his fortune!
Zh: “本地流浪汉破案发财两不误！”

En: Global superstar solves murder while making his fortune!
Zh: “超级巨星破案发财两不误！”

En: Local detective apologises for lengthy murder investigation while making his fortune!
Zh: “本地警探为久未破案致歉，同时发大财！”

En: Local doomsayer solves murder and makes his fortune!
Zh: “本地灾难预言者破案发财两不误！”

En: Local detective solves murder while making his fortune!
Zh: “本地警探破案发财两不误！”

En: Wealthy businessman renovates local eyesore!
Zh: “富商翻修本地碍眼建筑！”

En: And I didn't even lose anything! He stares into empty space, eyes like discarded bottle caps. "Okay, guess I'm back to where I was before. Square one. It's not like I haven't been here before."
Zh: “而我什么也没失去！”他看着跟前的一片空旷之地，眼睛像是废弃的酒瓶盖子。“好啊，看来我又回来了。这广场我又不是没来过。”

En: Anything else you want? He takes an enormous swig from the bottle, swaying back and forth.
Zh: “你还有什么别的需求吗？”他喝了一大口酒，身子前后晃动着。

En: But we *haven't* solved the murder yet, the lieutenant says. "We might have if you hadn't gotten distracted by this."
Zh: “但我们*还没*破获谋杀案呢，”警督说道。“要不是你因为这件事分了心，我们早就破案了。”

En: Anything else? He throws one leg over another, smug and content.
Zh: “还有别的事吗？”他翘起二郎腿，一脸得意而满足的样子。

En: He turns his swollen nose to face the sun, cheeks ruddy from the biting wind.
Zh: 他把肿胀的鼻子转向太阳，脸被刺骨的寒风吹得通红。

En: He turns his swollen face against the snow, droplets of beer freezing in his beard.
Zh: 他把肿胀的脸转过去迎着雪，滴在胡子上的啤酒慢慢结冰了。

En: He turns his swollen face against the wind, freezing rain battering his cheeks.
Zh: 他把肿胀的脸转过去迎着风，冰冷的雨水敲打着他的脸。

En: He turns his swollen nose to face the moon, cheeks ruddy from the biting wind.
Zh: 他把肿胀的鼻子转向月亮，脸被刺骨的寒风吹得通红。

En: Before you know it the man has put down the bottle, stood up, and left. Minutes pass; the clouds change shape up in the sky...
Zh: 还没等你反应过来，他把瓶子放下，站起来走了。几分钟过去了；天空中的云变化着形状……

En: Hmm, he seems even more drunk than before...
Zh: 嗯，他看起来醉得更厉害了……

En: Hey, Tequila! A thirty-something man clad in a two-piece Lickra(TM) tracksuit puts down his pilsner and extends his hand in greeting.
Zh: “嗨，龙舌兰！”一个30多岁、穿着利卡尔（TM）运动服两件套的男人放下手中的比尔森啤酒，伸出手向你打了打招呼。

En: What do you guys do around here?
Zh: “你们在这里做什么？”

En: Be seein' you. [Leave.]
Zh: “回见。”[离开。]

En: You too, Tequila Sunset!
Zh: “你也是，龙舌兰日落！”

En: We are saving the world! He looks at his comrades.
Zh: “我们正在拯救世界！”他看着自己的同伴说道。

En: Could you and your pals sign this document? (Show Evrart's letter.)
Zh: “你跟你的兄弟能签一下这份文件吗？”（拿出艾弗拉特的信。）

En: What's it about? He waves his hand apathetically. "Ah, I'll let my hand address the situation."
Zh: “这是干什么？”他冷淡地摆摆手。“啊，我会让我的手来解决这个问题。”

En: Thanks, Rosie. He grabs the pen and paper from your hands and, very carefully, scribbles on the dotted line: 'Idiot Doom Spiral'.
Zh: “谢了，罗西。”他从你手中接过笔和纸，非常小心地在横线上涂下几个词：‘白痴厄运漩涡’。

En: Hey Abs! He shouts to the non-responsive drunk still dribbling drool from the side of his mouth.
Zh: “喂，阿比！”他朝那个没有回应的醉汉——嘴角还在滴口水——大喊一声。

En: Hey Abs! He shouts to the mumbling drunkard next to him. "HEY ABS!"
Zh: “喂，阿比！”他朝旁边那个嘴里嘟嘟囔囔的人大喊一声。“阿比！”

En: I need at least two signatures on the document.
Zh: “这份文件上至少需要两个签名。”

En: The man looks around confused. "Don't... ya... ya call Abigail."
Zh: 男人困惑地四下看了看。“不要……不……打给阿比盖尔。”

En: I need you to sign this document right here. He waves the paper and pen in front of the man's face. "It's important."
Zh: “我需要你在这份文件上签字。”他在男人面前晃了晃纸和笔。“这很重要。”

En: Don't call her... His trembling hand catches the paperwork. He lays it out on his knee and starts writing, slowly. The handwriting is almost illegible due to his shaky hands.
Zh: “别打给她……”他用颤抖的手抓住了文件，铺在膝盖上，然后慢慢开始书写。由于手抖得厉害，字迹基本上很难辨认。

En: *Domwtclall Abheqoeel*? His friend grabs the document and admires it. "Great job, Abbs. Nailed it." He hands it back to you.
Zh: 他的朋友抓过文件欣赏着。“*尔盖比阿给打要不*？做得好，阿比。干得漂亮。”他把文件递还给你。

En: HEY ABS! ABS! He grabs his passed-out friend by the shoulder and shakes him a bit.
Zh: 然后他抓住不省人事的朋友的肩膀，用力晃了晃。“喂，阿比！阿比！”

En: Hey! Goddamn *Abigail* is here, wake up! WAKE UP! His efforts get no response.
Zh: “喂！该死的*阿比盖尔*来了，快醒醒！醒醒！”他的努力没有得到回应。

En: Maybe someone else can help? (Point to the third guy.)
Zh: “也许其他人可以帮忙？”（指向第三个人。）

En: I don't think he can help, he's completely out of it.
Zh: “我觉得他帮不上忙，他完全晕过去了。”

En: I'll be damned... uh, well, uh... that's what we have Rosemary for! He points to the third drunkard. "You can help, right?"
Zh: “该死的……呃，好吧……这就是罗斯马雷存在的原因！”他指向第三个醉汉。“你可以帮忙，对吧？”

En: The jolly man looks at you, then his friend in the tracksuit. "Sure I can fuckin' help. You need a signature -- I *got* you a signature."
Zh: 快乐的男人看了看你，然后是穿着运动服的朋友。“我当然可以帮忙了。你要一个签名是吧——那我就*给*你一个签名。”

En: Before you can reply he's already furiously scribbling something on the paper. When he hands the document back to you, the signature -- in upper case letters -- reads: "DON'T CALL ABIGAIL."
Zh: 你还没来得及回复，他已经在纸上猛烈地涂画了几笔。当他把文件交还给你的时候，那个签名——用大写字母写的——是：“不要打给阿比盖尔。”

En:
You've done a great service to the village, to the RCM, and to Revachol.	“你对这个村子，为RCM还有瑞瓦肖做出了巨大的贡献。”
0x0100005A0000FF59	Dialogue Text	VILLAGE / IDIOT DOOM SPIRAL	You	Uh, I guess that's it."
Zh: “呃，我想就这些了吧。”

En: Thanks.
Zh: “谢谢。”

En: Hey, guys -- we're heroes! He waves his hand erratically to the other guys.
Zh: “喂，伙计们——我们是英雄了！”他朝着其他两个人胡乱地挥了挥手。

En: Glad to have been of assistance! He gives you a thumbs up. "After all this time, I feel like I've made a difference..."
Zh: “很高兴能帮到你！”他朝你竖起了大拇指。“经过这么长时间以后，我觉得自己终于有所作为了……”

En: No problem! He looks around for a bottle. "Now where'd I put that..."
Zh: “没问题！”他四处寻找瓶子。“我把它放在哪里了……”

En: 'Bout fuckin' time, man. I've done my duty. He brings his hand up to his head for a salute.
Zh: “是时候了，老兄。我已经完成了自己的职责。”他把手举到头顶，行了个礼。

En: The incapacitated drunk lays there snoring like an ancient rusting tractor.
Zh: 不能正常活动的醉汉躺在那里，像一台生锈的老拖拉机一样打着鼾。

En: Don't call Abigail! He snorts and takes a swig from his bottle. The bottle is empty.
Zh: “不要打给阿比盖尔！”他哼了一声，拿起瓶子喝了一口。瓶子已经空了。

En: Feels like a... like a thousand fuckin' reál, man. Doing *good*.
Zh: “感觉就像……就像1000雷亚尔，老兄。做*好事*。”

En: Fuckin' 'ard work done for the day, man.
Zh: “他妈的干了一天苦活了，老兄。”

En: Have you seen a red-haired woman named Ruby around here lately?
Zh: “最近你有没有在附近见过一个叫露比的红发女人？”

En: Have you seen a woman named Ruby around here lately?
Zh: “最近你有没有在附近见过一个叫露比的女人？”

En: Can't really remember seeing any women after losing my keys.
Zh: “我把钥匙弄丢之后，就不记得见过什么女人了。”

En: Man, you're *really* bleedin' me dry here. I don't want to give you any more. (Don't do it now.)
Zh: “老兄，你*真*把我弄得口干舌燥的。我不想再给你了。”（现在不要做。）

En: Geez, I already gave you some. I don't want to keep doing that. (Don't do it now.)
Zh: “喂，我已经给你一些了。我不想继续这么下去。”（现在不要做。）

En: But I don't want to give you any of my precious alcohol. It's mine! (Don't do it now.)
Zh: “但我不想把宝贵的酒给你。它是我的！”（现在不要做。）

En: But I don't want to give you any of my alcohol... I might have more use for it. (Don't do it now.)
Zh: “但我什么酒也不想给给你……我可能还用得着呢。”（现在不要做。）

En: I don't want to give you any alcohol. I'm not an enabler. (Don't do it now.)
Zh: “我什么酒也不想给你。我不是幕后推手。”（现在不要做。）

En: It's very hard to tell a story when one's mouth is dry.
Zh: “嘴巴干了的话，讲故事就很困难了。”

En: Then I can't keep on telling the stories.
Zh: “那我就不能继续给你讲故事了。”

En: Suit yourself.
Zh: “随便你吧。”

En: That very well *might* be the case, but looking at you I can say with great confidence that I need it more.
Zh: “*很可能*是这样吧，不过看你的样子，我可以很自信地说我更需要它。”

En: In that case, I'm not a storyteller. He crosses his legs and turns his gaze towards the ground.
Zh: “如果那样的话，我也不是个讲故事的人。”他抱起双臂，双眼凝视地面。

En: You want booze, don't you?
Zh: “你想要酒，是吗？”

En: What do you need?
Zh: “你想要什么？”

En: I don't *want* it, man. His eyes light up. "I *need* it. Can't tell stories without it -- dry stories are boring, you see."
Zh: 他的眼睛亮了起来。“我不是*想要*它，老兄。我是*需要*它。没它就讲不了故事——干巴巴的嘴只能讲出枯燥无味的故事，你明白的。”

En: Booze. Did you already forget our party? He taps his finger to his temple. "The thing I relayed to you earlier?"
Zh: “酒。你忘记我们的派对了吗？”他用手指拍了拍太阳穴。“我之前告诉你的事？”

En: Let me see... He takes the bottle and inspects it closely. "Ah, very high concept, thanks."
Zh: “我来看看……”他拿起瓶子，仔细观察着。“啊，的确很刺激，谢谢。”

En: Here, I've got a Potent Pilsner. (Give it.)
Zh: “给你，我有一瓶高度比尔森啤酒。”（交给他。）

En: I've got this weird bottle of pale-aged vodka. (Give it.)
Zh: “我有一瓶不可思议的灰域陈酿伏特加。”（交给他。）

En: I've got some sweet *Commodore Red*. (Give it.)
Zh: “我有一瓶美妙的*海军准将红朗姆*。”（交给他。）

En: Not much, but it will do... He grabs the bottle from your hand and uncorks it immediately.
Zh: “不算多，不过也够了……”他一把从你手中抓过瓶子，转眼就拔掉了塞子。

En: Classy. He snatches the bottle and pushes the cork in through the bottleneck.
Zh: “很经典。”他一把抢过瓶子，推开了瓶颈上的塞子。

En: The tale I'm about to tell you is an urban legend particular to Martinaise. He lifts the bottle to his lips and takes a long, luxuriant sip. "That said, I first heard it from a former bicycle courier in Couron..."
Zh: “我要给你讲的是马丁内斯特有的一个都市传说。”他把瓶子举到嘴边，美美地抿了一口。“话虽如此，第一次我还是从科戎的一位前自行車快递员那里听来的……”

En: Not that I can think of, currently.
Zh: “我目前还没想起来。”

En: I guess that makes sense. You have been drinking *a lot*.
Zh: “我想这个可以理解。毕竟你喝了*这么多*。”

En: That might be the case, yes.
Zh: “很可能是这样，没错。”

En: I brought you some booze. Will you tell me about your name now?
Zh: “我给你带了点酒。现在可以把你的名字告诉我了吗？”

En: I got you some more booze, can you tell me the next story now?
Zh: “我又给你拿了些酒，现在可以给我讲下一个故事了吗？”

En: Found another batch of alcohol, can you tell me the third story now?
Zh: “我又找到了一批酒，现在能给我讲第三个故事了吗？”

En: What's in a name... He looks up to the sky as if pondering the question, then turns back to you.
Zh: “名字有什么关系……”他仰望天空，似乎在思索这个问题，然后他低下头看着你。

En: He breathes in deeply, preparing his recitation...
Zh: 他深吸一口气，准备开始讲述……

En: I have a feeling that this is going to cost me a lot.
Zh: “我有种感觉，这会让我付出很大的代价。”

En: No, the reality of the situation requires a rather modest contribution -- a little *motivational package*.
Zh: “不，现实情况只需要些适度的贡献——一点点*激励方案*。”

En: I don't have any on me right now.
Zh: “现在我身上没有。”

En: Then I will see you again once you've procured some. *Par example*, he lifts his hands and spreads them wide, "my good friend Rosemary here sells all kinds of stuff."
Zh: 他举起手，大大地伸展开来。“那等你弄到之后，我们再见面吧。*举个例子*——我的好朋友罗斯马雷就在卖各种各样的东西。”

En: Hey, Tequila! Welcome to reality. How's it treating you? A thirty-something Lickra(TM)-clad man takes a sip from his beer.
Zh: “嗨，龙舌兰！欢迎来到现实。它对你怎么样啊？”一个30多岁、穿着利卡尔（TM）运动服两件套的男人抿了一口啤酒。

En: Are there any more stories you can tell?
Zh: “你还有别的故事吗？”

En: Cotton mouth is keeping my tongue imprisoned. He shrugs his shoulders dramatically.
Zh: 他夸张地耸耸肩。“嘴巴太渴，舌头被囚禁在里面了。”

En: So, have you got anything for good old Idiot Doom Spiral? A bottle for a story. Seems fair to me.
Zh: “所以，你有没有什么好东西要给白痴厄运漩涡呢？一瓶酒换一个故事。在我看来这很公平。”

En: If you find any, I'd be extremely grateful.
Zh: “如果你找到的话，我会万分感激。”

En: Hey, sorry for being a buzzkill earlier. I'll supply the booze if you'll supply the stories.
Zh: “听着，很抱歉刚才扫了你们的兴。如果你能讲故事的话，我可以把酒给你。”

En: Wonderful! He claps his hands together.
Zh: “太棒了！”他拍了拍手。

En: Man, you're like, bleeding now. Reality's really messed you up.
Zh: “老兄，你现在好像，在流血啊。现实真是把你整得够呛啊。”

En: Tequila Sunset. He nods in appreciation.
Zh: “龙舌兰日落。”他点头表示赞赏。

En: It's good to hear that you're on top of things. Talking about used to -- did you know that I *used* to be a real mover and shaker? He thoughtfully picks at his shit-stained Lickra(TM) jacket.
Zh: “很高兴见到你能控制全局。说到曾经——你知道我*曾经*是个真正的大人物吗？”他若有所思地扯了扯沾着屎印的利卡尔（TM）夹克。

En: That's the spirit! I used to shape reality into my image. A long time ago. Those days are over now. He looks at his shit-stained Lickra(TM) jacket with a grim expression.
Zh: “就是这种精神！我过去经常会把现实塑造成我的形象。很久以前的事了。现在那些日子已经过去。”他看着沾着屎印的利卡尔（TM）运动服，表情十分冷酷。

En: Not this again. I was hoping that we were done with it.
Zh: “别又来一遍。我刚还希望已经结束了。”

En: Only echoes remaining of human faces? That's a pretty good apocalypse. I wouldn't mind if it came down to that. He nods appreciatively and picks at his shit-stained Lickra(TM) jacket.
Zh: “人类的面孔只留下回音？这是一种很好的启示。如果真是那样的话，我倒是不介意。”他赞赏地点点头，然后扯了扯沾着屎印的利卡尔（TM）夹克。

En: Did I say anything about my colleagues?
Zh: “我有说过我的同事吗？”

En: Did we talk about, uhm, politics?
Zh: “我们有没有聊过，嗯，政治？”

En: Did you get a read on what kind of cop I was?
Zh: “你了解我是哪种警察吗？”

En: Did I mention losing anything else?
Zh: “我有没有提过丢了别的什么东西？”

En: Did I tell you anything specific about this person that 'fucked me'?
Zh: “关于那个‘害我很惨的人’，我有没有说过什么详细信息？”

En: It definitely is.
Zh: “绝对是。”

En: How do you know this?
Zh: “你是怎么知道的？”

En: Do you know me?
Zh: “你认识我吗？”

En: Oh, do I? Of course. Don't you remember?
Zh: “哦，是吗？当然了。你不记得了？”

En: Nope, you sure don't.
Zh: 不，你当然不记得。

En: No.
Zh: “不记得。”

En: Maybe?
Zh: “也许？”

En: Aha! He takes a sip from his beer. "Do you want to know how Tequila Sunset came to be?"
Zh: “啊哈！”他抿了一口啤酒。“你想知道龙舌兰日落是怎么来的吗？”

En: Maybe? He takes a sip from his beer. "You look like you want to know how Tequila Sunset came to be. You look like you want to hear *the tale*."
Zh: “也许？”他抿了一口啤酒。“看来你想知道龙舌兰日落是怎么来的。看起来你很想听听那个*传说*。”

En: We've met before, don't you remember?
Zh: “我们之前见过，你不记得了吗？”

En: Go ahead.
Zh: “说吧。”

En: No, this sounds scary. (Back up.)
Zh: “不要，听起来有点吓人。”（后退。）

En: Mhm. Let me take a sip to moisten up my cords... He takes a big sip, then begins: "Tequila Sunset rolled into Martinaise last Friday. And by *Tequila Sunset* I mean *you* -- the man, the myth."
Zh: “嗯。让我先小酌一口润润嗓子……”他灌了一大口酒，然后开始说道：“龙舌兰日落上周五来到马丁内斯。我说的*龙舌兰日落*其实指的就是*你*——那个男人，那个神话。”

En: I wasn't alone, though. There were colleagues...
Zh: “不过，我不是一个人。还有我的同事……”

En: Was I alone?
Zh: “我一个人吗？”

En: Wait, did we meet on Friday?
Zh: “等等，我们周五见过吗？”

En: Hey, let's not jump ahead of ourselves, this is *your* story. Stop interrupting. He takes another sip -- then continues.
Zh: “喂，先别这么着急，这可是*你的*故事。别打断我。”他又抿了一口——然后继续说道。

En: You got here on Friday to solve a case, hoping to be the early bird who gets the worm. And by 'the worm' I mean 'the buzz', because as far as I know, all you did was get piss-drunk...
Zh: “你上周五来这里破案，希望能做那个早起有虫吃的小鸟。我说的‘虫’指的是‘喝酒的快感’，因为据我所知，你所做的一切就是把自己灌了个伶仃大醉……”

En: Word on the street is you went around the local hostel telling people that you're a police officer and that it would be *really* fucked up if you shot yourself in the head right in front of them. That's pretty high concept, if you ask me.
Zh: “坊间的说法是，你去了本地一家旅社，告诉别人你是个警察，还说如果你在他们面前打爆自己脑袋的话，*肯定*会变得乱七八糟。如果问我的话，我觉得挺高概念的。”

En: Does that sound like something you'd do? Yeah, probably.
Zh: 听起来像是你会做的事吗？是啊，可能吧。

En: What happened then?
Zh: “然后发生了什么？”

En: Wait. This doesn't sound like me at all.
Zh: “等等。听起来一点也不像我啊。”

En: The lieutenant's brow is furrowed. He's listening as casually as he can.
Zh: 警督眉头紧锁。他跟你一样，听得很专心。

En: Well, the *word on the street* may be a rumour, but what happened next is the truth. I was there to witness it, along with these bums here.
Zh: “这个嘛，*坊间传闻*可能是谣传，不过接下来发生的就是事实。我就在当场亲眼看见的，还有这里的几位兄弟。”

En: It was a late Saturday night, when we, the Union of Moribund Alcoholics, were getting our drink on. Nothing remarkable about this, we get our drink on 24/7. Makes everything warm and glowy, I trust you know the feeling.
Zh: “那是一个周六的深夜，当时我们，垂死酒鬼联盟，正在喝酒。这没什么值得注意的，我们一周7天24小时都在喝酒。这样能让一切变得温暖和缓慢起来，我相信你能懂那种感觉。”

En: One moment we hear the sound of a motor carriage revving up somewhere on the plaza, followed by a series of dings and bangs.
Zh: “突然，我们听见广场的某个地方传来汽車加速的声音，接着是一连串的叮铃咣啷的撞击声。”

En: Naturally, loud noises pique the interest of anybody owning a pair of ears. That's just the reality we're in.
Zh: “自然而然地，巨大的噪音会激起任何长了耳朵的人的好奇心。这就是我们身处的现实。”

En: Naturally.
Zh: “这很自然。”

En: Uhm.. I want to get off this story-train right now.
Zh: “嗯……我现在就想跳下这列讲故事的火車。”

En: Do you remember the sound of wood cracking? The billboard...
Zh: 你还记得木头断裂的声音吗？那个广告牌……

En: I think that we should let him continue with the story.
Zh: “我觉得我们应该让他继续说下去。”

En: You can't get off now.
Zh: 你现在下不去的。

En: Lies, lies, lies! He only speaks liessss.
Zh: 撒谎，撒谎，撒谎！他只会撒谎。

En: You have to stay.
Zh: 你必须留下来。

En: The throttle is jammed and the brakeman's nowhere to be seen! There's no getting off this ride!
Zh: 节流阀卡住了，制动员也不知去向！现在已经不可能下車了！

En: Anyway, there was a brief silence -- a *gasp* of silence, if you will -- followed by a real commotion. We heard the carriage careening towards the coast at top speed.
Zh: “总之，接下来是短暂的沉默——如果你愿意，也可以说是一阵沉默的*喘息*——紧接着就是一阵真正的骚动。我们听见汽車正全速朝海岸的方向斜着冲了过来。”

En: What train are you talking about? he asks, taking a sip of beer.
Zh: 他喝了一口啤酒，问到：“你说什么火車？”

En: That's right! I let you finish all of your stories that night.
Zh: “没错！那天晚上我可是听你说完了全部的故事。”

En: No train at all. Please continue.
Zh: “根本没有火車。请继续吧。”

En: Sounded like someone jumped the canal. We grabbed our brewskies and rushed to the jetty -- never underestimate the speed of an alcoholic...
Zh: “听起来有人跳过了运河。我们抓起酒瓶，冲向码头——永远不要低估酒鬼的速度……”

En: What we saw was a sight to behold. A beat up police carriage, containing you. Right there on the beach. You revved the engine and screamed at the top of your lungs...
Zh: “我们看见一副奇景。一辆残破的警車，你坐在里面。就在那边的海滩上。你发动引擎，声嘶力竭地尖叫着……”

En: 'THE TIME HATH COME.'
Zh: “‘时候到了。’”

En: So, naturally, being the curious cat I am, I asked what time hath come, to which you replied...
Zh: “所以，自然而然地，像我这种好奇心重的人，就问你什么时候到了，然后你回复说……”

En: THE TIME HATH COME FOR TEQUILA SUNSET. THE END OF ALL THINGS.
Zh: “龙舌兰日落的时间到了。一切终结的时候到了。”

En: Three men are standing on a wooden platform extending out from the shore of a dilapidated fishing village, their cheeks red, spirits high, beers in hand, eyes glazed over.
Zh: 破败渔村的海岸边延伸出一个木制平台，上面站着三个男人。他们的面颊通红，精神高亢，手握啤酒，目光呆滞。

En: After which your reality contracted -- you jammed the pedal, ploughed right off the jetty and through the ice.
Zh: “在你与现实妥协之后——你猛踩油门，冲出码头，穿过冰面。”

En: We ran towards the ice, whilst you crawled your way out, miraculously unhurt -- covered in seaweed and shit. Like some kind of sea monster.
Zh: “我们朝着冰面上跑，正好看着你爬了出来，奇迹般地毫发无损——身上满是海草和粪便。就像某种海怪。”

En: Say nothing. It's more dignified that way.
Zh: 什么也不说。那样更有尊严。

En: Oh god. What happened next?
Zh: “天啦。接下来呢？”

En: Your reality contracted -- you jammed the pedal, ploughed right off the jetty and through the ice.
Zh: “你与现实妥协了——你猛踩踏板，冲出码头，穿过冰面。”

En: In this way, you and your motor carriage have a lot in common.
Zh: 这么看来，你跟你的汽車有很多相似的地方。

En: Recognizing a brother in need, we offered our condolences and invited you to party with us, which you naturally agreed to...
Zh: “我们察觉到一位兄弟需要帮助，于是向你表达了慰问，邀请你跟我们一起派对，你也很自然地答应了……”

En: What else was there to do? Thank you brothers, for your helping hand.
Zh: 不然还能怎么做？感谢兄弟们，谢谢你们的援助之手。

En: We asked about the whole Tequila Sunset thing, and you told us it was your name now and insisted that we all call you that from then on.
Zh: “我们问龙舌兰日落是怎么一回事，你说现在这就是你的名字，而且还坚持我们以后都这么叫你。”

En: Wait, so is Tequila Sunset an event or a name?
Zh: “等等，所以龙舌兰日落是一个大事件还是一个名字？”

En: I'm not sure. I think *you* were the event. Tequila Sunset. You know, as opposed to a Tequila Sunrise, which is *long gone*.
Zh: “我不确定。我觉得*你*就是大事件。龙舌兰日落。你知道的，它与龙舌兰日出截然相反，而且那个已经是*过去式*了。”

En: I think you said that *you* were the event, and that you would smash the looms of reality. Yeah... I knew I'd heard that looms line before.
Zh: “我想你说过*自己*就是大事件，而且你要粉碎现实的阴影。是啊……我知道自己以前听说过那些织线什么的。”

En: How long did we party for?
Zh: “我们狂欢了多久？”

En: Hours. It was an all-night drink-a-thon. Then at some point -- I think it was Sunday morning -- you got belligerent and wanted to talk about *Revacholian women*.
Zh: “几个小时。那是一场通宵达旦的饮酒狂欢。然后到了某个时候——我想是周日早晨——你变得好战起来，想要讨论*瑞瓦肖女人*。”

En: You told us that they were a bunch of fucking losers whose main interest was 'cramping your style'...
Zh: “你说有一帮该死的失败者，他们的主要兴趣就是‘限制你的风格……’”

En: Tequila Sunset, huh? Sounds pretty good.
Zh: “龙舌兰日落，是吗？听起来挺不错的。”

En: Yeah, I agree. He smiles.
Zh: 他笑了。“没错，我同意。”

En: How they're beautiful and also whores and so on. How one of them fucked you real bad. After a short while you crossed the event horizon, looked sullen, got up, and left without saying anything.
Zh: “她们是怎么怎么漂亮，还有娼妓啊之类的。还有其中一个把你害的有多惨。过了一小会，你越过了事件视界，满脸阴沉地站起身，然后一言不发地离开了。”

En: Wow. That's *quite* a story.
Zh: “哇哦。这还真是个*了不得*的故事呢。”

En: Yeah, I bet Tequila's a fucking *legend* around the precinct. You must be proud to work with him.
Zh: “是啊，我敢打赌，龙舌兰肯定是辖区内的传说。你跟他一起工作肯定很骄傲吧。”

En: Now *that's* a story.
Zh: “*这*才算得上是故事嘛。”

En: Are you sure it happened like that?
Zh: “你确定是这么回事？”

En: Trust me, Tequila. It happened. Just ask one of these other witnesses.
Zh: “相信我，龙舌头。就是这么回事。问问其他目击者就知道了。”

En: It was the greatest thing this fishing village has seen since Rosemary tried to use a crab to open his beer.
Zh: “自从罗斯马雷尝试用螃蟹开啤酒瓶以来，这是渔村见证过的最伟大的事情了。”

En: The muscles in your right leg tense up.
Zh: 你右腿的肌肉变得紧绷起来。

En: You were pretty vague about it, but you kept saying you got fucked *real hard* and that we've all been fucked too...
Zh: “你说得很模糊，不过一直说你被搞得*很惨*，而且我们全都被搞得一塌糊涂……”
