En: It seemed pretty painful, to be honest with you. If I had to guess, I'd say you're still working through some shit.
Zh: “老实讲，好像挺痛苦的。如果要我猜的话，我会说你还在处理一些麻烦事。”

En: Beside your gun and your badge? You said something about your hope, or heart, or something. To be honest the details are a little hazy...
Zh: “除了你的枪和警徽？你说过什么希望啊，心啊，或者什么之类的。老实说，细节有点模糊……”

En: In retrospect, I guess you lost your motor carriage, too. That's a big one.
Zh: “回顾一下的话，我猜你还把自己的車弄丢了。那可不是个小东西。”

En: No specifics, though. It was more about *you* that night. You were the star of the show.
Zh: “不过，没什么细节。那天晚上更多是关于*你*的。你是整场演出的焦点。”

En: Yeah, you seemed really mad about immigrants for some reason... He rubs the back of his neck.
Zh: “是啊，出于某种原因，你似乎对移民真的很生气……”他摸了摸自己的后颈。

En: Yeah, you said that it was okay that you crashed your carriage because the spectral hand of the market was guiding you...
Zh: “是啊，你说撞坏了汽車也没关系，因为市场那只看不见的手会引导你……”

En: Yeah, you kept talking about how the *coal mine owners* were fucking us all over just like that woman fucked you...
Zh: “是啊，你一直说*煤矿所有者*是怎么把我们耍得团团转，就像那个女人骗了你一样……”

En: Yeah, you said that you'd really behaved unreasonably and failed to uphold your responsibilities as a representative of the Coalition.
Zh: “是啊，你说自己的行为真的很不合理，没能坚持自己作为联盟代表的责任。”

En: Did I say anything about the case?
Zh: “我有没有提案子的事？”

En: Yeah, you said it was 'no biggie' and that you'd solve it in no time. He takes a strong quaff of his beer. "And that you didn't need *anyone* to do it. You're doin' it *solo* now."
Zh: “有啊，你说没什么大不了的，很快就能破案。”他喝了一大口啤酒。“还有你不需要*任何人*。现在你要*一个人*办案。”

En: A lot of cops go *solo* and *hermit* once they reach that level of alcoholism.
Zh: “很多警察一旦醉到那种程度，都会想*一个人*，还有当*隐士*。”

En: I didn't agree with you, by the way. The spectral hand of the market makes sure everyone gets exactly what they deserve. He takes a long sip of beer.
Zh: “顺便说一句，我不赞同你的观点。市场那只看不见的手会保证每一个人得到自己应得的东西。”他喝了一大口啤酒。

En: You kept calling yourself a 'goddamn superstar' who'd cleared '501 cases'...
Zh: “你一直称呼自己是个‘该死的超级明星’，破获了‘501起案件’……”

En: You kept apologizing for being such a bad cop and for the damage you've inflicted on everyone around you...
Zh: “你一直道歉说自己是个坏警察，给周围所有人带来了很大的伤害……”

En: You said being a cop was real boring and that there was no reason to talk about it. He shrugs.
Zh: 他耸耸肩。“你说当警察很无聊，没有谈论这件事的理由。”

En: You kept telling us that in your work you've seen a lot of blood but that it's nothing compared to the 'Great Bloodletting' to come...
Zh: “你一直说自己在工作的时候见过不少血腥场面，但是跟即将到来的‘大杀戮’相比，根本不算什么……”

En: This was before you started talking about how the pinkies were always trying to fuck you.
Zh: “这是在你开始讨论那些白人总想搞你之前。”

En: I'd rather not get into the details. It was pretty low-concept.
Zh: “我宁愿不说细节了，那个真挺低概念的。”

En: 'No thanks to the squares at the precinct', as you put it.
Zh: “就像你说的，‘并不是因为分局的那些老古董’。”

En: You also kept pausing to knock the heel of your hand against your temples, saying 'Stupid, stupid, stupid.'
Zh: “你还会不断地停下来，一边用手指关节敲打自己的太阳穴，一边说‘愚蠢，愚蠢，愚蠢’。”

En: I believe you mentioned something about the ground cracking open to swallow cities and dreams alike. Yeah... I remember that *simile* or whatever the fuck they are.
Zh: “我相信你还提到地面裂开吞下这座城市，还有梦想之类的。是啊……我还记得那个*笑容*，或者谁知道是什么鬼东西呢。”

En: You seem like you're characterized by your storytelling ability -- wanna tell me another one some time?
Zh: “你好像很擅长讲故事——以后有时间能再给我说一个吗？”

En: Whoop-dee-doo. So now I'm a fucking storyteller... he says, slapping his knee. "Right. Why not? Better than a beach bum."
Zh: “好哇好哇。所以现在我他妈又变成讲故事的人了……”他拍了拍膝盖说道。“好吧。为什么不呢。总比在海滩边狂欢作乐好多了。”

En: No, actually I've heard enough stories for a lifetime. They s-u-c-k. Goodbye. [Leave.]
Zh: “不，其实吧，我这辈子已经听够故事了。它们糟糕透顶。再见。”[离开。]

En: Yes -- wanna tell me how you became Idiot Doom Spiral?
Zh: “是的——要不告诉我你是怎么变成白痴厄运漩涡的？”

En: It depends, really. Are you willing to help me out?
Zh: “老实说，这要看情况。你愿意帮我一个忙吗？”

En: Hey, Spiral-Boy, you gonna share that? one of the other bums interjects.
Zh: 他还没来得及开口，另一个流浪汉插了进来：“喂，漩涡小子，要跟我一起分享吗？”

En: Grllglrlllll, gurgles the near-comatose man.
Zh: “阿阿阿比盖，”近乎昏迷的男人嘟囔着。

En: Don't call Abigail!
Zh: “不要打给阿比盖尔！”

En: Shut up, guys, I'm telling a story here. He turns to you. "Something happened to you. Something happened to me, too -- my actual name is George, but around here... you already know..."
Zh: “闭嘴，伙计们，我在讲故事呢。”他转向你。“你发生了一些事。我也发生了一些事——我的真名叫乔治，不过在这里……你已经知道了……”

En: I was a once a reasonably high-net-worth individual. A founder slash junior partner at a high-concept creative services agency. When my story begins, I had just landed a major contract with an insurance firm...
Zh: “我曾经是一个相当富有的人。一家高概念创意服务机构的创始人兼新合伙人。我的故事开始的时候，刚刚和一家保险公司签了一份大合同……”

En: Go on.
Zh: “继续。”

En: I used the profits from my agency to finance what I called a 'cultural incubator' -- abstract value generation, value per person, high-concept stuff...
Zh: “我用机构的收益来资助我所谓的‘文化孵化基地’——抽象的价值创造，人均价值，高概念类的东西……”

En: I developed the paradigm, worked within the paradigm. But the burden of leadership weighed heavily on me, so I went jogging every so often to keep myself sane.
Zh: “我开发出了范式，采用范式工作。但是领导的负担很沉重，所以我时不时会去慢跑，让自己保持清醒。”

En: Did the jogging help?
Zh: “慢跑有帮助吗？”

En: Wait -- how many people did you have working for you?
Zh: “等等——有多少个人为你工作？”

En: It did. With my trusty Sansarique tracksuit I felt like I could conquer the world...
Zh: “有的。穿着那套可靠的桑萨瑞克运动服，我觉得自己可以征服整个世界……”

En: But now dreams are worn thin, much like my tracksuit, he says thoughtfully, brushing dust off his shit-stained pants.
Zh: “但是现在梦想被磨薄了，就像我的运动服一样，”他一边若有所思地说道，一边拍掉沾着屎印的裤子上的灰尘。

En: Twenty-two full-time employees. An all-star team. A potentially historical set of individuals. Worrying about them often kept me up well into the morning hours.
Zh: “22个全职员工。一支全明星团队。一个有潜力创造历史的群体。为他们感到担忧的我经常睁着眼睛直到天明。”

En: One day I left on my evening run. As you may know, it's impossible to clear your head when you're distracted by the sound of keys jangling in your pockets. He shakes the bottle and makes a ringing sound.
Zh: “一天傍晚，我出去跑步。你也知道，当你被口袋里钥匙的叮当声搞得心烦意乱时，是不可能清空大脑的。”他晃晃瓶子，发出清脆的响声。

En: So I removed the keyring and put the keys for the front gate and the apartment into different pockets, to stop the jangling, you see. At least that was the plan...
Zh: “所以我把钥匙扣拆了下来，把前门和公寓的钥匙放进不同的口袋，这样就不会响了。你知道的，至少我是这么计划的……”

En: His eyes are clouded, his dilated blood vessels encircling his irises like stinging brambles.
Zh: 他的眼里乌云密布，凸出的血管像带刺的荆棘一样环绕在虹膜周围。

En: I was halfway done with my usual lap when it started to rain. The reality situation became very wet, very quickly.
Zh: “我刚跑完寻常路线的一半，天就开始下雨了。现实情况很快就变得非常潮湿。”

En: Please, don't open that door.
Zh: 请不要打开那扇门。

En: I made my way back home and discovered that I didn't have the key to the front gate -- I'd mixed it up with the key to the letter box. Which was useless.
Zh: “在回家的路上，我发现前门钥匙没了——我把它跟信箱的钥匙搞混了。那把钥匙一点用也没有。”

En: How *wet* are we talking exactly?
Zh: “到底有多*潮湿*呢？”

En: Go on.
Zh: “继续。”

En: Naturally, the situation required me to climb over the gate, which I did. There was no climbing down, because I slipped and landed on my ass.
Zh: “很自然，现实情况要求我爬过大门，我也这么做了。不过并没有爬下去，因为我滑了一跤，直接屁股着地。”

En: Reality was looking rather grim, just then -- me lying on my ass in a mud pit in the middle of a heavy shower. But when life knocked me down, I always got up...
Zh: “现实情况看起来十分残酷，就在那时——我正仰躺在一个泥坑里，而且正下着滂沱大雨。但是每当生活将我击倒时，我总能再站起来……”

En: So I made my way across the yard. Standing in front of my apartment door, fumbling with my pockets, I realized that I'd *also* forgotten my apartment key!
Zh: “所以我穿过院子。站在公寓门口，手忙脚乱地在口袋里翻找，然后意识到——我*还*把公寓钥匙忘记了！”

En: You've got to be shitting me!
Zh: “你是在跟我开玩笑吧！”

En: I wish I were, Tequila. I wish I were. He turns his head towards the skies. "Instead of my apartment key, I'd taken the key to the office."
Zh: 他抬头仰望天空，说道，“我也希望如此，龙舌兰。真希望如此。我拿走的不是公寓钥匙，而是办公室的钥匙。”

En: Okay, so what happened next?
Zh: “好吧，那接下来呢？”

En: I rang my neighbours' buzzers. It was late, and most of them didn't even answer. Those who did assumed I was trying to sell them something and hung up before I could even explain the situation...
Zh: “我按响了邻居家的门铃。那时候很晚了，大部分人甚至都没有回应。还有一些回应的以为我是想推销什么东西，在我还没开口解释就挂断了……”

En: People are naturally wary of ad men, you see. One moment someone chats you up, five minutes later you've bought a box of edible lingerie and a strap-on. I don't begrudge them, especially since I was known to be one of the best... He pauses meaningfully.
Zh: “你也知道，大家天生会提防推销员。一会儿有人找你搭讪，5分钟之后你就买了一盒性感内衣和一个女性玩具。我并不妒忌他们，尤其是因为我自己就是最棒的那一种……”他意味深长地停顿了一下。

En: Heavy is the head that wears the crown.
Zh: 欲戴王冠，必承之重。

En: Just then I experienced a moment of clarity: I still had the key to my office! I could wait out the storm there...
Zh: “就在那时，我恍然大悟：我还有办公室钥匙！我可以在那里等待这场暴风雨过去……”

En: But when I reached my office I remembered that I'd asked one of my producers to change the locks that day -- and since I hired only the best, he'd already done it, and I couldn't get in...
Zh: “但是，当我赶到办公室的时候，我记起那天我让手下的一个制作人换锁了——而且因为我雇佣了最棒的员工，他已经换好了，所以我也进不去……”

En: Anyway, long story short, life spiralled out of control. I haven't gotten into my apartment for years, and my girlfriend left me because she didn't want to date a homeless man. The company, well, you see where I'm going with this...
Zh: “总之，长话短说，我的生活失去了控制。我已经几年没能回到自己的公寓，我的女朋友也离开了我，因为她不想跟一个无家可归的男人约会。至于公司，我想你明白我的意思了……”

En: He pinches his thigh as if to check whether this reality is *the reality*.
Zh: 他捏了一把大腿，似乎想确认这到底是不是真的*现实*。

En: I've only been a hobo cop for a few days now, but it doesn't seem so bad.
Zh: “现在我也做了几天的流浪警探，好像也没那么糟糕。”

En: The hobo lifestyle definitely has some perks -- not having to pay rent, first and foremost. Not being responsible for twenty other people is nice too.
Zh: “流浪的生活方式的确有些特权——首先就是不用付房租。不用对另外那20多个人负责也挺不错的。”

En: Look on the bright side -- you've got one hell of a story.
Zh: “往好的方面想——你得到了一个很棒的故事。”

En: There's this black point in the world, a hole. It's already claimed an entire commercial area, maybe you're another one of its victims?
Zh: “世界上有一个黑点，一个洞。它已经占据了整个商业区，也许你也是它的受害者之一？”

En: This would explain a lot -- I always believed it wasn't my fault that I ended up homeless on the beach with these two bums. He looks at his partners with a glint of sadness.
Zh: “这样就解释了很多事情：我一直相信——跟这两个流浪汉一样，沦落在海边无家可归的地步——不是我的错。”他看着自己的同伴，眼里带着一丝悲伤。

En: Yeah. Maybe I've exaggerated certain parts over the years. When you tell a story too often, it begins to take on a life of its own...
Zh: “是啊。也许这些年我夸大了某些部分。当你过于频繁地讲述一个故事的时候，它就开始有了自己的生命……”

En: But what matters is that it's true to my subjective experience. Anyway, that was all the story one bottle gets you. He looks at it. "Almost empty this one..."
Zh: “不过，最重要的是我的主观经验是真实的。不管怎么说，一瓶酒只能听这么多故事了。”他看着酒瓶。“这瓶都快空了……”

En: I don't need to hear any more. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我不用再听了。”（总结。）

En: It's a hard thing for a man to confront his past. He nods. "That's why I avoid mine at all costs."
Zh: 他点点头。“面对过去是一件很困难的事情。这就是我不惜一切代价避开它的原因。”

En: The gleam in his eyes and the slouch in his posture is so incredibly familiar...
Zh: 他闪烁的目光和没精打采的姿势看起来非常熟悉……

En: You might get scammed here.
Zh: 您可能是被骗了。

En: So this is how being on the other side of the barrel feels.
Zh: 所以这就是站在枪管另一边的感觉。

En: He clears his throat: "Summer of '44. Seventeen-year-old Gertrude Het is walking home from a late shift at the harbour. It's almost midnight. She stops for a cigarette near the canal..."
Zh: 他清了清喉咙：“44年的夏天。17岁的格特鲁德·海特在港口下了晚班，正走在回家的路上。那时已接近午夜时分。她在运河附近停了下来，准备抽根烟……”

En: Wait, I thought the Headless FALN Rider rode a bull? (Show him the figurine.)
Zh: “等等，我以为无头FALN骑士的坐骑是一头公牛？”（把雕像拿给他看。）

En: Wait, I thought that the Headless FALN Rider rode a bull?
Zh: “等等，我以为无头FALN骑士的坐骑是一头公牛？”

En: Our heroine finds herself enjoying the peace and quiet the canal provides... He looks up to the skies as if searching for peace himself...
Zh: “我们的女主角发现自己很享受运河带来的和平和安宁……”她仰望天空，似乎在寻找属于自己的那份平静……

En: There are many variations on the basic story, and the details often conflict. What everyone agrees on is that nobody knows the exact nature or identity of... the phenomenon.
Zh: “基础故事有很多不同的版本，细节通常都是冲突的。大家都赞同的只有一点，那就是没人知道那个……现象准确的性质或者是身份。”

En: What she doesn't know is that her peace is about to be shattered. From behind her comes the clattering of hooves. Startled, she turns around, and what does she see?
Zh: “她不知道的是，自己的平静很快就会被打破。来自身后，马蹄的咔哒声。她吃了一惊，转过身去，你猜她看见了什么？”

En: I don't know, what?
Zh: “我不知道，是什么？”

En: A horse?
Zh: “一匹马？”

En: A headless man, wearing a FALN tracksuit, riding a horse. That's right. It's the *Headless FALN Rider*.
Zh: “一个无头人，穿着一套FALN运动服，骑着一匹马。没错。它就是*无头FALN骑士*。”

En: Well, yes, but it's the man *on* the horse that's of interest here. A man...
Zh: “呃，是的，不过有意思的是*骑在*马背上的那个人。一个男人……”

En: Rumoured to be searching for his legendary FALN cap that went missing when he lost his head.
Zh: “有传闻说，他在寻找那顶传奇FLAN帽——当他丢掉脑袋的时候，那顶帽子也不见了。”

En: ... with no head on his shoulders! Wearing a FALN tracksuit. Searching for the legendary FALN cap that went missing -- when he lost his head.
Zh: “……肩膀上没有脑袋！穿着一套FALN运动服。寻找丢失的那顶传奇FALN帽——在他丢掉脑袋的时候不见的。”

En: That sounds pretty implausible to me.
Zh: “在我听起来不像是真的。”

En: That's super spooky.
Zh: “这也太吓人了吧。”

En: Yes. According to legend, young Gertrude Het had to endure years of psychotherapy before she was able to look at a horse or tracksuit again... And she's one of the *lucky ones*. He takes a sip from his bottle.
Zh: “是的。根据传闻，年轻的格特鲁德·海特经过几年的心理治疗才又敢看马或者运动服……而且她是一个*幸运儿*。”他从瓶子里抿了一口。

En: Well, there are many versions of this story, the most peculiar of which has the Headless FALN Rider riding on the back of another headless man.
Zh: “呃，这个故事有很多版本，其中最奇怪的是无头FALN骑士骑在另外一个无头人的身上。”

En: If I hadn't lost my keys that one time, I'd agree with you. But... life is a cruel mistress... He takes a sip.
Zh: “如果那天我没有弄丢钥匙，我会赞同你的观点。不过……生活是一个无情的女士……”他小酌了一口。

En: Gertrude Het may have been the first to witness the *Headless FALN Rider*, but she wasn't the last, oh no...
Zh: “格特鲁德·海特是第一个目击到*无头FALN骑士*的人，不过她并不是最后一个，哦不……”

En: I thought that he rode a headless pig? The lieutenant says with a little smirk.
Zh: “我还以为他的坐骑是无头猪？”警督假笑着说道。

En: Who was the Headless Rider before he died?
Zh: “无头骑士死前是什么人？”

En: I've got a hunch that it was the undercover cop. I'd want revenge, too.
Zh: “我有种直觉，应该是卧底。我也会想报仇的。”

En: Some say he was an undercover cop who blew his cover and got beheaded by the vicious gang he had infiltrated -- Now he rides, searching for his lost FALN cap, plotting revenge.
Zh: “有人说他是一个卧底，身份暴露之后，被潜入的那个邪恶帮派斩首了——现在他骑在马上，寻找自己丢失的FALN帽，计划复仇。”

En: Oh, headless brother, where art thou?
Zh: 哦，无头兄弟，你在哪？

En: Others claim he was a professional jockey who veered off-course during a steeple chase, ended up in somebody's back yard, and got decapitated by an exceptionally taut clothes line...
Zh: “其他人声称他是一个专业骑师，在一次马术障碍赛上，他偏离了路线，最后跑到某人的后院，被一根特别紧的晾衣绳割断了脑袋……”

En: Even decapitation couldn't stop his commitment to the sport. Are *you* that committed?
Zh: 就连斩首也阻止不了他为运动献身。*你*有那么忠贞吗？

En: Personally, I think he was just some guy who hanged himself from a really tall tree and the fall was so violent that his head came clean off...
Zh: “就我个人而言，我觉得他只是个想吊死在一棵高树上的人，最后掉下去的时候太过猛烈，脑袋都被扯断了……”

En: Coincidentally, at that exact moment, a horse happened to pass under him and his beheaded corpse mounted it, where it remains to this day... But then, no one really knows.
Zh: “巧合的是，就在那一瞬间，一匹马正好经过他身下，而他那具被斩首的尸身骑在了它上面，一直保持到现在……不过，没人真的知道是怎么回事。”

En: I bet he was the jockey. He seems super committed to his sport.
Zh: “我打赌是那个骑师。他似乎对这项运动非常投入。”

En: I've got to agree -- your theory sounds the most plausible.
Zh: “我必须同意——你的理论听起来更合理一些。”

En: None of those really sound plausible, to be honest.
Zh: “说真的，这些听起来都不是很合理。”

En: Okay. Whatever became of the Headless FALN Rider? (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧。那无头FALN骑士怎么样了？”（总结。）

En: For some reason this *does* strike you as the most plausible theory of them all.
Zh: 出于某种原因，你*的确*觉得这种理论是最合理的。

En: That explains the horse part, for sure. Don't know why he'd be wearing a tracksuit, though. Seems like a stretch, if you ask me...
Zh: “当然了，这就解释了马的部分。不过，还是不知道他为什么会穿着一套运动服。如果问我的话，似乎有点牵强……”

En: That would explain the tracksuit, and his need for revenge. Not sure how the horse fits in, though. I dunno, I don't buy it... He shrugs.
Zh: 他耸耸肩。“那就解释了运动服，还有报仇的渴望。不过，马的部分好像不太适合。我不知道，我不太相信……”

En: Isn't it? All the pieces fit together perfectly. That's how I know it's right...
Zh: “可不是吗？所有细节都能完美地拼接在一起。所以我知道我是对的……”

En: Pffft, he spits. "Like you could do better, Tequila..."
Zh: “呸，”他吐了一口唾沫，“说的好像你能做得更好一样，龙舌兰……”

En: No one knows. Some say he stalks Martinaise to this day, and can be seen near the canal when the clock strikes midnight. He makes a spooky gesture with his free hand.
Zh: “没人知道。有人说他一直在马丁内斯漫步，每当午夜钟声敲响，就能在运河附近看到他的身影。”他用空着的那只手比划了一个幽灵的形状。

En: The lieutenant smirks. "He won't though, because it's just a stupid legend."
Zh: 警督笑了笑。“他不会的，因为这只是一个愚蠢的传说。”

En: Aye! I saw him one night when I were right shit faced!
Zh: “哎呀！有天晚上我他妈正好跟他打了个照面！”

En: Have you got any more stories?
Zh: “你还有别的故事吗？”

En: I do -- but as you can see my fuel tank is running quite low, if you catch my drift... He spins the bottle in his hand. Not a single drop of liquid remains.
Zh: “有啊——不过你也看到了，我的油箱已经快耗尽了，如果你能明白我的意思……”他旋转着手中的酒瓶。里面的液体已经一滴不剩。

En: Have you got any more urban myths?
Zh: “你还知道更多都市神话吗？”

En: I actually do have one... the strangest of them all. But I'll need to *fortify* myself before I can tell that one...
Zh: “其实我确实知道一个……最奇怪的那个。不过再讲故事之前，我需要先*强化*一下自己……”

En: I got this special strike brew the Union uses. (Give it.)
Zh: “我有一种特别的罢工特酿，工会用的。”（交给他。）

En: Maybe you've heard? I used to be a very successful businessman. I've signed more than a few lease forms or whatever the fuck they were. He looks around. "Anyone got a pen? The pro's gonna do it."
Zh: “也许你听说过？我曾经是一个非常成功的商人。我签过很多份租约，或者诸如此类的鬼东西。”他四下看了看。“谁有笔吗？专业人士要表演了。”

En: Yes, I have this green ape one. (Lend him your pen.)
Zh: “是的，我有只绿人猿笔。”（把你的笔借给他。）

En: No, unfortunately.
Zh: “没有，很遗憾。”

En: Alright! He grabs the pen and paper from your hands and, very carefully, scribbles on the dotted line, next to 'Lilienne Carter': 'Idiot Doom Spiral'.
Zh: “好吧！”他从你手中接过纸和笔，然后非常小心地在虚线上，‘莉莉恩·卡特’的名字旁边，写下了：‘白痴厄运漩涡’。

En: Uh, can I have the pen back too?
Zh: “呃，笔也可以还给我吗？”

En: Don't know if I've mentioned it, but I used to be a businessman and *as* a businessman I am going to keep the pen for my trouble. He nods confidently.
Zh: “不知道我有没有提过，不过我曾经是一个非常成功的商人，而且，*作为*一个商人，我要留下这支笔来解决我遇到的麻烦。”他自信地点点头。

En: Fuckin' cops, man, always hasslin' the poor folk. He shakes his head. "You know what? I'm keepin' the pen and that's the end of it."
Zh: “死条子，总是骚扰穷人。”他摇摇头。“你知道吗？我要留着这支笔，这件事到此为止。”

En: I want to hear the story of your name again.
Zh: “我想再听一遍你名字的故事。”

En: I'd like to listen to the tale of the Headless FALN Rider again.
Zh: “我想再听一遍无头FALN骑士的故事。”

En: Can you tell me about the Cocaine Skull again?
Zh: “你能再跟我讲讲可卡因骷髅的故事吗？”

En: Of course -- drink first, story later. He holds out his palm.
Zh: “当然了——先喝酒，再讲故事。”他伸出手掌。

En: Interesting. He grabs the thermal cup and shakes it. "Ah, this is what they use to keep the working man going."
Zh: “有意思。”他抓过保温杯晃了晃。“啊，这就是他们让工人维持下去的东西。”

En: Right... a story specific to Martinaise and its history. He takes a sip and gargles the drink to prepare.
Zh: “没错……一个专属于马丁内斯和它的历史的故事。”他抿了一点漱漱口，做好准备。

En: For some reason the name Tequila fills me with foreboding. Maybe I *shouldn't* learn what it means.
Zh: “不知道为什么，龙舌兰这个名字让我有种不祥的预感。也许我*不应该*了解它的含义。”

En: You think you feel bad *now*, wait till you've heard the story.
Zh: “你觉得自己*现在*感觉很糟糕吗，等你听完整个故事再说吧。”

En: Suit yourself, Mr. Sunset. He shrugs.
Zh: “随你便，日落先生。”他耸耸肩。

En: Oh yeah, that's totally your style...
Zh: 哦耶，那完全就是你的风格嘛……

En: The lieutenant's brow is furrowed. He's listening as casually as he can.
Zh: 你的搭档眉头紧锁。他跟你一样，听得很用心。

En: Oh yeah, not the first time this comes up. (Cough awkwardly.)
Zh: “哦耶，反正也不是第一次了。”（尴尬地咳嗽两声。）

En: Yeah, sure, I did that thing. What of it?!
Zh: “是啊，当然，我做过那种事。怎么了？！”

En: Please, continue.
Zh: “请继续。”

En: Hey man, I'm not judging. He waves the bottle towards you. "This life's a valley of woes. Some of the highest-concept people in history have killed themselves -- *and* been drunks."
Zh: “喂，老兄，我不会评判什么。”他朝你挥了挥酒瓶。“生活就像一个痛苦的山谷。历史上有些最高概念的人自杀了——*而且*都是酒鬼。”

En: Either way, it was a late Saturday night, when we, the Union of Moribund Alcoholics, got our drink on. Nothing remarkable about this, we get our drink on 24/7. Makes everything warm and glowy, I trust you know the feeling.
Zh: “不管怎么说，那是一个周六的深夜，当时我们，垂死酒鬼联盟，正在喝酒。这没什么值得注意的，我们一周7天24小时都在喝酒。这样能让一切变得温暖和缓慢起来，我相信你能懂那种感觉。”

En: There is no train. Just you and the cold light of truth.
Zh: 没有什么火車。只有你和冰冷的真相之光。

En: Okay, I changed my mind. Sing me the song of Tequila Sunset.
Zh: “好吧，我改变主意了。给我唱一首龙舌兰日落之歌吧。”

En: No, not really... you talked a lot about some sad churches and how everyone's just out to fuck you.
Zh: “不，并没有……你说了很多悲伤小教堂的事，还有大家都是怎么想搞你的。”

En: Nothing much, you didn't want to talk about it. You just kept mumbling inaudibly to yourself.
Zh: “不多，你不太想谈起。你只是不停喃喃自语，根本听不见。”

En: Mostly you kept shouting 'I am The Law' and talking about how you were going to bring the law to all lawless places with your law jaw.
Zh: “基本上你就是不停地叫喊‘我就是法律’，还说要用你的法律之颚把法律带到所有不受法律约束的地方。”

En: Whuh...? Uh... geez, he stammers. "I told you I wasn't much of a storyteller. It's okay, I didn't want to tell them anyway."
Zh: “哈……？呃……天啦，”他结巴了。“我告诉过你，我不是什么讲故事的人。没关系的，反正我也不想说。”

En: ...is what you almost say, but the words choke in your throat. Do you really want to miss out on all this good stuff? Perhaps it's *significant*.
Zh: ……你差点就要说出来了，不过话语卡在了你的喉咙里。你真想错过所有那些好东西嘛？也许这很*重要*呢。

En: (Yes, say it. Say it!) "Stories *SUUCCKK*! I don't want to hear any of them. Ever!"
Zh: （是的，说吧。说出来吧！）”故事什么的*烂透*了！我一个也不想听。永远都不听！”

En: Oh, no. I wouldn't want that. Not at all.
Zh: 哦，不。我不想那样。一点也不想。

En: Exactly. So you know what to say.
Zh: 的确。所以你知道该怎么说了吧。

En: Why not? What's in it for me anyway?
Zh: 为什么不呢？再说了，对我能有什么好处呢？

En: There might be interesting details, fresh experiences, exciting rewards... you'd really ignore it all?
Zh: 可能会有一些有趣的细节，新鲜的体验，激动的奖励……你真想忽略掉这一切？

En: Yeah, you look like someone with a lot of cool stories. Wanna tell me how you even became Idiot Doom Spiral?
Zh: “是啊，你看起来像是那种知道很多酷炫故事的人。要不跟我讲讲你到底是怎么变成白痴厄运漩涡的？”

En: What is a... Tequila Sunset? You keep saying it.
Zh: “什么是……龙舌兰日落？你老在提这个。”

En: It's you. You're Tequila Sunset.
Zh: “是你。你是龙舌兰日落。”

En: Tequila Sunset? Yes, I prefer Tequila Sunset. It's my *nom de guerre*.
Zh: “龙舌兰日落？是啊，我更喜欢龙舌兰日落。那是我的*艺名*。”

En: Tequila Sunset? That's my *nom de guerre*!
Zh: “龙舌兰日落？那是我的*艺名*！”

En: Don't shake his hand.
Zh: 不要跟他握手。

En: So what's happening? He wipes his hand on his dirty Lickra pants and picks up his beer.\n
Zh: “所以，发生什么事了？”他把手放在脏兮兮的利卡尔裤子上擦了擦，然后拿起了啤酒瓶。

En: So what's happening? He picks up his beer.
Zh: “所以，发生什么事了？”他拿起自己的啤酒瓶。

En: Shake his hand.
Zh: 跟他握手。

En: Wait, Tequila?
Zh: “等等，龙舌兰？”

En: Reality? It makes me aggressively sad.
Zh: “现实？它让我非常难过。”

En: Don't know. People tell me I'm a cop, I'm getting used to that.
Zh: “不知道。大家说我是个警察。我已经习惯了。”

En: Yeah, Tequila Sunset! He takes a sip, "How are the, uhm, high-concept, reality-based adventures proceeding?"
Zh: “是的，龙舌兰日落！”他抿了一口，“你的，呃，高概念现实冒险进行地怎么样了？”

En: Not too hot. I'm on a 42-year losing streak...
Zh: “普普通通。我已经连续失败42年了……”

En: Not too hot. I'm on a 50-year losing streak.
Zh: “普普通通。我已经连续失败50年了。”

En: Sadly, things aren't going that well in Idiot Doom Spiral Land. Haven't found those keys yet; haven't won that great piece of ass back. No word from my business-buddies... He takes a sip from his beer.
Zh: “很可悲，在白痴厄运漩涡的土地上，一切都不会太顺利。还没有找到那些钥匙；还没有赢回那个女人的心。之前的商业合作伙伴也没有消息……”他抿了一口啤酒。

En: Things aren't going super well for Idiot Doom Spiral either. Haven't found those keys yet; haven't won that great piece of ass back. No word from my business-buddies... He takes a sip from his beer.
Zh: “白痴厄运漩涡这边的情况也不太好。还没有找到那些钥匙；还没有赢回那个女人的心。之前的商业合作伙伴也没有消息……”他抿了一口啤酒。

En: I have re-entered reality to conquer it. To bend it to my will. I am the law.
Zh: “我重新进入现实来将它征服。让它屈服于我的意志。我就是法律。”

En: Reality? It's perishing. Soon only glowing embers will remain of the atoms; of human faces, only echoes.
Zh: “现实？它很糟糕。很快原子只会剩下炽热的余烬；人类的脸庞，只有回音。”

En: Nothing special. Like everyone else. I have all sorts of wild thoughts running through my head, but then pick the most boring one.
Zh: “没什么特别的。跟其他人呢一样。我的脑袋里有各种各样稀奇古怪的想法，不过最后却挑了最无聊的那一个。”

En: Good to see you! How's business? How's the whole *reality situation* treating you?
Zh: “很高兴见到你！最近怎么样？整个*现实状况*对你怎么样？”

En: Well, nice meeting you but I've got to go. [Leave.]
Zh: “嗯，很高兴见到你，不过我该走了。”[离开。]

En: That's harsh. I'm like three or maybe four years into mine. Wait no, make it five. He looks at his shit-stained Lickra(TM) with a grim expression.
Zh: “那挺残酷的。我大概有三年或者四年了吧。不对，等一下，应该是五年。”他看着沾着屎印的利卡尔（TM）运动服，表情十分冷酷。

En: I know what you mean. Sitting around with these bums you really get to know the nitty-gritty of *reality*. And it isn't good. He looks at his buddies with mock appreciation.
Zh: “我知道你是什么意思。跟这帮流浪汉坐在一起，你才能真正了解到*现实*的真相。而且结果并不好。”他看着自己的同伴，脸上露出虚伪的欣赏之情。

En: What, like you've *never* threatened to paint the wall with your brains in front of some strangers?
Zh: “什么，你*从来没有*在一群陌生人面前威胁说要用自己的脑浆涂在墙上？”

En: Every word I said was true. Tequila Sunset will break the looms of reality.
Zh: “我说的每一句话都是真的。龙舌兰日落将会粉碎现实的阴影。”

En: Yeah -- looms. Why not? Then you jammed the pedal and ploughed right off the jetty and through the ice.
Zh: “是啊——阴影。为什么不呢？然后你猛踩踏板，冲出码头，穿过冰面。”

En: When we finally got there you were sitting on the beach, crying. You said that your badge and uniform were in the car. It was too late to get in there though, the carriage had sunk too deep.
Zh: “当我们最后赶到的时候，你正坐在海滩上。嚎啕大哭。你说自己的警徽和制服还在車里。不过现在想拿已经太晚了，汽車沉得太深了。”

En: That's not my name. I'm Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau. That's what you should call me.
Zh: “那不是我的名字。我叫拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托。你应该这么叫我。”

En: Nah, that name sucks. You're Tequila Sunset. Just embrace it, brother.
Zh: “不，那个名字糟透了。你就是龙舌兰日落。拥抱它吧，兄弟。”

En: My real name is Harry.
Zh: “我的真名叫哈里。”

En: No, that's just what your mother called you. Your real name is Tequila Sunset. Just embrace it, brother.
Zh: “不，那只是你妈这么叫你。你的真名是龙舌兰日落。拥抱它吧，兄弟。”

En: Ouch!
Zh: “好痛！”

En: I would've landed on my feet. I've got feline reflexes.
Zh: “我会双脚落地的。我拥有猫科动物的反射能力。”

En: Ouch, indeed. Reality was looking rather grim, just then -- me lying on my ass in a mud pit in the middle of a heavy shower. But when life knocked me down, I always got up...
Zh: “的确很疼。现实情况看起来十分残酷，就在那时——我正仰躺在一个泥坑里，而且正下着滂沱大雨。但是每当生活将我击倒时，我总能再站起来……”

En: So, now you've heard my tragic tale. What do you think? Like nothing you've ever heard, huh? He takes a long swig of his drink.
Zh: “所以，现在你听过我的悲惨故事了。你怎么觉得？以前没听过这种故事吧？”他喝了一大口酒。

En: I literally can't believe it.
Zh: “我真的不太相信。”

En: Tequila, I've thought about this series of events for a long time. If there was anything else to it, I would have thought of it by now.
Zh: “龙舌兰，这一系列事件我考虑了很长时间。如果还有什么的话，现在我早就想出来了。”

En: Wait, is that it? I feel like there are some steps missing.
Zh: “等等，就这些？我感觉好像少了几步。”

En: His eyes are your eyes.
Zh: 他的眼睛就是你的眼睛。

En: It was a day not unlike this one here... You both glance skyward.
Zh: “那天和今天不太一样……”你们都抬头望了望天空。

En: *Wet*, okay? It was raining really hard. There's a trace of derision in his voice.
Zh: “*潮湿*，好吗？雨下的真的很大。”他的声音里有一丝嘲弄的痕迹。

En: Are you telling the story of the Headless--
Zh: “你是在说那个故事吗，无头——”

En: Shut the fuck up, Rosemary!
Zh: “给我他妈的闭嘴，罗斯马雷！”

En: The streets are warmed by a southerly breeze. The lights of a passing motor carriage bloom and fade in the distance...
Zh: 一阵轻柔的南风温暖了整个街道。远处，一辆驶过的汽車車灯忽明忽暗……

En: The Headless FALN Rider?
Zh: “无头FALN骑士？”

En: Excuse me, are you telling the story, now? No? You're not? Well then... He scoffs.
Zh: “请原谅，现在是你在讲故事吗？不是？没有吗？那好吧……”他嘲弄到。

En: The Giant of Koko Nur?
Zh: “科科努尔巨人？”

En: What? No! I told you this is a *Martinaise* legend. It's a man...
Zh: “什么？不是！我说过这个一个*马丁内斯*的传说。是关于一个男人的……”

En: Tell 'em about the two feminists by the locks!
Zh: “跟他们说说水闸旁边那两个女权主义者的事！”

En: Fuck, Rosemary, they were dating -- no one said they were feminists. Everyone always misremembering this stuff...
Zh: “去你妈的，罗斯马雷，她们在约会——没人说过她们是女权主义者。每个人都会记错一些事情……”

En: Hmmm, this wouldn't be the Deponte-Delgado case, would it?
Zh: “嗯，不会是在说迪波特-迪尔加多案吧？”

En: What? You know it?
Zh: “什么？你知道吗？”

En: I've read the case file. But please, go on.
Zh: “我看过案件档案。不过你继续说吧。”

En: Right. *Ahem*. Early autumn of '46. Ulla Deponte and Eva Delgado are fishing near the water lock long after the sun has set...
Zh: “好吧。*呃哼*。那是46年的早秋。太阳落山之后，乌拉·迪波特和伊娃·迪尔加多在水闸附近钓鱼……”

En: The wind picks up, a sky already dark now blackens. Water starts falling from above, the first cold rain of the season...
Zh: “起风了，原本漆黑一片的天空变得更黑了。雨点开始从天上坠落，那个季节第一场寒冷的秋雨……”

En: Two women stand on a small outcropping of rocks. One of them is wearing a purple raincoat. Thin lines reach out from the rods into the sea. Small droplets start appearing on the surface with increasing frequency.
Zh: 两个女人站在一小块凸出的岩石上。其中一个人穿着一件紫色的雨衣。细线从鱼竿一直延伸到大海。地面上开始出现小小的雨滴，频率越来越快。

En: The women are caught in the downpour. They act quickly. Eva gathers the rods whilst Ulla turns around to reach for the tackle box...
Zh: “她们被困在了倾盆大雨中。她们的动作很迅速。伊娃收拾鱼竿，乌拉转过身去拿钓具盒……”

En: When she sees something! Her shriek is so violent that the residents of the nearby apartment building believe lightning has struck...
Zh: “然后她看到了什么东西！她的尖叫声如此惨烈，附近公寓大楼的居民还以为她被闪电击中了……”

En: Ulla makes a run for the shore, but Eva slips on a wet rock and disappears into the cold, cold canal with nary a sound...
Zh: “乌拉跑向岸边，不过伊娃踩在一块湿滑的石头上，几近无声地消失在冰冷的运河中……”

En: But there is no lightning. Only a heavy downpour and the silhouette of the *Headless FALN Rider* looming on the horizon!
Zh: “但是根本没有闪电。只有滂沱的大雨，还有地平线上若隐若现的*无头FALN骑士*的轮廓。”

En: The man falls ominously silent for a moment. "Her body is never recovered." He looks you straight in the eye.
Zh: 男人沉默了一会儿，然后直直地看着你的眼睛，说道：“她的尸体一直没有被找到。”

En: Don't turn around, don't turn around!
Zh: 别转身，别转身！

En: What happened to Ulla Deponte? (Ask Doom Spiral.)\n
Zh: “那乌拉·迪波特怎么样了？”（询问厄运漩涡）

En: What happened to Ulla Deponte?
Zh: “那乌拉·迪波特怎么样了？”

En: Word on the street is that she, you know... He makes a finger pistol with his right hand and lifts it up to his chin.
Zh: “坊间传闻说她，你知道的……”他用右手比了个手枪的样子，然后举到了自己的脸颊旁边。

En: What did the case file say? (Ask Kim.)
Zh: “案件档案上是怎么说的？”（询问金。）

En: Naturally, Ulla Deponte became the prime suspect in the disappearance of Eva Delgado. Deponte maintained that it was the so-called 'Headless FALN Rider' and that she ran, fearing for her life... He adjusts his glasses.
Zh: “很自然，乌拉·迪波特成为了伊娃·迪尔加多失踪案的头号嫌疑人。迪波特坚称是那个所谓的‘无头FALN骑士’，而且她因为怕死逃跑了……”他推了推眼镜。

En: No. She committed suicide before she could be taken into custody. They found her in the bathroom with a rifle, her face slowly peeling off the ceiling...
Zh: “没有。她在拘留前自杀了。他们在浴室里找到了她的尸体，旁边有一支步枪，她的脸正慢慢从天花板上脱落下来……”

En: During the investigation it became apparent that there was a love triangle, the third party being some small-time businessman -- I don't remember the exact details. The leading theory was that an argument broke out on the jetty and Deponte pushed Delgado into the canal, then cooked up this stupid cover story.
Zh: “在调查过程中，显然存在着一段三角关系，第三个人是个小商人——具体细节我不记得了。主要的推论是两人在码头爆发了争吵，迪波特把迪尔加多推进了运河里，然后编了个愚蠢的假故事。”

En: Was she arrested?
Zh: “她被逮捕了吗？”

En: Man, that's some grisly detail. Oh well, here's to another case closed! He takes a hearty swig from his bottle.
Zh: “天啦，这些细节也太可怕了。不过，又有个案子解决了！”他痛痛快快地从瓶子里喝了一大口。

En: Not a pretty scene.
Zh: “那场面可不是很赏心悦目。”

En: Gave herself facial deconstruction surgery. Real grisly stuff.
Zh: “给自己做了面部解构手术。真是太可怕了。”

En: You got a smoke on you? If yeah, you should light up, help set the scene...
Zh: 你身上有烟吗？如果有的话，应该点燃它，帮忙布置下场景……

En: Whoa.
Zh: “哇哦。”

En: Whoa is right, my friend. Gertrude Het may have been the first to witness the *Headless FALN Rider*, but she wasn't the last, oh no...
Zh: “哇哦就对了，我的朋友。格特鲁德·海特可能是第一个目击*无头FALN骑士*的人，不过她也不是最后一个，哦不……”

En: That part at least more or less corresponds to the case file.
Zh: “那部分或多或少跟案件档案是一致的。”

En: See? Told you. The man takes a long, self-satisfied sip.
Zh: “看见没？我告诉过你了吧。”男人满足地灌了一大口。

En: Anyway, that's the story of the *Headless FALN Rider*. Pretty crazy, huh?
Zh: “不管怎么说，这就是*无头FALN骑士*的故事。挺疯狂的，对吗？”

En: That's right. Getting a good run in's important to maintaining your physical *and* mental health.
Zh: 没错。好好跑步对保持身体*和*心理健康来说非常重要。

En: Speaking of, when's the last time you did your laps, porkchop?
Zh: 说到这里，你最后一次跑步是什么时候的事，猪猡？

En: I could probably use a good run myself.
Zh: “我自己也可以好好跑跑步。”

En: It helped, truly. With my trusty Sansarique Lickra(TM) tracksuit I felt like I could conquer the world...
Zh: “说真的，跑步很有帮助。穿着那套可靠的桑萨瑞克·利卡尔（TM）运动服，我觉得自己可以征服整个世界……”

En: No, you don't.
Zh: 不，你不用。

En: (Say nothing.)
Zh: （什么也不说。）

En: Whatever. The point is, reality was looking rather grim, just then -- me lying on my ass in a mud pit in the middle of a heavy shower. But when life knocked me down, I always got up...
Zh: “无所谓了。问题是，现实情况看起来十分残酷，就在那时——我正仰躺在一个泥坑里，而且正下着滂沱大雨。但是每当生活将我击倒时，我总能再站起来……”

En: If it sounds like it makes no sense, that's because it doesn't.
Zh: 如果听起来毫无意义，那是因为的确没有。

En: Why do you keep losing all your stuff?
Zh: “你为什么老是弄丢自己的东西呢？”

En: Good fucking question, Tequila! If I knew the answer, you think I'd be hanging out on a beach in this formerly premium but now extremely dirty two-piece Lickra(TM) tracksuit?
Zh: “这他妈真是个好问题，龙舌兰！如果我知道答案，你以为我还会穿着这套曾经很高档，现在却脏不拉几的两件套利卡尔（TM）运动服在海滩闲逛吗？”

En: I used to own my reality situation. My business buddies and I had our own creative services agency. I had a nice apartment, an even nicer piece of ass, but somehow it all got away from me...
Zh: “我曾经拥有自己的现实情况。我有商业合作伙伴，而且还有一家创意服务机构。我拥有一套漂亮的公寓，一个甚至比它还漂亮的女人，不过不知道为什么，这一起都离我而去了……”

En: Now I can't hang onto anything. Just last week I stole this nice new jacket, but then I lost it, too. The only things I haven't lost are these two drunks.
Zh: “现在的我什么也留不住。就在上周，我偷了一件漂亮的新夹克，然后把它也给搞丢了。我唯一没弄丢的只有这两个醉鬼。”

En: What was the name of your agency?
Zh: “你的机构叫什么名字？”

En: What's this about a lost jacket?
Zh: “丢失的夹克是怎么回事？”

En: What about the other drunks?
Zh: “其他醉汉呢？”

En: What's up with the tracksuit?
Zh: “运动服是怎么回事？”

En: Let me ask you something else. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我问你点别的事。”（总结。）

En: My agency, man... He takes a long, melancholic sip. "The Boom Boom Room. Our concept was combining high art with the lowest forms of marketing -- the colour red, breasts, and oil painting..."
Zh: “我的事务所，老兄……”他忧郁地灌了一大口酒。“叫砰砰砰。我们的概念是把高端艺术和最低层次的营销形式结合起来——红色、乳房和油画……”

En: I convinced my partners to reinvest some of our profits in an even-more high-concept 'cultural incubator' called 'Thin Air'. The artists were happy, the clients were happy.
Zh: “我说服自己的合伙人把一部分收益再投资到更加高概念的‘文化孵化基地’上，名叫“子虚乌有”。艺术家们很开心，客户也很开心。”

En: I was financing a group of poets in East Revachol who were developing a new, universal poetic language... but then it all went to shit... He looks toward the bay.
Zh: “我资助了东瑞瓦肖的一群诗人，他们正在开发一种新的、通用的诗歌语言……后来一切都搞砸了……”他看向海湾。

En: Sounds intriguing. What say you, art cop?
Zh: 听起来很吸引人。你觉得怎么样，艺术警探？

En: That's what's so cool about advertising -- It's kind of like art, except you can also get fucking *rich* doing it...
Zh: “这就是广告最酷的地方——它有点类似艺术，只不过你他妈的可以靠着它*变得富有*……”

En: Mixing oil painting and breasts to make ads isn't high art, it's just cynical wankery.
Zh: “把油画和涩情混合在一起打广告并不算高雅艺术，那只会被讥讽为低俗。”

En: Well, you know, sex sells. That's the first rule of advertising.
Zh: “呃，你也知道的，性就是卖点。这是广告的第一条规则。”

En: Maybe, but it's still tired.
Zh: “也许吧，不过还是挺让人厌烦的。”

En: I *know*... He takes a long, wistful sip of his beer. "It was fucking awesome. Until I went on a jog, unleashing a cascade of doom that washed it all away."
Zh: “我*知道*……”他若有所思地喝了一大口啤酒。“直到我去慢跑，释放出一连串的厄运，将一切都卷走了。”

En: I *know*... He takes a long, wistful sip of his pilsner. "It was fucking awesome. And now it's all gone."
Zh: “我*知道*……”他若有所思地喝了一大口比尔森啤酒。“它真他妈的太赞了。现在一切都没了。”

En: Man, mixing high and low, commodifying culture, that is extremely my shit.
Zh: “老兄，将高端和低层次混合，将文化商品化，这完全就是我的菜啊。”

En: The female isn't yours to objectify, not in art nor anywhere else!
Zh: “女性不是你物化的对象，不管是艺术，还是其他什么地方都不可以！”

En: That's rich coming from you, Tequila. You had some pretty wild views about women last weekend...
Zh: “你也太伪善了吧，龙舌兰。上周你对女人的看法还挺疯狂的……”

En: I don't know where he picked up these views, but wherever it was, he seems to be sincere about them.
Zh: “我不知道他是从哪里接收到这些观点的，不过不管是什么，他好像都是认真的。”

En: I *know*... He takes a long, wistful sip of his beer. "It was fucking awesome. Too bad I went on a jog, unleashing a cascade of doom that washed it all away."
Zh: “我*知道*……”他若有所思地喝了一大口啤酒。“它他妈的真的很棒。我*知道*……不幸的是我非要去慢跑，释放出一连串的厄运，将一切都卷走了。”

En: Yes, Tequila, yes! He punches you on the shoulder. "That was what we were all *about*... now it's all gone, of course."
Zh: “是的，龙舌兰，没错！”他一拳打在你的肩膀上。“这就是我们的*一切*……当然，现在都没了。”

En: Yeah, actually -- you're right. Let the market sort it out. No need for me to regulate this stuff.
Zh: “是啊，事实上——你说得对。让市场来解决这个问题。没必要让我来调节这玩意。”

En: That's so high-concept I have no idea what it means.
Zh: “这也太高概念了，我都不明白是什么意思。”

En: Anyway, that's not the point. The point is that mixing art and sex can make you fucking *rich*. Just don't go on a jog, unleashing a cascade of doom that washes it all away.\n
Zh: “不管怎么说，这不是关键。重要的是把艺术和性混合在一起，能他妈的让你*变得富有*。只是千万不要去慢跑，释放出一连串的厄运把一切全部冲走。”

En:
Anyway, that's not the point. The point is that mixing art and sex can make you fucking *rich*.	“不管怎么说，这不是关键。重要的是把艺术和性混合在一起，能他妈的让你*变得富有*。”
0x0100007B00001058	Dialogue Text	VILLAGE / IDIOT DOOM SPIRAL	Idiot Doom Spiral	Maybe you're right, who knows? Maybe we were behind the curve? We were fucking *rich*, though, that much I know..."
Zh: “也许你说得对，谁知道呢？也许是我们落后于潮流了？不过我们的确他妈的很*有钱*，这一点我还是知道的……”

En: Tequila, it's a verifiable tragedy. It was practically brand new. Sure, it didn't really go with my Lickra(TM) threads, but it did itch a lot less...
Zh: “龙舌兰，这是个经过验证的悲剧。它基本上是崭新的。当然了，它真的跟我的利卡尔（TM）运动服不太搭，但是穿上它的确没那么痒……”

En: Say, you're a detective, right? He looks at you, bleary-eyed. "Maybe you can help ol' Doom Spiral out... solve the case of the missing jacket! What do you say, Tequila?"
Zh: 他恍惚地看着你。“话说，你是个警探，对吧？也许你能帮一下厄运漩涡……解决失踪夹克的案子！你觉得怎么样，龙舌兰？”

En: Wait. You're asking a police officer to help find a jacket you stole and then lost?
Zh: “等等。你是在请求一名警察帮着寻找被你偷走然后又弄丢的一件夹克吗？”

En: Okay, sure. Where'd you lose it?
Zh: “好吧，当然可以。你在哪里弄丢的？”

En: I don't have time for this.
Zh: “我没这个时间。”

En: Yeah, exactly. You're here to serve, right?
Zh: “是啊，就是这样。你来这里是服务大众的，对吗？”

En: If I knew where I lost it don't you think I'd have it? I mean -- maybe I was up by the boardwalk? Or walking along the beach? Or checking out the abandoned fish market?
Zh: “如果我知道是在哪里弄丢的，你以为我会找不到？我的意思是说——也许是在木板道上弄丢的？或者是在海岸边散步弄丢的？或者是落在废弃鱼市了？”

En: Somewhere north of here, that's for sure. You could ask around, see if anyone's seen it.
Zh: “可以肯定的是，在北边的某个地方。你可以四处问问，看有没有人见过。”

En: Man, I thought you said you were a cool cop.
Zh: “老兄，你不是说自己是个很酷的警察吗。”

En: That might be the right idea. This here is one of the last of its kind. Should probably be in a museum, honestly. He takes another sip. "Can't get it anymore. It was too primo even for Graad."
Zh: 他点点头。“这也许是个好主意。这可是独一无二的了。老实说，可能应该存放在博物馆里。”他又喝了一口。“我再也弄不到了。对于格拉德来说，它都有些太过高端。”

En: What? You never seen 100% Lickra(TM) before? Go on, feel that primo material. The man extends his arm...
Zh: “什么？你之前从没见过100%利卡尔（TM）？来吧，感受一下这种高端的材料。”男人伸出了他的胳膊……

En: You really shouldn't touch it.
Zh: 你真不想碰。

En: I'll just admire it from afar, thanks.
Zh: “我从远处欣赏就好，谢谢。”

En: By 'primo' he means 'possibly carcinogenic'. Lickra(TM) was a synthetic fabric developed by  manufacturers in the middle '40s, notable mainly for the swishy sound it makes as the wearer passes by...
Zh: 他说的‘高端’指的是‘可能致癌’。利卡尔（TM）是一种40年代中期开发的混合纤维材料，主要是由于穿着者路过时发出的沙沙声而闻名的……

En: It's also known to become extremely itchy if not properly cared for.
Zh: 它出名的还有一点：如果没有得到恰当的照顾，穿起来会极其地痒。

En: Wow, you're lucky. He never lets me feel it.
Zh: “哇哦，你真走运。他从来都不敢让我碰。”

En: That's because your paws are fucking filthy, Rosemary! We're right next to the bay, you could wash them anytime.
Zh: “那是因为你的爪子他妈的太脏了，罗斯马雷！我们明明就在海湾旁边，你随时都能洗的。”

En: I'm all ears, Tequila.
Zh: “我洗耳恭听，龙舌兰。”

En: And then there's the smell, but you don't even want to think about that.
Zh: 然后还有那股味道，你甚至都不想去想。

En: Touch it.
Zh: 摸一下。

En: Good god, it's nearly impossible to describe how dirty this texture is. It's like rubbing two jellyfish skins together. You feel about 15% less human for having touched it.
Zh: 天啦，要描述质地到底有多脏基本是不可能的。感觉就像把两张海蜇皮放在一起摩擦。因为摸了这一下，你感觉自己少了15%的人性。

En: Randomized trials have also found Lickra(TM) to be associated with a number of exotic, highly malignant cancers. So you also have that to look forward to.
Zh: 随机检验还发现利卡尔（TM）跟几种奇异的、高度恶性的癌症有关。所以，你就自求多福吧。

En: Pretty nice, huh? This might be one of the last of its kind. Should probably be in a museum, honestly. He takes another sip.
Zh: “挺不错的，对吧？这可能那一类中的最后一件了。老实说，也许应该放进博物馆。”他又喝了一口。

En: Wow, you're lucky. He never lets me feel it.
Zh: “哇哦，你真走运。他从来都不愿意让我碰。”

En: That's because your paws are fucking filthy, Rosie! We're right next to the bay, you could wash them anytime.
Zh: “那是因为你的爪子他妈的太脏了，罗斯马雷！我们明明就在海湾旁边，你随时都能洗的。”

En: My fellow members of the Union of Moribund Alcoholics? They're exactly what they look like.
Zh: “垂死酒鬼联盟的其他成员？他们就是看起来那副样子。”

En: 'Ey! Tequila! You wanna buy some speed?
Zh: “哎！龙舌兰！你想买点思必得吗？”

En: Shut the fuck up, Rosemary! He's a cop, remember?
Zh: “他妈的闭嘴，罗斯马雷，他是个警察，还记得吗？”

En: I thought he was a cool cop.
Zh: “我以为他是那种很酷的警察。”

En: Don't call Abigail!
Zh: “不要打给阿比盖尔！”

En: A gurgling sound comes from the direction of the non-responsive man.
Zh: 没反应的那个醉鬼的方向传来一阵咕噜声。

En: And yeah, you're already acquainted with Abs. He points to the man in the pipe. "So yeah, that's basically us. We drink together."
Zh: “啊，你已经很熟悉阿比了吧。”他指向管道里的那个男人。“所以，基本上就是这几个人。我们一起喝酒。”

En: And this is Abs. He points to the man in the pipe. "So yeah, that's basically us. We drink together."
Zh: “还有这位是阿比。”他指向管道里的那个男人。“所以，基本上就是这几个人。我们一起喝酒。”

En: Me neither, Tequila. When I lost my keys, I lost more than access to my apartment -- I also lost my leverage over humanity. I wasn't a high-concept creative director anymore, I was just some homeless asshole with a premium Sansarique Lickra(TM) tracksuit.
Zh: “我也不信，龙舌兰。当我弄丢钥匙的时候，我丢掉的远远不止进入公寓的权利——还失去了对人类的影响力。我不再是一个高概念的创意总监，我只是个无家可归的混账，还穿着一套高端的桑萨瑞克利卡尔（TM）运动服。”

En: You can't imagine what that does to a man's confidence. He turns his eyes to the bottle. "Anyway, that was all the story one bottle gets you. Almost empty this one..."
Zh: “你无法想象这对一个男人的自信心有什么影响。”他看向酒瓶。“总之，一瓶酒只能换来这么多故事。这瓶都快空了……”

En: You do realize all of this is your own fault?
Zh: “你已经意识到这都是你自己的错了吧？”

En: I don't remember you being such a dick before, Tequila... He takes a slow, despondent sip from his bottle, then looks out across the bay toward the skyscrapers of La Delta.
Zh: “我不记得之前的你有这么混账，龙舌兰……”他慢慢地、沮丧地对着瓶嘴喝了一口，然后望向海湾对岸德尔塔的摩天大楼。

En: That doesn't mean you're wrong, though... Anyway, that was all the tale one bottle gets you. He turns his eyes to the bottle. "Almost empty this one..."
Zh: “不过，那并不意味着你错了……总之，一瓶酒只能换来这么多故事。”他看向酒瓶。“这瓶都快空了……”

En: I don't remember you being such a dick before, Tequila... He takes a slow, despondent sip from his bottle, then looks out across the bay toward the glittering lights of La Delta at night.
Zh: “我不记得之前的你有这么混账，龙舌兰……”他慢慢地、沮丧地对着瓶嘴喝了一口，然后望向海湾对岸德尔塔的点点灯光。

En: But that charm wears off pretty quickly. Before you know it, you're wearing a shit-stained tracksuit and spending your days picking tare.
Zh: “但是那种魅力很快就消失了。不知不觉，你已经穿着一套沾着屎印的运动服，整天都在捡回收品。”

En: Why didn't you go to the authorities?
Zh: “你为什么不去找有关当局呢？”

En: Well, at one point they came to me, but you know, I didn't have any ID on me -- so they tossed me in jail for two days...
Zh: “呃，在某个时刻，他们来找过我，不过你也知道，我身上没有任何身份证明——所以他们把我扔进监狱里关了两天……”

En: I can't say it increased my faith in the RCM. No offence, gentlemen. He shakes his head.
Zh: “不能说这样会增加我对RCM的信任。我无意冒犯，先生们。”他摇摇头。

En: Truly, he has the soul of an artist.
Zh: 说真的，他拥有一个艺术家的灵魂。

En: Don't call Abigail! Don't call!
Zh: “不要打给阿比盖尔！不要！”

En: See?
Zh: “看见了？”

En: Aye, every word he says is true.
Zh: “是啊，他说的每一个字都是真的。”

En: Aye, that were a classic, too.
Zh: “是啊，那个也挺经典的。”

En: If you only knew.
Zh: “你要是知道就好了。”

En: Well, Tequila, that's part of the legend -- no one knows for sure. There are a couple of possibilities, though...
Zh: “好吧，龙舌兰，那只是传说的一部分——没人能肯定。不过的确有几种可能性……”

En: Anyway, to each his own. You want to hear any other stories?
Zh: “无所谓了，各有所爱嘛。你还想听些别的故事吗？”

En: That's nothing. I've got an even *crazier* story.
Zh: “这没什么。我还有更*疯狂*的故事呢。”

En: I've already seen some weird shit on this case. A headless jockey in a tracksuit fits right in.
Zh: “我在这起案子里已经见过很奇怪的玩意了。穿着运动服的无头骑师好像最说得过去。”

En: Hard to argue with that, I suppose.
Zh: “我猜，这一点的确很难争辩吧。”

En: That's the reality situation for you. You think you got a handle on it, then *BLAM*! It throws some wild shit at you... He takes a long swig, then smiles. "That's why it's critical to stay well hydrated."
Zh: “那是你的现实情况。你觉得一切尽在自己的掌握，然后*砰*！它朝你扔了几坨疯狂的狗屎……”他喝了一大口酒，笑了笑。“这就是为什么多喝水很重要的原因。”

En: Yeah? Why don't you go eat shit, Tequila? There's no way you know a better one than that.
Zh: “是吗？那你为什么不去吃屎呢，龙舌兰？你不可能想出比那个更好的说法。”

En: Giants? That the best you got? Give me a break, Tequila.
Zh: “巨人？这就是你的真本事了？还是饶了我吧，龙舌兰。”

En: What about the Col Do Ma Ma Daqua? It's a magical self-replicating sound!
Zh: “那哈库多玛达塔呢？它是一种能自我复制的有魔力的声音！”

En: There's the Kind Green Ape. It's very kind... and green.
Zh: “还有善良绿人猿。它心肠很好……而且是绿色的。”

En: The Gnome of Geroma dissolves its victims with acid.
Zh: “赫罗玛侏儒会用酸液溶解掉它的被害人。”

En: The Giant of Koko Nur is as big as a mountain.
Zh: “科科努尔巨人跟山一样大。”

En: Acid gnomes? Sounds like a stupid, low-concept band name.
Zh: “酸液侏儒？听起来就是个愚蠢又低概念的品牌名。”

En: Do you always try to pick the lamest option possible? Come on, Tequila!
Zh: “你总是选择最没说服力的选项吗？拜托，龙舌兰！”

En: I have to admit, that's pretty high-concept. Still not as awesome as a headless rider in a FALN tracksuit, though.
Zh: “我必须承认，这个还挺高概念的。不过，跟穿着FALN运动服的无头骑士相比，还是差太多。”

En: Yeah, actually -- you're right. Nothing comes to mind.
Zh: “是啊，其实吧——你说得对。我什么也想不出来。”

En: Damn right it doesn't. So why don't you just shut up and leave it to the *master*?
Zh: “当然不行。所以你为什么不闭上嘴，然后交给*大师*呢？”

En: This last one is the most Martinaise story I've ever heard... He takes an almost comically long swig. Thus fortified, he continues.
Zh: “最后这个是我听过的最*马丁内斯*的故事……”他豪饮一口，时间长的几乎有些好笑。就这样，被强化过的他继续说道。

En: I've never heard it mentioned outside of here... At first I thought it was a joke, to be honest. But I've been on the coast eight, nine months now and in that time I've seen at least three expeditions come through, searching for something.
Zh: “出了这个地方，我就再也没听说过这个故事……说实在的，一开始我还以为这是个笑话。不过现在我已经在海岸边待了8、9个月，在这段时间里，我至少看见三支探险队来找过什么东西。”

En: Cryptids?
Zh: “神秘动物？”

En: Your lost keys?
Zh: “你不见的钥匙？”

En: A place to call home?
Zh: “一个叫做家的地方？”

En: Okay, what kind of expeditions?
Zh: “好吧，什么类型的探险队？”

En: Magic animals? No man, this is *serious stuff*...
Zh: “魔法动物？不是的，老兄，这可是很*严肃的玩意*……”

En: Hey, HEY. Fuck you, Tequila, he says, his voice level.
Zh: “哈，钥匙。去你的吧，龙舌兰，”他的声音很沉稳。

En: No, Tequila. Most people already know where they live. It's guys like you and me that are the exceptions...
Zh: “不，龙舌兰。大部分人都知道自己住在哪。只有你和我这种人才是例外……”

En: All kinds. I've seen archaeologists, gangsters, even a bunch of ad agency types. I'm telling you, Tequila -- this thing's got a *pull* on certain kinds of people.
Zh: “各种各样的。我看见考古学家，帮派分子，甚至还有广告公司之类的。告诉你，龙舌兰——这玩意对特定人群很有*吸引力*。”

En: Wait, what do you mean 'certain kinds of people'?
Zh: “等等，你说‘特定人群’是什么意思？”

En: Some of those expeditions come back after a week or so, looking haggard and dejected. Others don't return at all...
Zh: “有些探险队一周左右之后又回来了，看起来憔悴又沮丧。其他的根本就没回来……”

En: You know, *obsessive* types. People with *predilections*...
Zh: “你知道的，容易*着迷*的那种。有某种*嗜好*的人……”

En: The first time I saw one of these expeditions, I thought they were fucking with me. There was no way it could be true. It was just *too high-concept*, even for me...
Zh: “我第一次看见探险队的时候，还以为他们在跟我开玩笑。因为根本不可能是真的。就算对我来说，也有点*太高概念*了……”

En: I'm not even sure I should be telling you this story, to be perfectly honest. You're in a fragile state, and it might be too much for you to handle.
Zh: “凭良心讲，我甚至都不确定该不该告诉你这个故事。你现在的状态很脆弱，很可能承受不了。”

En: Wait, there's a tingling back here... the stirring of a faint sensation...
Zh: 等等，后面有些刺痛感……一种微弱的感觉在萌芽……

En: There's no way... it can't be... or *can* it?
Zh: 没办法……不可能……或者是*可能*？

En: Can't be what?
Zh: 什么不可能？

En: I can handle it.
Zh: “我可以承受的。”

En: You're right, maybe it's not a good idea.
Zh: “你说得对，也许这不是个好主意。”

En: Okay, fine, I'll tell you. But I'm warning you, it's pretty *out there*. His eyes narrow with mystery. "Our story begins at a legendary design studio, right here in Martinaise..."
Zh: “好吧，我可以告诉你。不过我要警告你，它真的挺*古怪的*。”他神秘地眯起眼睛。“我们的故事要从一家传奇的设计工作室说起，就在马丁内斯……”

En: There was this designer, his exact name is lost to history, but in life he was a legend. Made it big in Oranje, where he did some real pioneering work on grotesque and sans-serif typography. A fucking genius, man...
Zh: “有一个设计师，具体的名字已经被历史遗忘了，不过在生活中他是一个传奇。他在奥兰治很出名，在那里实现了畸形字体和无衬线印刷体的创举。真他妈是个天才，老兄……”

En: He takes a swig, wipes his mouth, then continues. "That is, if he even existed. Who knows? It's an urban legend, after all..."
Zh: 他灌了一口酒，擦擦嘴，然后继续说道。“如果他真的存在的话。谁知道呢？毕竟这只是个都市传说……”

En: Anyway, sometime later he started his own personal studio here in Martinaise, and that's when he started working on some *really* wild stuff. I'm talking some glass-smooth, forward looking design language, the kind of thing that would totally overthrow the old regime, design-wise.
Zh: “总之，过了一段时间，他在马丁内斯开了一间个人工作室。就在那时，他开始研究某些*真正*疯狂的东西。我说的可是某种如玻璃一般流畅的、前瞻性的设计语言，那种可以完全颠覆旧设计体制的东西。”

En: He existed alright. You feel it deep within your basal ganglia. He was as real as you are...
Zh: 他是真实存在的。你能在基底神经节的深处感觉到。他跟你一样真实……

En: What the fuck are you talking about?
Zh: 你他妈到底在说什么？

En: A paradigm-shattering revolutionary!?
Zh: 一场打破范式的革命！？

En: But then, something turned. You see, it's widely known that *nose candy* and pioneer graphic design work go hand-in-hand...
Zh: “但是后来，有什么东西变了。众所周知，*嗅用麻醉品*和先锋平面设计是密不可分的……”

En: Makes sense. I, too, need a variety of substances to operate at a high level.
Zh: “有道理。我也需要靠各种各样的药物来保持高水平的工作。”

En: Wait -- nose candy?
Zh: “等等——嗅用麻醉品？”

En: So what happened to him?
Zh: “所以他怎么样了？”

En: You know, Tequila. He taps the side of his nose twice. "*Nose* candy. The white railroad. *Party powder*!"
Zh: “你知道的，龙舌兰。”他拍了两下鼻翼。“*嗅用*麻醉品。白色铁路。*派对可可精*！”

En: HE'S TALKING ABOUT COCAINE.
Zh: 他说的是可卡因。

En: HE'S TALKING ABOUT COCAINE, BABY.
Zh: 他说的是可卡因，宝贝。

En: *Ohhh*, you mean *cocaine*.
Zh: “*哦哦哦*，你的意思是*可卡因*。”

En: The kids on the street also call it 'snow day'...
Zh: “街上的孩子把它叫做‘雪天’……”

En: 'Count C,' for its popularity among the aristocratic class of the prior century...
Zh: “‘C伯爵’，在上个世纪的贵族阶层很受欢迎……”

En: 'Sinus salt,' 'the white knight'....
Or 'Irmalan Gold,' for the plateau on which most of the world's supply is grown and harvested, typically by slave labour...
Zh: 或者是‘伊尔玛黄金’，因为世界上大部分供给都是在那片高原生长和收获的，通过典型的奴隶劳动……

En: Along with a number of more banal street names: Blow, of course, but also flake, powder, pearl. Really anything that's white will work.
Zh: 还有一些更老套的诨名：当然了，有白粉，除此之外，还有雪花，小白，珍珠。任何跟白色有关的都行。

En: Are you referring to... *cocaine*?
Zh: “你指的是……*可卡因*吗？”

En: Shit yeah, Tequila! You know *exactly* what I'm talking about. He gives you a comradely bump on the shoulder as a couple millilitres of booze slosh out of his bottle.
Zh: “当然是了，龙舌兰！你*完全*明白我在说什么。”他像兄弟一样撞了下你的肩膀，几毫升酒从瓶子里洒了出来。

En: You've got to understand... the work *this* guy was doing was *so* high-concept that regular amounts of cocaine just weren't cutting it. By the end they were bringing it in by the lorry load.
Zh: “你必须理解……*这个*家伙的工作实在是*太高概念*了，所以普通剂量的可卡因根本起不了作用。最后，他们是用货車来送货的。”

En: Now, as you might imagine, snorting that much cocaine can't be healthy for a regular human, right?
Zh: “现在，你可以想象一下，吸食那么多的可卡因对于一个普通人来讲肯定是不健康的，对吧？”

En: Right.
Zh: 对。

En: WRONG! Do it all the time.
Zh: 不对！我就一直抽啊。

En: WRONG. It's *super healthy*. Your brain tells you so, and when has your own brain ever lied to you?
Zh: 不对。这样*超级健康*。你的大脑是这么告诉你的，而且，你自己的大脑什么时候骗过你？

En: Hey, Tequila, pay attention! The story goes that one day he was balls-deep in work on what he thought would be his *pièce de résistance*: An advert so *minimal* it contained neither text nor images. Just... pure white...
Zh: “喂，龙舌兰，注意点！故事是这样的：有一天，他正完全沉浸在自己心目的*杰作*之中：那是一种*最低限度*的广告，完全不包含文本或图像。只有……纯粹的白……”

En: Apparently the idea was *too* high-concept even for this genius. He dropped dead right at his desk before he could finish. His last words are recorded to have been: It's as white as a blizzard... *of cocaine!*"
Zh: “显然，就算是对这位天才来说，这个主意也*太过*高概念了。还没来得及完成这件作品，他就倒在桌子前死了。他的临终遗言被记录了下来：“它就像一场白色的……*可卡因*暴风雪！”

En: What a loss for the world of design.
Zh: “对于设计界来说真是个莫大的损失。”

En: What a stupid fucking idea.
Zh: “这主意他妈的也太蠢了吧。”

En: (Say nothing.)
Zh: （什么也不说。）

En: But the story doesn't end there. Supposedly, when they performed the autopsy, the coroner discovered nearly a *quarter kilo of coke* jammed into his nasal cavity.
Zh: “但是故事并没有结束。按照惯例，当他们进行尸检的时候，验尸官发现有接近*1/4千克的可卡因*堵在了他的鼻腔里。”

En: That's... almost certainly anatomically impossible.
Zh: 这个……从生理结构上讲，基本可以肯定是不可能的。

En: WRONG, NERD! Where there's a will, there's a way.
Zh: 不对，书呆子！知道什么叫有志者事竟成吗。

En: WRONG AGAIN, NERD. Where there's a will, there's a way.
Zh: 又错了，书呆子。知道什么叫有志者事竟成吗。

En: That's right. Two-hundred fifty grams of blow had *accumulated* in there over the years. We're talking high-grade Saramirizian pure, not that cut-rate shit your grandma does.
Zh: “没错。多年以来，那里*积累*了250克的白粉。我们说的可是萨马拉的高级纯品，不是你爷爷抽的那种二流货色。”

En: There are those who believe that the designer was buried with this quarter-key of nose candy still lodged in his sinuses. *That's* what those expeditions are looking for...
Zh: “有些人相信那个设计师下葬的时候，鼻窦里还塞着那1/4的嗅用麻醉品。那些探险队寻找的就是*那个东西*……”

En: THE COCAINE SKULL.
Zh: 可卡因骷髅。

En: *The Cocaine Skull*!
Zh: “*可卡因骷髅*！”

En: Wait... There's no way... It can't be... or *can* it?
Zh: 等等……不对吧……不可能吧……或者，真有这个*可能*吗？

En: He existed alright. You feel it deep within your basal ganglia. He was as real as you are...
Zh: 他是真实存在的。你能在基底神经节的深处感觉到。他跟你一样真实……

En: Uhh... all the time?
Zh: 呃……始终如此吗？

En: Okay, I'm convinced. Let's go find it! (Undertake it!)
Zh: “好吧，我被说服了。我们去找吧！”（接下任务！）

En: Surprisingly, I'm not interested in looking for the skull.
Zh: “奇怪了，我居然对找骷髅没兴趣。”

En: Rosemary, you said something about islets?
Zh: “罗斯马雷，你说了些关于小岛的事？”

En: What have you learned from the other expeditions?
Zh: “你从其他探险队那里听到了什么？”

En: The Cocaine... Skull...
Zh: “可卡因……骷髅……”

En: Don't listen to him or his grandma. He's just making things up.
Zh: “别听他的，还有他的外婆。都是他自己瞎编的。”

En: No, my grandma told me -- I've heard other people say it too. That it's underwater. Or, no... He thinks. "Maybe it was the storm sewers..."
Zh: “不，我外婆说过——其他人也说过。它在水下。或者，不是……”他想了想。“也许是在雨水管……”

En: Or maybe it's in the *air*? Or in an ancient steppe pyramid off shore?
Zh: “也许在*空气*里？或者是离岸的一座远古草原金字塔里？”

En: They're pretty vague about it, in general. The gangsters like to claim they're looking for the grave of a friend... with picks and shovels...
Zh: “通常他们都说的很模糊。帮派分子喜欢说他们是在寻找朋友的坟墓……还带着铁镐和铁锹……”

En: The junkies, for some reason, are pretty upfront about it. They just say they're looking to snort some blow out of a dead man's nasal cavity.
Zh: “由于某种原因，毒贩子们反而比较坦率。他们说自己想从一个死人的鼻腔里弄出点白粉来。”

En: By now I'd say I know about as much about it as anyone on the coast.
Zh: “到如今，我会说自己了解的情况跟海岸上任何一个人都差不多。”

En: The archaeologists act all official about it, saying they're conducting 'serious research'. Honestly, I think they're not really scientists, just rich.
Zh: “考古学家们表现的很官方，说他们在进行‘严肃的研究’。老实说，我觉得他们并不是真正的科学家，只是比较有钱而已。”

En: Good thing, because I don't think you can find it right now anyway.
Zh: “这是好事，反正我觉得你也不可能瞬间找到。”

En: Your loss. Could've been an amazing, ridiculous adventure. He shrugs.
Zh: 自称为白痴厄运漩涡的男人耸耸肩。“那是你的损失。本来会有一场精彩绝伦又荒诞不羁的冒险旅程等待着你。”

En: That's alright. We've got plenty of adventures for one case.
Zh: “没错。就一起案子来说，我们已经有很多冒险活动了。”

En: Whoa, whoa, whoa. Hold on just a minute. Finding it right now is literally impossible.
Zh: “哇哇哇。先等一下。现在要找到它基本上是不可能的。”

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: For one, the way is blocked... by that big lorry that says 'Delta Logistics Company' on the side. You'd *definitely* have to search the area behind that lorry too. Yet it is impassable.
Zh: “首先，路堵住了……就是旁边那辆写着‘德尔塔物流公司’的大货車。而且你*绝对*需要搜查那台货車背后的区域。不过，根本通不过去。”

En: It's just not feasible within the economic and temporal frame of our current setup. Matter of fact, unless a bunch of money just falls out of the sky, we might *never* know what's up with that skull.
Zh: “在当前的经济和临时机构框架内，这是不可行的。事实上，除非天上掉下一大笔钱，我们可能*永远*也不会知道那个骷髅是怎么回事。”

En: And second, outfitting an expedition like that is expensive. It'd have to be a *big production* to do the Cocaine Skull justice. You need new gear, people who know what they're doing, all kinds of provisions...
Zh: “第二，筹备那样一支探险队是很昂贵的。要想公平对待可卡因骷髅，那就肯定需要*大手笔*。你需要新装备，知道自己在做什么的人，还有各种各样的补给品……”

En: I have to agree. We barely have what we need to solve the case we've got now. We can't afford to run around chasing after quasi-mythical pieces of drug paraphernalia...
Zh: “我不得不同意。基本上来说，我们连解决手上这起案子需要的东西都没有。更负担不起到处奔波，去寻找一件类似神话的吸毒用具……”

En: Besides, it would look *extremely* bad for the RCM to be caught up in something that has the word *COCAINE* writ large on it. The PR is tricky on this.
Zh: “再说了，如果别人发现RCM卷入跟*可卡因*有关的事情，情况肯定会*非常*糟糕的。处理这种公共关系很棘手。”

En: Damn, that's too bad.
Zh: “该死，太糟糕了。”

En: Yeah, well, that's the reality situation for you. Who knows, though? Maybe someday we'll get our chance...
Zh: “是啊，那是你的现实情况。不过谁知道呢？也许有一天，我们能有机会……”

En: Who cares? It was an idiotic idea anyway.
Zh: “谁在乎？反正这就是个很愚蠢的主意。”

En: What exactly are we talking about here?
Zh: “我们到底在说什么？”

En: Along with a number of more banal street names: Blow, of course, but also flake, powder, pearl. Really anything that's white will get the idea across.
Zh: 还有一些更老套的诨名：当然了，有白粉，除此之外，还有雪花，小白，珍珠。任何跟白色有关的都行。

En: I know, Tequila, I know... He takes a swig, considers pouring some out for this lost genius, then thinks better of it.
Zh: “我知道，龙舌兰，我知道的……”他灌了一大口酒，想倒一点出来纪念逝去的天才，不过考虑之后又放弃了。

En: A skull filled with cocaine?
Zh: “一个装满可卡因的骷髅？”

En: Oh, dear god, the lieutenant says, his palm rising to meet his face.
Zh: “哦，天啦，”警督一边说，一边举起手掌捂住了自己的脸庞。

En: Honest men on an honest quest. You should join them.
Zh: 实在人就该接受一个实在的任务。你应该加入他们。

En: WHAT?
Zh: 什么？

En: Do you have anything to fortify ol' Doom Spiral? Tell me you got some story juice.
Zh: “你还有什么可以强化厄运漩涡的东西吗？告诉我，你手上还有故事果汁。”

En: I changed my mind. I'll help you find your missing jacket.
Zh: “我改变主意了。我会帮你寻找那件丢失的夹克。”

En: Excellent. He rubs his hands together. "When can you get started? The sooner I get that jacket back, the sooner I can get my life back together."
Zh: “太棒了。”他搓了搓手。“你什么时候开始？我越快拿回那件夹克，我的生活就能越快回复正常。”

En: That said, I think maybe I was up by the boardwalk? Or walking along the beach? Or checking out the abandoned fish market?
Zh: “即便如此，我想也许是在木板道上弄丢的？或者是在海岸边散步弄丢的？或者是落在废弃鱼市了？”

En: If I knew where I lost it don't you think I'd have it? Come on, man!
Zh: “如果我知道是在哪里弄丢的，你以为我会找不到？拜托，老兄！”

En: Somewhere north of here, that's for sure. You could ask around, see if anyone's seen it.
Zh: “可以肯定的是，在北边的某个地方。你可以四处问问，看有没有人见过。”

En: Any idea where you last had it?
Zh: “你最后一次穿着它是什么时候？”

En: Okay, here's your jacket.
Zh: “好吧，这是你的夹克。”

En: I found your jacket.
Zh: “我找到你的夹克了。”

En: Ah, Tequila, I *knew* you'd come through. That's fucking great, man!
Zh: “啊，龙舌兰，我就*知道*你能成功。这他妈的真是太棒了，老兄！”

En: Let me see. What? This isn't my jacket. My jacket was beautiful. This is fucking filthy! What am I supposed to do with this?
Zh: “让我看看。什么？这不是我的夹克。我的夹克很漂亮。这他妈的也太脏了吧！我要它能干什么？”

En: (Give him the jacket.)
Zh: （把夹克给他。）

En: I'll start right away.
Zh: “我马上开始。”

En: You're a god damn hero, Tequila! He raises his bottle in salute, then takes a gigantic swig.
Zh: “你真他妈是个英雄，龙舌兰！”他举起瓶子向你致敬，然后猛灌了一口酒。

En: Look, I may have just seen an omen of my impending death. I don't care about the jacket.\n
Zh: “你看，我刚才看见了自己将不久于人世的预兆。所以我不在乎什么夹克了。”

En: Look, I just stumbled upon *another* dead body. I don't care about the jacket.
Zh: “你看，我刚才*又*偶然发现了一具尸体。我不在乎什么夹克。”

En: What do you expect? You left it outside for a week!
Zh: “你在期待什么？你把它落在外面一星期了！”

En: I'm sorry. I found it like this.
Zh: “很抱歉。我找到它的时候就是这个样子了。”

En: I'm not taking a disgusting pile of hobo rags. I may be in an irrecoverably decaying orbit, but I've still got standards...
Zh: “我才不要这个恶心的流浪汉破布堆呢。我也许是踏上了一条无法回头的衰败之路，但我自己还是有标准的……”

En: Either bring it back the way it was before, or find a dumpster to burn it in. He hands it to you.
Zh: 他把夹克递给你。“要么把它带回之前的地方，要么就找个垃圾箱把它烧了吧。”

En: The lieutenant inspects the jacket in your hands. "You know, despite the *guano* it looks like the jacket itself is stain-resistant. It may just need a good scrubbing."
Zh: 警督端详着你手中的夹克。“你知道的，尽管沾了*鸟粪*，夹克本身看起来像是防污渍的。可能只需要好好擦洗一下。”

En: My jacket?
Zh: “我的夹克？”

En: Yeah, the one you had me clean the seagull shit off of.
Zh: “是啊，你让我把海鸥屎清干净那件。”

En: A look of consternation crosses the man's face. He looks at you, then at his bottle, then back at you...
Zh: 男人脸上掠过惊慌失措的表情。他看看你，又看了看手中的酒瓶，然后又看向你……

En: What the fuck are you talking about, Tequila?
Zh: “你他妈到底在说什么呢，龙舌兰？”

En: What the fuck are *you* talking about?
Zh: “*你*他妈到底又在说什么？”

En: So you're saying this *isn't* your jacket?
Zh: “所以你是说，这*不是*你的夹克？”

En: 'FALN'? That's medium-concept stuff. Not my style at all... I can't believe I'm saying this, but maybe you should lay off the booze -- It's fucking with your head.
Zh: “‘FALN’？那是中概念的玩意。完全不是我的风格……我不敢相信自己居然会这么说，不过也许你应该戒酒——它把你的脑袋都弄坏了。”

En: Rosemary, what the fuck is Tequila talking about?
Zh: “罗斯马雷，龙舌兰到底在说什么？”

En: Aye, that's the jacket you stole two weeks ago. From the kid who was making it with his gal on the beach.
Zh: “啊，那是你两周之前偷的夹克。当时那个孩子正在海滩上跟女朋友亲热。”

En: That's disgusting. I've never done anything like that in my life. You're both delusional. He scoffs. "'FALN'? That's medium-concept stuff."
Zh: “真是太恶心了。我这辈子从来没做过那种事。你们俩都得妄想症了吧。”他嘲弄到。“‘FALN’？那是中概念的玩意。”

En: Your loss. I'm keeping the jacket for myself. (Keep it.)
Zh: “这是你的损失。夹克我就自己留着了。”（留下它。）

En: That shit is so medium-concept I wouldn't touch it with a stick. But yeah, okay, I'm sure it looks great on you.
Zh: “这狗屁玩意太中概念了，就算拿着棍子我都不会碰它一下。不过是啊，我肯定你穿着会很好看。”

En: Yeah, it was pretty filthy, though, so I got it cleaned for you.
Zh: “是啊，它是很脏，不过我帮你洗干净了。”

En: Underneath the accumulated filth the jacket itself appears to be stain-resistant. Maybe it just needs a good scrubbing?
Zh: 在堆积的污物下面，这件夹克本身似乎是防污渍的。也许只需要好好擦洗一下？

En: Wait! Maybe there's another way... maybe up around the coast? Don't give up now!
Zh: 等等！也许还有别的方法……也许就在海岸附近？不要现在放弃！

En: What are you talking about? Don't give up! Maybe there's another way... maybe up around the coast?
Zh: 你在说什么呢？不要放弃！也许还有别的方法……也许就在海岸附近？

En: I already found a filthy jacket near the boardwalk.
Zh: “我在木板道附近发现了一件肮脏的夹克。”

En: Holy shit! Tequila, how did you? Never mind -- I don't care, just let me see the jacket.
Zh: “天啦！龙舌兰，你是怎么做到的？无所谓了——我不在乎，让我看看那件夹克。”

En: I found a little more than I bargained for.
Zh: “我发现的比我预想的还要多。”

En: What's that supposed to mean?
Zh: “那是什么意思？”

En: Nothing. (Give him the jacket.)
Zh: “没什么。”（把夹克递给他。）

En: There was a dead body nearby.
Zh: “附近有一具尸体。”

En: What's that got to do with my jacket?
Zh: “那跟我的夹克有什么关系？”

En: Probably nothing. Forget about it. (Give him the jacket.)
Zh: “可能没有。忘了吧。”（把夹克递给他。）

En: It's a pretty freaky coincidence.
Zh: “可能只是个奇怪的巧合。”

En: What do I care about some local stiff? You gonna give me the jacket or no?
Zh: “我为什么要在乎本地人的事？你到底给不给我夹克了？”

En: Fine, take the stupid thing. (Give him the jacket.)
Zh: “好吧，拿着这个愚蠢的东西吧。”（把夹克递给他。）

En: It becomes abundantly clear to you how this man managed to lose his keys, business, friends and girlfriend.
Zh: 你已经非常清楚，这个男人是怎么弄丢自己的钥匙、生意、朋友，还有女朋友的。

En: I went through some dark shit to get this for you. Take the fucking jacket. (Give it to him.)
Zh: “帮你找这玩意让我吃了不少苦头。拿着这件该死的夹克吧。”（交给他。）

En: I went through a lot of trouble to get this for you. Take the fucking jacket. (Give it to him.)
Zh: “帮你找这玩意给我添了不少麻烦。拿着这件该死的夹克吧。”（交给他。）

En: You know what? Fine. Maybe I can pawn it for some booze.
Zh: “你知道吗？好吧，也许我可以把它当了换点酒喝。”

En: Aye, good thinking.
Zh: “是啊，这主意不错。”

En: In the harbour's dark, her cigarette is a beacon, dancing alone. The image comes to you effortlessly, as though you'd walked the same streets yourself a thousand times.
Zh: 在那黑暗的港口，她的香烟就像一座灯塔，孤独地舞动着。那个画面毫不费力地进入你的脑海，就好像你已经在那条街道上走过了几千遍。

En: That shit is so medium-concept I wouldn't touch it with a stick. But yeah, okay, I'm sure it looks great on you.
Zh: “这狗屁玩意太中概念了，就算拿着棍子我都不会碰它一下。不过是啊，我肯定你穿着会很好看。”

En: Oh, right, I've seen that!
Zh: “哦，好吧，我看到了！”

En: Second, outfitting an expedition like that is expensive. It'd have to be a *big production* -- to do the Cocaine Skull justice. You need new gear, people who know what they're doing, all kinds of provisions...
Zh: “第二，筹备那样一支探险队是很昂贵的。要想公平对待可卡因骷髅，那就肯定需要*大手笔*。你需要新装备，知道自己在做什么的人，还有各种各样的补给品……”

En: As you've already determined, the actual number is just over 200 -- but what's a little embellishment between friends?
Zh: 正如你断定的，实际数字应该是200多一点——不过朋友之间稍微挪动一点又怎么样呢？

En: As you've already determined, the actual number is 216 -- but what's a little embellishment between friends?
Zh: 正如你断定的，实际数字应该是216——不过朋友之间稍微挪动一点又怎么样呢？

En: I have this blue oblong pen. (Lend him your pen.)
Zh: “我有一支蓝色的椭圆形钢笔。”（把你的笔借给他。）

En: Don't know if I've mentioned it, but I used to be a businessman and *as* a businessman I am going to keep the pen for my trouble. He nods confidently.
Zh: “不知道我有没有提过，不过我曾经是一个非常成功的商人，而且，*作为*一个商人，我要留下这支笔来解决我遇到的麻烦。”他自信地点点头。

En: Fuckin' cops, man, always hasslin' the poor folk. He shakes his head. "You know what? I'm keepin' the pen and that's the end of it."
Zh: “死条子，总是骚扰穷人。”他摇摇头。“你知道吗？我要留着这支笔，这件事到此为止。”

En: Right, okay. Never mind that I asked.
Zh: “是的，好吧。别介意我这么问。”

En: He taps against his head. "Askin' is how you get ahead."
Zh: 他拍了拍脑袋。“询问是成功的秘诀。”

En: Now the only things I haven't lost are these two drunks.
Zh: “现在我唯一没弄丢的只有这两个醉鬼。”

En: It's an okay jacket. The lieutenant shrugs. "If you're into that look."
Zh: “这件夹克还可以。”警督耸耸肩。“如果你喜欢那种装扮的话。”

En: Okay, let's step aside for a second, I have something I want to talk about.
Zh: “好吧，我们去旁边待一会儿，我有些话要跟你说。”

En: “You mean *this* jacket that I discovered on the boardwalk and had cleaned on a whim?”
Zh: “你是说，我在木板道上发现的*这件*夹克，而且还心血来潮地把它洗干净了？”

En: Hmmm. The man examines the jacket for a moment, a look of consternation on his brow...
Zh: “嗯。”男人仔细看了一会儿夹克，眉间露出惊愕的表情……

En: At least you got a nice jacket for your troubles. The lieutenant shrugs.
Zh: “至少你费了这么大劲换来了一件不错的夹克。”警督耸耸肩。

En: I've got this blue medicinal spirit, how about that?
Zh: “我有瓶蓝色的医用烈酒，怎么样？”

En: He raises his palm as if to shun you back to wherever you came from. "No-no-no, contrary to popular belief, I enjoy being alive."
Zh: 他抬起手掌，似乎想把你赶回你来的地方。“不不不，跟民间信仰相反，我喜欢活着的感觉。”

En: Nah, this isn't it. 'FALN'? That's medium-concept stuff. Totally not my style.
Zh: “不，不是它。‘FALN’？那是中概念的玩意。完全不是我的风格。”

En: So you're saying there's *another* lost jacket out there somewhere?
Zh: “所以你是说，某个地方*还有*一件丢失的夹克？”

En: C'mon, man, this *is* your jacket. You just don't remember it.
Zh: “拜托，老兄，这*就是*你的夹克。你只是不记得了。”

En: No fucking way. Idiot Doom Spiral never forgets a piece of premium menswear...
Zh: “他妈的，绝对不可能。白痴厄运漩涡永远不会忘记高级男装的……”

En: It's a big world out there, Tequila. Lotta lost jackets in it. Don't know why you'd think this could possibly be mine...
Zh: “外面的世界很大，龙舌兰。里面有很多丢失的夹克。不知道你为什么会觉得这件可能是我丢的……”

En: Tell you what, Tequila: Why don't *you* just hang onto that one. I'll get another jacket, some day...
Zh: “跟你这么说吧，龙舌兰：*你*为什么不留着它呢。总有一天，我会再弄一件夹克……”

En: Ey, Doom Spiral! Ain't that the jacket you stole the other week?
Zh: “哎呀，厄运漩涡！那不是你几周前偷的那件夹克吗？”

En: Not on your fucking life, Rosemary! What's wrong with you people? Like I'd be caught dead wearing 'FALN' like some low-concept bicycle courier...
Zh: “妈的怎么可能，罗斯马雷！你们这群人有什么毛病？说的好像我会像那些低概念自行車快递员一样，穿着‘FALN’夹克死在什么地方……”

En: You're right to be scared. This is all your fault.
Zh: “你感到害怕是对的。这全都是你的错。”

En: You're in no danger. (Whisper) "The working class have no idea what's happening to them."
Zh: “你没有危险。”（低语到）“工人阶级还不知道发生了什么。”

En: That they don't... She leans against the railing, looking up at the grey sky.
Zh: “他们不会的……”她倚靠在栏杆上，抬头望着灰蒙蒙的天空。

En: So am I -- and I *do* know how to use one... She leans against the railing, looking up at the grey sky.
Zh: “我也是——而且我*的确*知道该怎么用……”她倚靠在栏杆上，抬头望着灰蒙蒙的天空。

En: This story she will tell only before she *leaves* Martinaise. At the very end of her stay.
Zh: 这个故事只有在她*离开*马丁内斯之前才会告诉你。在她停留在这里的最后时刻。

En: All right. Politics time. Let's *react*.
Zh: 好吧。政治时间到。让我们*作出反应*吧。

En: Real men, real politics, real thoughts in your head!
Zh: 真正的人，真实的政治，你脑袋里真正的想法！

En: Don't worry. I got her sword. (Show it.)
Zh: “别担心。她的剑在我这里。”（展示一下。）

En: Perhaps you shouldn't have. I sort of liked it on her... She leans against the railing, looking up at the grey sky.
Zh: “也许你不该这么做。我比较喜欢她佩戴的样子……”她靠在栏杆上，抬头望着灰色的天空。

En: The woman is tying a complicated nautical knot around the post.
Zh: 女人在柱子上打一个复杂的海员结。

En: I'll just keep the Cor-de-Leite in the channel, if that's okay. It's too shallow near the pier... She winds the mooring line around a post.
Zh: “如果可以的话，我还是想把考迪莱勒留在航道里。码头附近的水太浅……”她把锚绳缠绕在柱子上。

En: Aye, ma'am... And it's a jetty, by the way.
Zh: “是啊，夫人……顺便说一句，这是个栈桥。”

En: This island?
Zh: “这座小岛？”

En: Of course. Jetty. I prefer a good jetty to a pier any day. Jet-jet-jetty...
Zh: “当然了，栈桥。不管怎么说，我宁愿要个好栈桥也不要码头。栈栈栈桥……”

En: Hello, ma'am.
Zh: “你好，夫人。”

En: J-j-j-jettady-jetty.
Zh: “栈栈栈栈栈桥。”

En: Something about the way she says it makes you want to sing.
Zh: 她说话的方式让你想要唱歌。

En: This isola?
Zh: “这个洲？”

En: Oh jetty, oh jetty.... she responds mournfully...
Zh: “哦，栈桥啊栈桥……”她悲伤地回应着……

En: Yes -- Insulinde.
Zh: “是的——伊苏林迪。”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: It's good to see you here. I only just arrived myself.
Zh: “很高兴在这里见到你们。我刚刚才到。”

En: Hello, detective. She fastens the end of the line around the post and straightens her back.
Zh: “你好，警探。”她把锚绳末端系在一根柱子上，挺直了后背。

En: Godspeed, detective.
Zh: “祝你成功，警探。”

En: Do we? He glances at his watch. It doesn't look like he does.
Zh: “我们有吗？”他瞥了一眼手表。他的样子看起来并非如此。

En: You leave her fastening the mooring line around the post rotting in the water.
Zh: 你留下她一个人，把锚绳系在已经被水泡烂的一根柱子上。

En: Well, it was good running into you. [Leave.]
Zh: “呃，很高兴见到你。”[离开。]

En: She's tying a complicated nautical knot around the post.
Zh: 她在柱子上打一个复杂的海员结。

En: What brings you here?
Zh: “你为什么到这里来了？”

En: Tell me now, we have time.
Zh: “现在说吧，我们有时间。”

En: Tell me now, I have time.
Zh: “现在说吧，我有时间。”

En: I need your help with something, right away! No time to talk!
Zh: “我需要你的帮助，马上！没时间说话了！”

En: ...maybe there is something else I can assist you with? While you're *hot* in pursuit?
Zh: “……也许我能为你做点别的什么？在你*忙于*追查的时候？”

En: Nothing, really... I've had my eye on this jetty for weeks now. So I decided to investigate it personally. This cluster of buildings isn't on any of the official maps, as far as I can tell.
Zh: “没什么，真的……我已经盯着这个栈桥好几个星期了。所以我决定亲自调查一下。据我所知，这个建筑群并没有出现在任何官方地图上。”

En: I hear you have singled out a *suspect* and are in pursuit. This is cause for cautious optimism -- I would not want to delay you...
Zh: “我听说你挑出了一个*嫌疑人*，现在正在追查。这也是要保持谨慎的乐观态度的原因——我不想耽误你……”

En: ...then secures the mooring line. "It's good to see you here, detective. I only just arrived myself."
Zh: ……然后固定住锚绳。“很高兴在这里见到你，警探。我刚刚才到。”

En: ...then secures the mooring line. "It's good to see you here, detectives. I only just arrived myself."
Zh: ……然后固定住锚绳。“很高兴在这里见到你们，警探们。我刚刚才到。”

En: Their cloud shadows slide over the ruins of Revachol West -- wherever they pass, the temperature drops slightly but perceptibly.
Zh: 云影在瑞瓦肖西部的废墟上空滑过——不论经过哪里，温度都会缓慢而明显的下降。

En: I knew you would sympathize. She nods. "Most Revacholians will never know what this place means, our home -- this island of matter. Or why they were ferried over in the first place..."
Zh: “我知道你会有同感。“她点点头。“大部分瑞瓦肖人永远不会知道这个地方意味着什么，我们的家园——这个重要的洲。或者他们当初为什么会被运送过来……”

En: What brings you here, madam?
Zh: “是什么风把你给吹来了，夫人？”

En: Oblivion -- that's so me.
Zh: “遗忘——那就是我本人。”

En: I'm guessing you *don't* know then...
Zh: “那我猜你*并不*知道……”

En: What is an isola, exactly?
Zh: “洲到底是什么？”

En: Hello, detectives. She fastens the end of the line around the post and straightens her back.
Zh: “警探们，你们好。”她把锚绳末端系在一根柱子上，挺直了后背。

En: Fortunately for you, madam, the RCM is on the scene.
Zh: “你很幸运，夫人，RCM就在现场。”

En: So how do you like it here? (Look around.)
Zh: “所以你觉得这里怎么样？”（环顾四周。）

En: Hmh... How *do* I like it? She casts her gaze toward the village -- slush melting on the cinder blocks, construction work left half-finished ten years ago...
Zh: “嗯……我*觉得*这里怎么样？”她把目光投向了村子——十年前的建设工程修到一半就停了，焦渣砖上盖着雪泥……

En: This... fishing village?
Zh: “这个……渔村？”

En: That -- and she's also keeping an eye on *you*.
Zh: 那个——而且她也在关注着*你*。

En: Have you been spying on us?
Zh: “你一直在监视我们吗？”

En: Have you been spying on me?
Zh: “你一直在监视我吗？”

En: 'Spying' has such a negative connotation. I did track your progress along the coast, however, and decided I would be better able to assist you from here...
Zh: “‘监视’有一种特别负面的含义。我的确跟踪了你在海岸上的调查进度，而且显然我在这里能更好地协助你……”

En: No matter -- Lilienne and I are getting on much better. For one, she appreciates the concept of *rent*.
Zh: “没关系——莉莉恩和我的关系好多了。举个例子来说，她很欣赏*租借*的概念。”

En: Remind me to tell you one day. She corrects the scarf around her neck. "For now -- how can I assist you in this new location?"
Zh: “记得提醒我找一天告诉你。”她调整了一下脖子上的围巾。“现在——在这个新地方，我有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: You detect a note of longing in her voice.
Zh: 你在她的声音里察觉到一丝渴望。

En: The smell of salt and dog shit in the background.
Zh: 四周弥漫着盐和狗屎的味道。

En: Water drips down eaves of eternite. The jetty below her feet creaks to the tune.
Zh: 雨滴从亘石制成的屋檐滑落。她脚下的栈桥嘎吱作响。

En: Tell me something else then.
Zh: “那就跟我说点别的吧。”

En: It's pornographically poor. The street has no name, all the men are dead or missing... and is that the carcass of a motor carriage over there? She squints her eyes...
Zh: “这里穷得很性感。这条街道没有名字，所有人不是死了，就是失踪了……还有，那边是一辆汽車的残骸吗？”她眯起眼睛……

En: Of course. I hear you have singled out a *suspect* and are in pursuit -- this is cause for cautious optimism. How can I help you at this juncture?
Zh: “当然。我听说你挑出了一个*嫌疑人*，正在追查当中——这也是要保持谨慎的乐观态度的原因。在这个节骨眼下，我能怎么帮你呢？”

En: The place is doomed either way.
Zh: “反正这个地方也被诅咒了。”

En: Try not to be scared. This is just how the *real* city looks.
Zh: “尽量不要感到害怕。这才是这座城市*真正*的模样。”

En: It's a pity. Most of these people will never know what this place means -- this island of matter. Or why they were ferried over in the first place...
Zh: “很遗憾。这里大部分人永远不会知道这个地方意味着什么——这个重要的洲。或者他们当初为什么会被运送过来……”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Forty-five percent is around where I operate. Things are getting better though.
Zh: “我差不多就是45%。不过情况已经变得好多了。”

En: Maybe... She leans against the railing, looking up at the grey sky.
Zh: “也许……”她倚靠在栏杆上，抬头望着灰蒙蒙的天空。

En: Of course -- how fortuitous that I'm so close at hand. What do you need?
Zh: “当然——近在咫尺的我是多么幸运啊。你有什么需要吗？”

En: The children carry them up. Come May, the streets will be flooded... Outside the wind rattles the loose hatches. "*Flooded* with cheap weapons. In angry hands."
Zh: “孩子们会把它们拿上来。5月到来的时候，街道会被淹没……”外面的风把松动的舱门吹得咯吱作响。“被廉价的武器*淹没*。落在愤怒手里。”

En: The woman is tying a complicated nautical knot around the post while occasionally exchanging a few words with Lilienne, the Net Picker.
Zh: 女人在柱子上打一个复杂的海员结，时不时地跟莉莉恩，那个拾网人说几句话。

En: The woman is tying a complicated nautical knot around the post, while occasionally exchanging a few words with the other woman.
Zh: 女人在柱子上打一个复杂的海员结，时不时地跟另一个女人说几句话。

En: Dark eyes survey the coast leading up to Martinaise. Dull grey metal rests in her scabbard -- a sword. The wind is too loud for her to hear...
Zh: 黑色的眼睛眺望着通往马丁内斯的海岸。暗灰色的金属栖息在她的剑鞘里——那是一柄剑。风太大，她无法听见……

En: They're antiques, says Lieutenant John 'The Archetype' McCoy to his partner. "They're digging them up from the catacombs now, fixing them. Old caches from the Revolution. "
Zh: “这些是古董，”‘原型’约翰·麦考伊对他的搭档说道。“现在他们把东西从地下墓穴里挖出来了，修理好。大革命时代的旧藏品。”

En: Above you there forms a quilt of altocumulus clouds, twisting into each other. The wind tugs and stretches them over the bay.
Zh: 一层积雨云在你头顶聚集，互相缠绕着。轻风拖着它们，一路延伸到海湾对岸。

En: Oh, I'm not *frightened*, officer. I'd never... She leans against the railing, looking up at the grey sky.
Zh: “哦，我不*害怕*，警官。我永远也不会……”她倚靠在栏杆上，抬头仰望灰色的天空。

En: She could easily just... She leans against the railing, looking up at the grey sky.
Zh: “她很容易就能……”她倚靠在栏杆上，抬头仰望灰色的天空。

En: It's early spring and the rains are coming. An officer enters a low hut of stone and wood. Inside, weapons are piled against the walls. Rifles with splintering stocks... and swords. Tens if not hundreds.
Zh: 现在是早春，雨正淅淅沥沥地下着。一名警官走进由石头和木头搭建的低矮小屋里。武器堆放在屋里的墙面上。枪托断裂的步枪……还有剑。没有几百也有几十。

En: She's a woman. She doesn't know how to use a sword.
Zh: “她是个女人。她不知道怎么用剑。”

En: What is this? Destruction of property? Hooliganism? Elder abuse?
Zh: 这是干什么？破坏财物？流氓行为？虐待老人？

En: What is this? Destruction of property? Hooliganism? Elder abuse? And who is this *scamp*, anyway?
Zh: 这是干什么？破坏财物？流氓行为？虐待老人？还有，这个*流氓*到底是谁？

En: Thank you, ma'am. Do keep an eye on your boat.
Zh: “谢谢你，夫人。请留心你的船。”

En: Godspeed, detectives.
Zh: “祝你成功，警探。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Who is this *scamp*? Want me to look into it?
Zh: “这个*流氓*是谁？想让我调查一下吗？”

En: Sounds like Cindy the SKULL alright. Want me to book her?
Zh: “听起来像是骷髅头辛迪。要我给她警告处分吗？”

En: Leave it be.
Zh: 随它去吧。

En: No need -- Lilienne and I are getting on much better. For one, she appreciates the concept of *rent*.
Zh: “没必要——莉莉恩和我的关系好多了。举个例子来说，她很欣赏*租借*的概念。”

En: No, you haven't told me how they found it.
Zh: “不，你还没告诉我他们是怎么发现的。”

En: No, I don't know how they discovered it.
Zh: “不，我不知道他们是怎么发现的。”

En: Then there's the matter of that little scamp in old-lady clothes. She threatened to paint the Cor-de-Leite red. Like blood, you see. Well, I like it the way it is -- white.
Zh: “还有那个穿着老太太衣服的捣蛋鬼。她威胁说要把乳白涂成红色。像血一样，你也看见了。呃，我还是比较喜欢它现在样子——白色。”

En: No -- the Insulindian isola.
Zh: “不——是伊苏林迪洲。”

En: It's just as well -- Lilienne and I are getting on much better. For one, she appreciates the concept of *rent*.
Zh: “这样也好——莉莉恩和我的关系好多了。举个例子来说，她很欣赏*租借*的概念。”

En: That's just Cindy being Cindy. You can ignore her.
Zh: “那只是辛迪的本色而已。无视她就好。”

En: Have I told you how they discovered this place? The wind picks up, her raincoat flaps in the gust.
Zh: “我有没有告诉过你，他们是怎么发现这里的？”起风了，她的雨衣在风中翻飞。

En: Well, your condition has left you no worse off than most of these people -- the literacy rate is around 45% west of the river. Fifty years of occupation have left these people in an *oblivion* of poverty.\n
Zh: “呃，你的状况没有比大多数人更糟糕——河流以西的识字率大约在45%左右。50年的占领让这些人被*遗忘*在了贫穷里。”

En: You're not alone. Neither do most of these people -- the literacy rate is around 45% west of the river. Fifty years of occupation have left these people in an *oblivion* of poverty.
Zh: “不是只有你一个。这里大部分人也不知道——河流以西的识字率大约在45%左右。50年的占领让这些人被*遗忘*在了贫穷里。”

En: I'm surprised that woman hasn't put me to the sword yet. Maybe she will? You should ask your questions while you can.
Zh: “我很惊讶，那个女人还没有用剑杀了我。也许她会吧？趁着你还有机会，有问题还是快问吧。”

En: Do you know how they discovered this place? The wind picks up, her raincoat flaps in the gust.
Zh: “你知道他们是怎么发现这个地方的吗？”起风了，她的雨衣在风中翻飞。

En: Familiar faces.
Zh: “熟悉的脸庞。”

En: The board is not gonna like it.
Zh: “董事会是不会喜欢的。”

En: To think I even used to go dancing here.
Zh: “想起我甚至还在这里跳过舞呢。”

En: Silly nostalgia...
Zh: “愚蠢的乡愁……”

En: God, I was so young... and stupid.
Zh: “天啦，我是那么的年轻……那么的愚蠢。”

En: This place hasn't changed at all.
Zh: “这个地方一点也没变。”

En: Harry, we're here because we're worried.
Zh: “哈里，我们来这儿是因为我们担心你。”

En: Is little Lily your sister? (Point to the house.)
Zh: “小莉莉是你妹妹吗？”（指向房子。）

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: Yes?
Zh: “是吗？”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: That's it? You're being laconic about it?
Zh: “就这些？就这么简洁的吗？”

En: It doesn't look like he knows what that means.
Zh: 看起来他没明白是什么意思。

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: Then we're *definitely* doing it.
Zh: 那我们*绝对*要这么做。

En: Wow, so intellectually stimulating.
Zh: 哇哦，在智力上真是太刺激了。

En: The stone-kicking isn't even of very high calibre. Anyone can do that.
Zh: 踢石头根本不算什么高水准。所有人都做得到。

En: A suspicious request. Made out of fear?
Zh: 一个很可疑的请求。因为害怕吗？

En: A dull pain flashes through your mind.
Zh: 一阵隐痛闪过你的脑海。
