En: Lights? Fire guy? The lieutenant looks at you. "We should check up on that island and you..."
Zh: “灯光？火男？”警督看着你。“我们应该去那座岛上看看，还有你……”

En: He whispers: "You need to keep your *dark humour* in check in front of children, okay?"
Zh: 他小声说道：“在小孩子面前，控制一下你那套*黑色幽默*，好吗？”

En: I'm not telling you twice.
Zh: 我是不会再跟你说第二遍的。

En: The secrets of understanding the tongues of children have been lost to you a long time ago.
Zh: 理解小孩语言中的秘密这项技能，很久以前你就已经失去了。

En: Hey, where is your mom?
Zh: “喂，你们的妈妈在哪？”

En: The kids don't reply, absorbed in their little game.
Zh: 男孩没有回应，一心一意沉浸在自己的小游戏里。

En: Kids, have you seen any bad people around?
Zh: “孩子们，你们有没有在附近见到过坏人？”

En: What bad people? One of the twins looks up, his mouth slightly open.
Zh: 其中一个男孩抬起头，嘴巴微微张开。“什么坏人？”他问到。

En: I don't think questioning four-year-olds without their parents present is gonna crack the case, says the lieutenant.
Zh: “我不觉得在没有家长陪同的情况下询问一个4岁大的小孩会对破案有所帮助，”警督说道。

En: Bad people. Criminals. Dangerous women.
Zh: “坏人。犯人。危险的女人。”

En: No, we're not really supposed to talk to strangers, you know. The other twin shakes his head.
Zh: 另一个男孩摇摇头。“不，你知道的，我们真的不能跟陌生人说话。”

En: The scruffy-haired little boy kicks a stone. He can't be more than five years old.
Zh: 头发蓬乱的小男孩踢着一块石头。他应该没超过5岁。

En: The other one looks indistinguishable from him. He watches his brother kick the stone with his tongue lolling out of his mouth.
Zh: 另一个看起来跟他几乎一模一样。他看着自己的兄弟踢着石头，舌头从嘴巴里伸了出来。

En: You must be Lilienne's twins.
Zh: “你们肯定就是莉莉恩的双胞胎吧。”

En: You guys look identical.
Zh: “你们俩看起来真是一模一样。”

En: Children are stupid.
Zh: “小孩都很蠢。”

En: Bye kids, take care. [Leave.]
Zh: “再见了，孩子们，保重哦。”[离开。]

En: This one doesn't say anything, kicking the concrete with his worn-out sneaker.
Zh: 男孩什么也没说，只是用破烂的球鞋踢着混凝土块。

En: Lily's our mom, explains the other one, tongue still lolling out of his mouth.
Zh: “我们的妈妈是莉莉，”另一个男孩解释到，舌头还没收回去。

En: I already said that.
Zh: “那个我已经说过了。”

En: Bye. [Leave.]
Zh: “再见。”[离开。]

En: The stone-kicker laughs suddenly. His head is too large for his shoulders.
Zh: 踢石头的男孩突然笑了起来。对于他的肩膀来说，他的脑袋有些太大了。

En: The other one laughs as well.
Zh: 另一个也跟着笑了。

En: Maybe there's some kind of reason why they're laughing? An interesting reason somehow tied to the case!
Zh: 也许他们笑是有原因的？一个跟案子有关的有趣原因！

En: What are you laughing about?
Zh: “你们在笑什么呢？”

En: I don't care.
Zh: 我不在乎。

En: Ha-ha, the scruffy-haired little kid laughs, as though in agreement.
Zh: “哈哈，”头发蓬乱的小男孩笑了，似乎表示同意。

En: You're stupid, the other one tells him, suddenly very serious.
Zh: “你很蠢，”另外一个突然严肃地告诉他。

En: The stone-kicking one becomes frantic all of a sudden, as if that's something to be scared of -- the obvious fact that you just stated.
Zh: 踢石头的男孩突然慌了神，似乎有什么东西吓着他了——你刚才陈述的那个显而易见的事实。

En: He looks just like me, the other one says.
Zh: “他看起来跟我一样，”另一个男孩说道。

En: Yeah, I said that.
Zh: “是啊，我说过了。”

En: Bye. [Leave.]
Zh: “再见。”[离开。]

En: The boy doesn't answer. His brother throws another rock. Both of their hair is covered in some kind of dirt.
Zh: 男孩没有回答。他的兄弟又扔了一块石头。两人的头发上都覆盖着某种污垢。

En: You're bad with kids, the lieutenant remarks with evident glee.
Zh: “你很不擅长应付小孩子，”警督评论到，样子明摆着很高兴。

En: It's impossible to be *good* with kids, they're too stupid.
Zh: “*擅长*应付小孩是不可能的，他们太蠢了。”

En: I am, yes.
Zh: “是的，没错。”

En: And what are you, Kid Master General?
Zh: “那你呢，孩子王？”

En: Maybe I am? He grins. "Now, how about some actual police work? We're not getting anything here."
Zh: “也许我是呢？”他咧嘴一笑。“现在，不如做点实际的警察工作吧？我们从这里问不出什么的。”

En: Don't worry, everyone is. He looks at the little scruffian, then at you. "How about we do some police work now? We're not getting anything here."
Zh: “别担心，大家都一样。”他看了看小脏娃，然后看着你。“不如我们做点实际的警察工作吧？我们从这里问不出什么的。”

En: You're right there. He looks at the little scruffian, then at you. "Let's see if we can find some police work, shall we? We're not getting anything here."
Zh: “你说得对。”他看了看小脏娃，然后看着你。“让我们看看能不能找到实际的警察工作，怎么样？我们从这里问不出什么的。”

En: I'm just gonna leave now. I'm gonna leave and not talk to you anymore. [Leave.]
Zh: “我现在就走。我马上就走，再也不跟你们俩说话了。”[离开。]

En: Bye. [Leave.]
Zh: “再见。”[离开。]

En: Just a second ago, the first one was laughing. Now he's doing something with his lip and his gap tooth.
Zh: 就在一秒前，第一个孩子还在笑。现在他正在嘴巴和牙齿缝里捣鼓着什么。

En: There's no *system* to this.
Zh: 这里没有*方法*可言。

En: You're being *random*!
Zh: “你这也太*随便*了吧！”

En: Bye. [Leave.]
Zh: “再见。”[离开。]

En: You're stupid, the other one repeats, still deathly serious.
Zh: “你很蠢，”另外一个重复到，样子还是非常严肃。

En: The boy stops laughing and looks at his toes.
Zh: 男孩止住了笑声，看着自己的脚趾头发呆。

En: The boy keeps laughing. Then he bows down to inspect the rock as though there's something noteworthy about it.
Zh: 男孩还在笑个不停。然后他弯下腰去查看那块石头，仿佛有什么很值得注意的地方。

En: Okay kids. You've been to that island, right?
Zh: “好吧，孩子们。你们去过那个小岛，对吗？”

En: On that island? The one who's busy kicking his stone points to the bay.
Zh: “那个小岛？”忙着踢石头的男孩指着海湾说道。

En: Yes, that one. I need to know what's there.
Zh: “是的，就是那个。我需要知道那里有什么。”

En: No, some other island. *Of course* that one.
Zh: “不是，其他小岛。*当然*是那个啦。”

En: Someone lives on the island?
Zh: “有人住在岛上吗？”

En: No, the boy answers, shaking his head vehemently.
Zh: “没有，”男孩拼命摇头回答到。

En: Yes. His brother looks at him, then at you.
Zh: 他的兄弟看了看他，然后看着你。“是的，”他说道。

En: That's... uhm... nothing? The boy pauses to think with his finger in his mouth. "It's just a seafort and some plants. You can take a raft there. It's great."
Zh: “那个……呃……没什么？”男孩停下来思考着，手指头还放在嘴巴里。“只是一座海堡，还有些植物。你可以乘木筏过去。那里很棒的。”

En: And! And! the other one butts in. "We make a fire. We make a... we make a fire."
Zh: “还有！还有！”另一个男孩插了进来。“我们生火了。我们还……我们生火了。”

En: Mhm, his brother nods. "Gather the sticks for the fire -- and bullets. Or not real bullets, empty bullets."
Zh: “嗯，”他的兄弟点点头。“收集树枝生火——还有子弹。或者，不是真正的子弹，是空子弹。”

En: Why is he the *fire* guy?
Zh: “为什么他是*火*男呢？”

En: Because.... because... The boy pauses to think.
Zh: “因为……因为……”男孩停下来想了想。

En: Because he asks to put the fire out! the other explains.
Zh: “因为他要把火扑灭！”另一个男孩解释到。

En: Why does he ask you to put the fire out?
Zh: “他为什么让你把火扑灭呢？”

En: Uhm... I don't know. He doesn't like it. He doesn't like people to be there. You shouldn't go.
Zh: “呃……我不知道，他不喜欢……他不喜欢那里有人……你不应该过去。”

En: Yes, the other one adds laconically, standing with his hands glued to his sides like a little tin soldier.
Zh: “是的，”另一个男孩简短地补充了一句，双手贴在身体两侧，像个小锡兵一样站的笔直。

En: Okay. His brother answers for him.
Zh: “好吧。”他的兄弟替他做出了回答。

En: The scruffy-haired little boy kicks a stone while the other watches him do it.
Zh: 头发蓬乱的小男孩踢着一块石头，另一个在旁边看着他。

En: I need you to tell me what you know about it -- what's *on* the island?
Zh: “我需要你把知道的事情都告诉我——那个小岛*上面*有什么？”

En: You mentioned something about *lights*?
Zh: “你提到了*灯光*？”

En: I... starts one of them -- it's hard to tell which one now. "I don't know."
Zh: “我……”是其中一个先开始的——现在很难分清到底是哪一个——开了口。“我不知道。”

En: Did you mean there are *electrical* lights? He points to the streetlight.
Zh: “你的意思是那里有*电*灯吗？”他指向街灯。

En: Uhm... yes. The boy looks at his toes.
Zh: “呃……是的。”男孩看着自己的脚趾头。

En: What then?
Zh: “然后呢？”

En: Mhm. (Nod in agreement.)
Zh: “嗯。”（点头赞同。）

En: There are lights. The fire guy comes and asks us to put the fire out.
Zh: “那里有灯光。火男来了，告诉我们把火熄灭。”

En: Your nerve endings *sting* from the mention of a *guy*.
Zh: 听他提起一个*男人*，你的神经末梢就开始*刺痛*。

En: They must mean a human being. On that island? But it's cut off...
Zh: 他们肯定说的是一个人类。在那座小岛上吗？但那里早就已经废弃了……

En: Let's go with *yes*. The lieutenant raises his eyebrows and whips out his notebook.
Zh: 警督扬起眉头，掏出笔记本。“那就当答案是*是的*。”

En: Because he doesn't want to be found. Smoke traces...
Zh: 因为他不想被发现。烟雾的痕迹……

En: Mhm. Lily's our mom, says one of the twins.
Zh: “嗯。我们的妈妈是莉莉，”其中一个双胞胎说道。

En: One of the boys nods, the other one looks at his brother and shrugs.
Zh: 其中一个男孩点点头，另外一个看着自己的兄弟，耸了耸肩。

En: Okay, how about the policeman asks you something else now?
Zh: “好吧，那现在警察叔叔问你点别的事？”

En: Okay. The scruffy-haired boy squints up at you.
Zh: “好的。”头发蓬乱的小男孩眯起眼睛看着你。

En: Your father used to go to that island too, didn't he?
Zh: “你爸爸以前去过那座小岛，对吧？”

En: Our father killed himself.
Zh: “我们的爸爸自杀了。”

En: Don't say that. He didn't. His brother punches him. The boy's eyes well up like he's about to start crying.
Zh: “别这么说。他没有。”他的兄弟给了他一拳。男孩的眼睛有些湿润，好像要哭了。

En: Shut up. The lieutenant jabs you in the ribs with his elbow, then turns to the boy...
Zh: “闭嘴。”警督用胳膊肘撞了一下你的肋骨，然后转向男孩……

En: It's okay to kill yourself, you know. I'm gonna blow my brains out. Gonna close this one final case and then *blam* -- I'm outta here.
Zh: “你知道吗，自杀其实没什么问题。我就打算把自己的脑袋打爆。等结束最后这个案子之后，然后就*嘣*——我就滚蛋了。”

En: Your father did not kill himself.
Zh: “你爸爸没有自杀。”

En: He killed himself all right.
Zh: “他是自杀了。”

En: Is that all you know? Is there anything more you can tell me about the island? (Conclude.)
Zh: “你只知道这么多吗？关于那座小岛，你还能告诉我些别的事吗？”（总结。）

En: There's a... the boy says, rubbing his eyes. It's clear that he has no intention of finishing the sentence.
Zh: “有一个……”男孩揉揉眼睛说道。很明显，他没打算把话说完。

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: I'm sorry. The one says to the other, rubbing his brother's shoulder affectionately.
Zh: “很抱歉。”一个男孩对另一个说道，然后亲切地摸了摸兄弟的肩膀。

En: I don't know... The boy who made the claim finds himself unsure of it. He looks around.
Zh: “我不知道……”说话的男孩发现自己也不太确定。他看了看四周。

En: Don't punch your brother.... please. Don't start crying. We still need to ask more questions.
Zh: “别打你的兄弟……拜托。别哭。我们还有问题要问呢。”

En: I'm not gonna start *crying*, the one who almost started crying snaps, regaining control of his quivering chin.
Zh: “我不会*哭*的，”眼泪都快掉下来的男孩厉声说道，重新控制住了自己颤抖的下巴。

En: The boy's eyes well up as he looks at you. They're dark and round. The wind tussles his hair.
Zh: 男孩看着你，两只眼睛有些湿润，又黑又圆。风吹乱了他的头发。

En: Please don't... The boy's eyes well up as he looks at you. They're dark and round. The wind tussles his hair.
Zh: “请不要……”男孩看着你，两只眼睛有些湿润，又黑又圆。风吹乱了他的头发。

En: Don't start *crying*, his brother says, then, in a whisper, adds: "I'm sorry."
Zh: “别*哭*，”他的兄弟说道，接着，他小声补充了一句：“对不起。”

En: The other boy straightens his back. "I'm not gonna start *crying*, stupid," he snaps, regaining control of his quivering chin.
Zh: 另一个男孩挺直了背后。“我不会*哭*的，蠢货，”他重新控制住自己颤抖的下巴，厉声说道。

En: Is there anything else you can tell me about this *guy*? Age? Does he live there?
Zh: “关于那个*家伙*，你还有什么能告诉我的吗？比如年龄？他是住在那里吗？”

En: The other watches him, brows knitted. "It doesn't even have anything to do with this, you. Father isn't the fire guy."
Zh: 另一个男孩看着他，眉毛拧成了一个结。“这跟你一点关系都没有。爸爸不是火男。”

En: The two things are *unconnected*. Your question didn't make sense.
Zh: 这两件事*毫无关联*。你的问题没有任何意义。

En: No, he doesn't live there. I don't think.
Zh: “不，他不在那里住。我不觉得。”

En: No, he lives there. The other nods. "He's been there twice, two times."
Zh: “不，他住在那里。”另一个男孩点点头。“他去过两次，两次。”

En: Huh... The first one pauses to think, then comes to some kind of conclusion. "He doesn't live there. He isn't there sometimes."
Zh: “呃……”第一个男孩停下来想了想，然后得出了某种结论。“他不住在那里。他有时候不在。”

En: Anything else? What does this guy look like?
Zh: “还能告诉我点别的吗？这个家伙长什么样？”

En: Anything else? What does this guy look like?
Zh: “还能告诉我点别的吗？这个家伙长什么样？”

En: I don't know, they say almost in unison.
Zh: “我不知道，”他们几乎是异口同声。

En: How come?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: We... we ran. He just yelled we shouldn't be there.
Zh: “我们……我们跑了。他只是大喊着我们不应该去那里。”

En: Maybe *you're* stupid!
Zh: “也许*你*是很蠢！”

En: Don't say that, he's a *po-leees oooffi-ser*, his brother whispers.
Zh: “别这么说，他是一个*警警警警察察*，”他的兄弟低语到。

En: Lights? Fire guy? The lieutenant looks at you. "We should check up on that island."
Zh: “灯光？火男？”警督看着你。“我们应该去那座岛上看看。”

En: Hey, boys. Your sister told me you got in trouble for saying bad words.
Zh: “嘿，孩子们。你妹妹告诉我，你们因为说脏话所以惹上麻烦了。”

En: Kids, has anything *strange* happened lately?
Zh: “孩子们，最近发生过什么*奇怪的*事情吗？”

En: Yeah. The boy turns a rock over in his hand. "We heard *bad* words and said them, and got in trouble..."
Zh: 男孩把手中的石头翻了个转。“有啊。我们听到*脏*话还跟着说出来，结果就惹了麻烦……”

En: Uhm... The boy turns a rock over in his hand. "We heard *bad* words and said them, and got in trouble..."
Zh: “嗯……”男孩把手中的石头翻了个转。“我们听到*脏*话还跟着说出来，结果就惹了麻烦……”

En: Say *fuck.* It'd be HILARIOUS.
Zh: 说*操*。肯定会很搞笑的。

En: It would be hilarious, my man! Live it up! Say *FUCK!*
Zh: 肯定会很搞笑的，兄弟！放飞自我吧！说*操*！

En: Fuck.
This is a shame and a disappointment.
Zh: 多么令人羞愧又失望啊。

En: The boy blinks. "Fffff...." Despite his efforts, the unfamiliar word catches in his mouth.
Zh: 男孩眨了眨眼。“次奥……”他努力想挤出这个字，可是却说不出口。

En: Nothing. What happened? Where'd you hear bad words? Was it from someone on the coast? The radio?
Zh: “没关系。怎么了？你们是在哪儿听到脏话的？是哪个海岸上的人说的吗？还是收音机里放的？”

En: It's a bad word. The worst word.
Zh: “是脏话，最糟糕的话。”

En: Oh... The boy looks uncomfortable, at the jetty toward his mother.
Zh: “哦……”男孩看上去有些不安，顺着防波堤的方向看着他的母亲。

En: The lieutenant is deeply unimpressed.
Zh: 警督毫无反应。

En: Why does he have to be such a binoclard? It's just a funny word!
Zh: 他干嘛要这么傻乎乎的呢？这个词多有意思啊！

En: Preach it, brother! Spread the good word *fuck* all over town!
Zh: 好好宣传一下吧，兄弟！把*操*这个字眼传遍全城！

En: The boys seem less happy now.
Zh: 男孩们现在看上去不太高兴了。

En: We didn't hear that word. We heard other bad words...
Zh: “我们听到的不是这个字。我们听到的是别的脏话……”

En: Bad words are bad. Don't say them, kids. It's a first step to a life of crime.
Zh: “脏话不好，不要说脏话，孩子们。说脏话就是走向犯罪的第一步。”

En: We won't, the little boy says. He adjusts his cap in the cool air.
Zh: “我们不会的，”小一点的男孩一边说着，一边在海风中调整着帽檐。

En: We learned, the other replies.
Zh: “我们学会了，”另一个男孩回答道。

En: Good on you. I know you were tempted to shout *fuck*, but you controlled your impulse. Well done.
Zh: 你真棒。我知道你想大喊一声*操*，可是你忍住了。非常好。

En: Let's move on. Where did you hear the bad words -- was it from someone on the coast? The radio?
Zh: “我们继续吧。你们是在哪儿听到脏话的？是哪个海岸上的人说的吗？还是收音机里放的？”

En: Bad words? What bourgeois mentality, to deem certain *working class words* bad.
Zh: “脏话？这是什么资产阶级的心态，非要觉得*工人阶级的话*是脏话？”

En: Bou... what? The boy gently tosses his rock at the rock tower, and misses. "Was that a bad word?"
Zh: “资产……什么？”男孩轻轻地向石塔丢了一块石头，可是却没砸中。“那是脏话吗？”

En: Bourgeois? Yes. One of the worst, child.
Zh: “资产阶级吗？对，这算是最恶劣的脏话了吧，孩子。”

En: Nah. I just thought it was funny to say.
Zh: “不是，我只是觉得这样说很好玩。”

En: Don't say more bad words! The boy frowns.
Zh: 男孩皱了皱眉。“别再说脏话了！”

En: don't know why it's funny..." The boy frowns.
Zh: 男孩皱了皱眉。“我不知道这好玩在哪儿……”

En: Okay. What happened?
Zh: “好吧，发生什么事了？”

En: We just heard funny voices. The boy shrugs. His jacket whispers as his shoulders move.
Zh: 男孩耸耸肩。他的外套和肩膀摩擦，发出沙沙声。“我们只是听到了一些好玩的声音。”

En: We thought the voices were funny. The brother nods. "We said what they said and then we got in trouble..."
Zh: 双胞胎哥哥点点头。“我们觉得那声音很好玩。我们就跟着声音学了起来，结果遇上了麻烦……”

En: Where did you hear them? Did you overhear someone say them? Was it on the radio?
Zh: “你们是在哪儿听到声音的？是有人在说话吗？还是从收音机里听到的？”

En: Radio? What's that? The boy sniffs up a drip of snot. "We just heard them. In our head."
Zh: “收音机？那是什么东西？”男孩吸了吸鼻涕。“我们直接听到的。在我们脑子里。”

En: Could it be? Do their minds work in the same way?
Zh: 这可能吗？难道他们的大脑运作都一样？

En: Voices in your head? Like little bleeps and bloops of information?
Zh: “你们脑子里的声音？像是那种讯息传输的杂音吗？”

En: What do you mean you 'heard them in your head'?
Zh: “你们说‘在你们的脑子里听见’是什么意思？”

En: No. The boy shakes his head. "Just people talking real fast."
Zh: 男孩摇了摇头。“不是，就只是有人说话说得很快。”

En: You know. He points at his head. His fingers are red from the cold. "I heard people talking. Except just in my head!"
Zh: “你知道吗，”他用冻得通红的手指指着自己的脑袋。“我在脑子里听见有人说话！”

En: Okay. What did you hear?
Zh: “好吧，你听见什么了？”

En: Uh... He scratches his head. He looks at his twin, who nods -- and then he begins. "It was like... 'It is time at last, my nasty speedfreaks! Our brotherman is about to grow up!'"
Zh: “啊……”他挠了挠脑袋，看向双胞胎哥哥，哥哥点了点头——他便说道，“比如……‘时候到了，下流的狂飙怪人们！我们的兄弟要长大了！’”

En: A memory stirs. You've heard this quickfire patter before.
Zh: 你突然想起自己以前好像听过这种机关枪似的快节奏飙话。

En: 'DJ Flacio's big boy debut single is here! Buckle up, because this is the fastest trash that you're ever gonna hear.'
Zh: “‘DJ弗莱科的首张单曲来啦！系好安全带，你们闻所未闻的超高速垃圾要来了。’”

En: 'Turn up the volume, blow those speakers out. Now here's---'
Zh: “‘调大音量，让喇叭炸裂吧。接下来——’”

En: No! The brother cuts in, suddenly and sharply. "Don't say bad words, stupid!"
Zh: “不要！”双胞胎哥哥突然惊声打断弟弟的话。“别说脏话，蠢货！”

En: Elsewhere in Revachol, the Lieutenant switches on the radio. His head rocks subtly to a violent track. He needs to hear this story.
Zh: 在瑞瓦肖的某处，警督打开了收音机。他的脑袋伴随着激烈的旋律摇摆着。他可得听听这个故事。

En: A word when you're done. The lieutenant clears his throat. His eyes are wider than usual.
Zh: 警督的眼睛比平时瞪得还大。他清了清嗓子，然后说，“完事以后有话和你说。”

En: Okay kids, tell Kim here what happened with the voices.
Zh: “好了孩子们，告诉金那些声音是怎么回事吧。”

En: The one we heard say a bad word? The boy shrugs. "Okay. The voices were like..." He scratches his head -- then seems to remember.
Zh: “就是我们听到说脏话的那个吗？”男孩耸耸肩。“好吧，那声音就像……”他挠了挠脑袋——然后似乎记了起来。

En: 'It is time at last, my nasty speedfreaks! Our brotherman is about to grow up!'
Zh: “‘时候到了，下流的狂飙怪人们！我们的兄弟要长大了！’”

En: 'DJ Flacio's big boy debut single is here! Buckle up, because this is the fastest trash that you're ever gonna hear.'
Zh: “‘DJ麦斯的首张单曲来啦！系好安全带，你们闻所未闻的超高速垃圾要来了。’”

En: 'Turn up the volume, blow those speakers out. Now here's---'
Zh: “‘把音量调到最大，让喇叭炸裂。接下来——’”

En: I understand. The lieutenant's eyes are wide in surprise, despite his calm voice. "Thank you. Let's have a word when you're finished, detective."
Zh: “我明白了。”虽然警督的语气很冷静，但是他的眼睛因为吃惊而瞪得很大。“谢谢你，你搞定了之后我有话和你说，警探。”

En: Real quick, boys -- where were you when you heard the voices?
Zh: “真是挺快的啊，孩子们——你们是在哪儿听到这些声音的？”

En: There. The boy points toward one of the ruined monoliths nearby.
Zh: 男孩指向旁边的一座破损的建筑。“就在那儿。”

En: We were there. The other child nods. The Feld building looms overhead.
Zh: 菲尔德大厦在高空中若隐若现。另一个孩子点点头。“我们当时就在那儿。”

En: And so I return to thee, Feld.
Zh: “我又回来了，菲尔德。”

En: Thanks, kids.
Zh: “谢了，孩子们。”

En: Uh-huh! one says, throwing a rock into the sand.
Zh: “啊哈！”一个孩子一边叫着，一边将石头扔向沙地。

En: Points back to Feld. The lieutenant frowns slightly at the building, towering ominously. "Be sure you're ready when we go in, detective."
Zh: 警督皱了皱眉，看着很不吉利地杵在那里的建筑。“线索又指回了菲尔德啊。等你准备好了我们再进去吧，警探。”

En: Full circle. The lieutenant smiles to himself. "Back to Feld, then. When you're ready."
Zh: 警督笑着说道，“绕了一圈又回到菲尔德了啊。等你准备好就过去吧。”

En: Hah! I knew it!
Zh: “哈！我就知道！”

En: The boy draws a jagged rock across the ground. It leaves a white scratch.
Zh: 男孩捡起一块尖锐的石头，在地上划出一道白色痕迹。

En: The boy picks up a rock. Then another. He looks at you and blinks.
Zh: 一个男孩捡起一块石头。而另一个男孩则对着你眨眼睛。

En: The boy turns the rock over in his hands. He looks to you, then his brother, then back to you.
Zh: 男孩把手中的石头翻了过来。他看了看你，看了看自己的哥哥，然后又看了看你。

En: Why, what a splendid rendition these two rapsssssssssscallions have offered us here today! Faultless memorization! Perchance they'll enjoy greatness as a double-act in the Performing Artsss.
Zh: 今天这两个小流氓给我们献上了多——么精彩绝伦的表演啊！这一定会成为最美好的记忆！也许他们的双簧能在表演艺术上获得重大成就。

En: Voices from another self? Voices from *a deeper self*?  Voices from... *early schizophrenia*?
Zh: 来自另一个自己的声音？来自*内心深处自己*的声音？来自……*早期精神分裂症*的声音？

En: Any family history of schizophrenia?
Zh: “你们家族有精神分裂史吗？”

En: Is that a bad word? The boy blinks at you.
Zh: 男孩对你眨了眨眼。“那是脏话吗？”

En: No. Just tell me what you heard.
Zh: “不是，和我说说你听到了什么吧。”

En: Sure. But what words did you hear?
Zh: “是啊。但是你听到了什么呢？”

En: 'Fuck' is the bad word, remember? But tell me what you heard.
Zh: “操才是脏话，记住了吗？和我说说你听到了什么吧。”

En: Conduct yourself with some dignity. You don't need to swear in front of the children.
Zh: 你的举止应该表现得有威严。没必要当着孩子爆粗口。

En: Wait! Stop everything. Back this up. What are *bad words*?
Zh: 等等！先等等。我支持这个说法。可什么是*脏话*？

En: Bad words, the other says, nodding absent-mindedly. "Bad, bad words..."
Zh: “脏话，”另一个孩子心不在焉地点着头说道。“很脏很脏的话……”

En: It's a mystery. You'll have to find out yourselves.
Zh: “这是个谜，你们得自己找出谜底。”

En: Oh... The boy looks uncomfortable, at the jetty toward his mother.
Zh: “哦……”男孩看上去有些不安，顺着防波堤的方向看着他的母亲。

En: We didn't hear that word. We heard other bad words...
Zh: “我们听到的不是这个字。我们听到的是别的脏话……”

En: Ff... The other boy tries. He looks at his brother for understanding, then at you. "What does that mean?"
Zh: “次奥……”另一个男孩试着说道。他不解地看向他的哥哥，然后又望向你。“这个字是什么意思？”

En: Pipe down Puzzle Face, this is a Code Red situation! If we review all the *bad words*, that's only going to add more temptation to saying them -- in front of the *children*. And we *do not* want that.
Zh: 安静些，拼图脸，现在是紧急情况！如果我们要温习一下所有的*脏话*，那只会更想在*孩子*面前爆粗口。我们可*不想*那样。

En: COME ON BOYS, YOU CAN DO IT!
Zh: 来吧孩子们，你们能做到的！

En: Why does he have to be such a binoclard? It's just a funny word!
Zh: 他为什么非得这么假正经？就是个好玩的词罢了！

En: Preach it, brother! Spread the good word *fuck* all over town!
Zh: 传播它吧，兄弟！把*他妈的*这么优秀的词传遍整个小镇！

En: I heard you kids sometimes play in the abandoned Feld building?
Zh: “我听说你们有时候会去那栋废弃的菲尔德大厦玩？”

En: I dunno what you're talking about. The boy stuffs his hands deep into his pockets and kicks another rock.
Zh: “我不知道你在说什么。”男孩把手深深地插进口袋里，又踢走了一块石头。

En: What's a Feld building? one of the twins asks with a shrug.
Zh: “菲尔德大厦是什么？”其中一个男孩耸耸肩，问到。

En: He dunno what you're talking about. The other nods.
Zh: 另一个点点头。“他不知道你在说什么。”

En: Nah, we don't go anywhere, nah, the other mumbles.
Zh: “不，我们哪里也没去，”另一个嘟囔着。

En: Do *you* know what I'm talking about?
Zh: “*你*知道我在说什么吗？”

En: Bye. [Leave.]
Zh: “再见。”[离开。]

En: He dunno what you're talking about, the first kid says loudly. Or you think it's the first kid -- it could be the second.
Zh: “他不知道你在说什么，”第一个男孩大声说道。或者你以为是第一个孩子——也可能是第二个。

En: I dunno what you're talking about, the other kid nods along.
Zh: “我不知道你在说什么，”另一个男孩也跟着点点头。

En: You're not helping me..
Zh: “你没有在帮我……”

En: Bye. [Leave.]
Zh: “再见。”[离开。]

En: Ha ha, I dunno what you're talking about. One of the boys shrugs his shoulders and starts laughing.
Zh: “哈哈，我不知道你在说什么。”其中一个男孩耸耸肩，笑了起来。

En: You will never get an answer. That's how it is with these little idiots.
Zh: 你永远也得不到一个答案。这些小白痴就这样了。

En: Bullet shells? There are a lot of them left over from the war -- but this could be important.
Zh: 弹壳？战争期间留下了很多——不过这个也许很重要。

En: Wait, you mean *shells*?
Zh: “等等，你的意思是*弹壳*吗？”

En: I don't know what those are...
Zh: “我不知道那些是什么……”

En: It's a purposefully shrill laugh. Like he's practised it for the express purpose of annoying people.
Zh: 这个尖锐的笑声是故意的。就好像他已经练习过很多次，明显为了惹怒别人才这么做的。

En: Are there actually two of them? Are you sure you're not just hallucinating or seeing double?
Zh: 真的有两个小男孩吗？你确定不是产生了幻觉？看成了两个人？

En: The rock-kicker was just being *shy*, but now he's enthusiastic again.
Zh: 踢石头的男孩有些*害羞*，不过现在又热情了起来。

En: It looks like he's *sad*. Because you're going or... who knows, really?
Zh: 看起来他很*伤心*。因为你要走了，或者……谁又能真的知道呢？

En: Oh, okay. So now he's *shy*. Now he's not talking, just wobbling around like he's afraid of something.
Zh: 哦，好吧。所以现在他*害羞*了。他不再说话，只是晃来晃去的，似乎在害怕什么。

En: Rock. Rock!
Zh: “石头。石头！”

En: Yes. Rocks!
Zh: “是的。石头！”

En: Good rocks.
Zh: “好石头。”

En: Rock. No!!
Up, rock!
Zh: “上，石头！”

En: Down, rock!
Zh: “下，石头！”

En: Up, up, and away!
Zh: “上，上，离开！”

En: Ha-ha!
Zh: “哈哈！”

En: Congratulations -- that's the gauntlets down, then. We're doing good on the armour collection front.
Zh: “恭喜——手套找到了。我们在盔甲收集这方面做得很好。”

En: Weather has not been kind to Lily's little sandcastle. The once mighty towers are quickly eroding away. You can see something shining back to you from what must have been a vast underground catacomb network.
Zh: 天气对小莉莉的沙堡很不友善。曾经辉煌的塔楼很快就被侵蚀掉了。你可以从一个巨大的地下墓穴网络中看到一个闪闪发亮的东西。

En: Broken...
Reach in the catacombs and pull out the shiny object.
Zh: 把手伸进地下墓穴，拿出那个闪亮的物体。

En: Leave the sandcastle alone. [Leave.]
Zh: 别碰那个沙堡。[离开。]

En: The little castle? The lieutenant smiles a little. "The reigning lord must have come upon some really tough times to let it slip in such decrepitude."
Zh: “小城堡？”警督微微笑了笑。“在位的君主肯定经历了很艰难的时刻，才会让它衰败到这种地步。”

En: A gush of wind disintegrates another piece of the cellar ceiling, exposing more of the mysterious glimmer underneath. The shithole's about to collapse.
Zh: 一阵风吹落了另一块天花板，暴露出下面更为神秘的微光。这个深坑要坍塌了。

En: The building's coming down on you, you need to get out of here!
Zh: 建筑要塌下来了，你得赶紧出去！

En: Smooth reflective surface... are these the gloves you're looking for?
Zh: 光滑的反射面……这就是你要找的手套吗？

En: The walls and floors give way to the giant's greed, collapse and present you with a pair of ceramic gauntlets.
Zh: 墙壁和地板为贪婪的巨人让了路，城堡分崩离析，一双瓷手套出现在你面前。

En: The fortress is still defiantly standing. There's an inviting gleam of *something* coming from the depths of the deepest cellars.
Zh: 这座堡垒依然屹立着，有些挑衅的味道。在最深处的地窖里，有什么*东西*正散发着诱人的光芒。

En: What's this? (Show her the stuffed bird you took from the ceiling.)
Zh: “这是什么？”（把你从天花板上取下来的鸟类标本拿给她看。）

En: Wasn't Garte, the cafeteria manager, trying to repair a piece of taxidermy?
Zh: 那个餐厅经理，加尔特，不是想替换一个动物标本吗？

En: All right. You just need to grab it from the ceiling and go!
Zh: 好吧。你只需要把它从天花板上拿下来，然后就可以啦！

En: Lamby looks soft.
Zh: “羊羊看起来很柔软。”

En: Yes. Very soft. Suddenly she pushes the stuffed animal toward your face.
Zh: “是的。非常软。”突然间，她把毛绒玩具推到你面前。

En: Press your cheek against Lamby.
Zh: 把脸靠在羊羊身上。

En: I don't deserve it. I'm scum.
Zh: “我不配。我是个人渣。”

En: Press your damn cheek against Lamby, okay? The lieutenant sounds authoritative -- and surprisingly gruff.
Zh: “把你那张该死的脸靠在羊羊脸上，好吗？”警督听起来像是在发号施令——而且出乎意料地粗暴。

En: Okay, then. I'm sorry. She presses her own cheek against Lamby and smiles, sadly.
Zh: “那好吧。对不起。”她把自己的脸贴在羊羊身上，难过的笑了。

En: Isn't he soft? She's right, Lamby is very soft. She rubs the white fur against your cheek, then returns the lamb into her lap, cuddling it.
Zh: “它是不是很软？”她说得对，羊羊非常柔软。她用白色皮毛摩擦着你的脸颊，然后把小羊放回自己腿上，紧紧抱住了它。

En: Rebel?
Zh: “叛徒？”

En: Wait, you mean spooky, right?
Zh: “等等，你的意思是可怕，对吧？”

En: Mhmh, she replies, nodding. "It's very, very booky."
Zh: “嗯，”她点头回复到。“它非常非常鬼鬼。”

En: I heard there was a girl here who has armoured gloves. Is that you?
Zh: “我听说这里有个戴着盔甲手套的小女孩。是你吗？”

En: Ooooh. She looks alarmed. "I had gloves, very big ones! Heavy too."
Zh: “哦哦哦！”她看起来有些警觉。“我有手套，很大的手套！还很重。”

En: Where did you get these gloves?
Zh: “你是从哪里弄到这些手套的？”

En: Where did you get them?
Zh: “你是从哪里弄到的？”

En: Found them when Lamby and I were playing hide-and-seek. In an empty house where no one lives! I think someone hid them there...
Zh: “我和羊羊玩捉迷藏的时候发现的。在一个没人住的空房子里！我想是有人藏起来的……”

En: And where are the gloves now?
Zh: “那现在手套在哪里呢？”

En: She pouts. "I hid them. The twins were going to take them. They're stupid..." She lifts her stuffed toy up and looks into its one remaining eye, as though searching for confirmation.
Zh: 她撅起小嘴。“我藏起来了。双胞胎会把东西拿走的。他们很蠢……”她举起毛绒玩具，看着剩下的那只眼睛，仿佛在寻求它的肯定。

En: We're going to need those gloves. It's for important police business. He enunciates the last two words carefully.
Zh: “我们需要那双手套。这是很重要的警方事务。”最后两个词他的发音格外小心。

En: Why? She looks at you suspiciously.
Zh: “为什么？”她怀疑地看着你。

En: I need them for a very important police investigation.
Zh: “我需要它们来做一项非常重要的警方调查。”

En: It's grown-up business. You wouldn't understand. Just tell me where they are.
Zh: “这是大人的事。你不会明白的。只要告诉我东西在哪就好。”

En: Oooh... She doesn't seem to understand, but the lieutenant's tone has conveyed to her the 'important' part.
Zh: “哦哦哦……”她似乎并没有理解，不过警督的口气把*重要*的那部分传达给她了。

En: She frowns, delaying divulging her secret. "Oo-kay," she says finally with some reluctance. "I hid them inside my sandcastle. Behind the house." She points somewhere outside. "You can take them."
Zh: 她皱紧眉头，迟迟不肯透露自己的秘密。“好，好吧，”最后，她不情愿地开了口。“我把它们藏在我的沙堡里了。在房子后面。”她指向外面的某个地方。“你可以拿走。”

En: Oooh... She doesn't seem to understand, but you have conveyed the 'important' part, at least.
Zh: “哦哦哦……”她似乎并没有理解，不过至少，你把*重要*的那部分传达给她了。

En: She doesn't want you to think she stole them.
Zh: 她不想你以为这是她偷来的。

En: I need those gloves.
Zh: “我需要那些手套。”

En: They're in my sandcastle. She points somewhere outside. "Behind our house. Under the sand. You can break the castle, it's not very good."
Zh: “它们在我的沙堡里。”她指向外面的某个地方。“在我们的房子后面。沙子底下。你可以把沙堡弄坏，它不是很好。”

En: Sure! I mean you already took it. I don't like it anyway... it looks angry.
Zh: “当然啦！我的意思是你都已经拿到了。反正我也不喜欢它……它看起来很生气。”

En: Sure! I mean you already took it. I don't like it anyway... it looks angry.
Zh: “当然啦！我的意思是你都已经拿到了。反正我也不喜欢它……它看起来很生气。”

En: Where are your parents?
Zh: “你的爸爸妈妈在哪？”

En: My mom's outside, and I don't really know about my dad. She gives you a bright smile like it's a good thing.
Zh: “妈妈在外面，而且我真的不知道爸爸的事。”她朝你露出一个明朗的笑容，仿佛这是一件好事。

En: Do you know anyone named Ruby?
Zh: “你认识一个叫露比的人吗？“

En: Ll... Luby... Rr... R-luuby. Suddenly the girl gets all serious and leans in, as if she's about to tell you a secret.
Zh: “拉……拉比……露……露比。”女孩突然变得严肃起来，她身体前倾，似乎打算告诉你一个秘密。

En: My mom tells me that I'm a big girl, but she doesn't know that I can't say *ll*. Or like, sometimes I can, but then... *ll*... *rr*... *llrr*...
Zh: “妈妈说我是个大姑娘了，但是她不知道我发不好*拉*的音。或者是，有时候我可以，但是又……*拉*……*露*……*拉露*……”

En: Kids. The lieutenant shakes his head.
Zh: “小朋友啊。”警督摇摇头。

En: Uhhhhh... She sits her doll on her lap. Its ragged limbs reach out toward you, as if longing for a cuddle. "Like what?"
Zh: “啊……”她把布娃娃放在腿上。娃娃的破破烂烂的四肢伸向你，仿佛在寻求拥抱。“比如？”

En: Anything. Maybe something you saw or heard that was weird?
Zh: “什么事都行。你见过或者听到过什么奇怪的东西吗？”

En: Anything that will help me dismantle Capitalism, so that you can inherit Communism, O' Girl Child Revolution?
Zh: “你知道有什么可以帮助我废除资本主义的吗？这样你就能给康米主义接班，发起纯真革命了。”

En: Cap-capi-capeel... She struggles to pronounce the words. She shakes her head. "No."
Zh: “哎哟哟……”她费力地嘟囔着。然后摇了摇头。“没有。”

En: The lieutenant sighs, and kneels to be eye-level with the girl. "Maybe someone you know has acted a little different, lately?"
Zh: 警督叹着气蹲下身子，和女孩的视线齐平。“那你留意到最近有谁的行为不太正常吗？”

En: Poor Girl Child Revolution. What have the bourgeois done to you?
Zh: “可怜的纯真革命。那些资产阶级都对你做了些什么？”

En: Never mind. Have you just seen or heard anything weird lately?
Zh: “没关系。那你最近有见过或者听到过什么奇怪的东西吗？”

En: She turns the stuffed lamb to face her. "What does that mean, Lamby?" The doll offers no reply. Stuffing hangs out its rear.
Zh: 她把毛绒玩具转过来对着自己。“他说的是什么意思呢，羊羊？”玩具并没有回答她。填塞物从玩具的背后露出来。

En: Yes! She lights with recognition. "The twins got in trouble for saying *bad words*."
Zh: 她突然想起了什么。“对了！那对双胞胎因为说了*脏话*所以惹了麻烦。”

En: Anyway... I'll talk to your brothers about bad words. Thanks for the tip! (Move on.)
Zh: “好吧……我会去和你的哥哥们谈谈脏话的事情。感谢提醒！”（继续。）

En: I see. Thank you for the tip; I'll talk to them about it. (Move on.)
Zh: “我知道了。谢谢你的消息。我会跟他们谈谈的。”（继续。）

En: You're welcome!
Zh: “不客气！”

En: Bad words are for losers. Good on you for not saying them.
Zh: “只有失败者才说脏话。你不说脏话就是个好孩子。”

En: Yep! The girl nods firmly. "I don't say bad words. Lamby doesn't either."
Zh: “嗯！”小女孩坚定地点点头。“我不说脏话，羊羊也不说脏话。”

En: What's wrong with bad words? I love bad words.
Zh: “说脏话怎么了？我喜欢脏话。”

En: Is that it?
Zh: “就这样吗？”

En: Yes. She yawns, and leans back into the cushion.
Zh: “是呀。”她打了个哈欠，躺回靠垫上。

En: She gasps. "No! Don't say bad words! Promise?"
Zh: 她气喘吁吁。“不！别说脏话！答应我好吗？”

En: FUCK OFF.
Zh: 滚蛋。

En: Lamby remains silent. The fireplace flickers and cackles. She hugs the doll close to her chest and laughs.
Zh: 羊羊一言不发。壁炉闪烁着火光，发出咯咯的声响。她把娃娃抱在胸前笑了起来。

En: I'll keep an eye on him. The lieutenant cuts in faster than you can reply. "Don't worry."
Zh: “我会看紧他的。”你还没来得及回答，警督就飞快地插话。“别担心。”

En: He's not going to let you make a fool of yourself or the RCM in front of this girl. Not while he's here.
Zh: 只要他在这儿，就不会让你或是RCM在这个小女孩面前出洋相的。

En: But bad words can be good! It can be healthy to express yourself with them.
Zh: “可是脏话也有好的一面！用脏话表达情绪有益身心健康。”

En: No can do. I got a potty mouth till I die.
Zh: “不可能。我到死都会骂脏话的。”

En: Okay. (Lie.)
Zh: “好的。”（撒谎。）

En: Hey, why can't I swear off swearing for real?
Zh: 嘿，我怎么就不能假装发誓呢？

En: What? She covers Lamby's ears by cupping his whole head in her small palms.
Zh: “什么？”她用小手盖住羊羊的头，想要遮住它的耳朵。

En: Nooooo! Don't be bad too! Her face bunches up in a scowl.
Zh: “不要！不要做坏人！”她的五官因为生气而皱成一团。

En: Good. She hugs Lamby close to her again.
Zh: “那就好。”她又把羊羊搂紧了些。

En: She stares at you, waiting for an answer.
Zh: 她看着你，等着你的回答。

En: Lamby doesn't like bad words. Please promise!
Zh: “羊羊不喜欢脏话。你要保证不说！”

En: Guess you held your ground. It can be good, being honest with yourself.
Zh: 你坚持了自己的立场。对自己诚实是件好事。

En: Was it good to lie to a child? Maybe it doesn't matter. It's not your responsibility.
Zh: 对小孩撒谎真的好吗？大概没关系吧。毕竟这不是你的责任。

En: You can't abandon swearing for a simple reason: You're just a man who swears.
Zh: 你不能保证的原因很简单，因为你就是一个喜欢说脏话的人。

En: What? No clever answers from the body and brain? Nothing?
Zh: 什么？你的脑子已经想不出更聪明的答案了吗？什么都想不出来？

En: The little girl tilts her head. "Do you promise you won't say bad words anymore?"
Zh: 小女孩歪着脑袋。“你能保证以后再也不说脏话了吗？”

En: But what if I don't want to be a man who swears? What if I want to be a No-Bad-Word Cop?
Zh: 可如果我不想成为一个喜欢爆粗的人呢？如果我就是想成为一个爆粗的警察呢？

En: ... Fuck. I guess I am a man who swears.
Zh: ……操。我觉得我就是个喜欢爆粗的人。

En: Because it's just not you. I'm sorry.
Zh: 抱歉啊，你不是一个人。

En: Yes. There you are. It's okay -- you can breathe now. That wasn't so bad, see? It's just who you are.
Zh: 是的，你就是这样。没事——你现在能放轻松了。这感觉也不是那么糟，对吧？你就是这样的人。

En: Yes. There you are.
Zh: 是啊，你就是这样。

En: It has only lasted three seconds. The girl still waits for her answer.
Zh: 时间只过去了三秒，女孩仍在等待你的回答。

En: I won't swear! I just won't! I'll just go quiet, you watch!
Zh: 我不会骂脏话！我不会！我就这样保持安静！

En: Please, don't make this more difficult for yourself. It's okay to swear. It can be healthy, even.
Zh: 行了，别难为自己了。说脏话没什么的，甚至还能有益身心。

En: I'm talking to the children to learn from them. Has anything strange happened here lately?
Zh: “我在和孩子们交流，想从他们那儿了解一些信息。最近发生过什么奇怪的事情吗？”

En: Hello, mister! A young girl, barely four or five years old, sits on the sofa. She is looking at you with frank curiosity.
Zh: “你好，先生！”一个小女孩，约莫4、5岁的模样，正坐在一张沙发上。她看着你，带着显而易见的好奇心。

En: I heard you kids might have gotten into the Feld building?
Zh: “我听说你们好像进去过那栋菲尔德大厦？”

En: Noo, mister! Her eyes grow wider as she shakes her head. "Even Lamby doesn't like it." She hugs her stuffed toy tighter.
Zh: “没有，先生！”她摇摇头，眼睛瞪的圆溜溜的。“就连羊羊都不喜欢它。她把毛绒玩具抱得更紧了。”

En: Are you Lilienne's daughter?
Zh: “你是莉莉恩的女儿吗？”

En: Yesssss, I am! Little Lily! She gazes at you with her big eyes. "You know my mom?"
Zh: “是的，我是！小莉莉！”她用那双大眼睛看着你。“你认识我妈妈吗？”

En: Good-bye. [Leave.]
Zh: “再见。”[离开。]

En: Bye! The girl's large, curious eyes remain fixed on you.
Zh: “拜拜！”女孩那双好奇的大眼睛还是停留在你身上。

En: There was a phasmid in it! I was trying to catch it.
Zh: “里面有一只竹节虫！我想抓住它。”

En: Uh, mister, what were you doing in my fireplace?
Zh: “那个，先生，你刚才在我的壁炉里干什么呢？”

En: It's a *grouse*, she yelps, smiling broadly.
Zh: “这是一只*松鸡*，”她大声尖叫，笑的很开心。

En: What's that? (Point at the stuffed bird hanging from the ceiling.)
Zh: “那是什么？”（指向挂在天花板上的鸟类标本。）

En: Are the twins outside your brothers?
Zh: “外面那对双胞胎是你的兄弟吗？”

En: Well, can I have it?
Zh: “呃，能给我吗？”

En: Yes, but what's it *for*?
Zh: “是的，不过它是*做什么*的？”

En: I dunno.
Zh: “我不知道。”

En: Yesss. She frowns. "They don't want to play with me. They're older and play outside!"
Zh: “是的。”她皱皱眉。“他们不想跟我玩。他们比较大，所以在外面玩！”

En: Not really, but I'd like to.
Zh: “算不上吧，不过我很愿意。”

En: Yes, we met earlier.
Zh: “是的，我们之前见过。”

En: Uh huh, she nods. "Mom is great! You know what's great about my mom?"
Zh: “呃，呃，”她点点头。“我妈妈很厉害的！你知道妈妈哪里最厉害吗？”

En: That's nice! My mom is great, she nods. "She's never angry or anything."
Zh: “那很好啊！我妈妈很厉害的，”她点点头。“她从来都不生气呢。”

En: Hello, mister! The Net Picker's daughter waves at you.
Zh: “你好，先生！”拾网人的女儿朝你挥挥手。

En: She clutches a small stuffed animal. Occasionally she twirls it around.
Zh: 她抱着一只小毛绒玩具。时不时地还会扭动几下。

En: No, do tell me.
Zh: “不，请告诉我吧。”

En: Everything?
Zh: “什么事都厉害？”

En: Haa haa, yessss! She smiles, hugging her fuzzy toy.
Zh: “哈哈哈，是的！”她笑着抱住了自己的毛绒玩具。

En: *Everything*! She shouts with glee.
Zh: “*什么*都厉害！”她高兴地大喊到。

En: What's that thing you're holding? (Point at her toy.)
Zh: “你拿着的是什么？”（指向她的玩具。）

En: It's Lamby! He's my friend. Sort of, like... She holds the fuzzy beast up to demonstrate.
Zh: “这是羊羊！它是我的朋友！有点像……”她举起毛茸茸的动物给你看。

En: Lamby is a stuffed lamb that, admittedly, has seen better days. One of the eye-buttons is missing and the fur is tattered in several parts.
Zh: 羊羊是一只毛绒玩具，老实说，它看起来状态不佳。其中一只纽扣眼睛已经不见，有好几个地方的毛皮也破了。

En: Lamby looks like he's falling apart.
Zh: “羊羊好像要坏了。”

En: Oh, okay. Well, pleased to meet you, Lamby.
Zh: “哦，好吧。呃，很高兴见到你，羊羊。”

En: Lamby usually doesn't like strangers, but you're also fuzzy like Lamby.
Zh: “羊羊一般是不喜欢陌生人的，不过你跟羊羊一样，也是毛绒绒的。”

En: Yessss, Lamby has lived a long life. She waves the lamb around in the air. "Lamby was a... re.. a rev..." She stumbles on her words.
Zh: “是的，羊羊年纪很大了。”她在空中挥舞着玩具。“羊羊是个……革……那个……”她结巴了起来。

En: Revolutionary?
Zh: “革命者？”

En: Revacholian?
Zh: “瑞瓦肖？”

En: Noo-noooo, that's not it. She frowns, looking at her tattered toy. "You know what you are, Lamby."
Zh: “不，不是的。”她看着破烂的玩具，皱起眉头。“你知道你是谁，羊羊。”

En: Remote Viewer?
Zh: “遥视者？”

En: Revisionist?
Zh: “修正主义者？”

En: I think she means 'revolutionary'.
Zh: “我想她的意思是‘革命者’。”

En: Yessss, she yelps happily! "Lamby was a re-luz-ion...ry."
Zh: “是的，”她高兴地大叫起来。“羊羊是大-革……命。”

En: They look the same! Haa ha haa! Sometimes I can't tell them 'part.
Zh: 突然间，她吃吃地笑了起来。“他们看起来是一样的！哈哈哈哈！有时候我都没办法把他们分开。”

En: I guess they do look alike.
Zh: “他们的确看起来很像。”

En: They look identical, right? I said the same thing.
Zh: “他们看起来一模一样，不是吗？我也说过同样的话。”

En: Haa ha haa! She continues laughing. "Lamby tells them part." She waves her fuzzy doll in the air.
Zh: “哈哈哈哈！”她还在笑。“羊羊能分开他们。”她在空中挥舞着玩具。

En: They look... ai-dent-ik! She slowly processes the word, then snickers with laughter.
Zh: “他们看起来……模样！”她慢慢消化着这个词语，然后又吃吃地笑了起来。

En: We don't go there. Mom and auntie think we do, but it's very booky.
Zh: “我们没去过。妈妈和姑妈以为我们去过，不过它真的很鬼鬼。”

En: Is there anything you can tell me about the building?
Zh: “你能跟我讲讲那栋大厦吗？”

En: Why is it 'booky'?
Zh: “为什么会‘鬼鬼’呢？”

En: Vee-eery booky! She's clutching her stuffed toy and shaking her head. "Don't go there."
Zh: “非常鬼鬼哦！”她抱紧毛绒玩具，摇了摇头。“不要去那里。”

En: It's old and broken. She nods. "Dan-ze-ruus, Mom says. I listen to mom. Lamby also listens. Brothers listen. They tried going there, but it was scary and they ran away!"
Zh: “又老又破。”她点点头。“妈妈说，很险。我听妈妈的话。羊羊也听。哥哥们也听。他们想去那里，但是太吓人了，他们就跑了！”

En: Uh, okay, thanks.
Zh: “呃，好吧，谢谢。”

En: Sure! Just go and get it. I don't like it anyway... it looks angry.
Zh: “当然！你去拿吧。反正我不喜欢……它看起来很生气。”

En: She twirls the furry doll around in the air, giggling happily.
Zh: 她在空中旋转着手中的毛绒玩具，笑的很开心。

En: You might be able to get on Garte's good side if you make up for the skua you broke?
Zh: 如果你能把弄坏的贼鸥补上，也许就能获得加尔特的好感？

En: Nothing.
Zh: “没什么。”

En: Ohh, okay! She nods seriously.
Zh: “哦，好吧！”她严肃地点点头。

En: Ohh, okay! She twirls her fuzzy beast in the air.
Zh: “哦，好吧！”她在空中旋转着毛绒玩具。

En: You might be able to get on Garte's good side if you replace the broken skua you almost certainly broke?
Zh: 如果能换掉那个肯定是被你弄坏的贼鸥，也许就能获得加尔特的好感？

En: Can I have it? I know someone who really likes stuffed birds.
Zh: “能把它给我吗？我认识一个特别喜欢鸟类标本的人。”

En: Sure! Just go and get it. I don't like it anyway... it looks angry.
Zh: “当然！你去拿吧。反正我不喜欢……它看起来很生气。”

En: Revanchist?
Zh: “复仇？”

En: She knew you were one when you took her to Land's End.
Zh: 当你带她去地之角的时候，她就知道你是其中一员。

En: Keep it to yourself.
Zh: 自己保密。

En: Always seemed like there was something off about that islet to me...
Zh: “我总是觉得那座小岛有什么不对劲的地方……”

En: Can we maybe ask your twins about that place -- before we go? Would that be all right?
Zh: “关于那个地方，也许我们可以去问问你的双胞胎——在出发之前？这样可以吗？”

En: Be my guest. She looks at the boys. "They've a strange way of talking. See if you can get anything *useful* out of them. I seldom do..."
Zh: “请便。”她看着男孩们。“他们的说话方式很奇怪。看看你能不能从他们那里打听出什么*有用*的消息来。我很少做到……”

En: Indeed. You're always confused as to your whereabouts.
Zh: 确实。你总是不知道自己身在何处。

En: True. Most people I've met are scared.
Zh: “这是真的。我见过的大部分人都很害怕。”

En: Aye. No one wants to talk about how frightened they are. But only frightened people are *really* dangerous -- and *plenty* of them are dangerous.
Zh: “是啊。没人想说起自己到底有多害怕。不过只有感到害怕的人，才是*真正*危险的人——而且*很多*人都是危险的。”

En: *She's* not afraid, however. Does that make her not dangerous?
Zh: 不过，*她*并不害怕。这样她就不危险了吗？

En: She really liked those muscles though. It's in the way she pronounces 'sinewy'.
Zh: 不过她真的很喜欢那些肌肉。从*肌肉发达*这个词的发音方式，就能看出来。

En: Life didn't really change that much for me and the kids... She glances at the village where two little kids are playing with what look like rocks.
Zh: 她瞥了一眼村子的方向，有两个小孩正在玩看起来像是石头的东西。“对于我和孩子们来说，生活真的没有太大变化……”

En: Do it! Hit on the widow! It's the *right* thing to do.
Zh: 去吧！向这个寡妇献殷勤！这是一件*正确*的事。

En: My prediction? It will be at least two days.
Zh: 我的预言吗？至少两天吧。

En: This is what is called a *conversation*. You don't have to be guarded right now.
Zh: 这就是所谓的*对话*。现在你不用那么谨慎。

En: Keep it professional, man. Don't make it sound like you're hitting on her.
Zh: 专业一点，老兄。别搞得像是你在泡她一样。

En: Mines! Mines! You need mines.
Zh: 水雷！水雷！你需要水雷。

En: Aye, the sea's gonna calm down soon. I can feel it. The wind is turning southeast. She nods. "What's on your mind, officer?"
Zh: “是啊，大海很快就会平静下来。我能感觉到。风正在转向东南方。”她点点头。“你在想什么呢，警官？”

En: Sire, she doth not believe it wasn't you.
Zh: 大人，她不相信不是您做的。

En: Why odd? Our things are a part of our life-world. They're made with human sweat and they share human history. We should care about them as we care about humans, to some extent at least.
Zh: “为什么奇怪？我们的东西就是这个生活世界的一部分。它们是人类用汗水铸就，也分享着我们的历史。我们应该像关心人类一样关心它们，至少在某种程度上是这样。”

En: Life-world? Someone's been reading up on last century Gottwaldian philosophers. Play it cool now.
Zh: 生活世界？有人一直在研读上个世纪的戈特伍德哲学。现在冷静点。

En: Walks are fucking *great* cardio. Very stable fat-burner. Aerobic exercise.
Zh: 散步是他妈*超棒*的心肺锻炼。非常稳定的燃脂运动。有氧运动。

En: You need to understand that *nothing* is going to happen. We're just walking. That's it. Understood?
Zh: “你必须理解，*什么事*也不会发生。我们只是散步。就这样。明白了吗？”

En: You didn't *only* get shot.
Zh: 你不*仅仅*是中弹了。

En: That's cool. You boasting your bacterial infection like that.
Zh: 那样挺酷的，吹嘘自己的细菌感染。

En: This must be one of the many fortifications that was used in the dying days of the Revolution. Against Coalition forces -- before they took this city.
Zh: 这肯定是大革命末期使用的众多堡垒中的一个。这些堡垒被用来抵御联军的工事——直到这座城市被联军占领。

En: Aye-aye, captain.
Zh: 明白，船长。

En: She sounds incredulous about the niceness of the idea.
Zh: 听起来她很怀疑这个美好的想法。

En: Thank you. (Take it back.)
Zh: “谢谢你。”（收回来。）

En: Wait. Why did you sign it?
Zh: “等等。你为什么要签？”

En: You're welcome, she says with a little nod.
Zh: “不客气，”她点头说道。

En: I shouldn't have?
Zh: “我不该签吗？”

En: I've looked at the documents and no, you shouldn't have.
Zh: “我看过文件了，不，你不该签的。”

En: No-no, it's a youth centre. Why *shouldn't* you?
Zh: “不不不，这是个青少年活动中心。为什么*不*呢？”

En: I mean it's Evrart, so it's probably a mistake.
Zh: “我是说，这可是艾弗拉特，所以这可能是个错误。”

En: That's what I asked *you*. She tilts her head. "It's a youth centre and I have children. Got to give them *something* else to do. They're cute and small now, but they're growing up fast. I had my first drink when I was eleven, because I was so bored..."
Zh: “那是我问*你*的。”她歪起头。“这是一家青少年活动中心，而且我有孩子。总得让她们有些*别的事*可以做吧。她们现在还小，很可爱，不过很快就会长大。我11岁的时候第一次喝酒，因为实在是太无聊了……”

En: Yes. Well. I looked at it and thought I should -- because it says *youth centre* and I have my own children. I am, of course, just a stupid poor person, so what do I know?
Zh: “是的。好吧。我看过了，觉得我应该签——因为上面说这是个*青少年活动中心*，而且我自己也有孩子。当然了，我是个又穷又蠢的人，所以我怎么会知道呢？”

En: Living with the construction noise is going to be a challenge.
Zh: “在建筑噪音下生活会是个不小的挑战。”

En: Besides, you also need Isobel's signature, she says with a smile. "If it's a bad idea, she won't sign."
Zh: “而且，你还需要伊泽贝尔的签名，”她笑着说道。“如果这是个坏主意，那她是不会签的。”

En: Sure, but it's temporary and for a good cause and I need to give the children *something* else to do...
Zh: “当然，但这只是暂时的，有正当理由，而且我需要给孩子们一些*别的事*做……”

En: Probably. She smiles. "But it is a youth centre and I have children. Got to give them *something* else to do. They're cute and small now, but they're growing up fast. I had my first drink when I was eleven, because I was so bored..."
Zh: “可能吧。”她笑了。“不过这是个青少年活动中心，而且我有孩子。总得让她们有些*别的事*可以做吧。她们现在还小，很可爱，不过很快就会长大。我11岁的时候第一次喝酒，因为实在是太无聊了……”

En: Sound logic, division of labour. She leaves the scepticism to the sceptic.
Zh: 听起来很合乎逻辑，分工合作。她把怀疑论留给怀疑主义者了。

En: Sounds like *Isobel* is the leader around here.
Zh: 听起来*伊泽贝尔*是这里的领导。

En: Anyhow.... you also need Isobel's signature. If it's a bad idea, she won't sign. You'll find she's a tougher nut to crack than me.
Zh: “不管怎么说……你还需要伊泽贝尔的签名，如果这是个坏主意，那她是不会签的。你会发现，她比我要难对付的多。”

En: ... and those members have a vote, when electing the head of the local chapter.
Zh: ……当选举地方分会会长的时候，那些成员是有投票权的。

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: They're cute and small now, but they're growing up fast. Too fast, I had my first drink when I was eleven, because I was so bored...
Zh: “她们现在还小，很可爱，不过很快就会长大。太快了。我11岁的时候第一次喝酒，因为实在是太无聊了……”

En: Anyway... you'll have to get Isobel to sign it too. I saw the signatories --  she won't if it's a bad idea. She's a much tougher nut to crack.
Zh: “不管怎么说……你还需要伊泽贝尔的签名。我看见签订方了——如果这是个坏主意，那她是不会签的。她比我要难对付的多。”

En: I'm looking for someone, maybe you can help?
Zh: “我正在找人，也许你能帮忙？”

En: Let's see, she tilts her head ever so slightly. "Who are you looking for?"
Zh: “那就说说看，”她轻轻地歪了歪头。“你在找谁？”

En: I'm looking for a suspect who might have stayed in this neighbourhood.
Zh: “我在找一个嫌疑人，她很可能就待在这附近。”

En: I'm looking for missing cryptozoologists.
Zh: “我正在找一个失踪的神秘动物学家。”

En: A working class husband.
Zh: “一个工人阶级丈夫。”

En: That's it, I'm not looking for anyone else right now.
Zh: “就这样吧，我现在没找其他的人。”

En: Okay, when did this person stay here?
Zh: “好吧，这个人是什么时候待在这里的？”

En: Very recently, over the past few days. She might have arrived on Friday.
Zh: “就是最近，几天前的事。她可能是周五到的。”

En: Oh... She looks slightly disappointed. "I've been out on the sea for most of the past week. The weather's been good for fishing, so I usually start at four in the morning."
Zh: “哦……”她看起来有些失望。“上周我基本都出海了。天气很适合钓鱼，所以我一般早上4点就出门了。”

En: Really?
Zh: “真的吗？”

En: Yes, that's the optimal time. Got to make the most of the calm. I've been sleeping like a corpse after. The sea really takes its toll. Now I'm just waiting for the wind to settle to get out there again...
Zh: “是的，那是最佳时间。海面最平静的时间。之后我就睡的像具尸体一样了。大海真的能造成很大的伤害。现在我只是等待风停了之后再出海……”

En: Sorry I couldn't help you out. A faint smile runs across her face. "Maybe I can help you find someone else?"
Zh: “抱歉没帮到你。”她的脸上掠过一丝微笑。“也许我能帮你找到别人？”

En: Ugh... She frowns, thinking. "I don't think I know what these are. Care to elaborate?"
Zh: “呃……”她皱紧眉头，思索着。“我好像不知道是什么人。介意详细描述一下吗？”

En: People who look for imaginary animals.
Zh: “寻找假想动物的人。”

En: People who look for animals, who are hard to find.
Zh: “寻找很难找到的动物的人。”

En: People who look for animals *mainstream* scientists deny exist.
Zh: “寻找*主流*科学家否认存在的动物的人。”

En: Aha, she exclaims. "Like Snow Men!"
Zh: “啊哈，”她感叹到。“就像雪人一样！”

En: Snow Men? I haven't heard about those.
Zh: “雪人？我从来没有听说过。”

En: Two odd guys have been wandering around here, nose in sand, talking nonsense about Snow Men and the like.
Zh: “有两个奇怪的家伙总是在这里转来转去的，把鼻子埋进沙子里，嘴里说些雪人之类毫无意义的话。”

En: Wait, 'the like'?
Zh: “等等，‘之类的’？”

En: Where did they go?
Zh: “他们去哪了？”

En: Right, she nods. "Not only Snow Men -- also Green Men, Monkey Men, burning rhinos. You get the picture."
Zh: “是的，”她点点头。“不仅是雪人——还有绿人，猿人，燃烧的犀牛。你懂得。”

En: Oh, you're getting it and it *is* gorgeous.
Zh: 哦，你明白了，而且这*真是*太棒了。

En: I don't really know. Further down the peninsula I guess. I mean that's where they were heading. She points north. "Who else are you looking for, beside Snow Men?"
Zh: “我真的不知道。沿着半岛继续往下吧，我猜。我是说，他们就是往那里去了。”她指向北边。“你还在找谁吗，除了雪人之外？”

En: Yeah, *I'm* not really looking for that anymore. Not much into the middle-class ones either. She sighs. "I could use some *landed gentry*, but apparently they don't make those anymore."
Zh: “是啊，*我*也没在找那个了。对资产阶级也不太感兴趣。”她叹了口气。“*乡绅*还是可以的，不过显然已经没有了。”

En: The husband isn't for me. I'm looking for him for his wife.
Zh: “不是为我找丈夫。我是帮他的妻子找人。”

En: Wish I could help you with that, but I haven't seen your 'working class husband'. Maybe I can help you find someone else?
Zh: “希望我能帮到你，不过我真的没见过那位‘工人阶级丈夫’。也许我可以帮你找别人？”

En: She seems genuinely sorry for not being able to help you.
Zh: 她似乎真的为没帮到你感到很抱歉。

En: Well, how can I assist you then, officer?
Zh: “那有什么我能帮你的吗，警官？”

En: Really? Are you sure you're not also looking for Nadia Harnankur? Or Ignus Nilsen? Or the great lost city of Eis under the waves?
Zh: “真的吗？你确定你没找纳迪亚·哈纳库？或者是伊格纳斯·尼尔森？或者是海浪之下的伟大失落之城艾斯？”

En: I am looking for a lot of stuff, ain't I?
Zh: “我正在找很多东西，不是吗？”

En: A *lot* of stuff, she concurs with a smile.
Zh: “*很多*东西，”她笑着表示赞同。

En: I'm just doing my job. Let's change the subject.
Zh: “我只是在做自己的工作而已。我们换个话题吧。”

En: I guess that makes you into some sort of a terrier. She picks on her net. "Either way, I can't help you out."
Zh: “我猜这让你变成了某种小猎犬。”她拉扯着渔网。“不管怎么说，我帮不到你。”

En: Intense. She pulls her fingers through her hair. "You're like some kind of dark terrier with your endless searches. Maybe there's something else I can help you with then?"
Zh: “很热心。”她用手指捋过头发。“你就像只黑暗猎犬，在永无止尽地搜寻。也许我能帮你点别的？”

En: I'm an agent of unstoppable forces. No one can escape what's coming for them.
Zh: “我是一支无坚不摧的力量的代理人。没人能逃脱即将降临在他们身上的命运。”

En: I guess so. Doesn't feel like a lot to me. I could be doing *more*. Maybe there's someone missing from your life?
Zh: “我猜是吧。我感觉不是很多。我可以做的*更多*。也许有人从你的生活中失踪了？”

En: Yeah, that's what cops do.
Zh: “是啊，警察就是干这个的。”

En: Oh, she chuckles. "There's plenty missing, but that's too much for even an eager terrier like you to handle. Although you seem very thorough. Tell me instead, is there something else I can help you with?"
Zh: “哦，”她咯咯笑了。“很多东西都失踪了，不过即便是你这样热心的猎犬，恐怕都很难处理。尽管你似乎是个很细致的人。不如还是告诉我，还有什么我能帮你的吗？”

En: I'm looking for someone else actually.
Zh: “其实我正在找一个人。”

En: Oh? Who?
Idiot Doom Spiral over there needs his jacket. Have you seen it?
Zh: “白痴厄运漩涡要我帮他找夹克。你见过吗？”

En: Unbelievable, you *are* looking for more lost things. She smiles. "Doom Spiral losing his jacket on the other hand is *very* believable. I mean he has lost everything else already."
Zh: “难以置信，你*正在*寻找更多丢失的东西。”她笑了。“另一方面，厄运漩涡弄丢他的夹克就已经*非常*难以置信了。我是说，他已经把一切都弄丢了。”

En: Remarkable... She shakes her head. "That one already lost everything else and now his jacket too?"
Zh: “真是太厉害了……”她摇摇头。“那家伙本来就已经一无所有，现在连夹克也弄丢了？”

En: It's a good thing too that he has an actual police officer looking for it. A smile lights up her face. "Good old Doom Spiral, upper-management to the core."
Zh: “有个真正的警察帮他找东西是件好事。”她的脸被笑容点亮了。“老好人厄运漩涡，完全就是高级管理层。”

En: That's odd. Is she actually impressed?
Zh: 奇怪。她真的被打动了吗？

En: She's genuinely rooting for you, sire!
Zh: 她是真心支持你的，大人！

En: *No one* plays Johnny Jacket. I find it -- I keep it.
Zh: “*没人*会玩找皮夹克的游戏。被我找到——我就留下。”

En: Aye, we all feel that way sometimes... She turns her gaze towards the beach. "Sometimes I wonder if I'm the protagonist even in my own life story."
Zh: “是啊，有时候我们都会有那种感觉……”她望向海滩。“有时候我甚至会怀疑我到底是不是自己人生故事里的主人翁。”

En: A gush of sadness suddenly washes over her.
Zh: 一股悲伤突然涌上她的心头。

En: Anyway... She clears her throat. Twice. "I'd look near the fish market north of here, on the boardwalk. Drunks are *drawn* to markets, for some reason. Was there something else?"
Zh: “不管怎么说……”她清了清喉咙。两次。“要是我的话，会去北边的鱼市看看，在木板道上。酒鬼总是莫名其妙地会被市场*吸引*。还有别的事吗？”

En: Ask her about the cool sword -- helps to break the ice.
Zh: 问问她那把很酷的剑——有助于打破僵局。

En: But until that happens I can try to assist you the best I can, she adds with a smile. "So, what will it be, officer?"
Zh: “但是在那之前，我会尽我所能地帮助你，”她笑了笑。“所以，是什么事吗，警官？”

En: Of course. Question time!
Zh: 当然了。问题时间到！

En: But not when you're in a *tight* spot.
Zh: 但身处*急境*不行。

En: And *sometimes* it isn't.
Zh: *有时候*不是。

En: Is she flirting with you? A *little*? You think she might be...
Zh: 她是在跟你调情吗？有*一点*？你想她可能是……

En: That does *not* go for *real* men. It does not go for *you*. Show her. Show her The Wonder.
Zh: 这样*并不*适合*真正的*男人。这样并不适合*你*。让她看看。让她看看那个奇迹。

En: Coach means *The Expression*.
Zh: 教练是说那个*表情*。

En: Sounds like she'd prefer her men to be less 'real'.
Zh: 听起来她更喜欢没那么‘真实的’男人。

En: Do you like it?
Zh: “你喜欢吗？”

En: Not bad.
Zh: 还不赖。

En: Find someone else to laugh at, I'm not a clown!
Zh: “嘲笑别人去吧，我不是小丑！”

En: I apologize, she replies with a nod. "It looked like you could face-down any horror in the world with that unchanging grin. Looked like a shield."
Zh: “我很抱歉，”她点头回复到。“看起来你可以用同样不变的笑容降服这个世界上的一切恐惧。就像一面盾牌。”

En: The traces of her laughter are still there -- in her eyes, fading fast.
Zh: 微笑的痕迹还残留在她脸上——在她的眼睛里，很快就消退了。

En: Yeah, by himself. Inadvertently. She continues with a frown. "He never respected the sea. Went out there drunk like a skunk and sure enough one day he didn't come back."
Zh: “是啊，被他自己。不经意的。”她皱紧眉头，继续说道。“他从来不尊重大海。像只臭鼬一样喝的酩酊大醉，果然有一天他再也没有回来。”

En: Who knows, maybe he fell over? She shrugs. "He didn't respect the sea. Went out there drunk like a skunk and sure enough one day the boat was found floating empty. The bloated corpse turned up two weeks later."
Zh: “谁知道呢，也许掉下去了？”她耸耸肩。“他不尊重大海。像只臭鼬一样喝的酩酊大醉，果然有一天，别人只发现一条空空如也的船。两周之后，浮尸出现了。”

En: He didn't respect the sea. Went out there drunk like a skunk and sure enough one day the boat was found floating empty. The bloated corpse turned up two weeks later.
Zh: “他不尊重大海。像只臭鼬一样喝的酩酊大醉，果然有一天，别人只发现一条空空如也的船。两周之后，浮尸出现了。”

En: Now, before you tell me how sorry you are for my loss, know that it was four years ago and I've moved on. There's only so much mourning you can do for a drunk with sinewy muscles.
Zh: “现在，在你说为我的损失感到遗憾之前，我要告诉你，那是四年前的事了，我已经挺过来了。对于一个肌肉发达的酒鬼来说，我的哀痛也只有那么多了。”

En: Yeah, death is nothing. I shit on death.
Zh: “是啊，死亡什么也不是，我对死亡不屑一顾。”

En: You should have thrown yourself in the waves after him. (Nod sagely.)
Zh: “你应该也跳进海里，追随他而去。”（一本正经地点点头。）

En: Time really is the best cure for sorrow, isn't it?
Zh: “时间真是治疗悲伤最好的良药，不是吗？”

En: It's healthy to let go and move on. Gotta keep the wheels spinning.
Zh: “放开过去，继续生活的确很健康。人总还是活下去的。”

En: Us working folk don't have the luxury to be bed-sick with melancholy. She crosses her arms. "I buried him, mourned for an appropriate amount of time, and went on."
Zh: “因为悲伤而卧床不起对于我们这些要工作的人来说太过奢侈。”她抱紧双臂。“我埋葬了他，为此哀痛了适当的时间，然后继续生活。”

En: This is neither a touchy nor a very interesting topic for her.
Zh: 对于她来说，这个话题即不敏感，也没有特别有趣。

En: She looks like she's ready to go on a date with another -- better! -- drunk. Ask her. Both of you could need some action.
Zh: 看来她已经准备好跟其他人约会了——甚至更好！——醉酒。问问她。你们俩都需要行动起来。

En: I don't. But us working folk don't have the luxury to be bed-sick with melancholy. She crosses her arms. "I buried him, mourned for an appropriate amount of time and went on."
Zh: “我没有。不过，因为悲伤而卧床不起对于我们这些要工作的人来说太过奢侈。”她抱紧双臂。“我埋葬了他，为此哀痛了适当的时间，然后继续生活。”

En: Oh? The kids too, right? She doesn't wait for your reply. "Working class people don't have that luxury. That's for rich folks. We just go on."
Zh: “哦？还有孩子，对吗？”她没有等待你的回答。“对于工人阶级来说太奢侈了。那是有钱人的玩意。我们只能继续。”

En: I buried my man, mourned for a month and that was it. Life didn't really change that much for me and the kids. Not for the worse at least.
Zh: “我埋葬了自己的男人，哀痛了一个月，仅此而已。对于我和孩子们来说，生活真的没有太大变化。至少没有变得更加糟糕。”

En: All right! Major choice moment. You only get to ask ONE thing. It would be weird to say them all. Choose wisely.
Zh: 好吧！抉择时刻到来了。你只能问一件事情。全说出来有点太奇怪了。要做出明智的选择。

En: She would like to complain. She would like that very much. But she can't and she won't. 'Though' has gotten her this far and she's not giving it up.
Zh: 她想抱怨。她非常想。但是她不能，也不会。‘尽管’她已经走了这么远，但她还没有放弃。

En: Hordes of wild homeless people roaming the lands, nomads scattered across an endless plain in a dystopian world where tare is the only valid currency... and people eat each other to stay alive.
Zh: 大一群无家可归的人在这片土地上徘徊，游牧民分散在一个反乌托邦世界那无尽的平原之上，在那里，回收品就是唯一有效的货币……人们为了活下来而自相残杀。

En: He's right, *bratan*! You're descending and you need to climb higher. Soar on the wings of your spirit hawk. Or just do a line... whatever, right? Not a biggie.
Zh: 他说得对，*兄弟*！你在下降，你需要爬的更高一些。乘着灵魂之鹰的翅膀在空中翱翔。或者吸点可卡因……管他的呢，对吧？又不是什么大事。

En: Spikey? Must have been a naval contact mine.
Zh: 尖钉？肯定是一颗海军触发水雷。

En: Yeah, maybe steer clear of the things reminding her of the 'floater' she used to be married to. Just saying.
Zh: 是啊，也许避开这些事情会让她想起自己曾经嫁给过那具‘浮尸’。只是说说而已。

En: Fine by me. She brushes her hair aside, waiting for you to speak.
Zh: “我没意见。”她把头发拢到一边，等着你开口。

En: Yeah... She smiles, slowly shaking her head. "That's what I tell myself every morning."
Zh: “是啊……”她笑了，慢慢摇摇头。“每天早上我也是这么跟自己说的。”

En: When do you think it'll be ready? There's something we might have to check out on one of the islands. The origin of a shot.
Zh: “你觉得船什么时候能准备好？我们可能要去岛上检查一下。一枚子弹的源点。”

En: When do you think it'll be ready? There's something I might have to check out on one of the islands. The origin of a shot.
Zh: “你觉得船什么时候能准备好？我可能要去岛上检查一下。一枚子弹的源点。”

En: When do you think the boat will be ready?
Zh: “你觉得船什么时候能准备好？”

En: Khm... I wanna ask you something else.
Zh: “咳咳……我想问你点别的。”

En: Shot, huh? She gives you short nod and replies: "The boat will be ready when the sea turns and the winds settle. You can't command the weather, officer."
Zh: “子弹？”她快速点点头，回复到：“等到海面平息，风停下来的时候，船准备好了。你是没办法命令天气的，警官。”

En: In time, she replies with a nod and adds: "When the sea turns and the winds settle she will be ready."
Zh: “很快，”她点头回复到，然后又补充了一句：“等到海面平息，风停下来的时候就准备好了。”

En: Go right ahead. She looks at you, head slightly tilted.
Zh: “说下去。”她看着你，脑袋微微倾斜。

En: Waves wash the sand. A skiff moves across the mirror-smooth sea, far away from here. A lone passenger. A fast sloop in the distance. White sails.
Zh: 海浪冲刷着沙滩。远处，一艘小艇行驶在平滑如镜的海面。一个孤独的乘客。远处有一艘快速航行的单桅帆船。扬起白色的风帆。

En: It's an installation. (Wet your lips.) "Very conceptual."
Zh: “这是一种装置艺术。”（润润你的嘴唇。）“非常概念化。”

En: I got drunk and apparently drove it in the sea.
Zh: “我喝的酩酊大醉，显然是把它直接开进海里了。”

En: It was an accident. I'm sorry.
Zh: “这是个意外。我很抱歉。”

En: An official investigation has been launched -- by me -- to determine just that, ma'am.
Zh: “刚刚启动了一项官方调查——我启动的——来确定这件事，夫人。”

En: It is a sign. Of the carnage to come. The great Culling.
Zh: “这是一种征兆。大屠杀即将来到。大规模的优胜劣汰。”

En: I don't know.
Zh: “我不知道。”

En: I think I might, the lieutenant mumbles under his breath.
Zh: “我想我可能知道，”警督低声咕哝着。

En: She looks at you and shrugs. "This calls for a funeral if you ask me."
Zh: 她看着你，耸了耸肩。“如果你问我的话，这就需要一场葬礼了。”

En: I wasn't *feeling* it.
Zh: “我没有*感觉*到。”

En: Wasn't feeling it, she repeats thoughtfully. "That calls for a funeral if you ask me."
Zh: “没有感觉到，”她若有所思地重复到。“如果你问我，这就需要一场葬礼了。”

En: Quite the sign. An eye-sore too. She frowns. "The situation calls for a funeral if you ask me."
Zh: “真是个不得了的征兆。也是个眼中钉。”她皱紧眉头。“如果你问我的话，这种情况需要举办一场葬礼。”

En: Hm, an installation? She raises her eyebrows. "Us poor people are stupid and don't get 'installations'. All I see is a heap of trash. This actually calls for a funeral, I think."
Zh: “嗯，一种装置艺术？”她扬起眉头。“我们，穷人，太愚蠢，不懂什么‘装置艺术’。我唯一看见的只有一堆垃圾。我想需要为此办一场葬礼。”

En: That's great. The woman glances towards the wreckage again. "Until then you should maybe start thinking about the funeral?"
Zh: “那太棒了。”女人又瞥了一眼残骸。“在那之前，也许你应该考虑办一场葬礼？”

En: Aye. Feels deserved, don't you think? Falling in the line of duty like that and all.
Zh: “是啊。感觉很理所当然，不是吗？因公殉职什么的。”

En: You are suggesting we honour the carcass of my former motor carriage?
Zh: “你是在暗示我们应该纪念前座驾留下的残骸吗？”

En: No, that's senseless sentimentality. Inanimate objects don't have souls.
Zh: “不，这种多愁善感毫无意义。没有生命的物体是没有灵魂的。”

En: I'm actually in the middle of a murder investigation right now.
Zh: “其实我现在正在调查一起凶杀案。”

En: You never know, officer. *Some* things might have a soul. There's something playful in her smile. "But I get it. A rational guy like you probably has a lot on his plate anyway. So what can I do for you?"
Zh: “你永远也不会知道的，警官。*有些*东西可能有灵魂。”她的笑容里有一丝幽默的味道。“不过我能理解。反正像你这种理性的人肯定有很多事要做。所以我能帮你什么吗？”

En: Police business. I understand. She nods. "Anything I can help you with?"
Zh: “警方事务，我能理解。”她点点头。“还有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: She didn't appreciate your cold rationality.
Zh: 她并不欣赏你那种冰冰凉凉的理性。

En: Take it from someone who's been through a few funerals: It's easiest to just leave them there and let nature take care of it.
Zh: “相信一个经历过很多次葬礼的人的话：把它留在那里会容易得多，交给大自然去处理。”

En: It's going to be quite a few years before 'nature' is able to completely take care of a motor carriage. You can always come back in few months and see if you still want to give it a proper send-off.
Zh: ‘大自然’完全照顾好一辆汽車可能还要花上几年时间。你总是可以几个月之后再回来，看看你还想不想跟它来个合适的告别。

En: That's all we have time for right now anyway. The lieutenant looks at you sympathetically. "Come back here in June and see how you feel about it then. It's not like it's going anywhere."
Zh: “反正我们现在没时间管这个。”警督同情地看着你。“等到六月份再过来吧，看看那时候你会有什么感受。反正它也跑不到哪里去。”

En: It's a pity we don't get to do more frivolous side-activities. Like rejuvenate the Doomed Commercial Area, or...
Zh: “很遗憾，我们没时间做些其他琐碎的事情。比如让诅咒商业区恢复生机，或者……”

En: It's a pity we don't get to do more frivolous side-activities. I would have really liked that.
Zh: “很遗憾，我们没时间做些其他琐碎的事情。我真的挺想做的。”

En: Let's focus on the things we *can* actually do, alright?
Zh: “还是专注在我们*实际*能做的事情吧，好吗？”

En: Absolutely. She nods. "Things like what for example?"
Zh: “绝对可以。”她点点头。“比如说什么事？”

En: The lieutenant looks at you almost gently. "Yes. That is a pity. But for now let's focus on the things we *do* get to do. Like the murder investigation for example."
Zh: 警督看着你，目光可以说是很温柔。“没错，是很遗憾。不过还是先专注于我们*必须*要做的事情吧。比如说凶杀案调查。”

En: He liked the Coupris funeral idea.
Zh: 他喜欢为库普瑞斯办葬礼的主意。

En: I understand, believe me, I really do. She brushes back her hair. "I think about all the frivolous side-activities I *could* be doing every time I go out there with my nets."
Zh: “我能理解，相信我，我真的可以。”她把头发拢向后面。“每当我带着渔网出海的时候，总会想着各种各样*可以*做的琐碎小事。”

En: But daydreaming is a rich man's game. At sea you can't afford to be careless and dream. Anyway... She shakes her head. "What are we talking about here again?"
Zh: “但是白日做梦是有钱人的游戏。在海上，你可承担不起粗心大意和做梦的风险。不管怎么说……”她摇摇头。“我们刚才在说什么来着？”

En: Oh, yes she does -- you need to get your drink on. There is no other way for human beings to procreate. Not after six thousand years of yeast cultivation-based mating rituals.
Zh: 是的，她当然知道——你需要醉生梦死。人类没有其他的生殖方式。特别是在6000多年以来，人类采用的都是基于酵母培养的交配仪式。

En: You think she really needs more of that after her man died at sea?
Zh: 在她的酒鬼丈夫葬身大海之后，你真觉得她还需要那种狗屎吗？

En: Get the drink ON. It would be unimaginable for a human female to have sex with a man without Al-Gul as the master of ceremonies to guide them.
Zh: 喝起来吧。如果没有仪式之主阿尔古引导，一个人类女性跟男性发生关系是难以想象的。

En: Alcohol-man? That's *exactly* what she needs! Now make her see that.
Zh: 酒鬼？她需要的*就是*这个！现在要做的就是让她看到这一点。

En: I like you -- but I'm afraid to be around you, because you're a woman.
Zh: “我喜欢你——不过我很害怕待在你身边，因为你是个女人。”

En: I wanna be close to you and there's but one way to intimacy...
Zh: “我想靠近你，不过只有一种方法能让我们变得亲密无间……”

En: Since the dawn of mankind, Al-Gul has watched over the re-procreation of our species. Lay with me. Let's celebrate the Gul.
Zh: “自从人类诞生之日起，阿尔古就一直守护着我们这个种族的再繁殖过程。跟我一起躺下来。让我们一起赞美阿尔古。”

En: Alcohol makes closeness possible. Let's *connect*.
Zh: “酒精让亲密变得可能。让我们彼此*联系*在一起吧。”

En: Okay. She blinks. "Where is this going?"
Zh: “好吧。”她眨眨眼。“这是要干什么？”

En: I need to be drunk. You do too. Please get drunk with me on a drunk-date.
Zh: “我需要喝醉。你也要。请跟我一起踏上醉酒约会之旅吧。”

En: Absurdly and pointedly phrased. She doesn't laugh. "You can be quite funny, officer. Anyway, what did you want?"
Zh: “你的措辞真是荒谬又尖锐。”她的脸上没有笑容。“你还挺有意思的，警官。总之，你想要什么？”

En: She doesn't even *understand* you asked her out. Perhaps you're too sober to pull it off right now? Try again later.
Zh: 她甚至都没*明白*你在约她出去。也许你太过清醒，现在没办法办成这件事？那就之后再试试吧。

En: Eh... she looks confused. "What?"
Zh: “呃……”她看起来有些困惑。“什么？”

En: Drunk-date. (Tap on your throat.) "All we need is booze. The rest will follow."
Zh: “醉酒约会。”（拍拍你的喉咙。）“我们唯一需要的就是酒精。其他一切都会顺其自然的。”

En: She bursts out laughing. "That's a good impression. You really crack me up, officer. Now how can I help you?"
Zh: 她大笑出声。“这还真是个好印象。你真是要把我笑死了，警官。现在，我有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Now there's a whole shipment of big words. She looks at you from head to toe. "May mankind be plentiful, I guess? Can I get back to my nets now?"
Zh: “你还真是大言不惭呢。”她从头到脚打量了你一遍。“我猜还真是什么样的男人都有吧？现在能让我继续整理渔网了吗？”

En: Alcohol? Connect? I'm not sure I'm following you. There's confusion in her eyes.
Zh: “酒精？联系？我好像不太明白你的意思。”她的眼睛里满是困惑。

En: Get hammered with me. On a date. Drunk-date. It will be nice, I promise.
Zh: “跟我一起喝醉。一起约会。醉酒约会。我保证，肯定会很不错的。”

En: Funny. Funny sailor jokes. She nods, without smiling. "Sorry if I'm not laughing out loud, but the comedy has worn thin over the years."
Zh: “很有趣。有趣的水手笑话。”她点点头，但是脸上没有笑容。“对不起，我没能笑出声来，这么多年来，喜剧对我已经没什么太大用了。”

En: Great, now you reminded her of the husband.
Zh: 很好，现在你让她想起了自己的丈夫。

En: It's okay, she just hasn't figured out how these things work between the sexes. Keep at it. She'll come around.
Zh: 没事的，她只是还没搞清楚两性之间的关系是怎么运作的。坚持下去。她会觉醒的。

En: I'm a drunk -- don't know if you've noticed -- so I usually ask people to drink with me.
Zh: “我是个酒鬼——不知道你有没有注意到——所以我一般都会邀请别人跟我一起喝酒。”

En: *This* time, though... (Imitate drum roll.) I thought I'd ask you for a *walk*."
Zh: “不过，*这一次*……”（模仿击鼓的声音。）“我想邀请你去*散散步*。”

En: I have and I don't really... She hesitates.
Zh: “我也有过，而且我现在真不想……”她犹豫了。

En: But *what* do I do then?!
Zh: 但是我该怎么*做*呢？！

En: What if I told you... it is actually possible to go on a date sober?
Zh: 如果我告诉你……在清醒的状态下约会其实是可能的呢？

En: I'm listening.
Zh: 我正在听。

En: Get out of here with your crazy fringe theories!
Zh: 带着你那套疯狂的边缘理论滚出去！

En: What you're suggesting is highly experimental. Possibly dangerous.
Zh: 你暗示的东西有很高的实验性。可能还很危险。

En: *Before* recorded history, men and women were able to do simple, very primitive things together sober. Acknowledge the situation -- and keep it basic.
Zh: 在有文字记录的历史*之前*，男人和女人能够在清醒的状态下做出一些单纯的、非常原始的事情。承认这种状况——然后简单一点。

En: Now! Go for it!
Zh: 就是现在！开始吧！

En: You just wanna go for a walk. What kind of a *monster* doesn't wanna go for a walk? Walks are innocent.
Zh: 你只想出去散散步。什么样的*怪物*会不想散步呢？散步多天真啊。

En: Just a walk?! She feigns extreme surprise. "I don't know, officer -- I would not have taken you for an innocent perambulator."
Zh: “只是散散步？！”她假装一副极度震惊的样子。“我不知道，警官——我没想到你会是一个天真的巡视者。”

En: Officer... The lieutenant has discreetly taken a few steps back. "I thought we had a walk of our own in mind. One that results in a solved murder investigation?"
Zh: “警官……”警督谨慎地向后退了几步，插了进来。“我觉得我们应该在脑子里散步就好。最后还能完成凶杀案调查的那种？”

En: Is that a 'yes', ma'am? Is the walk happening?
Zh: “你的回答是‘可以’吗，夫人？要去散步吗？”

En: Please, *please* come to a sober walk with me.
Zh: “拜托，*请*跟我一起来场清醒的散步之旅吧。”

En: Let's call it a walk. The lieutenant adjusts his glasses and looks at you apologetically. "I will join you, if the ma'am insists."
Zh: “还是就叫它散步吧。”警督调了调眼镜，带着歉意地看着你。“如果这位女士坚持，我可以加入。”

En: Oh no, *you* come with us. Seriously, I insist. She smiles at the lieutenant and turns back to you...
Zh: “哦，不，*你*也要一起。我很坚持，真心的。”她朝着警督笑了笑，然后再次转向你……

En: I am! That's why you should definitely agree.
Zh: “我是啊！所以你一定要同意。”

En: She's thinking, eyes mapping your rugged features. "Where would this... walk take us, officer?"
Zh: 她在考虑，眼中映射出你粗旷的面容。”这次……散步会把我们带到什么地方去呢，警官？”

En: Nothing creepy, *strange*, or out there. Keep it airy and simple.
Zh: “不要说任何诡异的，*奇怪*的，或者远超常识的东西。轻松一点，简单一点。

En: Creepy? What is this kink-shaming?
Zh: 吓人？嘲笑别人的癖好是怎么回事？

En: To the island of Ecstasy in the carnal Sea of Lust, baby.
Zh: “前往缠绵的欲望之海中那座销魂的洲，宝贝。”

En: Wow... She shakes her head, saddened  somehow. "That is definitely a trek you're gonna take alone. Now if there isn't anything else I'd really like to get this net done."
Zh: “哇哦……”她摇摇头，不知怎么的还有些伤感。“那将会是一场你自己一个人踏上的艰苦旅程。现在，如果没有别的事，我想把渔网先处理好。”

En: Having the lieutenant along is a fail-safe measure against any possible funny-business.
Zh: 警督一起去是一种自动防故障措施，可以防止任何可能的不道德行为的发生。

En: That's exactly what I meant. Incredibly creepy.
Zh: 我说的就是这个意思。极其诡异。

En: You were good. Why did you have to get so creepy all of the sudden? Now it's just questions again.
Zh: 刚才还好好的。为什么突然就搞得那么吓人？现在又只能问问题了。

En: Nah, it's definitely not that. She probably just wasn't ovulating right now. Never mind, back to questions.
Zh: 不，肯定不是那么回事。她可能现在还不在排卵期。没关系的，还是回到问问题上面吧。

En: The tip of the peninsula is bound to have a good view. (Point northwest.) "Let's go there."
Zh: “半岛顶部的风景一定很好。”（指向西北方。）“我们去那边吧。”

En: There's a nice view on the beach I found. (Point northwest.) "Land's End."
Zh: “我在海滩上发现一个风景很好的地方。”（指向西北方。）“地之角。”

En: Alright, I will walk with you. She raises her finger. "But you need to understand that *nothing* is going to happen. We're just walking."
Zh: “好吧，我可以跟你去散步。”她举起手指。“但是你必须理解，*什么*也不会发生。我们只是散散步。”

En: Was that... acceptance? It feels so *normal*. Nourishing even.
Zh: 这是……同意了吗？感觉如此寻常。甚至有些滋润。

En: She doesn't wait for an answer. You better get ready.
Zh: 她没有等待答案。你最好做好准备。

En: Alright. It's a date... sort of.
Zh: “好吧。这是个约会……算是吧。”

En: It's gonna be weird with the three of us. Let's just drop it. (Waive the date.)
Zh: “三个人一起会有点奇怪吧。还是算了吧。”（放弃约会。）

En: Smile and nod.
Zh: 笑着点点头。

En: Fine, she replies with a shrug. "The date is dropped. Now what else can we discuss?"
Zh: “好吧，”她耸耸肩。“约会取消。现在还有什么可以讨论的吗？”

En: I guess I can come along for a quick stroll... The lieutenant looks at you apologetically. "I mean if the ma'am insists, of course."
Zh: “我猜，我可以稍微散散步……”警督略带歉意的看着你。“当然了，我是说如果女士坚持的话。”

En: I do, yes. A stroll on the beach sounds nice, doesn't it? She nods. "Alright. I'll see the kids haven't killed each other and we'll meet at Land's End in... fifteen minutes?"
Zh: “我坚持，是的。去海滩上散步听起来不错，不是吗？”她点点头。“好吧。我去看看孩子们有没有自相残杀，我们直接在地之角见面吧……15分钟后。”

En: I do, yes. A stroll on the beach sounds nice, doesn't it? She nods. "Alright. I'll see the kids haven't killed each other and we'll meet at Land's End in... fifteen minutes?"
Zh: “我坚持，是的。去海滩上散步听起来不错，不是吗？”她点点头。“好吧。我去看看孩子们有没有自相残杀，我们晚上直接在地之角见面吧。等到天黑的时候，好吗？”

En: I'm really sorry.
Zh: 我真的很抱歉。

En: I understand *nothing*.
Zh: “我*什么*也没理解。”

En: Something could happen...
Zh: “可能会发生一些事……”

En: Just a good old fashion walk. I like it.
Zh: “只是老派又美好的散步。我喜欢。”

En: Yes, she replies with a smile. "*Someone* could get pushed in the water. Maybe even get laughed at. But I'm sure that's not gonna happen."
Zh: “是的，”她笑着回复到。“*有人*可能会被推进水里。甚至是被嘲笑。不过我肯定那是不会发生的。”

En: A stroll on the beach sounds nice. She nods. "Alright, I guess I'll be seeing you at Land's End in the evening. When it gets dark, alright?"
Zh: “我坚持，是的。去海滩上散步听起来还不错。”她点点头。“好吧。我们晚上直接在地之角见面吧。等到天黑的时候，好吗？”

En: Alright. I'll go put the kids to bed and we'll meet at Land's End in... fifteen minutes.
Zh: “好吧。我去把孩子们哄上床，我们直接在地之角见面吧……15分钟后。”

En: Charming. My man was like that too. Had to knock two of his teeth out to establish a good relationship dynamic. She chuckles. "I'll see you at Land's End in the evening. Be there after dark, alright?"
Zh: “很可爱。我的男人也喜欢那个，为了建立一种良好的动态关系，必须敲掉他的两颗牙齿。”她轻笑出声。“我们晚上直接在地之角见面吧。等到天黑的时候，好吗？”

En: Charming. My man was like that too. Had to knock two of his teeth out to establish a good relationship dynamic. She chuckles. "I'll see you at Land's End in... let's say fifteen minutes."
Zh: “很可爱。我的男人也喜欢那个，为了建立一种良好的动态关系，必须敲掉他的两颗牙齿。”她轻笑出声。“我们在地之角见面吧……那就15分钟后吧。”

En: Me too, she replies with sudden sincerity. "It's been ages since I just had normal conversation with one of you guys."
Zh: “我也是，”她突然很真诚地回复到。“我已经很多年没跟你们这种人正常地交谈了。”

En: She means men.
Zh: 她的意思是男人。

En: Lilienne, walk with me.
Zh: “莉莉恩，跟我一起散散步。”

En: You made her sad. She actually really wanted to go.
Zh: 你让她感到悲伤。她真的很想去。

En: Why does she care about the waves so much?
Zh: 她为什么那么关心海浪？

En: Nevermind the waves. Let's stay on track here.
Zh: 别管海浪了。我们别跑题了。

En: Aye, officer. A woman in a rain coat stands on the quay, considering an overturned boat. A sword in a scabbard hangs from her hip.
Zh: “你好，警官。”一个穿着雨衣的女性站在码头，打量着一只翻过来的船。她的腰间挂着一把带鞘的剑。

En: Be seeing you. [Leave.]
Zh: “回见。”[离开。]

En: Aye, the sea's gonna calm down soon. I can feel it. The wind is turning southeast. She nods. "What's on your mind, officer?"
Zh: “是啊，大海很快就会平静下来。我能感觉到。风正在转向东南方。”她点点头。“你在想什么呢，警官？”

En: Sure is. *The Sun* I call her. Coated with a fresh layer of tar just yesterday. It'll take some time for it to dry, assuming the sunny days continue.
Zh: “当然。我叫她*太阳*。昨天才刚新刷了一层柏油。如果晴天继续下去的话，还需要一点时间才能变干。”

En: Anything I can help you with?
Zh: “有什么我能帮你的吗？”

En: And go where? The fish are plentiful here and we get enough orders to get by. It's not great, but it's something.
Zh: “去哪里？这里有很多鱼，我们也接到了足够过活的订单。并不是很多，不过也够了。”

En: Unfortunately, the factory sold this one with a three-year warranty instead of a story. She smiles at her own joke. "It's to intimidate folks, mostly."
Zh: “很可惜，工厂卖掉它的时候附带的是三年保修，而不是一个故事。”她被自己的笑话逗乐了。“主要是用来吓唬人的。”

En: It's a regular mass-produced sword, like a shovel or an axe. Nothing fancy, just for intimidation.
Zh: “这只是一把普通的量产剑，就像一把铁锹或者是斧头。没什么特别的，只是用来吓唬人。”

En: Sometimes I also walk the beach to see what the sea has given up. The sea is full of surprises.
Zh: “有时候我也会去海滩边散散步，看看大海都遗弃了什么。大海真是时刻充满惊喜啊。”

En: Wood. Pieces of glass. Every once in a while we see dead bodies -- human, animal, fish, other odd sea creatures. A mine washed ashore, once...
Zh: “木头。玻璃。偶尔还有尸体——人类的，动物的，鱼的，其他海洋生物的。曾经还有个水雷被冲上岸了……”

En: Don't have any, she replies with a shrug. "Those get cleaned out so fast even the local bums can only stand by and watch with watery mouths. Always by strangers too. Union folk don't stand this kind of stuff around here."
Zh: “没有，”她耸耸肩，回复到。“那些东西很快就被清理干净了，就连本地的流浪汉都只能站在一旁，留着口水干巴巴地看着。总是有陌生人。工会那帮家伙可受不了这种东西。”

En: Hold on... do you know how to use it?
Zh: “等一下……你怎么会用的？”

En: Not really. She glances at the blade. "I know some basic moves and I know it sure as hell beats a knife, when you're in a tough spot."
Zh: “不太会。”她瞥了一眼剑身。“我只知道一些基础动作，而且我也能肯定，当你陷入死角的时候，肯定比一把小刀要有用。”

En: From time to time people need a lesson in respect. That's just the way it is. Back in the day I caught the eyes of many men and believe me, she adds, tittering, "men need a lesson in manners from time to time."
Zh: “时不时的，有些人需要上一堂何为尊重的课。就是这么回事。在我的黄金年代里，我吸引过很多男人的目光，相信我，”她窃笑着补充到。“男人时不时都需要上一堂关于尊重的课。”

En: Why don't more women arm themselves if it's so effective?
Zh: “如果这么有效的话，为什么没有更多的女人武装起来呢？”

En: What makes you think we haven't? She smiles. "Heh. The truth is that almost everyone in this life is scared and tired and stupid and too *dull* for that."
Zh: “你为什么觉得我们没有呢？”她笑了。“嗨。事实上，生活中基本上每一个人都很害怕、疲惫、愚蠢，而且太过*迟钝*了。”

En: Behold! (Point to *The Expression* on your face.)
Zh: “看啦！”（指着你脸上的那个*表情*。）

En: Her eyes meet yours and suddenly she starts laughing. It's hoarse. As if she hasn't laughed in a while.
Zh: 你们四目相对，突然间，她干笑了起来。似乎她已经很久没笑了。

En: Sure. Her face straightens. "It looks as if you could face-down any horror in the world with that same unchanging grin. It's like a shield."
Zh: “当然。”她的脸又放松下来。“看起来你可以用同样不变的笑容降服这个世界上的一切恐惧。就像一面盾牌。”

En: Nice sword. (Point at the sabre on her hip.) "Does it come with a story?"
Zh: “剑不错啊。”（指向她腰间的佩剑。）“它有什么故事吗？”

En: What do you do around here?
Zh: “你在这里做什么？”

En: That's my motor carriage in the sea by the way. (Point to the sunken vehicle.)
Zh: “顺便说一句，海里那个是我的汽車。”（指向沉没的汽車。）

En: She needs to go on a date with *another* drunk. Badly.
Zh: 她需要跟*别的*酒鬼去约会。非常需要。

En: Kim's presence makes it awkward.
Zh: 金的存在让一切变得很尴尬。

En: Were honest about documents.
Zh: 对文件很诚实。

En: Reassured her about signing documents.
Zh: 让她放心签署文件。

En: Don't know a good spot yet (EXPLORE THE COAST)
Zh: 还不知道一个好地方（探索海岸）

En: Said you don't get attached.
Zh: 说你没有依赖。

En: Carrying the Amber Die.
Zh: 带着琥珀骰子。

En: Dresscode: Man of charms.
Zh: 着装要求：万人迷。

En: Can I borrow that sword?
Zh: “能把剑借给我吗？”

En: No, I'm afraid not. Her eyes are smiling as her hand moves to the hilt. "Attempting to confiscate the blade I use to keep these animals in check? You would put me in an early grave."
Zh: “不，恐怕不行。”她的手移向剑柄，笑了笑。“想没收我用来控制这些衣冠禽兽的剑吗？你会让我英年早逝的。”

En: Gone.
Zh: “走了。”

En: I've questions. The first is -- what's your name? (Move on.)
Zh: “我有些问题。第一个——你叫什么？”（继续。）

En: As always, I am... the Lawbringer.
Zh: “像往常一样，我是……执法者。”

En: That depends. Where are we exactly?
Zh: “看情况吧。我们到底在哪？”

En: You've graduated to *Lawbringer* now?
Zh: “现在你升级成执法者了？”

En: The name is Lilienne. People call me Net Picker. She gestures toward the fishnets. "I think I have time for questions. What are the other ones?"
Zh: “我的名字叫莉莉恩。大家都叫我拾网人。”她朝着渔网比了个手势。“我想我有时间回答你的问题。还有别的吗？”

En: A fishing village. On the seashore. She looks around. "This place doesn't really have a name. It's sometimes called... *Illisible*."
Zh: “在海滩边的一个渔村。”她四下看了看。“这个地方其实没有真正的名字。有时候被叫做……*不清楚*。”

En: I'm good. [Leave.]
Zh: “没事了。”[离开。]

En: She whistles quietly, picking at the fishnet at her feet. The wind ruffles her hair.
Zh: 她轻轻地吹着口哨，捡起了脚下的渔网。风吹乱了她的头发。

En: Are you now? She smirks. "There are a lot of lawbringers around Martinaise, but not a lot of law."
Zh: “是吗？”她笑了。“马丁内斯有很多执法者，但是法律并不多。”

En: This place looks bad. Why don't you leave?
Zh: “这个地方看起来很糟糕。你为什么不离开呢？”

En: It *is* imposing. (Nod.)
Zh: “它*的确*让人印象深刻。”（点头。）

En: Why do you need intimidation tactics?
Zh: “你为什么需要恐吓战术呢？”

En: Isn't that what guns are for?
Zh: “枪不就是用来做这个的吗？”

En: Guns are expensive... and fragile. I think. She shrugs. "Besides, I got kids. Can't have guns around them and sometimes a sharp blade is enough to keep folks at bay."
Zh: “枪很贵……而且很脆弱。我想是吧。”她耸耸肩。“再说了，我有孩子。不能让枪出现在他们周围，而且有时候，一把锋利的剑就足够让人们远离了。”

En: Figures. Typical patriarchal nonsense, mascu-venomosity.
Zh: “明白了。典型的家长式的鬼话，仇男症。”

En: So where are all the men now? (Proceed.)
Zh: “所以现在那些男人都到哪里去了？”（继续。）

En: Some went to patch their wounds, their lesson learned, others were more thick-headed... She looks down. "And one of them I ended up marrying."
Zh: “有些吸取教训之后，去包扎伤口了，其他的更蠢一些……”她低下头。“最后我跟其中一个结婚了。”

En: Ugh... sure. Venomosity. She nods. "But boys will be boys and god knows we don't have many around. So far the sword has been enough to keep them in line."
Zh: “呃……当然。充满仇恨。”她点点头。“但是小伙子就是小伙子，天知道我们这里可没那么多人。迄今为止，这把剑就足够让他们规规矩矩的了。”

En: Where's your husband now?
Zh: “你的丈夫现在在哪里？”

En: Gone where?
Zh: “去哪里了？”

En: To the waves. Her eyes stop in yours. "The sea took him. It was a long time ago."
Zh: “海浪里。”她的目光停留在你身上。“大海带走了他。很久以前的事了。”

En: I'm a proper man. Believe me.
Zh: “我是个正经人。相信我。”

En: That goes for men too, but they put on an act for us. Pretend like everything's good and living in shit doesn't bother them. Like anyone falls for that.
Zh: “男人也一样，不过他们在女人面前装。明明日子都过不下去了还假装啥都不是问题。真以为所有女人都喜欢这种吗？”

En: Oh... She shakes her head, smiling faintly. "Believe *me*, everyone here is a 'proper' man. Must be something about poverty that makes all the men *real*."
Zh: “哦……”她摇摇头，微微笑了笑。“相信*我*，这里每一个人都是‘正经’人。肯定是贫穷让所有男人变得如此*真实*。”

En: Wait, why? If they're thick-headed...
Zh: “等等，为什么？如果他们很蠢的话……”

En: Guess I enjoyed the way he bled. Her expression doesn't change. It's hard to say if it's a joke.
Zh: “我猜，是因为我喜欢他流血的样子吧。”她的表情没有变化。很难说这到底是不是个笑话。

En: If it is, then why the melancholy?
Zh: 如果是的，那为什么又这么忧郁呢？

En: Gone? Coward! I would never leave anyone.
Zh: “走了？胆小鬼！我永远不会离开任何人。”

En: He disappeared? Sounds like a missing persons case.
Zh: “他消失了？听起来像是一起人口失踪案。”

En: It absolutely does not, the lieutenant quietly interjects. "We are *not* going to look for him."
Zh: “绝对不行，”警督安静地插了一嘴。“我们*不会*去找他的。”

En: No, no. There's nothing to find. He's dead. Lost to the waves.
Zh: “不，不。没什么好找的。他死了。消失在海浪里了。”

En: Uh... She raises an eyebrow. "Good for you. He, however, was lost to the waves. It's been quite a while now."
Zh: “呃……”她扬起眉头。“你这样挺好的。不过，他消失在海浪里了。这是很久以前的事了。”

En: That's bad... What happened?
Zh: “挺糟糕的……发生什么事了？”

En: Oh... Say no more. Wait for her to continue.
Zh: “哦……”不要再说了。等她继续。

En: He died? Was he... murdered?
Zh: “他死了？他是……被谋杀了吗？”

En: The lieutenant shakes his head again.
Zh: 警督又摇了摇头。

En: The harbour seawall endures the wash of the freezing waves. Many things wash up against it and onto the beaches of Revachol. Some inanimate, some just no longer alive... You shudder.
Zh: 港口的栈桥忍耐着冰冷海浪的侵袭。很多东西被冲到了瑞瓦肖的海岸上。有些没有生命，有些失去了生命……你颤抖了起来。

En: She refuses?! But your word is the *law*. *You* are LAW!
Zh: 她拒绝了？！但你的话就是*法律*。*你*就是法律！

En: Like I said, fish, mostly. Sail the waves, take care of the kids. Pick nets... right now I'm tarring a little skiff.
Zh: “就像我说的，大部分跟鱼有关。扬帆出海，照顾孩子。编织鱼网……现在我正在给一艘小艇涂柏油。”

En: What else?
Zh: “还有别的吗？”

En: Is that enough to make a living?
Zh: “这样足够生活吗？”

En: I sell the fish to people in the Delta to serve at their fancy restaurants. Authentic Insulindian cuisine.
Zh: “我把鱼卖给德尔塔的那些高档餐厅。真正的伊苏林迪菜肴。”

En: Interesting. What have you found?
Zh: “有意思。你发现什么了？”

En: I never thought the sea brought in anything particularly interesting.
Zh: “我从没想过会带来什么特别有意思的东西。”

En: Alright, I think I get it. Let me ask you something else.
Zh: “好吧，我想我明白了。让我问你点别的事。”

En: Bottles. Drugs, also. Lost cargo in general. She looks at the beach. "But most of the time, it's just wood and glass."
Zh: 她看着海滩，继续说道：“瓶子。还有毒品。主要是丢失的货物。不过大多数时间，只是木头和玻璃。”

En: I need to know about those human bodies.
Zh: “我想知道那些尸体的事。”

En: Drugs? I need info on this. Major narc. (Point to yourself.)
Zh: “毒品？我需要这方面的信息。我是个缉毒刑警。”（指向自己。）
