En: Well, you're barking under the wrong tree then, officer. She shakes her head. "I have no interest in floaters. Seen enough of them in my life already. A very unattractive bunch."
Zh: “呃，那你找错人了，警官。”她摇摇头。“我对浮尸没什么兴趣。我这辈子已经见得够多了。真的是很让人反感的东西。”

En: A mine? The RCM could use a mine. Where is it?
Zh: “一颗水雷？RCM用得着。在哪里？”

En: Well, the RCM has to wait for another one, cause some army folks came by, took it in the middle of the bay and blew it up. She spits over the railing. "The blast was surprisingly timid for such a huge spiky thing."
Zh: “呃，RCM可能要等下一个了，因为军队的人来过，把它带到海湾中央，然后引爆了。”她朝栏杆外吐了口唾沫。“对于一个巨大的大尖钉样的东西来说，爆炸效果还真是出乎意料的小。”

En: You could use a hit right about now. Any hit really. Feel that itch in your hands, the stiffness of your neck? That strange *restlessness* in your bones?
Zh: 你现在就需要来一剂毒品。随便什么都可以。你感觉手在发痒，脖子僵硬？骨子里那种奇怪的*不安*？

En: Walking on the beach. Sounds quite... romantic.
Zh: “海滩漫步。听起来还挺……浪漫的。”

En: Oh, very. She laughs. "Finding pieces of glass, bits of wood. Every once in a while we get dead bodies -- human, animal, fish, other sea creatures. A mine washed ashore, once..."
Zh: “嗯，非常浪漫。”她笑了。“找玻璃和木头。偶尔还有尸体——人类的，动物的，鱼的，其他怪异海洋生物的。曾经还有个水雷被冲上岸了……”

En: What else? She looks at the beach. "Bottles. Drugs, also. Lost cargo in general. But most of the time, it's just wood and glass. *Very* romantic."
Zh: “还有什么？”她看向海滩。“瓶子。还有毒品。主要是丢失的货物。不过大多数时间，只是木头和玻璃。*非常*浪漫。”

En: Oh... she looks at the pointed direction. "That's good to know, I guess. Why is it in the sea?"
Zh: “哦……”她看着你手指的方向。“我猜，知道这一点挺好的。它为什么会在海里呢？”

En: People imagine picking up and finding *something better* is an easy solution. But how is that supposed to work, when all your time and energy goes to staying alive? You have almost nothing to set aside to actually fight your way out.
Zh: “在大家的想象中，捡起来和发现*更好的东西*是比较简单的解决方案。不过当你把所有的时间和精力都用在维持生命上的时候，这又怎么能行得通呢？你几乎没有什么能放在一边，去真正拼出一条道路。”

En: Anywhere with better opportunities.
Zh: “任何有更好机会的地方。”

En: You just gotta follow the work, that's where the money sleeps.
Zh: “你只能跟着工作走，因为钱就躺在那里。”

En: Just, somewhere... (Close your eyes.) "Away from all this sadness."
Zh: “就是，某个地方……”（闭上双眼。）“远离所有悲伤。”

En: So I take it that's your skiff? (Point at the overturned boat.)
Zh: “所以那就是你的小艇吧？”（指向翻过来的那艘船。）

En: Is that your boat? (Point at the overturned boat.)
Zh: “那是你的船吗？”（指向翻过来的那艘船。）

En: Okay. Enough about this place.
Zh: “好吧。这个地方的话题就到这里。”

En: I understand what you're saying. It could be worse elsewhere.
Zh: “我明白你在说什么。别的地方可能更糟糕。”

En: Oh, but of course -- why didn't I think of that? She rolls her eyes. "Look, I have mouths to feed and as much as I'd like a change, I... they need stability first."
Zh: “哦，当然了——我怎么没想到呢？”她翻了个白眼。“你看，我要养家糊口，尽管我也很想改变，我……首先，她们需要的是稳定。”

En: Exactly. She tries to smile and reaches in her net. "I don't mean to complain about my sad pauper life. We do manage alright. We're tough people here."
Zh: “没错。”她试着挤出一丝微笑，伸手去碰渔网。“我不是有意要抱怨自己悲伤的贫民生活。我们还能应付过去。这里的人坚强。”

En: I don't mean to complain about my pauper life. We are warm and fed here and... She smiles faintly and plucks some seaweed out of the net. "I don't know, there's just something about this shit hole."
Zh: 她微微一笑，从渔网上摘下一些海藻。“我不是有意要抱怨自己悲伤的贫民生活。我们的温饱不成问题，而且……我不知道，总觉得这个鬼地方有点特别的东西。”

En: A sudden gust of cold air blows in from the sea. The waves flow and ebb, around you the fishing village takes its slow and steady breaths... its many wooden structures worn by bitter years of salt and storm.
Zh: 海面突然吹来一阵冰冷的海风。海浪起起伏伏，你周围的渔村缓慢而又沉稳地呼吸着……在盐分和暴风雨多年的侵蚀下，很多木质结构已经磨损了。

En: She's making a joke. Her voice softens.
Zh: 她在开玩笑。她的声音柔和了许多。

En: Why would anyone like it here?
Zh: 为什么会有人喜欢这里呢？

En: It's home.
Zh: 这里是家。

En: Shake off the stupor.
Zh: 摆脱恍惚状态。

En: Even at the worst of times, Revachol cares for its inhabitants.
Zh: 就算是在最糟糕的时刻，瑞瓦肖也很关心它的居民。

En: I'd like to ask something else.
Zh: “我想问点别的事。”

En: Factory balanced... bakelite and stainless steel.
Zh: 普通的工厂工艺……人造树胶和不锈钢。

En: Aye. She looks at the rain circles on the water. "*Sunny* days. You got a problem with that?"
Zh: “是啊。”她看着雨点落在水面上，化成一个个圆圈。“*晴天*。你觉得有什么问题吗？”

En: Sunny days? (Put your hand in the rain.)
Zh: “晴天？”（把你的手伸向雨中。）

En: Sunny days? (Catch a snowflake in your palm.)
Zh: “晴天？”（用掌心接住一片雪花。）

En: Aye. She looks at the snow melt in the water. "*Sunny* days. You got a problem with that?"
Zh: “是啊。”她看着雪花融化在水面上。“*晴天*。你觉得有什么问题吗？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: She looks at the boat dripping with slush and nods, proudly.
Zh: 她看着污泥从船身滴落，骄傲地点点头。

En: No, ma'am. We have no quarrel with sunny days.
Zh: “不，夫人。我们对晴天没有任何意见。”

En: Good. 'Twould have been bad news had it turned out it wasn't a sunny day. Bad news for the skiff. Bad news for the nets. Bad news for the kids.
Zh: “很好。如果最后不是晴天的话，那就是个坏消息了。对于小艇来说是坏消息。渔网也是。孩子们也是。”

En: No, ma'am. No problem.
Zh: “不，夫人。没问题。”

En: But... it's not a sunny day.
Zh: “但是……并不是晴天啊。”

En: Sure it is. 'Twould be bad news for the skiff if it weren't. Bad news for the nets. Bad news for the kids.
Zh: “当然是。如果不是的话，对于小艇来说就是个坏消息了。渔网也是。孩子们也是。”

En: There's a moment's silence. She looks at the slush-like rain-thing melt on the yellow belly of the boat.
Zh: 片刻的沉默。她看着黄色船身上像烂泥一般的水滴在融化。

En: She sounds almost disappointed with you. *Reprimanding* you for falling and hurting your knee.
Zh: 听起来她似乎对你很失望。*谴责*你摔倒，还弄伤了膝盖。

En: I got shot in the foot. It was pretty bad-ass. You would've liked it.
Zh: “我的脚挨了一枪。挺混蛋的。你会喜欢的。”

En: I'm a cop. I don't merely exist in *this* world -- I live in the *between* life and death.
Zh: “我是个警察。我不仅仅存在于*这个*世界——我还生活在生与死*之间*。”

En: Some people hurt me.
Zh: “有些人伤害了我。”

En: I wanted to talk to you about something else -- your boat.
Zh: “我想跟你聊点别的——你的船。”

En: You seem angry. Why?
Zh: “你好像很生气。为什么？”

En: Look at you, you can barely walk!
Zh: “看看你，你都快不能走路了。”

En: You're not *limping*. You're *you*.
Zh: 你没有*一瘸一拐*。你就是*你*。

En: Everyone does that, in a way. You don't have to get shot for that. She waves her hand dismissively.
Zh: “在某种程度上，每个人都一样。你不必因此而挨枪子吧。”她轻蔑地挥挥手。

En: What happened to you anyway? We heard gunshots from the town. They were closer than usual.
Zh: “话说，你这到底是怎么了？”我们听到城里传来的枪声。比平常要近很多。”

En: Officer, what happened? You're limping. Why are you limping? You look terrible.
Zh: “警官，出什么事了？你这一瘸一拐的。怎么回事？你看起来很糟糕。”

En: Officer Harry... you're wounded. Why are you wounded? You look terrible.
Zh: “哈里警官……你这一瘸一拐的。怎么回事？你看起来很糟糕。”

En: Is this from the shooting in town? She's not letting it go that easy. "We heard gunshots. Not that we don't hear gunshots all the time, but they were closer than usual."
Zh: “跟城里的枪声有关吗？”她不会那么容易就放下的。“我们听到了枪声。虽然我们总是会听到枪声，不过这次比往常要更近一些。”

En: There was an exchange of fire on Rue de Saint-Ghislaine. It's nothing to be worried about, ma'am.
Zh: “圣吉莱纳路上发生了交火事件。没什么好担心的，夫人。”

En: Of course, ma'am. It's only for a day or two -- official police business.
Zh: “当然了，夫人。只是一两天而已——警方事务。”

En: That won't be a problem. It's windstill -- and the tar just dried. She points to her skiff next to the jetty. "We've got two days of *relative* sunshine ahead."
Zh: “那没问题。风停了——柏油也刚干。”她指向栈桥旁的小艇。“后面还会出现两个*相对的*晴天。”

En: Can we borrow your boat?
Zh: “能把船借给我们吗？”

En: Can I borrow your boat?
Zh: “能把船借给我吗？”

En: What's on that island? I saw some kind of ruins through the binoculars...
Zh: “那座小岛上有什么？我通过望远镜看到了某种废墟……”

En: What's on that island?
Zh: “那座小岛上有什么？”

En: Thank you, we'll use your skiff to get there then.
Zh: “谢谢，我们会用你的小艇到那边去。”

En: Thank you, I'll use your skiff to get there then.
Zh: “谢谢，我们会用你的小艇到那边去。”

En: We need to get to that island. (Point to it.)
Zh: “我们需要到那座小岛上去。”（指向它。）

En: I need to get to that island. (Point to it.)
Zh: “我需要到那座小岛上去。”（指向它。）

En: I dodged the second shot. I can also get *not shot*.
Zh: “我躲开了第二枪。我也可以*不中枪*的。”

En: I also got shot in the chest. (Point to your chest.)
Zh: “我的胸口也中了一枪。”（指向你的胸口。）

En: You should see the other guys. Two dead, one in the hospital.
Zh: “你应该看看其他人。两个死了，还有一个在医院里。”

En: You should see the other guys. They're all dead.
Zh: “你应该看看其他人。他们全都死了。”

En: I have a question for you. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我有个问题要问你。”（总结。）

En: Well, good for you. She smiles.
Zh: “嗯，你厉害。”她笑了。

En: Officer Harry, what happened? You're limping. Why are you limping? You look terrible.
Zh: “哈里警官，出什么事了？你这一瘸一拐的。怎么回事？你看起来很糟糕。”

En: Officer, what happened? You're wounded. Why are you wounded? You look terrible.
Zh: “警官，出什么事了？你这一瘸一拐的。怎么回事？你看起来很糟糕。”

En: It's nothing.
Zh: “没什么。”

En: Doesn't look like nothing. Looks like you got banged up real good.
Zh: “看起来可不像没什么的样子。一定伤的很重吧。”

En: That's nothing to brag about, officer.
Zh: “没什么值得炫耀的，警官。”

En: It's not serious. He'll be okay.
Zh: “没那么严重。他会没事的。”

En: You feel the ligament move on your left shoulder. And a small, warm drip of blood. It's nothing.
Zh: 你感觉左肩的韧带动了一下。还有一滴温热的血珠。这没什么。

En: So you're a killer. She nods solemnly. "That's good, I guess. I guess... Better than being dead."
Zh: “所以你是个杀手。”她严肃地点点头。“我猜这是好事。我猜……总比死了好。”

En: Most cops are killers.
Zh: “大部分警察都是杀手。”

En: I'm not a killer, I'm a cop.
Zh: “我不是杀手，我是警察。”

En: Scoff in a masculine manner.
Zh: 以男子汉的方式嘲弄。

En: Aye, I bet they are. The good ones at least.
Zh: “是啊，我打赌是吧。至少是好的那些。”

En: Aye, I guess you are. She nods. "I understand that's how it goes."
Zh: “是啊，我猜你是吧。”她点点头。“我理解是怎么一回事。”

En: She just looks at you, her tea-length skirt flapping in the wind.
Zh: 她只是看着你，及踝长的裙子在风中飞舞。

En: Of course. Can I help you with something?
Zh: “当然。我能帮你什么吗？”

En: I was a killer long before this happened.
Zh: “在这一切发生之前，我早就已经是个杀手了。”

En: Aye. She nods solemnly. "Not a lot of RCM men who aren't killers."
Zh: “是啊。”她严肃地点点头。“RCM里没几个不是杀手的。”

En: Aye. She nods solemnly. "I've always taken you for one, that's for sure. Not a lot of RCM men who aren't killers."
Zh: “是啊。”她严肃地点点头。“我一直都觉得你是，这是肯定的。RCM里没几个不是杀手的。”

En: I'm sad to hear that. Take care of that with ether, will you? Don't get too many RCM men around here. Be sad to lose the first one.
Zh: “听到这个我很难过。用乙醚处理一下，好吗？这里RCM的人可不多。如果失去第一个的话，那就太难过了。”

En: If you promise to bring it back. And no scraping the hull -- I just got it nice and yellow. And *no* drinking on the boat. Her eyes narrow. "And no joyriding, either."
Zh: “只要你答应把它还回来就可以。还有，不要刮伤船身——我才刚刚把它刷成了漂亮的黄色。还有，*不要*在船上喝酒。”她眯起眼睛。“而且，也不能到处乱开。”

En: Aye, she nods.
Zh: “是啊，”她点点头。

En: Nod along attentively.
Zh: 礼貌地点点头。

En: What if I wanna rock?
Zh: “如果我想摇滚一点呢？”

En: See, that makes me not want to lend you my skiff. On a boat, *rocking* leads to capsizing. That there is an absolutely one-hundred percent *rock-free* skiff, you got that?
Zh: “你看，这样我就不想把小艇借给你了。在船上*摇滚*的话只会导致翻船。而且这肯定不是一条100%*免疫礁石*的小艇，明白了吗？”

En: The crow's feet disappear from the corners of her eyes, as she smiles at you.
Zh: 当她对你微笑时，她眼角的鱼尾纹消失了。

En: Mhm, used to be some kind of fortification there before the war -- for the communards. An anti-aircraft gun, I think. Bombed to bits in the landing... I haven't been there myself -- always steered clear of it.
Zh: “嗯，战前那里曾经是个堡垒——公社用的。我想好像是有台高射炮，登陆战时候被炸成了碎片……我自己没去过——总是会绕开它走。”

En: Two days of sunshine? I just got a bacterial infection.
Zh: “出了两天太阳？我刚被细菌感染了。”

En: Is there anything I should know about getting there?
Zh: “去那边之前，有什么是我应该知道的吗？”

En: Well -- most of it's sunken. Underwater. That means concrete under water -- cut your boat if you're not careful. Be sure to enter from the south side, water's deep there.
Zh: “呃——那里大部分都沉没了。在水底。这就意味着水下有混凝土——如果不小心的话，可能会割伤船。记得从南边进去，那里的水比较深。”

En: Who *has* been there?
Zh: “谁*去过*那里？”

En: My husband used to drink there. Him and his drinking buddies. Always seemed like a bad place to drink to me. People died there during the landing, you know, my mother told me...
Zh: “我丈夫以前会去那里喝酒。他和他的酒友。在我看来，那里一直都不是适合喝酒的好地方。登陆的时候有人死在了那里，你知道的，母亲跟我说过的……”

En: She looks around. "The kids sometimes go there too, I know they do -- on rafts. I tell them not to but they bring back old bullet casings and such."
Zh: 她四下看了看。“孩子们有时候也会去，我知道的——乘着皮筏过去。我让他们不要去，不过他们会带回来旧弹壳之类的东西。”

En: Which kids?
Zh: “哪些小孩？”

En: The twins. She points to the two kids playing on the concrete yard. "God forbid they bring the girl along on some rickety barge."
Zh: “双胞胎。”她指向在混凝土院子里玩耍的两个小孩。“老天保佑，他们千万别把女孩带到东倒西歪的船上去。”

En: Nothing, just ruins. Used to be some kind of fortification there before the war, for the communards. An anti-aircraft gun, I think. Bombed to bits in the landing... I haven't been there myself -- always steered clear of it.
Zh: “没什么，只有废墟。战前那里曾经是个堡垒，公社用的。我想好像是有台高射炮，登陆战的时候被炸成了碎片……我自己没去过——总是会绕开它走。”

En: Hasn't been there *herself*... who has then?
Zh: *她自己*没有去过……那谁去过？

En: You said you haven't been there *yourself* -- who has then? If not you?
Zh: “你说你*自己*没去过——那除了你还有谁会去呢？”

En: The twins. She points to the two kids playing on the concrete yard. "God forbid they bring the girl along on a rickety barge."
Zh: “双胞胎。”她指向在混凝土院子里玩耍的两个小孩。“老天保佑，他们千万别把女孩带到东倒西歪的船上去。”

En: Which kids?
Zh: “哪些小孩？”

En: Hundreds. Maybe thousands even... She looks around. "The kids sometimes go there too, I know they do. On barges. I tell them not to but they bring back old bullet casings and such."
Zh: “几百个。甚至几千个……”她四下看了看。“孩子们有时候也会去，我知道的。乘着驳船过去。我让他们不要去，不过他们会带回来旧弹壳之类的东西。”

En: My husband used to drink there. Him and his drinking buddies. Always seemed like a bad place to drink to me. People died there during the landing, you know -- my mother told me...
Zh: “我丈夫以前会去那里喝酒。他和他的酒友。在我看来，那里一直都不是适合喝酒的好地方。登陆的时候有人死在了那里，你能明白吧——我妈跟我说过的……”

En: She nods. "Please be conservative with the fuel, will you? I just filled her up, but it's a small tank."
Zh: 她点点头。“请节约一点燃料，好吗？我才刚刚加满，不过那只是个小油箱。”

En: Be careful out there. Sea's calm as death, but still... there are ruins underwater. You can scrape the paint... or worse.
Zh: “去的路上要小心。大海像死亡一样平静，不过……水下有废墟。你可能会刮伤油漆……甚至更糟。”

En: I don't want a funeral. I *hate* death.
Zh: “我可不想要葬礼。我*痛恨*死亡。”

En: Holding a funeral doesn't mean you love death. Don't be childish. You're a police officer...
Zh: “举办葬礼并不意味着你喜欢死亡。别这么孩子气了。你是一个警察……”

En: I don't think they have funerals for motor carriages.
Zh: “我觉得应该没有汽車的葬礼吧。”

En: What an... odd thought. Maybe I should.
Zh: “真是个……奇怪的想法。也许我应该这么做。”

En: And why so? Our things are a part of our life-world. They're made with human sweat and they share human history. We should care about them as we care about humans, to some extent at least.
Zh: “为什么呢？我们的东西就是这个生活世界的一部分。它们是人类的汗水制成的，而且还分享着人类的历史。我们应该像关心人类一样关心它们，至少在某种程度上是这样。”

En: Our things are a part of our life-world. They're made with human sweat and they share human history. We should care about them as we care about humans. To some extent at least, she adds.
Zh: “我们的东西就是这个生活世界的一部分。它们是人类的汗水制成的，而且还分享着人类的历史。我们应该像关心人类一样关心它们。至少在某种程度上是这样，”她补充了一句。

En: Alright, I'm in. But organizing a funeral takes lot of time and effort, doesn't it?
Zh: “好吧，算我一个。不过组织一场葬礼需要很多花费很多时间和精力，不是吗？”

En: I don't get attached. Definitely not to things.
Zh: “我没有依赖。绝对没有依赖任何东西。”

En: She shrugs. "If you say so."
Zh: 她耸耸肩。“随你怎么说吧。”

En: Oh yeah, she says with a chuckle. "You won't even be able to get it out of the water before early June and where are you gonna bury it? Who to invite? What music to play at the wake?"
Zh: “是的，”她轻笑着说道。“在六月初之前，你甚至都没办法把它从水里捞出来的，而且你打算把它埋在哪里呢？邀请谁参加？守灵的时候播放什么音乐？”

En: If you haven't been there yourself, who has?
Zh: “如果你自己没去过，那谁去过？”

En: Men with authority have their quirks. Comes with never being second-guessed about anything... She waves you off.
Zh: “有威信的人都有自己的怪癖。永远不会被事后批评……”她朝你摆摆手。

En: I guess men with authority have their quirks. She waves you off. "What brings you here, Lawbringer?"
Zh: “我猜有威信的人都有自己的怪癖吧。”她朝你挥挥手。“你来这里做什么，执法者？”

En: So what brings you here, Lawbringer?
Zh: “所以你来这里做什么，执法者？”

En: The sign on the street leading here is *illegible*. Has been since they built this place. The wind rattles her earrings.
Zh: “街上通往这里的标志很*难以辨认*。从他们建了这个地方开始就是如此。”风吹响了她的耳环。

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: The name is Lilienne. People call me Net Picker. She gestures toward the fishnets. "I think I have time for questions. And that was actually the second one."
Zh: “我的名字叫莉莉恩。大家都叫我拾网人。”她朝着渔网比了个手势。“我想我有时间回答你的问题。而且，其实这已经是第二个了。”

En: I see you haven't left our little Martinaise yet. Still running around, she observes. "Like some kind of cross-country law official."
Zh: “看来你还没有离开我们的小马丁内斯。还在附近晃荡，”她打量着你。“看起来像是某种越野执法人员。”

En: The waves are beginning to die down... look at those little bastards. Simmer down. Simmer down, bastards.
Zh: “海浪开始平静下来了……看看那些小杂种。冷静下来。冷静下来，杂种。”

En: What is it with you and those waves?
Zh: “你跟那些海浪之间是怎么回事？”

En: What is it with waves and fishermen? She tilts her head and looks at the sea. "We need to be out there, with them. Fishing, making a living. So I ask them to accommodate me."
Zh: “海浪和渔民之间是怎么回事？”她歪着脑袋，看向大海。“我们需要出海，跟它们一起。打渔，挣钱生活。所以我让它们适应我。”

En: I still have a case to solve.
Zh: “我还有案子要破。”

En: I have some loose ends to tie up.
Zh: “我还有些未知的线索没有查明。”

En: This place is kinda growing on me, you know.
Zh: “你知道吗，我越来越喜欢这个地方了。”

En: Aye, but don't let it grow too much... this is not the place to settle down. Now, what's on your mind, Officer Harry?
Zh: “是啊，不过别太喜欢……这可不是个适合定居的地方。现在，你在想什么呢，哈里警官？”

En: Aye, I guess you do. What's on your mind, Officer Harry?
Zh: “是啊，我猜是的。你在想什么呢，哈里警官？”

En: Aye, I guess you do. What's on your mind, officer?
Zh: “是啊，我猜是的。你在想什么呢，警官？”

En: Aye, but don't let it grow too much... this is not the place to settle down. Now, what's on your mind, officer?
Zh: “是啊，不过别太喜欢……这可不是个适合定居的地方。现在，你在想什么呢，警官？”

En: Were you? Her eyes dart back and forth on the paper. "Hmm... this says by signing I agree to living with construction noise. What exactly is the Union building?"
Zh: “是吗？”她的眼睛在纸上来回扫视着。“嗯……这上面说，如果签名就代表同意生活在建筑噪音里。工会到底想建什么？”

En: I was asked to get your signature. (Hand her Evrart's envelope.)
Zh: “有人要我来找你要签名。”（把艾弗拉特的信封递给她。）

En: What a *nice* idea. Wouldn't have thought that... Her voice trails off.
Zh: “真是个*好*主意。真没想到……”她的声音越变越小。

En: Like I said, Evrart's planning to turn some of the village into a youth centre.\n
Zh: “我说过了，艾弗拉特打算把村子变成一个青少年活动中心。”

En: Evrart's planning to turn some of the village into a youth centre.
Zh: “艾弗拉特打算把村子变成一个青少年活动中心。”

En: On second thought, don't sign the papers.
Zh: “仔细一想，你还是别签了。”

En: Here, use my pen. (Give her your pen.)
Zh: “给你，用我的笔吧。”（把你的笔递给她。）

En: So, will you sign it?
Zh: “所以，你会签吗？”

En: She thinks in silence, glances at the sea and finally speaks: "Aye, why not?"
Zh: 她沉默地想了想，瞥了一眼大海，最后开了口：“好吧，为什么不呢？”

En: Aye, if you say so. She plucks on her net. "Probably better that way. I mean -- who likes construction noise?"
Zh: “好吧，如果你这么说的话。”她拉扯着渔网。“可能这样更好吧。我是说——谁会喜欢建筑噪音呢？”

En: He has a pen and everything... She takes it from you, a doubtful look in her eyes, clicks it and places the tip on the paper.
Zh: “他有笔，还有一切……”她接过笔，眼中满是怀疑，接着咔哒一声，把笔尖放在了纸上。

En: Thought what exactly?
Zh: “没想到什么？”

En: That Evrart and the Union have nice plans for anything. I thought they only cared about themselves. She shakes her head. "Well, I guess Union members have children too."
Zh: “艾弗拉特和工会对任何事情都有很好的计划。我以为他们只在乎自己。”她摇摇头。“好吧，我猜工会成员也有小孩吧。”

En: Fine. The pen clicks. "Here you go."
Zh: 她拿出笔，在上面点了几下，然后又瞥了一眼文件。“好吧，给你吧，”她一边说，一边在纸上签下自己的名字，然后交还给了你。

En: Here you go.
Zh: 她又看了一眼文件，摇摇头，接着在上面签下自己的名字，交还给了你。“给你吧。”

En: Here you go.
Zh: 她又看了一眼文件，摇摇头，接着在上面签下自己的名字，交还给了你。“给你吧。”

En: Fine. The pen clicks. "Here you go."
Zh: 她拿出笔，在上面点了几下，然后又瞥了一眼文件。“好吧，给你吧，”她一边说，一边在纸上签下自己的名字，然后交还给了你。

En: I changed my mind. I would like you to sign the documents. (Hand her the envelope.)
Zh: “我改变主意了。我想让你签署这份文件。”（把信封交给她。）

En: She pulls out the papers. "Evrart and his plan for the village. I guess it does sound nice enough..."
Zh: 她抽出文件。“艾弗拉特和他的村庄计划。听起来就够可以的……”

En: There's a moment's silence. She looks at the slushy snow-melt on the yellow belly of the boat.
Zh: 沉默了一阵。她看着黄色船身上雪在融化。

En: There's a moment's silence. She looks at the rain streaming down the yellow belly of the boat.
Zh: 沉默了一阵。她看着雨点从黄色船身上流淌而下。

En: I'm just trying to help out a stranger in need.
Zh: “我只是想帮一个需要帮助的陌生人。”

En:
I'm collecting evidence to charge him with petty theft.	“我正在收集证据，要控告他小偷小摸。”
0x0100007B00001BEE	Dialogue Text	VILLAGE / NETPICKER	You	I don't really know why I do things. Everything just *happens* to me."
Zh: “我真不知道自己为什么会这么做。一切事情就这么*发生*在我身上了。”

En: That's sweet of you. It really is. I'd check around the abandoned fish market on the boardwalk. Drunks are inexplicably drawn to markets... might be why they have such trouble staying in business...
Zh: “你人真好。的确如此。要是我，会去木板道的废弃鱼市看看。酒鬼总是莫名其妙地会被市场吸引……也许就是因为这样，他们才难以维持经营……”

En: Johnny what now? She's trying to resist a smile. "That's not proper cop behaviour, is it?"
Zh: “什么游戏？”她努力忍住不笑。“这可不是恰当的警察行为啊，不是吗？”

En: Not the best use of your time, is it, Mr. Lawman? Harassin' homeless people on the beach and the like...
Zh: “这可不是利用你时间的最好方式，不是吗，警官？骚扰无家可归的失业者之类的……”

En: You think *that's* bad? Wait till you hear about *civil asset forfeiture*!
Zh: “你觉得*那个*就叫糟糕？等你听到*公民财产没收法*再说吧！”

En: I've done worse.
Zh: “我做过更糟糕的事。”

En: I am the law.
Zh: “我就是法律。”

En: I'm sure you have, she replies with a peculiar look. "I don't have to hear about it, but I do wish you luck finding the thing. Look near the fish market north of here, on the boardwalk. Drunks are *drawn* to markets."
Zh: “我肯定你做过，”她用一种奇怪的表情回复到。“不用说给我听，不过我还是祝愿你能找到它。去北边的鱼市看看吧，在木板道上。酒鬼很容易被市场*吸引*。”

En: Sure, sure... that makes it alright. She rolls her eyes, but then suddenly a smile appears. "Still, I do hope you find it. Look near the fish market north of here, on the boardwalk. Drunks are *drawn* to markets."
Zh: “当然，当然了……那样就好。”她翻了个白眼，然后突然笑了起来。“不过，我还是希望你能找到它。去北边的鱼市看看吧，在木板道上。酒鬼很容易被市场*吸引*。”

En: If one of 'em lost something, that's as good a place to start looking as any.
Zh: “如果其中有人弄丢东西的话，那里就是个开始寻找的好地方。”

En: A phenomenon that the spectral hand theory of the market fails to account for.
Zh: 这是市场那套‘看不见的手’的理论都无法解释的一种现象。

En: Goddamnit... I *just* bought that net...
Zh: “该死……我才*刚刚*买下这张渔网……”

En: It's yellowfin season soon.
Zh: “捕捞黄鳍金枪鱼的季节就快到了。”

En: Huh... haven't seen the RCM around for ages.
Zh: “呃……已经好多年没见过RCM的人了。”

En: Thought you guys forgot this place even exists.
Zh: “还以为你们这些家伙早就忘了这个地方的存在。”

En: It's kinda nice seeing cops around...
Zh: “在这里看到警察其实还挺好的……”

En: I really need to get out there, catch something.
Zh: “我真的应该出海去抓点什么。”

En: Hey, kids! Oh... never mind.
Zh: “喂，孩子们！呃……没事了。”

En: The throw-nets just aren't paying off this time of the year...
Zh: “每年到了这个时候，抛网都没什么收获……”

En: Did you? He adjusts his tie. "Or did you literally not recognize my face? We've been partners for how long, Harry? Don't answer that -- you don't *remember*."
Zh: “真的吗？”他调整了一下领带。“或者你是真的认不出我的脸了？我们搭档多久了，哈里？不用回答——你不*记得*了。”

En: Pig's lying. He's clearly got something real fucked up going on up in here. He twists his index finger at his temple.
Zh: “猪猡在撒谎。很明显，他这里肯定有很多乱七八糟的东西。”他用食指拧了一把太阳穴。

En: Got that right, kid -- he's a psychopath, and he made up this whole amnesia story to fuck with us.
Zh: “说对了，孩子——他是个精神病患者，而且还编了一整套健忘症的鬼话来欺骗我们。”

En: Aw, c'mon, you gotta cut him some slack. Pig's been working hard. Digging through the guts of corpses and shit. Getting shot and shit.
Zh: “哎呀，拜托，你得放他一马。猪猡一直很努力在工作。翻遍了尸体的内脏什么的。还中枪了什么的。”

En: Shitkid. What an interesting moniker.
Zh: 臭小子。多么有趣的一个绰号啊。

En: The line is from Lu Jiatun's 'Mirova 82', isn't it? About Girl Child Communism -- the titular *returning* character. The ghostly apparition of... He looks around and, noticing the impatience of his companions, stops himself. "Good choice, Harry."
Zh: “这句台词是出自卢·贾图恩的‘米诺瓦82’，对吗？关于纯真康米主义——有名无实的*回归*角色。幽灵的幻影……”他四下看了看，注意到同伴不耐烦的样子，于是停了下来。“很棒的选择，哈里。”

En: Judging by the familiarity you feel toward him -- two years minimum? Or maybe a short, but close stint on the task force...
Zh: 从你对他的熟悉程度判断——最少有两年了？或者更短一些，不过特别小组的工作时间很紧张……

En: Absolutely no idea. A hundred years?
Zh: 完全不知道。一百年？

En: He's right. Don't start guessing. Now's not a good time.
Zh: 他说得对。别猜了。现在不是好时候。

En: Had *what* coming? Not recognizing people you work with *every* day? He squints. "Brain damage?"
Zh: “*什么*报应？没有认出*每天*跟你一起工作的人？”他眯起眼睛。“大脑损伤？”

En: A disguise... why?
Zh: “一个伪装……为什么？”

En: The 41st isn't... He trails off, not wishing to finish the sentence.
Zh: “41分局没有……”他拖长了音节，不想把话说完。

En: Where have *we* been? We've been fucking off, as far as I remember. He crosses his arms.
Zh: “*我们*去哪了？在我的记忆里，你不是要我们滚蛋吗。”他抱起双臂。

En: He *has* hinted at the possibility before. I have trouble believing him, of course, but he seems to want to convince himself of it. And I've seen him work. His methods are... unorthodox.
Zh: “他之前就*已经*暗示过这种可能性。当然，我很难相信他，但他似乎很想说服自己相信这一点。而且我见过他工作的样子。他的方法很……不传统。”

En: The two cases... in your ledger. The Unsolvable Case and the Next World Mural. Those were recent.
Zh: 两起案件……在你的手册里。无解案件和来世壁画。那些是最近的。

En: Those cases were hard on you...
Zh: 那些案子对你来说很难……

En: You may have accidentally admitted to losing it. Just now.
Zh: 你可能不小心承认把它弄丢了。就在刚才。

En: Then you'll be fired. This is a hearing -- toughen up.
Zh: 那你肯定会被开除。会有一场听证会——坚强一点。

En: We've read the reports. Lieutenant Kitsuragi's. We know.
Zh: “我们看过报告了。曷城警督的报告。我们知道的。”

En: We've read the reports, Harry. Lieutenant Kitsuragi's. We know.
Zh: “我们看过报告了，哈里。曷城警督的报告。我们知道的。”

En: We've read the reports, Harry. Lieutenant Kitsuragi's. We know.
Zh: “我们看过报告了，哈里。曷城警督的报告。我们知道的。”

En: We've read the reports, Harry. Lieutenant Kitsuragi's. We know.
Zh: “我们看过报告了，哈里。曷城警督的报告。我们知道的。”

En: We've read the reports, Harry. Lieutenant Kitsuragi's. We know.
Zh: “我们看过报告了，哈里。曷城警督的报告。我们知道的。”

En: We've read the reports, Harry. Lieutenant Kitsuragi's. We know.
Zh: “我们看过报告了，哈里。曷城警督的报告。我们知道的。”

En: We've read the reports, Harry. Lieutenant Kitsuragi's. We know.
Zh: “我们看过报告了，哈里。曷城警督的报告。我们知道的。”

En: We've read the reports, Harry. Lieutenant Kitsuragi's. We know.
Zh: “我们看过报告了，哈里。曷城警督的报告。我们知道的。”

En: We've read the reports, Harry. Lieutenant Kitsuragi's. We know.
Zh: “我们看过报告了，哈里。曷城警督的报告。我们知道的。”

En: We've read the reports, Harry. Lieutenant Kitsuragi's. We know.
Zh: “我们看过报告了，哈里。曷城警督的报告。我们知道的。”

En: We've read the reports. Lieutenant Kitsuragi's. We know.
Zh: “我们看过报告了。曷城警督的报告。我们知道的。”

En: We've read the reports. Lieutenant Kitsuragi's. We know.
Zh: “我们看过报告了。曷城警督的报告。我们知道的。”

En: He's still *dwelling* on that jacket.
Zh: 他还在*惦记*那件夹克。

En: Sylvie's a girl I hooked Garte up with. I did that *and* saved his establishment!
Zh: “塞尔维是我帮加尔特勾搭上的一个女孩。不只这样，我*还*拯救了他的公司！”

En: Still caught up in this crossfire, too...
Zh: 还是被卷入火力之中了……

En: If you investigated further, you -- or Kim -- would have more to say. But you didn't, so here we are.
Zh: 如果你能调查地深入一些，你——或者金——会有更多话说。但是你没有，所以才会这样。

En: If you investigated further, you would have more to say. But you didn't. So here we are.
Zh: 如果你能调查地深入一些，你会有更多话说。但是你没有。所以才会这样。

En: Leadership -- now.
Zh: 领导能力——现在。

En: Ouch, you strained your elbow trying to catch that stupid thing... dammit.
Zh: 哎哟，你试着去抓住那个愚蠢的东西，结果扯到胳膊肘了……见鬼。

En: Precisely, Satellite-Officer Vicquemare. It's Martinaise.
Zh: 完全正确，随迁警官维克玛。这是马丁内斯。

En: Yeah, I don't have that. I don't have my sidearm. (Lie.)
Zh: “是啊，我没有。我没有配枪。”（撒谎。）

En: Maybe you've *deserved* it?
Zh: 也许这是你*应得*的？

En: You *know* what she means by 'friends'. But ask, just to be sure.
Zh: 你*知道*她说的‘朋友’是什么意思。不过为了肯定，还是问问吧。

En: No idea *what* you're smelling. I washed death off me.
Zh: “不知道你闻到的是*什么*。我把身上尸体的味道都洗掉了。”

En: Hold on, I just haven't washed myself after the autopsy...
Zh: “等等，尸检之后我还没洗过澡……”

En: Without a reply the satellite-officer covers his nose.
Zh: 随迁警官没有回应，只是捂住了鼻子。

En: The man doesn't reply, but his expression speaks for him.
Zh: 男人没有回应，不过他的表情已经说明了一切。

En: 'You should've done a better job' is what it's saying.
Zh: 意思是说‘你应该做得更好’。

En: I may have pawned it... anyway, it's gone now.
Zh: “我也许是当掉了……不管怎么说，现在它不在了。”

En: Yeah, I checked the local pawnshop. You sold the pawnbroker a Villiers 9mm pistol -- to pay for booze!
Zh: “是啊，我去本地当铺查过了。你把维利耶9毫米手枪卖给了当铺老板——为了换酒钱！”

En: Harry recruited
Zh: 已招募哈里

En: Harry left behind
Zh: 哈里留了下来

En: Kim recruited
Zh: 已招募金

En: Wait, what smell?! I didn't even get close to the damn corpse and I *still* solved the case. That's how good I am.
Zh: “等等，什么味道？！我根本都没接近那具该死的尸体，而且我*还*在继续破案呢。我就有这么出色。”

En: He's wounded. He looks at you. "It's been a long week."
Zh: “他受伤了。”他看着你。“这真是漫长的一周。”

En: From the way he covers his nose you can tell the satellite-officer is still fixated on the smell and not your incredible achievement.
Zh: 从他捂鼻子的样子来看，你可以看出随迁警官还专注在味道上面，而不是你惊人的成就。

En: I'm truly sorry, Officer Minot.
Zh: “我真的很抱歉，迈诺特警官。”

En: Friends with... benefits?
Zh: “朋友……炮友那种？”

En: No. She shakes her head. "Without *any* benefits."
Zh: “不是。”她摇摇头。“没有*那层*关系。”

En: We've come to scrape what's left of you off the pavement.
Zh: “我们是来这里把你的残渣从马路上铲回去的。”

En: The kid's about to say he's Cuno!
Zh: 这个孩子打算说自己是坤诺！

En: The armour? No one else seems bothered by it.
Zh: “盔甲？好像没有其他人为它困扰了。”

En: Bothered by it? Harry, you're a *goddamn* cop! They're afraid of you!
Zh: “为它困扰？哈里，你*他妈的*是个警察！他们都怕你！”

En: Vic, calm down...
Zh: “维克，冷静点……”

En: Hello, I'm Trant Heidelstam. I believe we've met on several occasions.
Zh: “你好，我是特兰特·海德斯塔姆。我想我们已经见过好几次了。”

En: Who are you people?
Zh: “你们是谁？”

En: I'm your goddamn *partner* Jean Vicquemare, and this is *your* special task force, he says, gesturing toward his companions. "Or what's left of it."
Zh: “我他妈是你的*搭档*让·维克玛，这是*你的*特别任务小组，”他一边说，一边朝着他的同伴比了个手势。“或者说，小组的残部。”

En: Hi.
Harry... you're bleeding all over the place. You're half dead.
Zh: “哈里……你到处都在流血。你已经半死不活了。”

En: You look like a fucking clown, Harry. *Not* like a funny police officer, but like a real-life, full-time, pie-in-the-face, unicycle-riding circus employee.
Zh: “你看起来就像个该死的小丑，哈里。*不像*一个可笑的警察，而是一个现实的、全职的、脸上糊了个馅饼、还骑着独轮車的马戏团雇员。”

En: What does that say on your back? 'Fuck the World'? So you've turned into some kind of... nihilistic rock-and-roll *world ender*?
Zh: “你背后这写的是什么？”他眯起眼睛。“花花世界我一人干翻？所以你变成了某种……虚无主义的摇滚*世界终结者*？”

En: What is that, FALN AC? What's with all the *performance gear*? Are you back in high school?
Zh: “那是什么，FALNAC？这套*运动装备*是怎么回事？你又回到高中时代了吗？”

En: What does that say on your back... He squints. "'Pissf****ts'... what the *fuck* is wrong with you?!"
Zh: “你的背后写的是什么？”他眯起眼睛。“弱水三千只取一尿饮……你*他妈的*有什么毛病吗？！”

En: I wanna call you a teapot, but I'm honestly kind of impressed... no idea where you got all that gear -- but there's no doubt in my mind that some bad, bad people are looking for it...
Zh: “我本来想说你只是个小人物罢了，不过说实在的，你让我记住你了……不知道你从哪里搞来这些装备的——不过我可以肯定的说，有些很坏、很坏的家伙正在寻找它……”

En: Also, you look like a fucking idiot.
Zh: “而且，你他妈看起来像个白痴一样。”

En: Uhm... He takes a step back. "I don't *quite* know what I'm doing here. I was asked to participate as an expert -- I think I need to manage your expectations a little. I'm at best an enthusiast in cognitive science."
Zh: “呃……”他后退一步。“我*不太*清楚自己到底在这里做什么。我被邀请以专家的身份加入——我想我需要压低一下你们的期望。我充其量就是个认知科学的爱好者。”

En: What does *he* have to say for *himself*? He left you to catch the bullets!
Zh: *他*能为*自己*辩护些什么？他留下你自己一个人去挡子弹了！

En: Way to feed me to the wolves...
Zh: “把我留下来喂狼……”

En: Task force. Cool. I'm the Cuno. We just came from the fucking island. He points to the seafort behind him.
Zh: “特别小组。酷毙了。我是坤诺。我们刚从那座该死的小岛回来。”他指向背后的海堡。

En: Lieutenant Kim Kitsuragi, Precinct 57. We've just come from the island... He points to the seafort. "Where our investigation led us."
Zh: “我是曷城警督，57分局的。我们刚从那座小岛回来……”他指向背后的海堡。“我们的调查结果指向了那里。”

En: It's good to meet you, Lieutenant Kitsuragi, she says warmly, flashing the lieutenant the tiniest of smiles.
Zh: “很高兴见到你，曷城警督，”她热情地说道，对警督微微一笑。

En: Thank you, lieutenant.
Zh: “谢谢你，警督。”

En: Wait... (Elbow him in the ribs.)
Zh: “等等……”（用胳膊肘撞向他的肋骨。）

En: Let him.
Zh: 让他这样做。

En: The scene is making even him feel as though he has to justify your actions.
Zh: 这个场景甚至让他觉得要为你的行为辩护。

En: We might need your help with something later, he adds, suddenly regaining his confidence.
Zh: “之后可能还会需要你们的协助，”突然找回自信的他补充了一句。

En: But this is clearly a *departmental matter*, so I'm  going to leave you to discuss it among yourselves.
Zh: “不过，这显然是你们的*部门事务*，所以我还是回避一下，让你们自己讨论吧。”

En: Letting the lieutenant know *he* shouldn't feel embarrassed over the shitstorm that's about to befall you.
Zh: 让警督知道*他*不应该为即将降临到你身上的激烈争执感到尴尬。

En: Listen... Cuno. The man assesses the kid like a strike team leader about to kick in a door.
Zh: “听着……坤诺。”男人打量着男孩，就像一个打算破门而入的突击队长。

En: Harrier and I are old friends... from way back. Mind letting us have a moment?
Zh: “哈里尔和我是老朋友了……很久以前就是。介意我们单独待一会儿吗？”

En: Won't be long. I just need to talk to Harry about some of the things I think he's done wrong lately.
Zh: “不会太久的。只是跟哈里聊聊我觉得他最近做过的一些错事。”

En: Good luck with that. He turns to you. "Sounds like you're in some shit."
Zh: “那就祝你好运了。”他转向你。“听起来你有麻烦了。”

En: My background is in something else entirely. I engage in *neurology*... -- he makes air quotes -- "... on a merely theoretical level. In fact, I should probably get going..."
Zh: “我的背景完全不同。我从事的是*神经病学*研究……”——他在空中比了个引号——“……不过仅仅是在理论层次的。事实上，我可能该走了……”

En: Harry, we want to help you. Trant, I believe this is where you come in?
Zh: “哈里，我们想帮你。特兰特，我想现在该你出手了？”

En: None of this is ringing any bells... (Move on.)
Zh: “我什么都没想起来……”（继续。）

En: You aren't the Man with Sunglasses at all... you're not even blond!
Zh: “你根本不是那个墨镜男……而且你都不是金发！”

En: You! You never told me you're not the Horse-Faced Woman! (Point at her.)
Zh: “是你！你从来没有告诉过我，你不是那个马脸女人！”（指着她。）

En: So *Trant Heidelstam* turns out to be... *Special Consultant Trant Heidelstam*...
Zh: “所以*特兰特·海德斯塔姆*原来是……*特别顾问特兰特·海德斯塔姆*……”

En: You mentioned a 'task force'?
Zh: “你提到了什么‘特别行动小组’？”

En: Guilty as charged. He exchanges a look with the special consultant. "I heard you'd lost your mind *and* your memory. I wanted to see if it was true."
Zh: “罪名成立。”他跟特别顾问交换了一个眼神。“我听说你疯了，*而且*还失忆了。我想看看是不是真的。”

En: He was too sarcastic for you to realize who he was.
Zh: 他太爱挖苦人了，你没明白他到底是谁。

En: I'm clinically depressed, Harry. Sorry if I wasn't in the mood to butter you up after you told us to fuck off.
Zh: “我被诊断出抑郁症了，哈里。你叫我们都滚蛋之后，很抱歉我当时没那个心情奉承你。”

En: Maybe if you hadn't been so *sarcastic*, I would have realized I knew you.
Zh: “也许如果你没有那么爱*挖苦*人的话，我可能会意识到自己认识你。”

En: Actually, I suspected something was off.
Zh: “其实，我怀疑有些不太对劲。”

En: I don't like being lied to!
Zh: “我不喜欢被骗！”

En: Okay. I had that coming.
Zh: “好吧。这是我的报应。”

En: I didn't *lie* to you -- no one *lies* to you. You were so fucked up on booze you couldn't recognize your own partner.
Zh: “我没有*骗*你——没有人*骗*你。你的脑子被酒精搞得乱七八糟，连自己的搭档都认不出来了。”

En: Lieutenant, she says with a heavy sigh. "My name is not 'Horse-Faced Woman'. It's Judit Minot. I was assigned to your unit two months ago. I thought we were friends..."
Zh: “警督，”她重重地叹了一口气。“我的名字不叫‘马脸女人’。我是茱蒂特·迈诺特。两个月前被分配到你的小队。我还以为我们是朋友……”

En: Patrol Officer Minot, glad to make the acquaintance. Let's put the past behind us.
Zh: “巡警迈诺特，很高兴认识你。让我们把过去抛在脑后吧。”

En: Right, sir. Her tone is suddenly very cold.
Zh: “好吧，先生。”她的口气突然变得非常冷淡。

En: Yes, I'm Trant Heidelstam. I never said I wasn't Trant Heidelstam.
Zh: “是的，我是特兰特·海德斯塔姆。我从没说过自己不是特兰特·海德斯塔姆。”

En: I wouldn't... quite say that. But, yes, he is displaying aggression not atypical of late-stage alcoholism.
Zh: “我不会……那么说的。不过，是的，他表现出的攻击性不是晚期酒精中毒的非典型症状。”

En: I told you. He's one drink away from killing someone.
Zh: “我告诉过你。他只要再喝一杯就会杀人了。”

En: His son? What a joke... *Everyone* is lying to you!
Zh: 他的儿子？真是个笑话……*所有人*都在骗你！

En: Oh yeah?! Where the fuck is your *son* now, *special consultant*?!
Zh: “哦，是吗？！那现在你的*儿子*去哪里了？*特别顾问*？！”

En: I sent him home, he responds calmly. "Mikael is with his mother. We share custody."
Zh: “我把他送回家了，”他平静地回应到。“米克尔跟他妈妈在一起。我们共享监护权。”

En: Mikael? Mikael's my son.
Zh: “米克尔？米克尔是我的儿子。”

En: Wait, what was up with the kid then?
Zh: “等等，那个孩子是怎么回事？”

En: No problem.
Zh: “没关系。”

En: Oh... okay. Sorry.
Zh: “哦……好吧。抱歉。”

En: So what are you *special consulting* here?
Zh: “所以你在这里*特别顾问*什么呢？”

En: What indeed? He looks at the dilapidated shacks, then you. "I was asked to share my take on some of the more *obscure* theories developed in Königstein in the Thirties. Like -- partial psychotraumatic amnesia, group personality theory..."
Zh: “确实，什么呢？”他看了看破败的棚屋，然后看了看你。“他们要求我分享一下对某种比较*晦涩*的理论的看法，那是30年代在柯尼希斯萌发出来的。比如说——部分精神创伤性失忆症，群体人格理论……”

En: Okay, you're Trant Heidelstam. Special Consultant. I get it.
Zh: “好吧。你是特兰特·海德斯塔姆。特别顾问。我明白了。”

En: Again... He looks around. "I was asked to share my take on some of the more *fringe academic* theories developed in Königstein in the Thirties. For example -- partial psychotraumatic amnesia, group personality theory..."
Zh: “重申一遍……”他四下看了看。“他们要求我分享一下对某种比较*边缘的学术理论*的看法，那是30年代在柯尼希斯萌发出来的。比如说——部分精神创伤性失忆症，群体人格理论……”

En: See? He really *doesn't* remember. It wasn't a 'sick joke' -- at least that's good.
Zh: “看见了吧。他真的*不记得*了。这不是什么‘恶作剧’——至少这一点是好的。”

En: What's a shitkid?
Zh: “臭小子是什么？”

En: You. *Shitkid* -- that's you.
Zh: “你。*臭小子*——就是你。”

En: Despite *all* that you've done -- the deserter, the phasmid, the case?!
Zh: 尽管你做了*所有*那些事情——那个逃兵，竹节虫，还有这起案子，还要这么叫我吗？！

En: Despite *all* that I've done?
Zh: “尽管我做了*所有*那些事情，还要这么叫我吗？”

En: Better not to piss him off by arguing.
Zh: 最好不要跟他争辩来惹他生气。

En: No -- *because* of all that you've done.
Zh: “不——就是*因为*你做的所有那些事情。”

En: Good idea. It doesn't look like he thinks you're in a position to correct him on the shitkid issue.
Zh: 好主意。看来他不认为你能在臭小子的问题上纠正他。

En: Now nothing. Now we're just going to stand here.
Zh: “现在什么都不做。我们就在这里站着。”

En: Really?
Zh: “真的吗？”

En: No -- now we discuss THAT. He points to the water. "What the fuck did you do to our motor carriage?! Why is it *there*, Harry?"
Zh: “不——现在我们要讨论那个。”他指向水面。“你他妈对我们的汽車做了什么？它为什么在*那里*，哈里？”

En: And it was. Good work, Harry. You're insane now. There's one less person for me -- and everyone else -- to rely on.
Zh: “的确。做得好，哈里。现在你真的疯了。对于我——还有其他所有人来说——又少了一个人可以依靠。”

En: Did you? Or did you think I was a god damn FIREFIGHTER? We've been partners for how long, Harry? He adjusts his tie. "Don't answer that -- you don't *remember*."
Zh: “有吗？还是你以为我他妈是个消防员？我们已经搭档多久了，哈里？”他调整了一下脖子上的领带。“不用回答——反正你也不*记得*了。”

En: Did you? Or did you think I was a god damn MALE NURSE? We've been partners for how long, Harry? He adjusts his tie. "Don't answer that -- you don't *remember*."
Zh: “有吗？还是你以为我他妈是个男护士？我们已经搭档多久了，哈里？”他调整了一下脖子上的领带。“不用回答——反正你也不*记得*了。”

En: Did you? Or did you think I was a god damn ANIMAL HANDLER? We've been partners for how long, Harry? He adjusts his tie. "Don't answer that -- you don't *remember*."
Zh: “有吗？还是你以为我他妈是个驯兽员？我们已经搭档多久了，哈里？”他调整了一下脖子上的领带。“不用回答——反正你也不*记得*了。”

En: We're still friends, I just have a stupid head.
Zh: “我们还是朋友，我只是脑袋太笨了。”

En: How did you know I was here?
Zh: “你怎么知道我在这里？”

En: How did you know I was here?
Zh: “你怎么知道我在这里？”

En: The cafeteria manager you fucked over told us where you went.
Zh: “那个被你整得够呛的餐厅经理告诉我们的。”

En: After all that Sylvie stuff, he betrays me...
Zh: “我帮他料理了塞尔维的事，现在他居然背叛我……”

En: (Turn to face the general direction of the Whirling and yell.) "Damn you, cafeteria manager! You've betrayed me for the last time!"
Zh: （面向飞旋旅社的大致方向，然后大声叫喊。）“去你妈的，餐厅经理！这是你最后一次背叛我！”

En: I understand. Okay. Garte told you.
Zh: “我明白了。好吧。是加尔特告诉你的。”

En: Shitkid, he says, shaking his index finger, "he didn't *betray* you. He just told us the direction you went in. Who's Sylvie?"
Zh: “臭小子，”他摇晃着食指，说道，“他没有*背叛*你。他只是告诉我们你往哪个方向去了。塞尔维又是谁？”

En: Uhm, and... she interjects, trying to defuse the situation. "People on this street helped us too."
Zh: “呃，还有……”她插了进来，试图缓和一下局势。“街上的人也帮了我们。”

En: I saved his establishment and still he betrays me?!
Zh: “我拯救了他的企业，结果他还是背叛我了？！”

En: Sylvie's a whore. She rides the cock carousel... and foreigners.
Zh: “塞尔维是个婊子。她跟很多人上过床……还有外国人。”

En: No one. It's not important.
Zh: “谁也不是。这不重要。”

En: It better not be.
Zh: “最好不是。”

En: He means: "You better not be *partying* with this Sylvie, shitkid."
Zh: 他的意思是：“你最好不要跟这个塞尔维一起*派对*了，臭小子。”

En: Super, he says with a nod. "Whore. Foreigners. Hatred."
Zh: “好极了，”他点头说道。“婊子。外国人。仇恨。”

En: You're a legend among the drunks, Harry. A legendary local *drunk*.
Zh: “你在酒鬼之间是个传奇人物，哈里。一个传说中的本地*酒鬼*。”

En: Strange. He didn't mention that. In fact, the establishment didn't look saved at all -- there was a giant *aero-graffito* in front of the building. It was on fire.
Zh: “奇怪。他倒是没提过这个。事实上，那个公司看起来完全没有获救——建筑前面有一个巨大的*空气涂鸦*。它着火了。”

En: Speaking of which. The giant *aero-graffito* in front of his building, in red, government-marked fuel -- did you do that?
Zh: “说到这里。他的建筑前面那个巨大的*空气涂鸦*，用政府的红色燃油画的——你知道是怎么回事吗？”

En: Strange. He didn't mention that. In fact, the establishment didn't look saved at all -- there was a giant *aero-graffito* in front of the building. Mixed with *blood*.
Zh: “奇怪。他倒是没提过这个。事实上，那个公司看起来完全没有获救——建筑前面有一个巨大的*空气涂鸦*。里面还混合着*血液*。”

En: Speaking of which. The giant *aero-graffito* in front of the building. The one that's *burning* -- did you do that?
Zh: “说到这里。他的建筑前面那个巨大的*空气涂鸦*。就是*燃烧*的那个——你知道是怎么回事吗？”

En: It was this one junior delinquent.
Zh: “不是我。是个少年犯干的。”

En: Yeah, I wrote that. It was a poetic gesture. (Lie.)
Zh: “是啊，我写的。那是一种充满诗意的致敬。”（撒谎。）

En: Yeah, I lit it on fire. It was a poetic gesture.
Zh: “是啊，我把它点燃的。那是一种诗意的姿态。”

En: I don't know... He squints at you suspiciously. "It had *shitkid* written all over it."
Zh: “我不知道……”他怀疑地眯起眼睛打量你。“在我看来完全就是*臭小子*的风格。”

En: I knew it. Didn't I tell you, Trant? I told you it was our shitkid.
Zh: “我就知道。我不是告诉过你吗，特兰特？我就说是那个臭小子干的。”

En: The line is from Lu Jiatun's 'Mirova Cycle', isn't it? About Girl Child Communism -- the titular *returning* character. The ghostly apparition of... He looks around and, noticing the impatience of his companions, stops himself. "Good choice, Harry."
Zh: “这句台词是出自卢·贾图恩的‘米诺瓦循环’，对吗？关于纯真康米主义——有名无实的*回归*角色。幽灵的幻影……”他四下看了看，注意到同伴不耐烦的样子，于是自己停了下来。“很不错的选择，哈里。”

En: I knew it. Didn't I tell you, Trant? I told you it was our shitkid.
Zh: “我就知道。我不是告诉过你吗，特兰特？我就说是那个臭小子干的。”

En: Uhm, and... She looks around. "People on this street helped us too. With your whereabouts."
Zh: “呃，还有……”她四下看了看。“街上的人也帮了我们。关于你的下落问题。”

En: You're a legend among the drunks, Harry. A legendary local *drunk*.
Zh: “你在酒鬼之间是个传奇人物，哈里。一个传说中的本地*酒鬼*。”

En: Don't encourage him, Trant.
Zh: “别鼓励他，特兰特。”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: He is correct. It was the Seraise poet Lu Jiatun who in the Fifties of the last century composed a...
Zh: 他说的没错。那是一位萨弗里诗人卢·贾图恩，在上个世界50年代他……

En: Have you been *spying* on me?
Zh: “你一直在*暗中监视*我吗？”

En: Yes, I *was* spying on you. I was in the bar, wearing a wig and stupid sunglasses. You didn't *recognize* me -- your own partner. Walked right past me...
Zh: “是的，我*是*在暗中监视你。我去过酒吧，戴着一顶假发，还有一副愚蠢的墨镜。你没有*认出*我来——你自己的搭档。直接从我身边走过去了……”

En: I heard you'd lost your mind *and* your memory. I wanted to see if it was true. And it is, Harry -- you're totally insane now. Good job.
Zh: “我听说你疯了，*而且*还失忆了。我想看看是不是真的。而且，的确是，哈里——现在你完全疯了。干得漂亮。”

En: In the crowd, there was a man and a woman in RCM uniform, he says to you, then turns to Vicquemare. "In his defence -- we were in a hurry."
Zh: “在人群里，有一对穿着RCM制服的男女，”他转向维克玛，对你说道。“为他辩解的话——我们在赶时间。”

En: Cuno knew dress-up-pigs were in town. Cuno meant to dust them -- Cuno pig-duster -- but didn't, 'cause Cuno cares. The kid nods solemnly.
Zh: “坤诺知道乔装打扮的猪猡进城了。坤诺本来打算给他们掸掸灰——用坤诺的猪猡除尘器——不过坤诺没有这么做，因为他在乎。”男孩严肃地点点头。

En: This is the Horse-Faced Woman. I don't know *why* you named her that, but it was beyond idiotic. You should *never* address her using those words again.
Zh: 这就是那个马脸女人。我不知道你*为什么*要这么称呼她，不过这已经超出了愚蠢的范围。你*永远*也不应该再用这种词称呼她。

En: You fucking idiot. You stupid fucking idiot. You're even worse than before. How is that even *possible*?
Zh: “你他妈的是白痴吗。他妈的，你个愚蠢的白痴。你甚至比之前还要糟糕。这怎么*可能*呢？”

En: Okay. Another sigh. "You're my commanding officer. I... I really want to respect you. I want us to have a normal relationship."
Zh: “好吧。”又是一声叹息。“你是我的指挥官。我……我真的很想尊敬你。我希望能跟你保持正常的关系。”

En: That will never happen, Jude. He's the rudest man on Earth. He is the reason why the rest of us have to take sensitivity training. And I *hate* sensitivity training...
Zh: “永远也不可能发生，茱蒂。他是这个地球上最粗鲁的男人。他就是我们所有人要接受敏感性训练的原因。而且我*痛恨*敏感性训练……”

En: It's true. You have a problem.
Zh: 这是真的。你有问题。

En: Okay. Another sigh. "Because you're my commanding officer. I... I really want to respect you. I want us to have a normal relationship."
Zh: “好吧。”又是一声叹息。“你是我的指挥官。我……我真的很想尊敬你。我希望能跟你保持正常的关系。”

En: You. (Nod to the female officer.) "I'm sorry I didn't recognize you before."
Zh: “是你。”（朝着女警官点点头。）“很抱歉我之前没认出你来。”

En: It's okay, she sighs. "I didn't come here to gloat, or to fool you. Neither did he, actually." She gestures toward Vicquemare. "We're just worried."
Zh: “没事的，”她叹了口气。“我不是来看你的笑话，或者是愚弄你的。其实，他也不是。”她朝维克玛比了个手势。“我们只是很担心你。”

En: That's right -- 'worried'. I'm always worried about you. Every time you don't show up to work, or when you do -- but *stink*.
Zh: “每次你不来上班，或者你出现了——却*一身酒气*、*臭气熏天*、*讨厌透顶*的时候。”

En: You're a *worry-fest*. She's worried about you. I'm worried about you. Even Special Consultant Backpedal is worried about you. Everyone worries -- instead of *working*.
Zh: “你是个*担心集合体*。她担心你。我担心你。就连特别顾问都在担心你。每个人都在担心——而不是在*工作*。”

En: He's got a very, very solid case there. Actually, I've been trying to find a good moment to tell you...
Zh: 他的理由非常、非常充分。事实上，我一直想找个合适的机会告诉你……

En: You're worried *too*?
Zh: 你*也*在担心？

En: Yes. You often sound like a brutal idiot.
Zh: 是的。大多数时候，你听起来就像个毫不讲理的白痴。

En: Fuck you too! I don't need help!
Zh: 你他妈也给我滚蛋吧！我不需要帮助！

En: Listen to yourself. You sound like a brutal idiot. No matter what I do.
Zh: 听听你自己说的话。你听起来就像个毫不讲理的白痴。无论我做什么都没用。

En: So, *Trant Heidelstam*. What are you *special consulting* here?
Zh: “所以，*特兰特·海德斯塔姆*。你在这里*特别顾问*些什么呢？”

En: He's here to see if you're insane. He's smart. Let's move on.
Zh: “他来看看你是不是真的疯了。然后他很聪明。我们继续吧。”

En: Oh yeah?! What was up with all the *interesting history*? Spying on me?
Zh: “哦，是吗？！那所有那些*趣味历史*是怎么回事？暗中监视我吗？”

En: Him being there with his son -- it was not a coincidence. It's difficult to see, but he was worried about you. And *also* interested in the Feld building.
Zh: 他跟儿子出现在那里——并不是一个巧合。虽然很难看出来，不过他在担心你。而且*也*对菲尔德大厦很感兴趣。

En: No. I was just interested in the Feld building and the Martinaise Beachhead. And Mikael wanted to see Martinaise. It was a coincidence.
Zh: “不。我只是对菲尔德大厦和马丁内斯滩头堡很感兴趣。而且米克尔想看看马丁内斯。这是个巧合。”

En: What *am* I? He taps his foot. "I was asked to share my take on some of the more *fringe academic* theories developed in Königstein in the Thirties. Regarding partial psychotraumatic amnesia. Group personality theory..."
Zh: “我*是*什么？”他跺跺脚。“他们要求我分享一下对某种比较*边缘的学术理论*的看法，那是30年代在柯尼希斯开发出来的。比如说——部分精神创伤性失忆症。群体人格理论……”

En: He's here to see if you're insane. He's smart. Let's move on.
Zh: “他来看看你是不是真的疯了。他很聪明。我们继续吧。”

En: Duped again! No one's who they say they are!
Zh: “又在骗我！你们说的身份都是假的！”

En: *Duped*? Hey, here's a brilliant idea -- don't be a morbid drunk and you won't be duped so easily.
Zh: “*骗你*？喂，我有个好主意——别当个病态的酒鬼，这样你就没那么容易被骗了。”

En: It's the gardener situation all over again... (Turn to Cuno.) "Tell me at least *you* are who you said you were."
Zh: “又出现园丁的情况了……”（转向坤诺。）“告诉我，至少*你*没有对自己的身份撒谎吧。”

En: It's the gardener situation all over again... (Turn to the lieutenant.) "Tell me at least *you* are who you said you were."
Zh: “又出现园丁的情况了……”（转向警督。）“告诉我，至少*你*没有对自己的身份撒谎吧。”

En: Gardener, scab leader, *this*... (Turn to Cuno.) "Tell me at least *you* are who you said you were!"
Zh: “园丁，工贼首领，*这个*……”（转向坤诺。）“告诉我，至少*你*没有对自己的身份撒谎吧。”

En: Gardener, scab leader, *this*... (Turn to the lieutenant.) "Tell me at least *you* are who you said you were!"
Zh: “园丁，工贼首领，*这个*……”（转向警督。）“告诉我，至少*你*没有对自己的身份撒谎吧。”

En: Okay. That... does have something to do with it, yes.
Zh: “好吧。那个……确实跟它有点关系，是的。”

En: Yes. I'm still Kim Kitsuragi -- still a lieutenant from Precinct 57.
Zh: “是的。我还是金·曷城——还是来自57分局的一名警督。”

En: Fuck, pig... As you snooped out, the name's Kuuno, not *Cuno*. Let's get on with our lives. I'm Kuuno. It's known now. Stop staring at me.
Zh: “该死的，猪猡……被你发现了。我叫库诺，不是*坤诺*。让我们继续生活下去吧。我是库诺。现在你知道了啊。别再盯着我看了。”

En: Fuck, pig... The boy looks slightly uncomfortable. "The name's Kuuno, not *Cuno*. It's... lamer. My name's lamer than I said it was."
Zh: “该死的，猪猡……”男孩看起来有点不自在。“我叫库诺，不是*坤诺*。它很……傻。我的名字比我告诉你的还要傻。”

En: He looks repentant. "Just try not to shit yourself -- please."
Zh: 他看起来有些后悔。“只要别吓出屎来就行——拜托了。”

En: No. Everyone's just a brilliant master of disguises.
Zh: “不。每个人都是伪装的高手。”

En: Yeah -- Major Crimes Unit, under Lieutenants Du Bois and Vicquemare -- ring any bells?
Zh: “是啊——杜博阿和维克玛警督领导下的重案组——想起什么了吗？”

En: Refresh my memory -- who *else* is in this?
Zh: “再唤醒一下我的记忆——这里面还有*谁*？”

En: Yeah. Totally. I get it. Major Crimes Unit.
Zh: “是啊。完全明白了。重案组。”

En: Refresh your memory? It's a god damn Major Crimes Unit. There's you, me, Jude, Trant fucking Heidelstam, and Guillaume Bevy... He stares at you.
Zh: “唤醒你的记忆？这是个该死的重案组。有你，我，茱蒂，该死的特兰特·海德斯塔姆，还有纪尧姆·贝维……”他凝视着你。

En: He looks at Minot. "He doesn't remember."
Zh: 他看着迈诺特，说道，“他不记得了。”

En: Yeah, that's *very* good.
Zh: “是啊，这真是*太好*了。”

En: Yeah, um -- first, who's Guillaume Bevy?
Zh: “是啊，呃——首先，纪尧姆·贝维是谁？”

En: Okay. So what does the unit *do*?
Zh: “好吧。那重案组是*做*什么的呢？”

En: I'm technically just a civilian advisor.
Zh: “从严格意义上讲，我只是个平民顾问。”

En: Fuck you, you're part of this shit-show.
Zh: “去你妈的，你也是这场大闹剧的一部分。”

En: Oh, that's an interesting story, actually! He's not smiling. "Guillaume Bevy is a police reporter who joined our team. He was really good. Then he left, because he lost faith in your ability to lead the unit."
Zh: “哦，其实吧，那个故事很有意思！”他的脸上没有笑容。“纪尧姆·贝维是一名警方记者，加入了我们的团队。他真的很能干。然后他离开了，因为他对你领导小组的能力失去了信心。”

En: Other people have left too. Good, smart people. People we won't get back. Only me and this *really patient* patrol officer are still here. And Trant -- because I'm *forcing* him to stay.
Zh: “其他人也离开了。那些能干又聪明的人。大家不会回来的。只有我和这位*真的很有耐性*的巡警还在这里。还有特兰特——因为我*强迫*他留下来的。”

En: Is this Guillaume Bevy blond and partial to sunglasses?
Zh: 这位纪尧姆·贝维是一头金发，还特别喜欢墨镜吗？

En: Is this Guillaume Bevy blond, with sunglasses? Like you were...
Zh: “这位纪尧姆·贝维是一头金发，还戴着墨镜吗？就跟你之前一样……”

En: See -- there! He wags his finger at you. "He's getting it! I was *impersonating* him. Look at me, I'm G-Bevy. It was going to be funny. But then you really did have brain damage -- so not so much anymore."
Zh: “看见没——看见没！”他朝你挥舞着手指。“他明白了！我在*模仿*他。看看我，我是纪尧姆·贝维。本来我是想搞个有趣的恶作剧，却发现你是真有脑损伤——结果就是有趣不起来了。”

En: He sincerely thought it was going to be amusing. For both of you.
Zh: 他是由衷地觉得这样会很有趣。对于你们俩来说都是。

En: Do? It's a Major Crimes Unit! We clear the desk of cases so Precinct 41 doesn't look like the worst station in town. We're *shit tier* now, Harry. Because of you.
Zh: “做什么？那是个重案小组！我们处理过的案子卷宗能堆满一整张桌子，这样41分局看起来就不像是城里最糟糕的了。现在我们就是*垃圾*，哈里。因为你。”

En: They're your posse. Or what remains of it. Hand-picked. Hand-lost.
Zh: 他们是你的队员。或者是仅存的队员。亲手挑选的。亲手弄丢的。

En: You told us to fuck off. You said we're *cramping your style*. You're *Detective God*. Fuck everything. All will burn. Detect or die!
Zh: “你让我们滚开。你说我们*限制了你的风格*。你说自己是*侦探之神*。一切全都滚蛋。一切都会燃烧。不侦查，毋宁死！”

En: Goddamnit, Harry... He shifts his weight, crosses his arms, and looks you in the eye.
Zh: “该死的，哈里……”他转移了重心，交叉双臂，直直地望向你的眼睛。

En: You talking about Cuno? He points to himself. "Major Cuno wasn't abducted, you fucking fat dink! Fuck outta here..."
Zh: “你是在说坤诺吗？”他指着自己。“大坤诺没有被诱拐，你个死呆子胖子！赶紧滚开……”

En: And you were probably right too -- I would have never let you abduct a kid and take him on a creepy boat trip in the middle of fucking March!
Zh: “你可能也是对的——我永远不会让你诱拐一个孩子，带着他在三月中旬搞什么诡异的乘船旅行！”

En: All *will* burn, Satellite-Officer Vicquemare. Make no mistake about it.
Zh: “一切*都会*燃烧的。随迁警官维克玛。千万别搞错了。”

En: Why didn't you detect or die then?
Zh: “那为什么你没有‘不侦查，毋宁死’呢？”

En: Why would you leave a literal *Police God*?
Zh: “你为什么要抛下一个字面意义上的*侦探之神*呢？”

En: I said all those things? I'm not like that anymore.
Zh: “我说过那些话吗？我已经不像以前那样了。”

En: Sure... he squints at you. "You're not a... *superstar law official*. Talk on the town is you're Guillaume Le Million reincarnate. I know this shit. Nothing has changed."
Zh: “当然……”他眯起眼睛看着你。“你不是一个……*超级明星执法人员*。城里的人都说你是纪尧姆·列米利翁的转世。我知道这他妈是怎么回事。什么也没有改变。”

En: You were crying hysterically. You were drunk, breaking things, being emotionally abusive. You said we were going 'into the abyss'.
Zh: “你歇斯底里的大哭。烂醉如泥，到处砸东西，情感上受到了虐待。你说我们都会‘陷入深渊’。”

En: Oh, you think it was *cool* -- you saying that? *Aesthetic* somehow? You were crying when we got here. Breaking things. You said we were going 'into the abyss'.
Zh: “哦，你觉得这样很*酷*吗——你真这么觉得？还有些莫名的*唯美*？我们来的时候，你正在哭。到处砸东西。你说我们都会‘陷入深渊’。”

En: Here we go -- alcoholic delirium. *Visions*. All must pay... He shakes his head.
Zh: “又来了——酒精中毒性谵妄。*幻觉*。所有人都要付出代价……”他摇摇头。

En: Where have you been all this time -- there was a mercenary tribunal.
Zh: “这么久以来你到底在哪——我还经历了一场雇佣兵审判。”

En: Where have you *been* all this time?
Zh: “这么久以来，你到底去了*哪里*？”

En: Wait, so you let me face a squad of trained killers alone just to teach me a lesson?
Zh: “等等，所以你让我独自面对一队训练有素的杀手，就是为了给我上一课？”

En: It wasn't like that...
Zh: “不是那样的……”

En: Fuck you, Harry -- we didn't *know* there was gonna be a tribunal, did we?
Zh: “该死的，哈里……我们*不知道*会有一场审判，不是吗？”

En: Sure... he squints at you. "Talk on the town is you're the 'mouthpiece of the Gloaming.' Deranged apocalyptic rhetoric... Nothing has changed."
Zh: “当然……”他眯起眼睛看着你。“城里的人都说你是‘黄昏的代言人’。疯狂的世界末日理论……什么也没有改变。”

En: Yes. You're *sorry*. You're the sorriest cop who ever lived. Nothing has changed, Harry. I've heard this *repentance* shit a million times over.
Zh: “是的。你很*抱歉*。你是有史以来最抱歉的警察。什么也没有改变，哈里。这种*忏悔*我已经听过不下几百万次了。”

En: Are you sure? He squints at you. "So you haven't been telling everyone what a *boring cop* you are -- and that you should kill yourself? People say you've been out of your mind. I asked around."
Zh: “你确定吗？”他眯起眼睛看着你。“所以你没跟所有人说自己是个多么*无聊的警察*——还说你应该自杀？大家都说你疯了。我已经四下打听过。”

En: None of us wanted to see the abyss, so we fucked off. He sighs. "Like you told us to."
Zh: “我们没有人想凝视深渊，所以我们就滚开了。”他叹了口气。“就像你要求的那样。”

En: The *bells* aren't ringing because you have brain damage, Detective God. Trant, he turns to the blond, "this is where you come in -- how bad is it?"
Zh: “你*想不起来*是因为你有脑损伤，侦探之神。特兰特，”他转向金发男子，“你的任务来了——情况有多糟糕？”

En: The *bells* aren't ringing because you have brain damage. Trant, he turns to the blond, "this is where you come in -- how bad is it?"
Zh: “你*想不起来*是因为你有脑损伤。特兰特，”他转向金发男子，“你的任务来了——情况有多糟糕？”

En: Well... He doesn't have visible tremors. He talks without slurring. He can drive a boat. He's standing, reasoning. All good signs. But -- complete retrograde amnesia, episodic *and* semantic...
Zh: “呃……他没有明显的颤抖。说话也没有口齿不清。他能开船。他还好好地站着，据理力争。都是良好迹象。不过——在片段记忆*和*语义上，他有完全的逆行性遗忘……”

En: As displayed in the station call, our interactions with him, and -- I don't want to be a 'snitch', he makes air quotes, "but also mine with him before, when Harry did not seem to know who I was... It's all very interesting."
Zh: “根据警局通话的表现，我们跟他的互动，还有——我不想当个‘告密者’，”他在空中比了个引号，“之前我跟他的会面，那时哈里似乎没认出我是谁……这一切都非常有意思。”

En: Interesting?
Zh: “有意思？”

En: Yes. Interesting. I have my theories, but I would like to hear Harry's thoughts first. Harry, he turns to you, "what do *you* think happened to you? Neurologically? Psychologically? And -- why not -- *socioeconomically*?"
Zh: “是的。有意思。我有自己的一套理论，不过我想先听听哈里的想法，”他转向你，“*你*觉得自己出了什么事？神经方面的？心理上的？还有——*社会经济学方面的*？”

En: Meaning: you forgot both who you are and the definitions of 'money,' 'isola,' 'pale,' and so on.
Zh: 意思就是：你忘了自己是谁。还有‘金钱’，‘洲’，‘灰域’等等之类的定义。

En: As displayed in your interactions with him here and previously, at the bar, and -- I don't want to be a 'snitch', he makes air quotes, "but also mine with him before, when Harry did not seem to know who I was... It's all very interesting."
Zh: “根据你现在跟他的互动，还有之前在酒吧的互动，还有——我不想当个‘告密者’，”他在空中比了个引号，“还有之前我跟他的会面，那时哈里似乎没认出我是谁……这一切都非常有意思。”

En: As displayed in our interactions with him here, and also his interactions with the locals, where he did not remember being a law official... It's all very interesting.
Zh: “根据你现在跟他的互动，他跟本地人的互动，还有他不记得自己是执法人员的事实……这一切都非常有意思。”

En: Wait, socioeconomically? You think I'm so *poor* I lost my memory?
Zh: “等等，社会经济学？你觉得我是因为太*穷*才失去记忆的吗？”

En: I drank so much I lost my memory. And I'm now *slowly* recovering it.
Zh: “我喝的太多，所以失忆了。而且现在我在*慢慢地*恢复。”

En: I made it all up. As I already told Lieutenant Kitsuragi, I know what I am.
Zh: “这一切都是我瞎编的。就像我跟曷城警督说的，我知道自己是谁。”

En: I made it all up. It was a fantasy, a stupid joke. I know what I am.
Zh: “这一切都是我瞎编的。这是一场白日梦，一个愚蠢的玩笑。我知道自己是谁。”

En: It might have something to do with an anomaly in the church. A two-millimetre hole in the world.
Zh: “这跟教堂里的异常现象有关。这个世界上有一个2毫米的洞。”

En: I am a highly experimental detective. This was a method I used to solve the case.
Zh: “我是个高度实验性的警探。这是我用来破案的一种方法。”

En: Interesting. So at first he dipped his toes into it. Prepared. That's where he would have gotten the idea -- yes. Practice. And then he used alcohol to 'get there', so to speak...
Zh: “有意思。他一开始会小心尝试。做好准备。那是他的灵感源泉——对。实践。然后借用酒精”达成目标“。可以这么说……”

En: Not when you phrase it like that. But I don't think critical theory -- I know everyone thinks this is far-fetched, pink academia, but still -- I don't think it should be off the table here.
Zh: “你这么说就不是了。我知道大家以为这有些牵强附会，不过我不认为批判理论是华而不实的学术话题。所以，我认为还是应该考虑这一点的。”

En: What?! He lost his memory because of *capitalism*?
Zh: “什么？！他是因为*资本主义*而失忆的吗？”

En: No, not like that, I'm not talking Vredefort School here. But, Harry, I asked you -- what do *you* think happened?
Zh: “不，不是这么回事。我没在说弗里德堡学派。不过，哈里，我问你——*你*觉得是怎么回事？”

En: And what is that?
Zh: “那又是什么？”

En: A drunk and a loser.
Zh: “一个醉鬼，一个失败者。”

En: A heartbroken drudge.
Zh: “一个心碎的苦力。”

En: A police detective.
Zh: “一个警探。”

En: Something so sad happened to me that I couldn't be me anymore. It was a defence mechanism.
Zh: “我身上发生过一件非常悲伤的事情，让我变得不像自己了。这是一种防御机制。”

En: Capitalism. Capitalism fucked me beyond all recognition. Trant is right.
Zh: “资本主义。资本主义把我搞得面目全非。特兰特是对的。”

En: Well it's *not* capitalism. So we can cross that off the list, you lefty dink.
Zh: “呃，并*不是*资本主义。所以可以把这一条从清单上划掉了，你这个左翼呆子。”

En: Lefty dink? Well, actually -- I don't consider myself a classical leftist at all. Not in the Mazovian sense at least. But, fine -- theory aside, what happened?
Zh: “左翼呆子？呃，事实上——我完全不觉得自己个典型的左翼分子。至少就马佐夫主义理论而言不是。不过，好吧——把批判理论放在一边，到底发生了什么？”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: He is -- he's getting better. And I can confirm that he drank a *lot* of alcohol prior to it happening.
Zh: “他是的——他在慢慢变好。我可以肯定在事情发生之前，他喝了*很多*酒。”

En: I believe he *drank*, he turns to you. "People do that -- especially this one. What they don't do is forget their *whole life* because of drinking."
Zh: “我相信他是*喝多*了，”他转向你。“谁都会喝醉——特别是这家伙。但是他们不会因为酗酒把*整个人生*都忘了个精光。”

En: I believe you *drank*. People do that -- you especially. What they don't do is forget their *whole life* because of drinking.
Zh: “我相信你是*喝多*了，”他转向你。“谁都会喝醉——尤其是你。但是他们不会因为酗酒把*整个人生*都忘了个精光。”

En: But, Detective Vicquemare, she interjects. "He *has* blanked out before."
Zh: “但是，维克玛警探，”她插了一嘴。“他之前*也*昏倒过。”

En: I have?
Zh: “我有吗？”

En: Yes, a couple of times. After some of the more... serious benders. She pauses, remembering. "One was after the Two Drunks case, the other when we looked into that mural."
Zh: “是的。好几次。在几次更加……严重的饮酒作乐之后。”想到这里，她停了下来。“一次是在破获双酒鬼案之后，还有一次是我们调查那副壁画的时候。”

En: So you don't *remember* not remembering. Beautiful.
Zh: 所以你不*记得*自己不记得了。赞。

En: Well -- here is my theory: What if this is an absolutely normal reaction to the world we're living in? What if this is *not* a significant anomaly at all, something to be explained, approached as a defect? Look at the sensory input here... He gestures toward the scenery.
Zh: “呃——我的理论是这样的。如果这是对我们生活的这个世界的一种完全正常的反应呢？如果这根本不是什么*异常现象*、某种需要解释的，被当成是缺陷的东西呢？看看这里的感官输入吧……”他朝着周围的风景比了个手势。

En: Look at the ruins, the neon, listen to the radio, the multitudes. The people. Live here for forty years... As a police detective, he's like a magnetic reader on the world-tape -- to borrow a known metaphor. Harry's been pushed *flat against it*. Total input.
Zh: “看看这片废墟，看看霓虹灯，听听广播，听听芸芸众生，所有人。在这里生活了四十年……作为一名警探，他就像世界这盘磁带的磁带阅读器——这里借用了一个众所周知的比喻。哈里被它*压扁*了。完全的输入。”

En: Okay, Trant, thank you. That's... absolutely meaningless. I'm glad we brought you. Will he or will he not be able to work in the Major Crimes Unit? Is he a cretin now? I want to know *that*.
Zh: “好吧，特兰特，谢谢你。这还真是完全……没有任何意义。很高兴我们把你也带来了。他到底还能不能在重案组继续工作？现在他是个白痴病患者了吗？我想知道的是*这个*。”

En: Hard-wired to the free market... He nods confidently. "He just needed for it to end."
Zh: “与自由市场密切相关……”他自信地点点头。“他只是需要结束这一切。”

En: Okay, Trant, thank you. That's absolutely meaningless. You really are a lefty dink. Will he or will he not be able to work in the Major Crimes Unit? Is he a cretin now? I want to know *that*.
Zh: “好吧，特兰特，谢谢你。这还真是完全没有任何意义。你还真是个左翼呆子。他到底还能不能在重案组继续工作？现在他是个白痴病患者了吗？我想知道的是*这个*。”

En: He is *not* a cretin. And he *is* able to do work -- if not in his previous leadership role, then as a line detective.
Zh: “他*不是*一个白痴病患者。而且他*的确*可以工作——即便不能承担之前的领导角色，也可以当个普通的警探。”

En: What now? (Proceed.)
Zh: “现在怎么办？”（继续。）

En: I'm ready to lead again.
Zh: “我已经准备好再次走上领导岗位。”

En: Line detective is good for now.
Zh: “目前当个普通警探就好。”

En: He's wrong. I'm too far gone for work.
Zh: “他错了。我状态太差，不适合工作了。”

En: No one even *mentioned* that. He looks at you, then at Trant. "I misphrased my question. It should have been: Is he able to put his clothes on, and use the potty, or do we need to get him on a disability pension?"
Zh: “根本没人*提过*这事。”他看了看你，然后转向特兰特。“我的问题提的不太对。应该这样问：他能自己穿衣服，自己用便盆吗？还是我们需要给他发放伤残退休金？”

En: For *now*? He looks at you, then at Trant. "I misphrased my question. It should have been: Is he able to put his clothes on, and use the potty, or do we need to get him on a disability pension?"
Zh: “*目前*？”他看了看你，然后转向特兰特。“我的问题提的不太对。应该这样问：他能自己穿衣服，自己用便盆，还是我们需要给他发放伤残退休金？”

En: That is what I thought -- at first. That it was a fantasy. But... I've seen him work. It's not. He really is experiencing something out of the ordinary. He really does not remember.
Zh: “一开始我也是这么想的。那只是一个幻想。不过……我见过他工作的样子。那并不是什么幻想。他真的经历了某种很不同寻常的事情。他真的不记得了。”

En: Lieutenant, I'm sorry, but you've been *bewitched by the shitkid*. It happens. I know it's hard to accept, but he made it all up -- because he's a psychopath. It's a simple explanation.
Zh: “警督，很抱歉，不过你被*臭小子弄得鬼迷心窍*了。有时候是会发生这种情况。我知道这很难接受，不过这全都是他自己编出来的——因为他是个精神病患者。这是个很简单的解释。”

En: I can believe that. Not the *detective* part, that's a tall order, but that he fucked with us. This is typical Harry behaviour. A sick joke. I told you, Jude.\n
Zh: “这个我信。不是*警探*那部分，那是个无理的要求，不过他把我们都骗了。这是哈里典型的行为方式。一个恶心的玩笑。我告诉过你的，茱蒂。”

En: I can believe that. Shitkid made it all up to fuck with us -- because he's a psychopath. This is typical Harry behaviour. A sick joke. I told you, Jude.
Zh: “这个我信。臭小子编了这套谎话，把我们都骗了——因为他是个精神病患者。这是哈里典型的行为方式。一个恶心的玩笑。我告诉过你的，茱蒂。”

En: Detective Vicquemare... he *has* blacked out before. We should take this seriously.
Zh: “维克玛警探……他之前*也*晕倒过。我们应该严肃对待这件事。”

En: Corresponding to the twenty-centimetre hole in your brain? Sure. This theory has great symmetry. I see how it folds into itself neatly.
Zh: “和你脑袋里那个20厘米的洞相对应吗？当然。这套理论有很大的对称性。我能看出它是怎么巧妙地融入进去的。”

En: I knew you would be too closed-minded to understand *the anomaly*.
Zh: “我知道你太过保守，没办法理解*异常现象*。”

En: I'll explain later, but there's another man who's lost his memory. A *crab-man*.
Zh: “我之后再解释，不过还有一个人也失忆了。那个*蟹男*。”

En: Take this seriously. I suspect there might be something wrong with Martinaise.
Zh: “严肃一点。我怀疑马丁内斯有点不太对劲。”

En: Thank you, Lieutenant Kitsuragi. Just to clarify -- I do not think isolary entroponetics are a hoax. Pale produces global phenomena -- it's proven. However...
Zh: “谢谢你，曷城警督。澄清一下——我不认为跨洲灰域学是一个骗局。灰域引起了全球性的现象——这是已经证实过的。然而……”

En: This sounds odd -- but I was there. The church on the coast was shaking from an audio-spatial anomaly. It may have been entroponetic, or perhaps related to radio waves...
Zh: “可能听起来很奇怪——不过我也在那里。海岸上的教堂因为一个音频空间的异常现象产生了震动。可能是灰域，或者也许跟无线电波有关……”

En: *Crab-man* is an unfortunate choice of words -- but I was there. The church on the coast shook from an audio-spatial anomaly. It may have been entroponetic, or perhaps related to radio waves...
Zh: “*蟹男*这个词选的很不成功——不过我也在那里。海岸上的教堂因为一个音频空间的异常现象产生了震动。可能是灰域，或者也许跟无线电波有关……”

En: Either way, I have put this into my report -- you should read it. I do not, however, think it has anything to do with him drinking himself to the point of brain damage.
Zh: “不管怎么说，我把这些都记录在报告里了——你应该看看。不过，我觉得这跟他喝酒喝到大脑受损没什么关系。”

En: Either way, it was real. I've even put it in my report -- you should read it. I do not, however, think it has anything to do with him drinking himself to the point of brain damage.
Zh: “不管怎么说，这是真的。我把这些都记录在报告里了——你应该看看。不过，我觉得这跟他喝酒喝到大脑受损没什么关系。”

En: ...what has *not* been proven is total memory loss after drinking too much Commodore Red. Honestly, I think he's just lying to us.
Zh: “……还*没有*被证实的是喝了那么多海军准将红朗姆之后，会不会导致记忆完全丧失。说实在的——我觉得他只是在欺骗我们。”

En: Crab-man... Look, I get it. In your delirium you came up with an *entroponetic* explanation for why you're such an insane drunk.
Zh: “蟹男……好吧，我明白了。在你精神错乱的时候，你想出了一种*灰域学*说法，来解释你为什么会变成个疯狂的酒鬼。”

En: Yes-yes. Anomaly. Look, I get it. In your delirium you came up with an *entroponetic* explanation for why you're such an insane drunk. And I understand.
Zh: “是的，是的。异常现象。你看，我明白的。在你精神错乱的时候，你想出了一种*灰域学*说法，来解释你为什么会变成个疯狂的酒鬼。而且我完全能理解。”

En: I've been there: Perhaps it's *not* because he's a petulant teenager in a 40-year-old man's body. Perhaps it's pale-related? Perhaps it's extraphysical?
Zh: “这些我也经历过：也许*不是*因为他是个住在40多岁的中年男人身体里的暴躁小青年。也许这跟灰域有关？也许跟超物质有关？”

En: Or perhaps it can be cured with mesmerism? But then I think: No, that's unlikely. He's probably just an *asshole*. A drunk asshole who *pretends* to have amnesia to get out of shit.
Zh: “或者可以用催眠治愈？不过之后我就想了：不，不太可能。他可能只是个*混账*而已。一个混账酒鬼，*假装*失忆来摆脱这一切困境。”

En: Son of fucking Lung here! Detective Far Out Son of Lung on the case!
Zh: “该死的肺之子在这里！激进肺之子警探正在调查这起案件！”

En: Is it really so hard to believe?
Zh: “真有那么难以相信吗？”

En: I have accomplished things no normal detective ever has. I have detected a...
Zh: “我完成了任何普通警探都做不到的事。我发现了一个……”

En: This is all *pointless*. I have completely TRANSCENDED all of this. You have no idea what I just saw!
Zh: “这全都*毫无意义*。我已经完全超越了这一切。你根本不知道我刚才看见了什么！”

En: Yes. It's really, really hard to believe. Because it's *idiotic* and *not true*. It's not possible to wipe your own memory. Even with the amount of *Potent Pilsner* you down every *hour*.
Zh: “是的。真的、真的很难相信。因为这很*愚蠢*，而且*不是真的*。要抹去自己的记忆是不可能的。即便你*每小时*灌进去那么多*高度比尔森啤酒*也不可能。”

En: He *did* present this theory to me -- once before. I had trouble believing it then, but he seems to be convinced. And I've seen him work. His methods are... unorthodox.
Zh: “他*的确*向我展现过这套理论——曾经有过一次。当时我很难相信，不过他似乎是被说服了。而且我见过他工作的方式。他的方法有些……不太传统。”

En: With all due respect, lieutenant -- you're *bewitched by the shitkid*. It happens. What doesn't happen is a cop wiping his own memory to solve a case.
Zh: “恕我直言，警督——你被*臭小子弄得鬼迷心窍*了。有时候是会发生这种情况。没发生的是，一个警察为了破案而抹掉自己的记忆。”

En: In fact, it's not possible to wipe one's memory -- at all. Even with the amount of Potent Pilsner he downs every *hour*. He's simply lying. Or insane. Or both.
Zh: “事实上，要抹掉一个人的记忆是不可能的——完全不可能。即便*每小时*灌进去那么大量的高度比尔森啤酒也不可能。他就是在撒谎。要么就是疯了。或者两者都有。”

En: You're simply lying. Or insane. Or both.
Zh: “你就是在撒谎。要么就是疯了。或者两者都有。”

En: Detective Vicquemare... I'm not saying he's the Son of Lung. But lying? I mean -- he *has* blacked out before...
Zh: “维克玛警探……我不会说他是肺之子。不过撒谎？我的意思是——他之前*也*晕倒过……”

En: Shut up! You didn't wipe your own memory. We're not even *considering* that option. It's not possible. Even with the amount of *Potent Pilsner* you down every *hour*.
Zh: “闭嘴！抹掉自己的记忆是不可能的。我们根本不会*考虑*那个选项。这是不可能的。即便你*每小时*灌进去那么多*高度比尔森啤酒*也不可能。

En: Who was the Son of Lung again?
Zh: “再说一次，肺之子是谁？”

En: Who's Son of Lung?
Zh: “肺之子是谁？”

En: It's an urban myth -- a police detective who employs highly experimental investigative techniques. It's a *cultural* reference, detective.
Zh: “这是个都市神话——是一个采用高实验性调查技巧的警探。这是一种*文化的*参照物，警探。”

En: Psychotraumatic amnesia? Trant? He turns to the special consultant. "I can go for that -- shitkid is a broken man, always has been. Who isn't? I know I am. But you know what?"
Zh: “精神创伤性失忆症？特兰特？”他转向特别顾问。“这个我可以接受——臭小子是个绝望的人，一直都是。谁不是呢？我知道我是。但是你知道吗？”

En: He turns back to you. "I keep my shit together. Also, I *know* a person can't wipe their own mind -- however traumatic it gets. That doesn't happen. You're lying. Or insane. Or both."
Zh: 他又转向你。“我会努力生活下去。而且，我*知道*一个人不能抹掉自己的记忆——不管到底受过多大的创伤。就是不可能发生。你在撒谎。要么就是疯了。或者两者都有。”

En: Look at F. Booho here! Victim of the ethanol-industrial complex. What are you, an inva-communist now? You think this is a joke?
Zh: “看看这位哭鼻子先生！乙醇大工业中心的受害者。你是什么，现在又变成波动康米主义者了？你觉得这是个笑话？”

En: He turns to the special consultant. "Asking him was a mistake -- he's a teenage psycho. I asked *you*. It's not possible to wipe your own memory with alcohol alone, right? He's either lying or insane."
Zh: 他转向特别顾问。“问他是个错误——他就是个十几岁的精神病患者。我问*你*。只靠酒精是不可能抹掉自己的记忆的，对吧？他不是在撒谎，就是疯了。”

En: Fuck all of you, I don't *want* to be in your unit.
Zh: “你们全都去死吧，我*不想*待在你的小组。”

En: No, Harry -- FUCK YOU. You already fucked us -- I've already explained this shit to Pryce *twice*. To Berdyayeva -- *four times*. I'm your partner. I answer for you when you're not there.
Zh: “不，哈里——你去死吧。你已经把我们搞得乱七八糟——我已经跟普赖斯解释过*两次*了。在别尔佳耶夫那里——*四次*。我是你的搭档。你不在那里的时候，我会替你回答。”

En: Agreed, Harry. He nods. "Just don't expect us to get you a disability pension. Cops who actually gave a shit are waiting in line. You're not gonna hog their seat."
Zh: “我同意，哈里。”他点点头。“别指望我们给你申请伤残退休金。那些真正在乎的警察还排队等着呢。你不会占用他们的位置。”

En: They can keep that pension, you're rock solid. You can put your clothes on *hard*.
Zh: 退休金他们自己留着吧，你可是摇滚硬汉。你可以穿上*硬核*的衣服。

En: When you clocked out, *I* became responsible for your cases -- and your special task force.
Zh: 当你打卡下班的时候，*我*要对你的案子负责——还有你的特别行动小组。

En: Hold on, in the ocean? (Feign ignorance.)
Zh: “等等，在海里吗？”（假装不懂。）

En: It was stolen by Jacob Irw.
Zh: “它被雅各布·欧文偷走了。”

En: That wasn't me. It was stolen by traffic hooligans.
Zh: “不是我。它被交通流氓偷走了。”

En: I drove it into the ocean when I was drunk.
Zh: “我醉酒的时候把它开进海里了。”

En: Yes. In the ocean. Under the sea. Our work vehicle -- with fish and clams and other sea-shit.
Zh: “是的。在海里。我们的工作車——现在跟鱼，蛤蚌还有其他狗屁海洋生物在一起。”

En: I thought the killer would be underwater. He wasn't.
Zh: “我以为杀手可能藏在水下。但是并没有。”

En: Lie. Just -- bide your time, ask something. Then lie.
Zh: 撒谎。只要——等待时机，问些问题。然后撒谎。

En: Tequila Sunset. I also jumped the canal by the way.
Zh: “龙舌兰日落。话说，我还跳过了运河。”

En: The time had come. Tequila Sunset.
Zh: “时间到了。龙舌兰日落。”

En: Jacob Irw?
Zh: “雅各布·欧文？”

En: The famous TipTop Tournée driver.
Zh: “著名的大师赛車手。”

En: I *know* who Jacob Irw is. I wanted to give you a chance to stop *fucking* me. How naive of me. You drove a 45,000 reál police vehicle into the ocean -- what did I expect?
Zh: “我*知道*雅各布·欧文是谁。我本来想给你一次不再*骗*我的机会。我真是太天真了。你把价值45,000雷亚尔的警車开进了大海里——我还能期待什么呢？”

En: I don't know. I don't know what it's doing there.
Zh: “我不知道。我不知道它在那里做什么。”

En: Oh -- well, if it wasn't *you* then it's okay. You only let someone *steal* our 45,000 reál police vehicle, drive it into the sea -- and then just *left* it there.
Zh: “哦——好吧，如果不是*你*，那就没事了。你只不过是让人*偷走*了价值45,000雷亚尔的警車，开进了海里——而且就这么把它*留在*那里。”

En: Don't answer that. He exhales to calm his breathing. "Your badge, Harry. Show me your badge."
Zh: “不要回答。”他呼出一口气，想稳住自己的呼吸。“你的警徽，哈里。把你的警徽拿给我看看。”

En: What I *do* have is this futuristic armour. (Tap your helmet.)
Zh: “我*拥有*这一身未来主义盔甲。”（拍拍你的头盔。）

En: What I *do* have is this futuristic armour. (Tap your cuirass.)
Zh: “我*拥有*这一身未来主义盔甲。”（拍拍你的胸甲。）

En: What I *do* have is this futuristic armour. (Click the heels of your boots.)
Zh: “我*拥有*这一身未来主义盔甲。”（掂一下靴子的后跟。）

En: What I *do* have is this futuristic armour. (Clench your armored fist.)
Zh: “我*拥有*这一身未来主义盔甲。”（攥紧戴着盔甲手套的拳头。）

En: What I *do* have is this futuristic armour. (Point to yourself.)
Zh: “我*拥有*这一身未来主义盔甲。”（指向你自己。）

En: We're not armour-cops, we're cop-cops! People don't know us by our armour, they know us by our badges. You called in and said you lost yours -- have you *found* it?
Zh: “我们不是武装警察，我们就是普通警察！大家不会通过盔甲来辨认我们，而是我们的警徽。你打来电话说把警徽弄丢了——你*找到*它了吗？”

En: We're not armour-cops, we're cop-cops! People don't know us by our armour, they know us by our badges. Where the fuck is your badge?
Zh: “我们不是武装警察，我们就是普通警察！大家不会通过盔甲来辨认我们，而是我们的警徽。你那个该死的警徽在哪？”

En: For the record, you pressured us into getting it. 'It'll be cool Jean, we'll have wheels, rapid response...' I was fine being an equestrian cop. I hope you're fine driving a *bicycle*.
Zh: “郑重声明，是你逼我们买的。‘那样多酷啊，让，我们有車之后，就能快速响应……’我觉得当骑警挺不错的。希望你骑*自行車*也没问题。”

En: So *refreshing*. He just admits it. Thank you for your honesty. Thank you for destroying 45,000 reál of police property that's coming out of *everyone's* payslip.
Zh: “真是让人*神清气爽*。他刚刚承认了。感谢你的诚实。感谢你毁掉了价值45,000雷亚尔的警方财产，这下*所有人*的工资单都要遭殃了。”

En: I do. You drove it into the water. Everyone on this street saw you do it. It's going to be a local landmark too -- on the brochure. Thank you for fucking us, Harry.
Zh: “我知道。你把它开进海里的。街上所有人都看见了。它也会成为本地的一个地标——记录在宣传册上。感谢你，给我们惹了这么大的烂摊子，哈里。”

En: Ha-ha-ha, ho-ho-ho. He grabs his stomach in humourless laughter. "Tequila Sunset. Jumped the canal. So funny, Harry. Thank you for fucking me."
Zh: “哈哈哈，呵呵呵。”他苦笑着抓紧自己的腹部。“龙舌兰日落。跳过运河。真好笑，哈里。感谢你，给我惹了这么大的烂摊子。”

En: Ha-ha-ha, ho-ho-ho. He grabs his stomach in mirthless laughter. "Tequila Sunset -- not Sunrise, because you're almost dead. So funny, Harry. Thank you for fucking me."
Zh: “哈哈哈，呵呵呵。”他苦笑着抓紧自己的腹部。“龙舌兰日落——不是日出，因为你就快死了。真好笑，哈里。感谢你，给我惹了这么大的烂摊子。”

En: Ha-ha-ha, ho-ho-ho. He grabs his stomach in mirthless laughter. "Underwater killer. So funny, Harry. Thank you for fucking me."
Zh: “哈哈哈，呵呵呵。”他苦笑着抓紧自己的腹部。“藏在水下的杀手。真好笑，哈里。感谢你，给我惹了这么大的烂摊子。”

En: Thank you for destroying 45,000 reál of police property that's coming out of *my* payslip. You know that, right? You're gonna get fired. And I'm gonna pay till I die...
Zh: “感谢你毁掉了价值45,000雷亚尔的警方财产，这下*我*的工资单要遭殃了。你知道的，对吗？你会被开除的。而我会到死都要为你支付这笔账单……”

En: It doesn't matter. He exhales to calm his breathing. "Your badge, Harry. Show me your badge."
Zh: “没关系。”他呼出一口气，想稳住自己的呼吸。“你的警徽，哈里。把你的警徽拿给我看看。”

En: Wait, there's a motor carriage in the ocean?
Zh: “等等，大海里有一辆汽車？”

En: Yes. *Your* motor carriage. White and blue. Under the sea. With fish and clams and other sea-shit.
Zh: “是的。*你的*汽車。蓝白相间的。就在海底。跟鱼，蛤蚌还有其他狗屁海洋生物在一起。”

En: Now that you look where he's pointing -- to the northeast -- then yes. There does seem to be a motor carriage in the ocean. Strange. How did it get there?
Zh: 你看着他手指的方向——在东南边——没错。海里好像真有一台汽車。奇怪。它是怎么跑到那里去的？

En: Just stand there.
Zh: 就这么站在那里。

En: Yeah, yeah. Just stand there. It's cool.
Zh: “是啊，是啊。就这么傻站着。挺好的。

En: I lost it. I haven't found it yet. (Lie.)
Zh: “我把它弄丢了，还没有找到。”（撒谎。）

En: I haven't found it yet.
Zh: “还没有找到。”

En: I lost it. I haven't found it yet.
Zh: “我把它弄丢了，还没有找到。”

En: I got my badge right here. (Show it to them.)
Zh: “我的警徽就在这里啊。”（把它拿给他们看。）

En: He squints at it suspiciously.
Zh: 他怀疑地眯起眼睛打量着。

En: He doesn't have it... He points to the sea. "Here's an idea. Maybe you could look in the mother fucking motor carriage you drove into the sea? Maybe LOOK THERE?!"
Zh: “他没有……”他指向大海。“我有个主意。也许你他妈的可以去那辆被你开进海里的車里看看？要不要去那边看看？！”

En: Wait, my badge?
Zh: “等等，我的警徽？”

En: The thing that tells people you're a police officer.
Zh: “就是告诉别人你是警察的东西。”

En: Fuck it. I'll get it myself, just tell me you have your *gun*. He collects himself again -- dusts off his black suit, although it's completely clean.
Zh: 他又镇定了一下——拍了拍黑西服上的灰尘，虽然那上面一尘不染的。“见鬼去吧。我自己去找，告诉我，*枪*总在你身上上吧。”

En: Catch it!
Zh: 抓住它！

En: You're proficient in catching small objects.
Zh: 在抓小东西方面你很熟练。

En: Not today, badge.
Zh: 今天不能让你得逞，警徽。

En: You juggle the badge for a second, unsuccessfully, and it lands on the ground some two metres away.
Zh: 你想耍一耍手中的警徽，但是没成功，它掉落在大概2米开外的地上。

En: You got it all right! You're showing it to him, victorious. They're *seeing* the badge.
Zh: 你做得很对！你向他展示着胜利。他们*看见*徽章了。

En: And your gun? he asks, unimpressed by the piece of plastic in your hand.
Zh: 男人对你手中那块塑料毫无兴趣。“那你的枪呢？”他问到。

En: The drama is unnecessary, I got the badge, all is well.
Zh: 没必要搞得这么戏剧化，警徽在我这里，一切都很顺利。

En: He has it! The patrol officer picks it up and gives it back to you -- slippery and cold. "He has it, Jean. It's his badge!"
Zh: “他找到了！”巡警把它捡起来，交还给你——滑腻又冰冷。“他找到了，让。是他的警徽！”

En: He found it! The patrol officer picks it up and gives it back to you -- slippery and cold. "He found it, Jean. It's his badge!"
Zh: “他找到了！”巡警把它捡起来，交还给你——滑腻又冰冷。“他找到了，让。是他的警徽！”

En: Behold, my badge!
Zh: “看，我的警徽！”

En: Did I get the badge?
Zh: 我有警徽吗？

En: In a rush to demonstrate your badge, your eager fingers can't sustain a grip on the smooth plastic, and the badge slips out of your hand.
Zh: 你急于向他们展示警徽，热切的手指没能抓住那块湿漉漉的塑料，警徽从你手中溜了出去。

En: Let it fall to the ground.
Zh: 让它掉在地上。

En: And your *gun*? The man stares at you, unimpressed.
Zh: 男人盯着你，无动于衷。“那你的*枪*呢？”

En: As if having your badge *and* gun are natural states, not achievements.
Zh: 似乎拥有警徽*和*配枪是一种很自然的状态，而不是成就。

En: Patrol Officer Minot picks it up and gives it back to you -- slippery and cold.
Zh: 巡警迈诺特把警徽捡了起来，交还给你——滑腻又冰冷。

En: He has it. It's his badge. I can confirm.
Zh: “他有。是他的警徽。我可以确认。”

En: He found it. It's his badge. I can confirm.
Zh: “他找到了。是他的警徽。我可以确认。”

En: And your *gun*? The man stares at you, unimpressed with the badge-finding.
Zh: 男人盯着你，对找到的警徽毫无兴趣。“那你的*枪*呢？”

En: Okay. Finally, he nods, unimpressed. "And your *gun*?"
Zh: 最后，他点点头，还是一副毫无兴趣的样子。“好吧。那你的*枪*呢？”

En: Trying to ascertain if it's not a blue candy wrapper or a piece of vinyl.
Zh: 试着确认那不是一片蓝色的糖果包装纸，或者是黑胶唱片。

En: Wait, *how* did you know I lost my gun?
Zh: “等等，你*怎么*知道我的枪不见了？”

En: My gun is right here! (Show him the Villiers 9mm.)
Zh: “我的枪就在这里！”（把你的维利耶9毫米手枪拿给他看。）

En: I have a new gun now. (Present Ruby's gun.)
Zh: “现在我有一支新枪了。”（掏出露比的枪。）

En: I don't care if you have a *new gun*, I only care about your *old gun*. Your official sidearm. You lost it, didn't you?
Zh: “我不在乎你有没有*新枪*，我只在乎你的*旧枪*。你的官方配枪。你弄丢了，是吗？”

En: Gun? Behold *all* my guns. If anything, I need more *hands*! (Show him your two pistols and two rifles.)
Zh: “枪？看看我*所有*的枪！如果说还有什么的话，我需要更多的*手*才对！”（拿出你的两支手枪和两支步枪。）

En: I upgraded my gun to a rifle. (Present the Triangong 4.46 rifle.)
Zh: “我把枪升级成步枪了。”（拿出特里安戈4.46步枪。）

En: It *worked* too, didn't it? You're drunk right now, aren't you? You fucking bum, I can smell it all the way over here!
Zh: “这样也*能行*，是吧？你现在还是醉的，对吗？你这个该死的流浪汉，我一路走过来就闻到了！”

En: Did you check the local pawnshop? Because *I* did  -- and you're not gonna believe it, but you sold the pawnbroker a Villiers 9mm pistol -- to pay for booze!
Zh: “你去本地当铺看了吗？因为*我*去过了——你是不会相信的，不过你把维利耶9毫米手枪卖给当铺老板了——为了换酒钱！”

En: You lost your fucking gun!? He looks around. "I knew it. I knew he lost it. Didn't I tell you? I told you he lost his gun too."
Zh: “你他妈把枪弄丢了！？”他四下看了看。“我就知道。我就知道他弄丢了。我不是告诉过你吗？我告诉过你他的枪也丢了。”

En: I lost my gun. And I didn't find it. It's gone.
Zh: “我的枪丢了。没找到。它没了。”

En: I found my gun, but then I realized I didn't need it, so I left it behind.
Zh: “枪是找到了，然后我意识到我不需要它了，所以就丢掉了。”

En: Are you drunk right now? You're drunk right now, aren't you, you fucking *bum*. I can smell it!
Zh: “你现在就是醉的对吗？你现在就不清醒，是不是，你这个该死的*流浪汉*。我都闻到了！”

En: Gun. Gun... (Just repeat *gun*.)
Zh: “枪。枪……”（不停地重复*枪*。）

En: Yeah -- gun. The thing that was given to you to *kill people*.
Zh: “是啊——枪。给你用来*杀人*的玩意。”

En: This isn't cause for celebration. It's... bad.
Zh: “这不是庆祝的理由。这个……很糟糕。”

En: I would say it's *very* bad, yes. He turns to you. "You lost your service weapon -- while drunk. And then you couldn't find it, because you were *still* drunk?! You're drunk right now, aren't you?"
Zh: “要我说，是*非常*糟糕，没错。”他转向你。“你弄丢了自己的配枪——在喝醉的时候。然后你找不到了，因为你*还在*醉酒？！你现在也喝醉了，是不是？”

En: Wow, you're being brutalized... Pig on pig violence.
Zh: “哇哦，他们对你真是够残忍的……猪猡对猪猡使用暴力了。”

En: I'm on it. 24/7. I'm tracking it down *right now*.
Zh: “我正在找，7X24小时不停歇。我*现在*正在追查。”

En: Gun? Badge? Car? These are all *things*. Things don't matter. People do.
Zh: “枪？警徽？車？这些都是*东西*。东西不重要。重要的是人。”

En: Gun? Yeah, you don't have that. Maybe you can *philosophize* your way out of it?
Zh: 枪？是啊，你没有。也许你可以通过*哲学探讨*让自己脱身？

En: I don't care about your collection, you hoarding freak. He waves your arsenal away without looking. "Is one of them your service weapon? I only want to know about that *one* gun."
Zh: 他看都没看你的军火库一眼，挥挥手让你拿开。“我不在乎你那些藏品，你这个囤积狂——那里面有你的配枪吗？我只想知道那*一支*枪怎么样了。”

En: Yeah, yeah, I got that. (Show him the boring sidearm.)
Zh: “是啊，是啊，在我这里。”（把无聊的配枪拿给他看。）

En: What?! These are *great* guns. Superior to my sidearm any day.
Zh: “什么？！这些都是*很棒*的枪。比我的配枪好多了。”

En: Shut up! Show me your sidearm, or fuck off.
Zh: “闭嘴！要么把你的配枪拿出来，要么赶紧滚蛋。”

En: Lost gun, lost gun! I won't be *terrorized* with this lost gun shit anymore.
Zh: “失枪，失枪！我再也不会被什么狗屁失枪*恐吓*了。”

En: Here we go again! "Lost gun, lost gun" -- trying to terrorize you!
Zh: 又来了。“失枪，失枪”——想要恐吓你！

En: You're *drunk*, aren't you? You lost your gun and you're drunk! You're a drunk, gunless bum! I can smell it!
Zh: “你*喝醉*了，是吗？你弄丢了枪，而且还喝醉了！你就是个酒鬼，没有枪的流浪汉！我都闻出来了！”
