En: Thank you for the input, Lieutenant Kitsuragi. I didn't mean to suggest you didn't handle the situation... He brushes a stray strand of hair out of his eye and coughs.
Zh: “感谢您的见解，曷城警督。我并不是在暗示你没有处理好那种情况……”

En: You've spent the week with him -- on this case. What is your take?
Zh: 他拨开掉进眼睛里的一缕头发，咳嗽了几声。“你跟他一起度过了一星期——调查这起案子。你有什么看法？”

En: He thinks of apologizing but decides against it.
Zh: 他想过道歉，不过还是决定放弃。

En: It's so much better if he does this. A million times better.
Zh: 如果让他来说，肯定会好很多。好上100万倍。

En: The firefight is a trivial matter compared to the greatest discovery of this *century*.
Zh: “跟本*世纪*最伟大的发现相比，那场枪战根本微不足道。”

En: Wait, it's better if I... he says in a lowered voice.
Zh: “等等，最好还是我……”他压低了声音。

En: Detective, it's better if *I* do that... he says in a lowered voice.
Zh: “警探，这一点由*我*来说效果会更好……”他压低了声音。

En: He did everything he could, the lieutenant interrupts him. "*We* did everything we could. The company hired unvetted mercenaries. Lieutenant Du Bois got between them and the locals."
Zh: “他做了自己能做的一切，”警督打断了他。“*我们*已经尽力了。公司雇佣了未经审查的雇佣兵。杜博阿警督把他们跟本地人隔开了。”

En: He did so at considerable risk to his own life. He was shot -- not once, but twice. We stopped an execution, not a negotiation. The loss of life was minimal compared to what it could have been.
Zh: “他这样做承受了相当大的生命风险。他中枪了——不是一枪，而是二枪。我们阻止了一场处决行动，而不是谈判。与可能发生的情况相比，死亡人数已经最小化了。”

En: He did so at considerable risk to his person. Remember -- he was shot. We stopped an execution, not a negotiation. The loss of life was minimal compared to what it could have been.
Zh: “他这样做承受了相当大的生命风险。记住——他中枪了。我们阻止了一场处决行动，而不是谈判。与可能发生的情况相比，死亡人数已经最小化了。”

En: On the case?
Zh: “对案子的看法吗？”

En: On Lieutenant-Yefreitor Du Bois.
Zh: “对荣誉警督杜博阿的看法。”

En: Well... A cold gust of wind. He pulls up his collar. "The drinking, the lost gun, also losing his badge -- that's all true. *And* he's been drinking on the job."
Zh: “这个嘛……”一阵冷风袭来。他竖起了衣领。“酗酒，失枪，还有弄丢警徽——这都是真的。*而且*他还会在工作期间酗酒。”

En: Here comes the cavalry.
Zh: 救兵要来了。

En: He did so at considerable risk to his own life. He was shot, and survived only because of his armour. We stopped an execution, not a negotiation. The loss of life was minimal compared to what it could have been.
Zh: “他这样做承受了相当大的生命风险。他中枪了，而且仅仅是因为这身盔甲才活了下来。我们阻止了一场处决行动，而不是谈判。与可能发生的情况相比，死亡人数已经最小化了。”

En: I'm just sorry.
Zh: “我只能说我很抱歉。”

En: Stop being sorry for five minutes, he says in a lowered voice.
Zh: “别道歉了，5分钟就好，”他压低声音说道。

En: Well... He pulls up his collar. "The drinking, the gun-losing, also losing the badge -- that's all true. Although he has *not* been drinking on the job this week."
Zh: “这个嘛……”他竖起了衣领。“酗酒，失枪，还有弄丢警徽——这都是真的。不过这周他工作的时候*没有*喝酒。”

En: The man sighs. Deeply.
Zh: 男人叹了口气。深深地。

En: And then there's the motor carriage in the sea, and the drugs, of course, some kind of anti-radiation drug he uses to induce visions... He breathes in sharply. "But -- despite all this -- he is a great detective. One of the best I've seen, in fact."
Zh: “还有海里那辆汽車，毒品，当然了，还有他服用的某种用来感应幻觉的抗辐射药物……”他深吸一口气。“不过——尽管如此——他是个很棒的警探。事实上，他是我见过的最棒的警探之一。”

En: And then there's the motor carriage in the sea, and the drugs, of course -- amphetamine, mostly... He breathes in sharply. "But -- despite all this -- he is a great detective. One of the best I've seen, in fact."
Zh: “还有海里那辆汽車，毒品，当然了——主要是安非他命……”他深吸一口气。“不过——尽管如此——他是个很棒的警探。事实上，他是我见过的最棒的警探之一。”

En: And then there's the motor carriage in the sea, and the constant smoking everywhere, all the time... He breathes in sharply. "But -- despite all this -- he is a great detective. One of the best I've seen, in fact."
Zh: “还有海里那辆汽車，到处不停抽烟，一直在抽……”他深吸一口气。“不过——尽管如此——他是个很棒的警探。事实上，他是我见过的最棒的警探之一。”

En: And then there's the motor carriage in the sea -- something I was *not* present for... He breathes in sharply. "But -- despite all this -- he is a great detective. One of the best I've seen, in fact."
Zh: “还有海里那辆汽車——我当时*并不在*现场……”他深吸一口气。“不过——尽管如此——他是个很棒的警探。事实上，他是我见过的最棒的警探之一。”

En: See?
Zh: “看见没？”

En: *One* week.
Zh: “*一*个星期。”

En: Then there's the apocalypse thing. At first I thought it was a joke -- but it's not. He actually thinks the world is about to end. In a *bloodletting*, or a *gloaming*. We're about to *become vapour*, even...
Zh: “还有那些世界末日的东西。一开始我以为是个玩笑——不过并不是。他真以为这个世界将要走向终结。因为一场*大屠杀*，或者是*黄昏*。我们都会*变成水蒸气*，甚至……”

En: It's worrying. Especially considering his political views. Detective Du Bois is -- as you may know -- a Mazovian socio-economist. He wants to liquidate the ruling class. Which -- again -- for a police officer... is a little odd.
Zh: “很让人担心。特别是考虑到他的政治观点。杜博阿警探——你可能也知道了——是一位马佐夫主义的社会经济学家。他想铲除掉统治阶级。这一点——同样的——作为一名警官来说……有点奇怪。”

En: Then there's the... superstardom. He likes to -- from time to time -- allude to being a *superstar law official*. At first I thought it was a joke, but... now I'm not so sure. He says *disco* about twenty times a day.
Zh: “然后还有……超级明星的事。他喜欢——时不时的——暗指自己是个*超级明星执法人员*。一开始我以为是个玩笑，不过……现在我没那么确定了。他每天大概会说20次*迪斯科*吧。”

En: As you can see -- it's about three metres tall. In fact, we think it may be the largest land invertebrate ever discovered.
Zh: “你也看到了——它大约有3米高。事实上，我们认为它可能是迄今为止发现的最大的陆地无脊椎动物。”

En: You should yell something.
Zh: 你应该喊点什么。

En: I AM A POLICEMAN OF THE STATE TO COME!
Zh: “我是一名未来国家的警察！”

En: That's not a good idea.
Zh: 这不是个好主意。

En: The RCM consists of policemen of the state that *is*. So -- a little discrepancy there. He turns back to Vicquemare.
Zh: “RCM是有警察组成的，也*就是*国家机关。所以——这里有点小矛盾。”他转向维克玛。

En: It's worrying. Especially in light of his political views. Detective Du Bois is -- as you may know -- a fascist. A far-right woman-hater and, of course, also into race-stuff. Which -- for a police officer -- He shakes his head. "Not good."
Zh: “很让人担心。特别是考虑到他的政治观点。杜博阿警探——你可能也知道了——是一个法西斯主义者。一个极右派仇女者，当然，还有种族主义之类的。这一点——作为一名警官来说——”他摇摇头。“不太好。”

En: I had a major collision with him on the subject. He yielded, and is now striving to be a crypto-fascist. That is: a regular police officer who *thinks* these things but doesn't say them. So that's... yeah.
Zh: “在这个问题上我跟他之间发生了很大的冲突。他屈服了，现在正努力成为一个秘密法西斯主义者。也就是说：一个会*想到*这些事情的普通警察，但是并不会说出来。所以那就有点……嗯。”

En: Then there's the... self-flagellation issue. He likes to apologize -- profusely. Making it sound like he's guilty of at least first degree murder. It's not a good communication strategy for an officer. It's...
Zh: “然后还有……自虐的问题。他喜欢道歉——疯狂道歉。听起来就像他至少犯下了一级谋杀罪。对于一个警官来说，这不是一种很好的沟通策略。这就……”

En: Then there's the *boring cop* issue. Despite all the obvious and almost grotesque mannerisms and sartorial choices he still insists he isn't *characteristic* enough. It's...
Zh: “然后还有*无聊警探*的问题。尽管有那么多显而易见和几乎可以说是怪诞的特殊习惯和着装选择，他还是坚持自己不够*有个性*。这就……”

En: Odder still -- he is also a Mazovian socio-economist, who believes in liquidating the ruling class. How he reconciles these two points of view, I do not know -- but he is *vocal* on both of them.
Zh: “更奇怪的是——他还是个马佐夫主义的社会经济学家，相信必须要铲除统治阶级。他是怎么调和这两种观点的，我不太清楚——不过他对这两者都*直言不讳*。”

En: Odder still -- he is also a full blown fascist. An ultranationalist woman-hater and more than a little racist. How he reconciles these two points of view... He shrugs. "But he is *vocal* about both."
Zh: “更奇怪的是——他还是个彻头彻尾的法西斯主义者。一个极端民族主义的仇女者，还是个相当严重的种族主义者。他是怎么调和这两种观点的……”他耸耸肩。“不过他对这两者都*直言不讳*。”

En: It's just strange. Especially in light of his political views. Detective Du Bois is -- as you may know -- a true blue moralist. A man of the centre. Not prone to political outbursts. Which is commendable. But also... at odds with his behaviour.
Zh: “真的太奇怪了。特别是考虑到他的政治观点。杜博阿警探——你可能也知道了——是一个忠实的道德家。一个中间派。不容易引发政治冲突，这一点是可圈可点的。不过……跟他的行为并不一致。”

En: Odder still -- he is also a full blown fascist. An ultranationalist woman-hater and more than a little racist. Which, I guess, goes well with the *grind*? I do not know -- but he is *vocal* about both beliefs.
Zh: “更奇怪的是——他还是个彻头彻尾的法西斯主义者。一个极端民族主义的仇女者，还是个相当严重的种族主义者。这样一来，我猜，跟*刻苦耐劳*挺相配的？我不知道——不过他对两种信仰都挺*直言不讳*的。”

En: Odder still -- he is also an ultraliberal 'hustler' who is always... The lieutenant pinches the root of his nose. "*On the grind.* How he reconciles these two points of view, I do not know -- but he is vocal about both of them."
Zh: “更奇怪的是——他还是个极端自由主义的‘实干家’，总是……”警督捏了捏鼻根。“马力全开。他是怎么调和这两种观点的，我不太清楚——不过他对这两者都直言不讳。”

En: Odder still -- he is also an ultraliberal 'hustler' who is always... The lieutenant pinches the root of his nose. "*On the grind.* Which I guess, goes well with some of his racism. I don't know -- he is vocal about both beliefs though."
Zh: “更奇怪的是——他还是个极端自由主义的‘实干家’，总是……”警督捏了捏鼻根。“*马力全开*。这样一来，我猜跟他的某些种族主义观点挺相配的。我不知道——不过他对两种信仰都挺直言不讳的。”

En: Yes. Let's let the big boys talk.
Zh: 是的，让大人物说话吧。

En: He can talk human beings into telling him *everything*. And he doesn't stop. In all the time I've spent with him, he has not once stopped pursuing leads, however far-fetched and tangential. He is tireless. Madly driven.
Zh: “他可以说服人类把知道的*一切*都告诉他。而且他永远不知停歇。在我跟他相处的这段日子里，他从未停止过追查线索，不管到底有多不靠谱，有多不着边际。他不知疲倦。被某种疯狂的东西驱动着。”

En: He can talk human beings into telling him anything. And he doesn't stop. In all the time I've spent with him, he has not once stopped working on the case. He is tireless. Madly driven.
Zh: “他可以说服人类把知道的一切都告诉他。而且他永远不知停歇。在我跟他相处的这段日子里，他从未停止过对这个案子的调查。他不知疲倦。被某种疯狂的东西驱动着。”

En: Well, except that one time when he stopped to sing karaoke. Which -- by the way -- was a valiant effort. He really sang his heart out.
Zh: “呃，除了有一次，他停下来唱了一次卡拉OK。这一点——顺便说一句——是很英勇的举动。他真的唱出了自己的心声。”

En: Well... except that one time when he stopped to sing karaoke. Which, by the way -- I have to disagree with you, Mr. Vicquemare -- was a valiant effort. He really sang his heart out.
Zh: “呃……除了有一次，他停下来唱了一次卡拉OK。这一点，顺便说一句——我跟你意见不同，维克玛先生——这是一种很英勇的举动。他真的唱出了自己的心声。”

En: Other than that one time, he has tirelessly worked on the case. And he solved it. We have a confession, a murder weapon, *and* the perpetrator -- locked on the island right now, awaiting transportation.
Zh: “除了那一次之外，他一直不知疲倦地调查这起案子。而且还解决了它。我们得到了一份供词，一件凶器，*还有*犯人——现在就锁在小岛上，等待押送。”

En: Okay, he did *something*...
Zh: “好吧，他确实做了点*事情*……”

En: He apprehended a revolutionary brigade who stayed hidden for fifty years, ever since the Revolution; who's probably committed other murders over those years... He pauses. "Oh -- and he also discovered a new species."
Zh: “他逮捕了一名大革命时期的士兵，自从大革命结束之后，他一直隐藏了50年；在这些年间，他可能还犯下了其他凶杀案……”他停顿了一下。“哦——而且他还发现了一个新物种。”

En: And he *solved* it. Near-perfectly. In one week we have: a confession, a murder weapon -- *and* the perpetrator. Locked on the island right now, awaiting transportation.
Zh: “而且他还*破获*了这起案件。近乎完美。在这一周内，我们拿到了：一份供词，一件凶器——*还有*犯人。现在就锁在小岛上，等待押送。”

En: He apprehended a straggler who stayed hidden for fifty years, ever since the Revolution; who's probably committed other murders over those years... He pauses. "Oh -- and he also discovered a new species."
Zh: “他逮捕了一名大革命时期的士兵，自从大革命结束之后，他一直隐藏了50年；在这些年间，他可能还犯下了其他凶杀案……”他停顿了一下。“哦——而且他还发现了一个新物种。”

En: A... new species?
Zh: “一个……新物种？”

En: A colossal stick insect. It was on the island, camouflaged as the reeds. It... unfolded from the reeds. I think we may be dealing with the Insulindian phasmid.
Zh: “一只巨大的竹节虫。它就在那座小岛上，伪装成芦苇的样子。它……从芦苇丛中现出了真身。我想我们可能发现了伊苏林迪竹节虫。”

En: A colossal stick insect -- three metres tall. It's on the island, camouflaged as the reeds. It... unfolded from them. I did not -- unfortunately -- get a photo. But I believe it may have been the Insulindian phasmid.
Zh: “一只巨大的竹节虫——有三米高。它就在那座小岛上，伪装成芦苇的样子。它……从芦苇丛中现出了真身。我没能拍到照片——这一点很不幸。不过我相信它很可能是伊苏林迪竹节虫。”

En: I know this sounds fantastic. But I'm a four times decorated lieutenant of the RCM. I do not make up encounters with *cryptids* to spice up my day. I am very, very sane.
Zh: “我知道这听起来有点异想天开。不过我是一名四次授勋的RCM警督。我不会编出一套邂逅*神秘动物*的说辞，来为我的一天增添趣味。我的神智非常、非常清醒。”

En: I don't doubt you, lieutenant. If you say it was the Insulindian phasmid then it was the Insulindian Phasmid.
Zh: “我不会怀疑你的，警督。如果你说是伊苏林迪竹节虫，那就是伊苏林迪竹节虫。”

En: He takes out the photo of the phasmid and shows it to the officers across the yard. The wind blows, flapping the glossy rectangle in his hand.
Zh: 他拿出竹节虫的相片，展示给院子里的警官看。起风了，那个光滑的长方形物体拍打着他的手心。

En: He takes out the photo and shows it to the officers across the yard. Rain comes down, he covers the glossy photo of the phasmid with his hand.
Zh: 他拿出竹节虫的相片，展示给院子里的警官看。雨滴从天空降落，他用手遮挡住那张光滑的竹节虫相片。

En: He takes out the photo and shows it to the officers across the yard. Thick white snow falls all around you -- flakes stick to the glossy photo of the phasmid.
Zh: 他拿出竹节虫的相片，展示给院子里的警官看。漫天大雪在你周围飞舞——雪花粘在了光滑的竹节虫相片上。

En: Fucking hell is that... The man cranes his neck, still looking at the photo. "Is this somehow... *connected* to the case?"
Zh: “这他妈的是什么……”男人伸长了脖子，目光依然停留在相片上。“这个……跟案子有*关系*吗？”

En: Boom shakalaka, motherfucker!
Zh: “哦耶哦耶，牛逼吗，混帐东西！”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Fucking hell, is that... Is this somehow *connected* to the case? He ignores you, still staring at the phasmid.
Zh: 他没有理会你，目光依然停留在相片上。“他妈的见鬼了，那玩意……跟案子有*关系*吗？”

En: I must say, this, he points to the photo, "is absolutely extraordinary. It's... I don't even have *words* for it."
Zh: “我必须说，这个，”他指向相片。“实在是太超乎寻常了。这……我甚至不知道该*说*什么好了。”

En: The killer did not seem to be *aware* of the phasmid's presence. Exhibiting a strange, atypical dementia, he fell into a stupor after its appearance. He became near-catatonic.
Zh: “凶手似乎并没有*意识*到竹节虫的存在。他表现出一种奇怪的非典型痴呆状态，在它出现之后，他陷入了恍惚。他变得跟紧张性精神症患者没什么区别。”

En: Detective? He turns to you.
Zh: “警探？”他转向你。

En: Boom. Boom. Boom.
Khm... yes. I believe the pheromone it emits may be responsible for the killer's mental degradation.
Zh: “呃……是的。我相信凶手的精神退化问题可能跟它释放出的费洛蒙有关。”

En: The old man was not *aware* of the phasmid's presence. Exhibiting a strange, atypical dementia, he fell into a stupor after its appearance. He became near-catatonic.
Zh: “那个老人似乎并没有*意识*到竹节虫的存在。他表现出一种奇怪的非典型痴呆状态，在它出现之后，他陷入了恍惚。他变得跟紧张性精神症患者没什么区别。”

En: Yes -- but *also*...
So it *is* connected...?
Zh: “所以*确实*有关联……”

En: Yes. It really does make it hard to fire the drunk... His tired eyes follow the photo as the lieutenant puts it away.
Zh: “是的。这真的让人很难解雇这个醉醺醺的混账……”他的目光追随着收起相片的警督。

En: So what do you say? Wanna take this hot shit back? (Point to yourself.)
Zh: “所以你怎么说？还想要我这个高手高手高高手吗？”（指着自己。）

En: This is a very, very sad man who has just seen something that's made him forget his sadness.
Zh: 这是一个非常、非常悲伤的男人，他刚才看见了足以让他忘掉悲伤的东西。

En: Boom. Boom. Boom.
Khm... yes, the phasmid may have contributed to his mental state in some way. Over the years.
Zh: “嗯..是的，竹节虫可能在某种程度上影响了他的精神状态。这么多年以来。”

En: Yes -- but *also*...
It's probably not connected per se -- but the perpetrator knew of its existence.
Zh: “从本质上讲，可能没有关联——不过犯人可能知道它的存在。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: It's incredible. But it was real.
Zh: “很难以置信。不过它是真实存在的。”

En: Boom shakalaka.
Zh: “哦耶哦耶哦耶。”

En: By god... is it somehow *connected* to the case?
Zh: “天啦……那跟这起案子有什么*关联*吗？”

En: Lieutenant -- is it somehow *connected* to the case? He pays you no heed.
Zh: 他没有理会你。“警督——这跟案子有什么*关联*吗？”

En: We could order a search. I could get people from EPIS on the island, entomologists, doctors. There must be signs of it in the reeds -- if we found the Insulindian phasmid, well -- that would be absolutely *exceptional*. The PR value alone...
Zh: “我们可以派人搜查。我可以召集EPIS上岛，还有昆虫学家，医生。芦苇丛里肯定有它的痕迹——如果我们能找到伊苏林迪竹节虫，呃——那就真的太*非同一般*了。仅仅从公关价值考虑……”

En: There! You need to make your case -- now. The floor is all yours, he prepared it well.
Zh: 有了！你需要表达你的观点——就是现在。他已经为你做好了铺垫，现在就看你的表现了。

En: So -- as you can see, I'm a *pretty okay* detective -- and an absolutely GIANT FASCIST.
Zh: “所以——你也看到了，我是个*很不错*的警探——而且还是个绝对的大法西斯主义者。”

En: So -- as you can see, I'm a *pretty okay* detective -- and an absolutely GIANT COMMUNIST.
Zh: “所以——你也看到了，我是个*很不错*的警探——而且还是个绝对的大康米主义者。”

En: So -- as you can see, I'm a *pretty okay* detective -- and an absolutely visionary entrepeneur.
Zh: “所以——你也看到了，我是个*很不错*的警探——而且绝对是个很有远见的企业家。”

En: So -- as you can see, I'm a *pretty okay* detective.
Zh: “所以——你也看到了，我是个*很不错*的警探。”

En: Khm... I *also* started a night club -- and a drug lab -- in the church.
Zh: “咳咳……我*还*开了一家夜店——还有一间毒品实验室——在教堂里。”

En: Khm... I *also* started a night club. In the church.
Zh: “咳咳……我*还*开了一家夜店。在教堂里。”

En: Also, the phasmid was female. The reeds are its nest.
Zh: “还有，那只竹节虫是雌性。芦苇丛就是它的巢穴。”

En: The killer -- Lilianovich Dros -- I have a strong motive for him.
Zh: “那个凶手——利连诺维奇·德罗斯——我找到了他的强力动机。”

En: The killer -- Lilianovich Dros -- we have a strong motive for him.
Zh: “那个凶手——利连诺维奇·德罗斯——我们找到了他的强力动机。”

En: *And* I also fixed the strike situation.
Zh: “*而且*我还解决了罢工问题。”

En: There was also a dead man -- on the boardwalk. A missing person I found.
Zh: “还有一个死人——在木板道上。我发现了一个失踪人口。”

En: It may only be a small thing, but I found a man's jacket!
Zh: “这可能是件小事，不过我帮一个人找到了他的夹克！”

En: I also looked into the mystery of the Doomed Commercial Area.
Zh: “我还调查了诅咒商业区的秘密。”

En: I think it reproduces by parthenogenesis.
Zh: “我认为它是通过单性生殖繁衍的。”

En: You're right. It's *is* a small thing. He looks around. "Does anyone care? About a jacket? No one?"
Zh: “你说得对。这*是*一件小事。”他四下看了看。“有人在乎吗？一件夹克？没人？”

En: The previous head of the Débardeurs' Union was assassinated by *our* killer.
Zh: “装卸工会的前任领导被*我们*这位凶手杀了。”

En: I lied. There *was* a phasmid. It's real. You have to believe me.
Zh: “我撒谎了。那里*有*一只竹节虫。是真的。你必须相信我。”

En: You have to believe me. There *was* a giant phasmid on the island.
Zh: “你必须相信我。岛上*有*一只巨大的竹节虫。”

En: The curse turned out to be possibly entroponetical. Part of my larger investigation -- into Martinaise itself.
Zh: “这个诅咒可能跟灰域有关。这是我大调查的一部分——对马丁内斯本身的调查。”

En: It doesn't look like the lieutenant wishes you to push this angle further.
Zh: 看来警督并不希望你更深入地探讨这个问题。

En: 'Cut it out' is indeed what he is thinking.
Zh: 他现在想的是“闭嘴吧”。

En: Yo -- Imma stop you right there, fuck-face. It's Cuno-time.
Zh: “哟——你先打住吧，混球。现在是坤诺的时间。”

En: No. He does not take the order. "Cuno's going *all in*."
Zh: “不。”他没有接受命令。“坤诺要*全盘*参与。”

En: It's just strange. Especially in light of his political views. Detective Du Bois is -- as you may know -- a true blue moralist. He recently attempted to contact a Coalition warship in order to assign 'la responsabilité', which is commendable. But also... at odds with his own behaviour.

It's just strange. Especially in light of his political views. Detective Du Bois is -- as you may know -- a true blue moralist. A man of the centre. Not prone to political outbursts. Which is commendable. But also... at odds with his behaviour.

It's worrying. Especially considering his political views. Detective Du Bois is -- as you may know -- a Mazovian socio-economist. He's even gotten involved in a highly theoretical underground reading group. Which -- again -- for a police officer... is unusual.

It's worrying. Especially considering his political views. Detective Du Bois is -- as you may know -- a Mazovian socio-economist. He wants to liquidate the ruling class. Which -- again -- for a police officer... is a little odd.

It's worrying. Especially in light of his political views. Detective Du Bois is -- as you may know -- a fascist. He's spent *hours* trying to find a way to 'reverse time' with other far-right individuals. And, of course, there's the race-stuff. Which -- for a police officer -- He shakes his head. "Not good."

It's worrying. Especially in light of his political views. Detective Du Bois is -- as you may know -- a fascist. A far-right woman-hater and, of course, also into race-stuff. Which -- for a police officer -- He shakes his head. "Not good."

It's odd. Especially in light of his political views. Detective Du Bois is -- as you may know -- a 'hustler'. He... the lieutenant pinches the root of his nose, "recently commissioned a monument to his own 'grind' in the middle of the roundabout, which is just..." He shakes his head.

It's odd. Especially in light of his political views. Detective Du Bois is -- as you may know -- a 'hustler'. He... The lieutenant pinches the root of his nose. "Grinds. He makes *the reál*. He's a 'visionary' and a 'money engineer', which is just..." He shakes his head.
Well, good luck finding her once we get back.
Zh: “等我们回去之后，祝愿你能找到她。”

En: There's an enormous sadness in that admission.
Zh: 这份坦白里隐含着一种巨大的悲伤。

En: Jean -- I think he hasn't. I can see it on his face...
Zh: “让——我想他没有。我能从他的脸上看出来……”

En: Even the insect -- I don't care. But you're an *alcoholic*. And you've been drinking -- again. I won't let my life unravel because of this.
Zh: “就算是有什么昆虫——我不在乎。不过你是一个*酒鬼*。而且你——又开始喝酒了。我不会因为这件事把自己的生活搞得乱七八糟。”

En: He has... That's true.
Zh: “他……这是真的。”

En: It's tough. One of the toughest addictions to overcome. Comparable *only* to heavy synthetic opiates. Even morphine is easier to kick than alcohol -- statistically. The odds are against him. Especially at his age.
Zh: “这很难。这是最难戒掉的一种瘾。*只有*重度合成阿片类药物与它相匹敌。就连吗啡都比酒精容易戒——从统计学上看是这样的。他戒掉的可能性不大。特别是对于他这把年纪来说。”

En: The bloating *has* gone down since you woke up that morning...
Zh: 自从你那天早上醒来后，你脸上那种浮肿*已经*减轻了……

En: Okay, so he's stayed clear for what? A week? He sighs.
Zh: “好吧，所以他清醒了多久？一星期？”他叹了口气。

En: No. You can figure it out. *Replace* it! Replace the alcohol with amphetamine. Or GBL! Fuck it -- morphine! Graffito removal agent! Anything. It'll buy you time. All you need is time.
Zh: 不。你可以搞定的。*代替*它！用安非他命代替酒精。或者是丁内酯！去他的——吗啡！涂鸦清除剂！任何东西。这样能为你争取时间。你唯一需要的只有时间。

En: No. You *can* stop. Just wade through the hell. Month after month. Year after year -- you against the nothingness. It's possible, because *time* is possible.
Zh: 不。你*可以*停下来。只要趟过地狱就可以。月复一月。年复一年——你将会跟虚无对抗。这是可能的，因为*时间*充满可能性。

En: If you declare your life forfeit; if you declare yourself a doomed man... A doomed man does not need comfort. From *anything*. He can go on without drink, or help. Like a clockwork.
Zh: 如果你断言生活已经了无希望；如果你断言自己是个被诅咒的人……被诅咒的人不需要安慰。*任何*安慰都不需要。他可以继续走下去，没有酒精，没有帮助。就像是一个时钟。

En: For the world.
Zh: 为了世界。

En: For Revachol.
Zh: 为了瑞瓦肖。

En: For the planetary proletariat.
Zh: 为了行星的工人阶级。

En: For the Revachol Citizens Militia. A blue forget-me-not in the grey sky.
Zh: 为了瑞瓦肖公民武装。灰色天空中的那一朵勿忘我。

En: For revenge.
Zh: 为了复仇。

En: He nods. "He's too old. He's been like this for too long. I've seen him try many times. It's a farce by now."
Zh: 他点点头。“他太老了。他这个样子已经持续很久了。我见他尝试过很多次。现在已经变成了一场闹剧。”

En: Compared to the intersection, in front of Video Revachol 24 -- it's nothing. Compared to that, everything is possible.
Zh: 跟瑞瓦肖24小时录像店前的十字路口相比——这根本不算什么。跟那个相比，一切都是可能的。

En: Think! You can *detect*. In Jamrock. Boogie Street. Couron. Le Royaume, underground. Coal City. On the 8/81-- it will all be yours to sieve through when the last snow has melted.
Zh: 想一想！你能够*察觉*到。在加姆洛克。布吉街。科戎。王国。煤城。在8/81高速公路上——当最后一场雪融化的时候，一切都将由你来筛选。

En: Stop and you can go to the Burnt Out Quarter. And meet me there -- wafting above the burnt ruins. Like the smell of caramel -- Le Retour is waiting.
Zh: 停下来，你可以去燃尽区。在那里与我相遇——飘过燃尽的废墟上空。就像焦糖的味道——归来正在等待。

En: The houses, the streets, the motorways singing. Hold on. This is a fight we can *win*.
Zh: 房子，街道，高速公司，都在歌唱。坚持住。这是一场我们可以*打赢*的战争。

En: They're leaving. They're all turning away from you.
Zh: 他们要走了。他们全都离开了你。

En: I can -- I will just pass time until my mind reconfigures from the abuse it's taken. I can do that now. I've taught myself.
Zh: “我可以——我可以消磨时光，直到我的大脑从受到的虐待当中恢复过来。我现在就可以这么做。我教会自己了。”

En: My life is forfeit. All I'm good for is this work. I *will* quit.
Zh: “我的生活已经了无希望。我唯一能胜任的只有这份工作。我*会*戒掉的。”

En: I know it's difficult to believe, but I will do it. For the Kingdom.
Zh: “我知道这很难相信，不过我会做的。为了王国。”

En: I know it's difficult to believe, but I will do it. For myself. For *my* life.
Zh: “我知道这很难相信，不过我会做的。为了我自己。为了*我的*生活。”

En: I know it's difficult to believe, but I will do it. For the RCM.
Zh: “我知道这很难相信，不过我会做的。为了RCM。”

En: I know it's difficult to believe, but I will do it. For the white banner of the Commune.
Zh: “我知道这很难相信，不过我会做的。为了公社的白色旗帜。”

En: I'm not that person anymore. I became something else and that something else *will* quit.
Zh: “我已经不是那个人了。我变成了别的人，而且那个人*会*戒掉的。”

En: I'm not that person anymore. I became something else. And that something else *has* quit.
Zh: “我已经不是那个人了。我变成了别的人，而且那个人*已经*戒掉了。”

En: I need to be the greatest, Jean. I was *born* for this. I *will* stop. To be better.
Zh: “我需要成为最伟大的人，让。我就是*为此而生*的。我*会*停下来。我会变好的。”

En: I will win, Jean. I swear to god and to Revachol, I will win.
Zh: “我会成功的，让。我向老天和瑞瓦肖发誓，我会成功的。”

En: Listen to this shit, man... He throws up his hands.
Zh: “听听这些屁话，老兄……”他举起双手。

En: I cannot. I cannot live without this job... fuck you then! And fuck me! I'll go.
Zh: “我不能。没有这份工作的话，我没办法活下去……你他妈的去死吧！我也该死！我会走的。”

En: Fucking Harry... Fuck you for bringing this kid with you. It's *only* because he's defending you -- it's the *only* reason you're not staying here to die.
Zh: “去你妈的，哈里……你他妈的居然让这个小孩跟着你。*只是*因为他在为你辩护——这是你*唯一*不会留在这里等死的理由。”

En: You manipulative son of a bitch... He shakes his head and sniffles. "It *is* cold, and we have vehicles in the square. The perp needs to be taken into custody. Let's get a move on."
Zh: “你这个喜欢指使别人的狗杂种……”他摇摇头，吸了吸鼻子。“这里*实在*是太冷了，我们的車就停在广场。罪犯需要被拘留起来。我们赶紧走吧。”

En: It's more than that. He just can't. One final time...
Zh: 远远不止这些。他就是做不到。最后一次……

En: Detective. She looks at Vicquemare. "I just don't want this *trial* to go on any longer. It's cold outside, and..."
Zh: “警探。”她看着维克玛。“我只是不想这场*审判*继续下去。外面太冷了，而且……”

En: He slowly nods.
Zh: 他慢慢点点头。

En: You manipulative fuck... Fine. It's cold and we have vehicles in the square. And the perp needs to be taken into custody too -- let's get a move on.
Zh: “你这个喜欢指使别人的混账……好吧。这里实在是太冷了，我们的車就停在广场。而且罪犯也需要被拘留起来——我们赶紧走吧。”

En: No. We're done, Harry. You're drunk. You'll get a station call once you've sobered up.
Zh: “不。我们完事了，哈里。你喝醉了。等你清醒之后，你会收到一份警局传唤通知。”

En: No one knows what to say. But they agree. Keeping you on the force endangers civilians.
Zh: 没人知道该说什么。不过他们也表示同意。让你继续留在警队会危及平民的安全。

En: No. There's a silence. "Sure, you got the case together -- you can *do* policework -- in bouts. That's not a surprise to me."
Zh: “不。”一阵沉默。“当然了，你破案了——你可以*完成*警察工作——不过是一阵一阵的。这在我看来没什么好惊讶的。”

En: And Station 41 and the glow of its desk lamps, the clicking of coffee mugs, clouds of cigarette smoke and the ringing telephone... your youth. There is still a way back.
Zh: 还有41分局，台灯的微光，咖啡杯叮当声，香烟的烟雾，铃铃作响的电话……你的青春。还有一条回去的路。

En: Okay. He sighs. "We have vehicles in the square. And the perpetrator needs to be taken into custody. Let's go."
Zh: “好吧。”他叹了一口气。“我们的車就停在广场。而且罪犯也需要被拘留起来。我们走吧。”

En: Agreed. The public relations potential of this is too valuable to let go.
Zh: “同意。这个的公共关系潜力太有价值了，不能就这么放弃。”

En: I don't *want* to. But you discovered a new species. And solved the murder... He shrugs.
Zh: “我*不想*这样。但是你发现了一个新物种。而且还破获了凶杀案……”他耸耸肩。

En: You haven't been drinking, she thinks. So maybe this time...
Zh: 你没有喝酒，她想到。所以也许这次……

En: But he's been drinking, she thinks. This is exactly how he gets out of this every time. It's bad for him, but...
Zh: 不过他在喝酒，她想到。他每次都是这样脱身的。这样对他很不好，不过……

En: Anything that ends the *trial* is okay with me. A quick nod.
Zh: 她飞快地点点头。“只要能结束这场*审判*，我怎样都可以。”

En: Honestly -- anything that ends this *trial* is okay with me. A quick nod.
Zh: 飞快地点点头。“说实在的——只要能结束这场*审判*，我怎样都可以。”

En: Let's go. (No time.)
Zh: “我们走吧。”（马上出发。）

En: Wait. I have a *few* questions before we go. About who I am.
Zh: “等等。在我们离开之前，我还有*几个*问题。关于我是谁。”

En: The man looks westward, impatiently.
Zh: 男人看向西边，有些不耐烦。

En: Who *am* I?
Why am I *like* this?
Zh: “我为什么会*这样*？”

En: Okay. Am I a dirty cop working for La Puta Madre?
Zh: “好吧。我是帮马德雷教父做事的黑警吗？”

En: Precinct 41... what kind of station is it?
Zh: “41分局……是一个什么样的分局？”

En: The phasmid -- I need to tell Lena about this ASAP.
Zh: “竹节虫——我需要把这件事立即告诉莉娜。”

En: Lieutenant Kitsuragi -- what will you do now?
Zh: “曷城警督——现在你怎么办？”

En: Cuno -- what will you do now?
Zh: “坤诺——现在你怎么办？”

En: I'm ready. (End.)
Zh: “我准备好了。”（总结。）

En: Okay then. Fuck it. I have a shack here. I don't need you all.
Zh: “那好吧。去他妈的。我在这里有一间小棚户。我不需要你们。”

En: Wait. I have a *few* questions before you go. About who I am...
Zh: “等等。在你们离开之前，我还有*一些*问题。关于我是谁……”

En: I'll just go back to the island and *live* there. I don't care.
Zh: “我会回到岛上，在那里*生活*。我不在乎。”

En: Good bye then. I'll just become a bum now. A bum cop.
Zh: “那就再见吧。现在我就当我的流浪汉去。流浪警探。”

En: About who you *are*?
Zh: “关于你*是*谁？”

En: Don't pity yourself. You've managed not to die thus far. You'll manage now.
Zh: “不要自哀自怜的。到目前为止，你还没把自己弄死。不过现在差不多了。”

En: The farce needs to end. Everyone sees that.
Zh: 这场闹剧必须结束。大家都能看出来。

En: Detective. She looks at Vicquemare. "I just don't want this *trial* to go on any longer. It's cold outside, and... let's just go."
Zh: “警探。”她看着维克玛。“我只是不想这场*审判*继续下去。外面太冷了，而且……我们还是走吧。”

En: Jingling his car keys in his pocket.
Zh: 車钥匙在他的口袋里叮当直响。

En: Who *are* you? You're a gym teacher, Harry.
Zh: “你*是*谁？你是个体育老师，哈里。”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: Well -- now you're a cop, he pauses, "or... not anymore, so I guess you're a gym teacher again."
Zh: “呃——现在你是个警察，”他停顿下来，“或者……不再是了，所以我猜你又变成体育老师了。”

En: Well -- obviously you're not a gym teacher anymore, but...
Zh: “好吧——显然你不再是个体育老师了，不过……”

En: Before you were a cop, you were a gym teacher in Couron. She looks around. "It's getting really cold outside. Should we maybe..."

En: Mack 'The Torso' Torson and Chester McLaine -- She arches an eyebrow. "They're not fit to run a wing. Believe me. Things are shaky as it is."
Zh: “‘躯干’麦克·托森和切斯特·麦克莱恩——”她弯起眉头。“他们不适合管理。相信我。情况本来就不太稳定。”

En: Us? We're the *Bloody Murder Station*, haven't you heard? We're the bad guys. No one likes us.
Zh: “我们？我们现在是*血腥杀手分局*了，你没听说过吗？我们是坏人。没人喜欢我们。”

En: That's not true. Jamrock is too big for one precinct. You're just understaffed. And everyone respects the 41st -- you have Captain Pryce.
Zh: “这不是真的。加姆洛克对于一个辖区来说太大了。你们人手不足。不过每个人都很尊敬41分局——你们有普赖斯警长。”

En: Thank you, lieutenant. You're being kind. It *is* an understaffed station and the district *is* too big -- which is why we need to... He tilts his head northward...
Zh: “谢谢你，警督。你很宽容。我们分局*的确*人手不足，辖区也*的确*太大了——这就是我们为什么需要……”他把头歪向北边……

En: They *are* damn iconic though -- Torson and McLaine!
Zh: 不过他们*的确*是该死的标志性人物——托森和麦克莱恩！

En: An iconic duo I take it?
Zh: “我猜是一个偶像双人组？”

En: Yeah -- not like *us*. Two clinically depressed old men. Where's the contrast here? We're garbage.
Zh: “是啊——不像*我们*。两个被临床诊断为抑郁症的老人。这里有什么好对比的？我们是垃圾。”

En: And the C-Wing is...
Zh: “那C翼是……”

En: God... He sighs. "There are four wings, Harry: A, B, C, and D. We're in C. It's made of losers and clock-punchers. You and I *re-conceptualized* it as a task force. It was a mistake."
Zh: “天啦……”他叹了口气。“一共有4翼，哈里。A，B，C还有D。我们属于C翼。里面都是一帮失败者，还有每天只等着下班打卡的人。你和我把它*重新概念化*了，变成一个特别行动小组。这是个错误。”

En: There's also a lot of outside help involved. Not only me. He smiles. "Other losers too."
Zh: “还有很多外援也参与了。不仅仅是我。”他笑了。“还有其他失败者。”

En: And Pryce is...
Zh: “那普赖斯是……”

En: Ptolemy Pryce? He's the son of the old Pryce -- one of the founders of the RCM.
Zh: “托勒密·普赖斯？他是老普赖斯的儿子——RCM的创始人之一。”

En: He's one of the most highly regarded men in the force. You're lucky.
Zh: “他是警队里最受尊敬的人之一。你很幸运。”

En: So I work in the Bloody Murder Station? (Conclude.)
Zh: “所以我在血腥杀手分局工作？”（总结。）

En: Jamrock is the largest ghetto in Revachol. Faubourg, technically... but it's divided into *eleven* districts. Jamrock only has us.
Zh: “加姆洛克是瑞瓦肖最大的贫民区。严格意义上讲，是市郊……不过它被划分成了*11*个区域。加姆洛克只有我们分局。”

En: The press will blow over, he says in a reassuring tone. "Jamrock is lucky to have you. And it's often considered to be the greatest of the districts -- you're lucky to have it."
Zh: “舆论会平息的，”他的口吻很让人宽慰。“加姆洛克有你是一种幸运。而且它通常被认为是最伟大的区域——这也是你的幸运。”

En: He's anything but a loser. Although he would like to be seen as one. It's cooler that way.
Zh: 他可不是什么失败者。尽管他也愿意被看作是一个失败者。这样更酷一些。

En: Thank you again, lieutenant.
Zh: “再次感谢你，警督。”

En: Okay. It's an understaffed station where there's too much work, so we need to get back to it. It's not your problem anymore.
Zh: “好吧。我们的警局人手不足，工作却多得堆成山，所以我们要回去了。这已经不再是你的问题了。”

En: A lowset house, windswept -- and without you. Traffic whooshes by...
Zh: 一栋低矮的房子，暴露在风雨中——而且没有你。車流在旁边呼啸而过……

En: That's not true...
Zh: “这不是真的……”

En: What?! Bitches dream of the 41st. Why you think Cuno's *in* this shit? Word is it's fucking violent. Your Captain Pryce dusted like... a thousand people.
Zh: “什么？！婊子们都很向往41分局。你以为坤诺为什么要*参与*这种破事？都说那里他妈的很暴力。你们的普赖斯警长干掉了……差不多几千号人吧。”

En: Thank you, Cuno, he says with an amused half-smile. "You're being kind. It's an understaffed station and the district is too big -- which is why we need to..." He tilts his head northward and clicks his tongue.
Zh: “谢谢你，坤诺，”他被逗乐了。“你很善良。那是个人手不足的分局，而且辖区还特别大——这就是我们为什么需要……”他把头歪向北边，咂了下舌头。

En: ... get back to it. We left Torson and McLaine to run the C-Wing. It's not good.
Zh: “……马上回去。我们留下托森和麦克莱恩管理C翼。这样不太好。”

En: Okay -- it's not the Bloody Murder Station. It's an old converted silk mill with green desk lamps and a coffee corner. A lot of good people work there. Hard. Every day.
Zh: “好吧——不是什么血腥杀手分局，只是一个改造过的旧丝绸厂，里面有绿色的台灯，还有个咖啡角。很多很棒的人在那里工作。很努力。每天如此。”

En: Did we recently shoot up a church by any chance? (Point to the church.)
Zh: “我们最近有没有碰巧突袭过一座教堂？”（指向教堂。）

En: So he remembers *that*... Yes, there may have been a raid on *some* churches. It wasn't good press.
Zh: “所以他还记得*那个*……是的。可能是有针对*某些*教堂的突袭。这可不是什么好新闻。”

En: What happened? Why did we need to go there?
Zh: “出什么事了？我们为什么要去那里？”

En: Our *enemies* were hiding in *a* church -- to the best of our information. That's it. I'm not talking about this anymore. Your security clearance is *shit-tier* right now. You have to wait for it to go up.
Zh: “我们的*敌人*藏在*一座*教堂里——根据我们得到的消息。就这些。我不会再谈论这个话题了。你的安全许可现在很*低级*。等它升级之后再说。”

En: Shooting up churches never is. I was out of town, to be clear....
Zh: “突袭教堂从来不是什么好新闻。我得声明一点，我当时不在城里……”

En: He means it. The RCM and its enemies will not be discussed on this coast.
Zh: 他是认真的。不能在海岸上讨论RCM和它的敌人的话题。

En: Cuno's likin' this shit. Jamrock is the real shit -- Martinaise is fucking white as balls. Sea-shit. This ain't a real ghetto.
Zh: “坤诺喜欢这种狗屁玩意。加姆洛克真的跟屎一样——马丁内斯他妈的白的跟个球一样。大海什么的狗屁。这里不是真正的贫民区。”

En: Your clearance will not go up while you're within earshot of the Union headquarters.
Zh: 你现在还在工会总部的听力所及范围之内，你的等级是不会提升的。

En: Yeah, that shit is fucking famous on Channel 8. Cuno hears this shit.
Zh: “是啊，而且这狗屁玩意在8频道他妈的很出名。坤诺听过。”

En: They speak of change. The city. The tension on the streets. They speak of the events of April and the blood on the streets in May.
Zh: 他们说到了变化。这座城市。街道上的紧张氛围。他们说的是四月的大事件，还有五月街上的流血事件。

En: Thank you, Cuno. You bring light to my day.
Zh: “谢谢你，坤诺。你照亮了我的一天。”

En: Well -- first I will go back to my station and write the most detailed report anyone has ever seen. It will have to be good to cover all this. Then I will have a serious talk with my captain.
Zh: “呃——首先我会回警局，写下一篇所有人都前所未见的详尽报告。必须要写好一点，把所有细节都包含进去。然后我会跟我们警长严肃地谈一谈。”

En: Detective, we just stopped a small-scale war. Something is happening to Revachol. He pulls up his collar and looks around, the cold spring light reflected in the lenses of his glasses.
Zh: 他拉起衣领，四下看了看，春光反射在他的镜片上。“警探，我们阻止了一场小规模的战争。有些事正在瑞瓦肖悄悄酝酿。”

En: About what?
Zh: “关于什么的？”

En: Detective, we just stopped a small-scale war. Something is happening to Revachol. He takes a look around -- into the deepening shadows of the streets -- then pulls up his collar.
Zh: 他环顾四周——看着街道上越来越深的阴影——然后拉起衣领。“警探，我们阻止了一场小规模的战争。有些事正在瑞瓦肖发生。”

En: Le Retour...
Zh: 归来……

En: Distant traffic. A scrap of newspaper drifts by, carried by the wind.
Zh: 远处的交通。一张废弃的报纸随风飘荡。

En: It says: "Tensions rise in Terminal YC In light of the Débardeurs' strike in Terminal B, among representatives of heavy industry in Coal City..." you read.
Zh: 它说：“鉴于B中转站的装卸工罢工事件，YC中转站的紧张局势正在加剧，煤城的重工业代表们……”你念到。

En: I don't know what, yet -- but it's going to be a hard spring for the RCM. We need to get ready. Infiltrate. Investigate.
Zh: “我暂时还不知道是什么——不过对于RCM来说，这将会是一个艰难的春天。我们必须做好准备。潜入。调查。”

En: Talk to Captain Pryce? I'd rather not ruffle the feathers of *two* captains with my doom-mongering.
Zh: “跟普赖斯警长聊聊？还是不要用我的灾难性预言去触怒*两位*警长比较好。”

En: Want to do that at Station 41?
Zh: “何不在41分局做这些呢？”

En: No, I meant *investigate*. Come work in Precinct 41.
Zh: “不，我的意思是*调查*。来41分局工作。”

En: Work *with* Pryce? A crooked smile quivers on his lips. "I'm flattered, but I don't know if I..."
Zh: “跟普赖斯*一起*工作？”他的嘴角露出一弯微笑。“我受宠若惊，不过我不知道能不能……”

En: Would fit in? Am crazy enough? Can take the stress? He doesn't know how to finish the sentence.
Zh: 能不能适应？我够疯狂吗？能承受住压力吗？他不知道该怎么说完这句话。

En: This truly came as a surprise to him. Not a bad one. But he's at a loss.
Zh: 这确实让他大吃一惊。倒也不坏。但他有些不知所措。

En: I would have to tie things up in GRIH first. But... I mean, whatever is coming -- Jamrock *will* be more central to it than the harbour. The lieutenant turns very serious all of a sudden.
Zh: 突然间，警督变得非常严肃。“好吧，我要先处理好GRIH的事。但是……我的意思是，不管会发生什么——加姆洛克都*会*比港口更加重要。”

En: He meant *join* us. At Precinct 41. Transfer.
Zh: “他的意思是*加入*我们。来41分局。调动。”

En: Yeah. Sure.
Zh: “是啊。当然。”

En: Flattered? You're Lieutenant Kitsuragi. *We* would be flattered if you even considered...
Zh: “受宠若惊？你可是曷城警督。哪怕是你愿意考虑一下，受宠若惊的应该是*我们*……”

En: Perhaps... He watches the scrap of newspaper hit the ground. "There are people who say the Zone of Control will not hold up long. They're not on the radio -- yet. But there are parties out there..."
Zh: “也许吧……”他看着报纸掉落在地上。“有人说控制区撑不了多久。收音机里还没提——暂时没有。不过外面有些党派……”

En: Perhaps... He pauses, deep in thought. "There are people who say the Zone of Control will not hold up long. They're not on the radio -- yet. But there are parties out there..."
Zh: “也许吧……”他停下来，陷入沉思。“有人说控制区撑不了多久。收音机里还没提——暂时没有。不过外面有些党派……”

En: Wait, you mean The Return?
Zh: “等等，你的意思是归来？”

En: No -- good luck with *your* report. The lieutenant smiles warmly.
Zh: “不——祝愿*你的*报告一切顺利，”警督亲切地笑了。

En: Dammit. Yes. *That*.
Zh: 该死。是的。还有*那个*。

En: We're looking at a real brick here. The sooner you start the better.
Zh: 我们看到的是一个真正的好心人。你越早开始越好。

En: Good luck with your report.
Zh: “祝愿你的报告一切顺利。”

En: Yes. His face darkens. "I hear worries -- from systems analysts in Vesper and Messina. International risks departments in banks. People like that."
Zh: “是的。”他的脸沉了下来。“我听过类似的担忧——来自维斯珀和墨西拿的系统分析师。银行的国际风险部。就是他们一类的人。”

En: We've been hearing whispers in Jamrock too. Ever since fall. I agree that something is happening. People are tense. I hear a fan spinning. Fast. Something is about to hit it.
Zh: “我在加姆洛克也听到一些传闻。自从秋天就开始了。我同意有些事正在发生。大家都很紧张。我听见风扇转动的声音。很快。有什么东西就要砸中它了。”

En: And we also have a huge case load, lieutenant, she says with a smile. "Piles that we need to get back to. Mountains, even."
Zh: “而且我们还有一大堆案子要处理，警督，”她笑着说道。“成堆的东西等着我们去处理。像小山一样。”

En: Who is Lena?
Zh: “莉娜是谁？”

En: No one. Forget it.
Zh: “谁也不是。忘了吧。”

En: A cryptozoologist. She lives in Jamrock. She told me about this phasmid.
Zh: “一个神秘动物学家。她住在加姆洛克。她跟我说过竹节虫的事。”

En: A cryptozoologist. She lives in Jamrock, on Tabernacle road. She told me about this phasmid.
Zh: “一个神秘动物学家。她住在加姆洛克的圣堂路。她跟我说过竹节虫的事。”

En: Tabernacle? It's on the way over. Near where you live, on Perdition... She looks at Vicquemare.
Zh: “圣堂路？正好路过。离你住的地方很近，永劫路……”她看着维克玛。

En: She and her husband were conducting the search for the phasmid. It's their discovery -- in part. He turns to you. "I'm sure we'll find them."
Zh: “她和她丈夫在海岸边搜寻竹节虫。这是他们的发现——在某种程度上是。”他转向你。“我们肯定会找到他们的。”

En: She and her husband were conducting the search for the phasmid. It's their discovery -- in part.
Zh: “她和她丈夫在海岸边搜寻竹节虫。这是他们的发现——在某种程度上是。”

En: Fine. If we're gonna drop you off anyway.
Zh: “好吧。反正我们要把你送回去。”

En: She lives at 1113 Tabernacle Road, in Jamrock. Remember?
Zh: 她说自己住在圣堂路1113号，加姆洛克。还记得吗？

En: They should know as soon as possible. Even if we didn't get a photo...
Zh: “应该把这个消息立即告诉他们。尽管我们没有相片……”

En: They should know as soon as possible. It would do you good to deliver some positive news for a change.
Zh: “应该把这个消息立即告诉他们。送去好消息对你来说是个很好的转变。”

En: Already did. Let's go?
Zh: “已经办好了。我们走吧？”

En: She is going to be over the moon. It will do you good to deliver some *good* news to someone for a change.
Zh: 她一定会欣喜若狂的。给某人送去些*好*消息对你来说是个很好的转变。

En: Watch out or she'll faint.
Zh: 要小心些，不然她会晕过去的。

En: Well, you can do that in all the spare time you're going to have.
Zh: “呃，你很快会有大把大把的业余时间去做这件事。”

En: She's a cryptozoologist. She told me about this phasmid...
Zh: “她是个神秘动物学家。她跟我说过竹节虫的事……”

En: She listens to you sadly. The wind blows.
Zh: 她难过地听着你说话。起风了。

En: Jean... She stops.
Zh: “让……”她停了下来。

En: THE FUCK?! He spreads his arms. "Cuno's been *seen* with you, out in the open. He's free game right now. Singin' to the popophone. Fuckin' informant shit. Got pig all over me -- smoked pig."
Zh: “什么鬼？！”他张开双臂。“坤诺被*看到*跟你在一起，就在外面。现在他是自由人了。跟着条子一起混。是该死的线人。我现在完完全全就是个猪猡了——被烟熏过的猪猡。”

En: Fuck do you think? Gonna rock that law enforcement shit with you guys -- Detective Cuno. He chews on a piece of imaginary chewing gum. "Like you promised."
Zh: “你他妈在想什么？当然是跟你们这帮家伙一起让执法部门摇滚起来啊——警探坤诺。”他咀嚼着一片幻想中的口香糖。“就像你承诺过的。”

En: But I didn't promise anything.
Zh: “但我什么都没承诺过。”

En: Guys. Cuno's enrolling in a junior officer program.
Zh: “伙计们。坤诺要加入初级警员项目。”

En: Yeah... no. You're staying here, Cuno. You're too young.
Zh: “是啊……不。你要待在这里，坤诺。你还太年轻了。”

En: Like you... promised?
Zh: “你……承诺过？”

En: You gonna *lie* after what we just been through? Let Cuno refresh you: Cuno said he'd take your crippled ass to the island, if you take Cuno to Jamrock. Boatman Cuno.
Zh: “我们刚刚一起经历了那么多之后，你打算*撒谎*吗？让坤诺再提醒你一下：坤诺说可以把你这个死瘸子带到岛上去，如果你带坤诺去加姆洛克。船夫坤诺。”

En: I'm quite sure it wasn't like that, sire.
Zh: 吾辈很确定不是那样的，大人。

En: Perhaps it was an *unspoken* agreement between you and the junior officer?
Zh: 也许你和这位初级警员之间有过一些*不言而喻*的协议？

En: No, he's not. He's twelve.
Zh: “不，他不行。他才12岁。”

En: Cuno's fuckin' nineteen.
Zh: “坤诺他妈的19了。”

En: Hey, f****t! You don't get it. Cuno's got pig all over him -- Cuno smells of bacon grease. Been *seen* with you. Fuckin' informant shit. You think no one *sees* this? He spreads his arms.
Zh: “喂，混球！你没明白。坤诺现在是个完完全全的猪猡了——坤诺闻起来都是一股培根的油腻味。被人*看见*跟你在一起。他妈的坤诺就像个线人。你以为没人*看见*吗？”他张开双臂。

En: You have to be *fifteen* for the junior officer program -- you're not even that.
Zh: “你必须到*15*岁才能参加初级警员项目——你甚至还没到呢。”

En: They're gonna hang me by the nuts if you leave me here. You want that on you? A dead kid? He narrows his eyes. "Or you want Cuno at your station, solving shit? Like we *just* did."
Zh: “如果你把我留在这里，他们会把我绞死的。你希望这样吗？一个死去的小孩？”他眯起眼睛。“或者你想让坤诺加入你们分局，解决这摊破事？就像我们*刚刚*做的一样。”

En: Jean. I want Cuno at our station, solving this shit.
Zh: “让。我想让坤诺加入我们分局，一起解决这摊破事。”

En: His dad's in a coma and his only friend is gone.
Zh: “他的爸爸还在昏迷，而且他唯一的朋友也不在了。”

En: He was good. On the island. He can do this.
Zh: “他挺好的。在岛上的时候。他可以的。”

En: (Lower your voice.) "Jean, he has nothing."
Zh: （压低声音。）“让，他什么都没有了。”

En: I know a hundred kids with nothing, Harry. We can't make them all cops.
Zh: “我认识一百个一无所有的孩子，哈里。我们不能把他们都变成警察吧。”

En: You don't have to make *them* cops -- only Cuno. Cuno's an asset.
Zh: “你不用把*他们*变成警察——只要坤诺就行。坤诺是个值得培养的人。”

En: His hands are shaking and his face is white.
Zh: 他双手颤抖，面色发白。

En: I'm sorry. We just can't. It's not possible.
Zh: “很抱歉。我们真的做不到。这不可能。”

En: Not POSSIBLE is me staying here. *Fucking* understand that. I'm not dying here, f****t. I'm coming with you.
Zh: “我待在这里才是不可能。*他妈的*你能明白吗。我不会死在这里的，混球。我要跟你一起走。”

En: You're what? Ten? Eleven?
Zh: “你多大了？10岁？11岁？”

En: Cuno's fuckin' nineteen.
Zh: “坤诺他妈的19了。”

En: You have to be *fifteen* for the junior officer program -- and you are not.
Zh: “你必须到*15*岁才能参加初级警员项目——但是你没有。”

En: Hey, f****t! Understand this -- they're gonna hang me by the nuts if you leave me here. You want that on you? A dead kid? *Or* you want the Cuno at your station, solving shit? Like we *just* did?
Zh: “喂，混球！你要明白——如果你把我留在这里，他们会把我绞死的。你希望这样吗？一个死去的小孩？*或者*你想让坤诺加入你们分局，解决这摊破事？就像我们*刚刚*做的一样？”

En: I won't say it! I won't say it anymore.
Zh: “我不会说的！我再也不会说了。”

En: His teeth are clenched and his throat moves in a gulping motion.
Zh: 他咬紧牙关，喉咙哽咽着。

En: Maybe you can take the kid with you, the special consultant says, looking at the boy. "Enrol him a year earlier and say he's fourteen. *If* he..."
Zh: “也许你可以带走这个孩子，”特别顾问看着男孩说道。“提前一年登记，就说他14岁了。*如果*他……”

En: Detective Kuuno de Ruyter.
Zh: 库洛·德鲁伊特警探。

En: I promise I won't say it. He shakes his head so hard his face turns red. "I won't say any of it anymore."
Zh: “我保证我不会说的。”他拼命摇头，脸都红了。“我再也不会说了。”

En: Fuck it, let's go. The man points down the street. "Trant brought his motor carriage. It's a 20 minute drive to Jamrock."
Zh: “妈的，我们走吧。”男人指着街道的方向。*特兰特把車开来了。开回加姆洛克要20分钟。”

En: For god's sake... She sighs. "Yes, we'll do it."
Zh: “看在老天爷的份上……”她叹了口气。“好吧，就这么办吧。”

En: Good bye, Harry. And -- I know it won't happen, but... He looks at you, heart steeled and eyes cold.
Zh: “再见，哈里。还有——我知道这不会发生的，不过……”他看着你，心如钢铁，眼神冰冷。

En: *If* you make it -- if you've been sober for 10 months -- tell us. I'll work with you again. But not like this. Never like this again. This is over.
Zh: “*如果*你能做到——如果你能戒酒10个月——告诉我们。我还会跟你一起工作。不过不像这样。永远也不要这样。这一切都结束了。”

En: Even now -- you're his colleague.
Zh: 即便是到了现在——你还是他的同事。

En: Now! Now you will *finally* get to know who you are!
Zh: 现在！现在你*总算*是能知道你是谁了！

En: I do like the sound of that... He returns her smile.
Zh: “听起来确实挺不错的……”他朝她还了一个微笑。

En: He's *really* considering it.
Zh: 他是*真的*在考虑。

En: Another sigh -- of surrender.
Zh: 又是一声叹息——因为屈服。

En: Under the night sky the great district sleeps: A black chessboard of old wooden houses, 80,000 living souls inside. Firetraps as far as the eye can see -- from Main Street to Precinct 41 atop the motorway, to Boogie Street forking into the darkened horizon...
Zh: 夜空之下，整片大区域都陷入沉睡：旧木屋组成一张黑色棋盘，里面生活着80,000条生命。一眼望去全是容易失火的建筑物——从大街到高速公路顶部的41分局，再到嵌入黑色地平线的布吉街……

En: Under the night sky the great district sleeps: A dark chessboard of wooden houses in the rain, 80,000 living souls inside. Firetraps as far as the eye can see -- from Main Street to Precinct 41 atop the motorway, to Boogie Street glowing on the rainswept horizon...
Zh: 夜空之下，整片大区域都陷入沉睡：雨中的旧木屋组成一张黑色棋盘，里面生活着80,000条生命。一眼望去全是容易失火的建筑物——从大街到高速公路顶部的41分局，再到在大雨滂沱的地平线上闪闪发亮的布吉街……

En: You close your eyes and hear the dogs bark. A lone woman sits by a factory window, dreaming of meteorite strikes. On Rue Saint-Gérôme a square bullet slides into a square-shaped chamber. In Old South a man without eyelids smiles. Spring has come. It's time.
Zh: 你闭上双眼，听见狗在吠叫。一个孤独的女人坐在工厂窗户旁边，梦想着陨石撞击这个星球。圣杰罗姆路上，一枚方形子弹滑入方形枪膛。旧南城里，一个没有眼睑的男人在微笑。春天来了。到时间了。

En: Under the night sky the great district sleeps: A chessboard of old wooden houses in the snow, 80,000 living souls inside. Firetraps as far as the eye can see -- from Main Street to Precinct 41 atop the motorway, to Boogie Street glowing on the horizon...
Zh: 夜空之下，整片大区域都陷入沉睡：雪中的旧木屋组成一张黑色棋盘，里面生活着80,000条生命。一眼望去全是容易失火的建筑物——从大街到高速公路顶部的41分局，再到在地平线上闪闪发亮的布吉街……

En: Under the evening sky the great district turns on its lights: A chessboard of wooden houses, 80,000 living souls inside. Firetraps as far as the eye can see -- from Main Street to Precinct 41 atop the motorway, to Boogie Street forking into the snowswept horizon...
Zh: 黄昏的天空之下，整片大区灯火通明：旧木屋组成一张黑色棋盘，里面生活着80,000条生命。一眼望去全是容易失火的建筑物——从大街到高速公路顶部的41分局，再到嵌入在大雪纷飞地平线上的布吉街……

En: Under the evening sky the great district turns on its lights: A chessboard of wooden houses, 80,000 living souls inside. Firetraps as far as the eye can see -- from Main Street to Precinct 41 atop the motorway, to Boogie Street disappearing into the rain on the horizon...
Zh: 黄昏的天空之下，整片大区灯火通明：旧木屋组成一张黑色棋盘，里面生活着80,000条生命。一眼望去全是容易失火的建筑物——从大街到高速公路顶部的41分局，再到消失在细雨交织地平线上的布吉街……

En: Under the evening sky the great district turns on its lights: A chessboard of wooden houses, 80,000 living souls inside. Firetraps as far as the eye can see -- from Main Street to Precinct 41 atop the motorway, to Boogie Street forking into the dark horizon...
Zh: 黄昏的天空之下，整片大区灯火通明：旧木屋组成一张黑色棋盘，里面生活着80,000条生命。一眼望去全是容易失火的建筑物——从大街到高速公路顶部的41分局，再到嵌入在黑暗地平线上的布吉街……

En: Under the afternoon sky the great district hums: A chessboard of wooden houses, 80,000 living souls and chimney stacks. Firetraps as far as the eye can see -- from Main Street to Precinct 41, to Boogie Street forking into the snowswept horizon...
Zh: 午后的日光之下，整片大区域轰鸣着：旧木屋组成一张黑色棋盘，里面生活着80,000条生命，还有层层叠叠的烟囱。一眼望去全是容易失火的建筑物——从大街到41分局，再到嵌入在大雪纷飞地平线上的布吉街……

En: The great district hums in the slowly falling snow: A chessboard of wooden houses, 80,000 living souls and chimney stacks. Firetraps as far as the eye can see -- from Main Street to Precinct 41, to Boogie Street forking into the white horizon...
Zh: 整片大区在缓缓飘落的雪花中低吟：旧木屋组成一张黑色棋盘，里面生活着80,000条生命，还有层层叠叠的烟囱。一眼望去全是容易失火的建筑物——从大街到41分局，再到嵌入在白色地平线上的布吉街……

En: The great district hums in the falling rain: A chessboard of wooden houses, 80,000 living souls. Firetraps as far as the eye can see -- from Main Street to Grand Couron, from Precinct 41 to Boogie Street forking into the rainswept horizon...
Zh: 整片大区在落雨中低吟：旧木屋组成一张黑色棋盘，里面生活着80,000条生命。一眼望去全是容易失火的建筑物——从大街到大科戎，从41分局到嵌入在滂沱大雨地平线上的布吉街……

En: Under the violet morning sky the great district wakes: A chessboard of wooden houses, 80,000 living souls and chimney stacks. Firetraps as far as the eye can see -- from Main Street to Precinct 41, to Boogie Street forking into the glowing horizon...
Zh: 紫色晨光中，整片大区逐渐苏醒：旧木屋组成一张黑色棋盘，里面生活着80,000条生命，还有层层叠叠的烟囱。一眼望去全是容易失火的建筑物——从大街到41分局，再到嵌入在发光地平线上的布吉街……

En: Under the pink morning sky the great district wakes: A chessboard of wooden houses, 80,000 living souls and chimney stacks. Firetraps as far as the eye can see -- from Main Street to Precinct 41, to Boogie Street forking into the snowswept horizon...
Zh: 粉色晨光中，整片大区逐渐苏醒：旧木屋组成一张黑色棋盘，里面生活着80,000条生命，还有层层叠叠的烟囱。一眼望去全是容易失火的建筑物——从大街到41分局，再到嵌入在大雪纷飞地平线上的布吉街……

En: A grey rain falls on Martinaise. The city soaks in it, cold and dripping. Waves hit the concrete breakers. The homeless huddle by the fires behind the fences. There, among the shacks, is your home. Stay. Have a drink. Forever.
Zh: 一场灰色的大雨降落在马丁内斯。城市浸染其中，冷冰冰，湿淋淋。海浪拍打着混凝土破碎机。无家可归的人们蜷缩在栅栏后的火堆周围。就在那堆棚屋之间，是你的家。留下来。喝上一杯。直到永远。

En: So I *have* to. Jude?
Zh: “所以我*必须*这么做吗。茱蒂？”

En: Look... She turns to face the sea. "It's about to rain soon. It's time to go home."
Zh: “看……”她转身面向大海。“很快就会下雨。是时候回家了。”

En: Look... She turns to face the sea. "It's raining. It's time to go home."
Zh: “看……”她转身面向大海。“下雨了。是时候回家了。”

En: I told you it's not that bad.
Zh: 我告诉过你，没那么糟糕的。

En: Not now.
Zh: 现在不行。

En: Of course.
Zh: 当然。

En: She is going to be over the moon.
Zh: 她一定会欣喜若狂的。

En: Six years and you haven't gotten over it, what the hell is wrong with you?
Zh: 6年了，你还没走出来，你到底有什么问题啊？

En:
Torson?
"Yes."
"McLaine?"
"Yes."
"Heidelstam?"
"No."
"Vicquemare?"
"Yes."
"Du Bois?"
"Of course."
Zh:
“托森？”
“有。”
“麦克莱恩?”
“有。”
“海德斯塔姆？”
“没有。”
“维克玛？”
“有。”
“杜博阿？”
“当然。”

En:
Really? Nix Gottlieb looks up from the list. "I hear he's unstable."
"You say that like it's a *bad* thing," Captain Ptolemy Pryce gestures with a ball point pen. It's dim in the office and the curtains are drawn. "Harry's our man, he'll pull through. When he does, he'll side with the people."
"Understood." Gottlieb returns to the list. "Minot?"
"Of course."
Zh:
“真的吗？”尼克斯·戈特利布看着名单，突然抬起头。“我听说他很不稳定。”
“你这口气好像这是件*坏事*，”警长托勒密·普赖斯用圆珠笔比划了一下。他的办公室很暗，窗帘拉上了。“哈里是我们的人，他会渡过难关的。等他恢复过来之后，他会站在人民这一边。”
“明白了。”戈特利布又回到名单上。“迈诺特？”
“当然。”

“当然。”

En:
Really? Nix Gottlieb looks up from the list. "I hear he's quite unstable."
"You say that like it's a *bad* thing," Captain Ptolemy Pryce gestures with a ball point pen. It's dim in the office and the curtains are drawn. "Harry's our man, he'll pull through. When he does, he'll side with Revachol."
"Understood." Gottlieb returns to the list. "Minot?"
"Of course."

En:
Really? Nix Gottlieb looks up from the list. "I hear he's quite unstable."
"You say that like it's a *bad* thing," Captain Ptolemy Pryce points his pen at the doctor. It's dim in his office and the curtains are drawn. "Harry's our man, he'll pull through -- and when he does, he'll side with Revachol."
"Understood." Gottlieb returns to the list. "Minot?"
"Of course."
Zh:
“真的吗？”尼克斯·戈特利布看着名单，突然抬起头。“我听说他很不稳定。”
“你这口气好像这是件*坏事*，”警长托勒密·普赖斯用笔指着医生。他的办公室很暗，窗帘拉上了。“哈里是我们的人，他会渡过难关的——而且等他恢复过来之后，他会站在瑞瓦肖这一边。”
“明白了。”戈特利布又回到名单上。“迈诺特？”
“当然。”

En: Wonderful, the woman looks north. "Then can we please just go back to Jamrock now?"
Zh: “太棒了，”女人看向北边。“那我们能不能现在就回加姆洛克？”

En: Under the pink morning sky the great district wakes: A chessboard of wooden houses, 80,000 living souls and chimney stacks. Firetraps as far as the eye can see -- from Main Street to Precinct 41. To Boogie Street forking into the snowswept horizon...
Zh: 粉色晨光中，整片大区逐渐苏醒：旧木屋组成一张黑色棋盘，里面生活着80,000条生命，还有层层叠叠的烟囱。一眼望去全是容易失火的建筑物——从大街到41分局，再到嵌入在大雪纷飞地平线上的布吉街……

En: Under the violet morning sky the great district wakes: A chessboard of wooden houses, 80,000 living souls and chimney stacks. Firetraps as far as the eye can see -- from Main Street to Precinct 41. To Boogie Street forking into the glowing horizon...
Zh: 紫色晨光中，整片大区逐渐苏醒：旧木屋组成一张黑色棋盘，里面生活着80,000条生命，还有层层叠叠的烟囱。一眼望去全是容易失火的建筑物——从大街到41分局，再到嵌入在发光地平线上的布吉街……

En: The great district hums with the noise of human habitation: A chessboard of wooden houses, 80,000 living souls. Firetraps as far as the eye can see -- from Main Street to Grand Couron, from Precinct 41 to Boogie Street forking into the distant horizon...
Zh: 整片大区人声鼎沸：旧木屋组成一张黑色棋盘，里面生活着80,000条生命。一眼望去全是容易失火的建筑物——从主路到大科戎，从41分局到嵌入遥远地平线的布吉街……

En:
Torson?
"Yes."
"McLaine?"
"Yes."
"Heidelstam?"
"No."
"Vicquemare?"
"Yes."
"Du Bois?"
"Yes."
Zh:
“托森？”
“有。”
“麦克莱恩?”
“有。”
“海德斯塔姆？”
“没有。”
“维克玛？”
“有。”
“杜博阿？”
“当然。”

En: No, he's not. What is he, ten? Eleven?
Zh: “不，他没有。他多大了，10岁？11岁？”

En: The great district hums with the noise of human habitation: A chessboard of wooden houses, 80,000 living souls. Firetraps as far as the eye can see -- from Main Street to Grand Couron, from Precinct 41 to Boogie Street forking into the distant horizon...
Zh: 整片大区人声鼎沸：旧木屋组成一张黑色棋盘，里面生活着80,000条生命。一眼望去全是容易失火的建筑物——从主路到大科戎，从41分局到嵌入遥远地平线的布吉街……

En: He's genuinely scared of being left behind.
Zh: 他的真的害怕被留下来。

En: It's not the locals he's worried about -- but an eleven year old girl.
Zh: 他担心的不是本地人——而是一个11岁大的小女孩。

En: You can't be a cop. You're twelve. And you say 'f****t' every four seconds.
Zh: “你不能当警察。你才12岁。而且你每隔四秒就会说一句‘混球’。”

En: You can't be a cop. You're a child. And you say 'f****t' every four seconds.
Zh: “你不能当警察。你还是个小孩。而且你每隔四秒就会说一句‘混球’。”

En: I don't care -- he can't be a cop. He's twelve. And says 'f****t' every four seconds.
Zh: “我不在乎——他不能当警察。他只有12岁。而且每隔四秒就会说一句‘混球’。”

En: I don't care -- he can't be a cop. He's just a kid. A kid who says 'f****t' every four seconds.
Zh: “我不在乎——他不能当警察。他还是个孩子。一个每隔四秒就会说一句‘混球’的孩子。”

En: He can't be a cop, Harry. He's a child. A child who says 'f****t' every four seconds.
Zh: “他不能当警察。哈里。他还是个小孩。一个每隔四秒就会说一句‘混球’的孩子。”

En: He can't be a cop, Harry. He's twelve. And he says 'f****t' every four seconds.
Zh: “他不能当警察。哈里。他只有12岁。一而且每隔四秒就会说一句‘混球’。”

En: She's avoiding your gaze.
Zh: 她在回避你的目光。

En: It explains everything. All the running around. The jumping. The *bicep girth*.
Zh: 这就解释了一切。到处跑来跑去。那个跳跃。还有*发达的二头肌*。

En: The collection of FALN sportswear I've amassed...
Zh: 我收集的FALN运动套装……

En: How I can run for *five hours* a day...
Zh: 为什么我能一天跑*5个小时*……

En: The FALN gear...
Zh: FALN装备……

En: The fact that you don't even know what homo-sexuality is.
Zh: 你甚至都不知道同性恋是什么的事实。

En: Some of your more *old-school* social views. Your love of retro music.
Zh: 你那些*老派*的社会观点。你对复古音乐的热爱。

En: How you're able to perform a 360-degree spin-kick despite your less than stellar workout routine.
Zh: 尽管你没有什么持久的锻炼习惯，还是能做出360度回旋踢的动作。

En: Your moves on the church floor -- which -- let's be honest -- were mostly *jump aerobics*.
Zh: 你在教堂地板上的动作——老实说——那就是在*跳健美操*。

En: Your posture. The constant *stretches*... The disco star aspirations.
Zh: 你的姿态。不断的*伸展*……成为迪斯科明星的愿望。

En: Wonderful, the woman looks north. "Then can we please just go back to Jamrock now?"
Zh: “太棒了，”女人看向北边。“那我们能不能现在就回加姆洛克？”

En: Wonderful, the woman looks north. "Then can we please just go back to Jamrock now?"
Zh: “太棒了，”女人看向北边。“那我们能不能现在就回加姆洛克？”

En: Wonderful, the woman looks north. "Then can we please just go back to Jamrock now?"
Zh: “太棒了，”女人看向北边。“那我们能不能现在就回加姆洛克？”

En: I'm ready. (End.)
Zh: “我准备好了。”（总结。）

En: I'm ready. (End.)
Zh: “我准备好了。”（总结。）

En: I'm ready. (End.)
Zh: “我准备好了。”（总结。）

En: Are you *seriously* using this moment to assert male privilege!?!
Zh: “你*真的*打算在这种时刻维护男性特权吗！？！”

En: He is very passionate about this.
Zh: 他对此很有激情。

En: Okay, you're right. She was extremely fuckable, Harry. Gorgeous. A gorgeous bourgeois woman. Waifish. Like a *welkin* basically.
Zh: “好吧，你是对的。她的确非常有吸引力，哈里。漂亮。一个顶级资产女性。弱不禁风。基本上就像个*精灵*一样。”

En: Yeah, Rosie was there too, the tracksuited man nods, "for your ocean-based antics. We all were. Unforgettable night."
Zh: 穿着运动服的男人点点头。“是啊，罗西也在那里——为了看你的海上滑稽表演。我们都在。真是难忘的一夜。”

En: Okay, how?
Zh: “好吧，怎么回事？”

En: The idiots left me alone in there. Now, I used to teach high school biology. I *know* what doctors use to preserve dead thingies... He gets an excited gleam in his eyes.
Zh: “那帮白痴把我一个人留在那里。我以前是在中学教生物的。我*知道*医生会用什么来保存死掉的东西……”他的眼睛里闪烁着兴奋的光芒。

En: Conversation might bring a discount, no?
Zh: 聊一聊说不定能打折呢，不是吗？

En: Well done. You got it.
Zh: 做得好。你明白了。

En: That's a much more reasonable price right there. Makes sense now.
Zh: 那个价格就合理多了。现在还比较讲得通。

En: That is unhygienic. And dangerous. And it does not endear you to me -- just wanted to make that clear, okay?
Zh: “那个不健康。而且很危险。而且这样不会提高我对你的评价——我只想澄清这一点，好吗？”

En: Pfff... unhygienic. This stuff is very hygienic. Anything else?
Zh: “呸……不健康。这玩意非常健康。还有别的吗？”

En: Soon. The time will come soon. Have patience, brave one.
Zh: 很快。时间很快就到。耐心一点，勇敢的人。

En: The tie is *SO* on the money there! Up the bum it goes!
Zh: 这条领带真是*特别*恰到好处！那就放到屁股上吧！

En: I'm a police officer, not your 'friend'.
Zh: “我是一名警官，不是你的‘朋友’。”

En: You acted with great compassion and dignity, not taking advantage of this man. Praise thine honourable heart!
Zh: 你没有利用这个男人的行为，充满极大的同情和尊严。我称赞你那颗正直而荣耀的心！

En: In all fairness, that might be attributed to the retrograde amnesia.
Zh: 说句公道话，这可能要归因于逆行性遗忘。

En: He's exaggerating. It bites, but only a little.
Zh: 他太夸张了。它会咬人，不过只有一点点。

En: The hook -- where is it? I can't see it.
Zh: 那个钩子——在哪里？我看不见。

En: I'm already hooking for Cuno... I think.
Zh: “我已经被坤诺钩搭上了……我觉得。”

En: Seriously? The lieutenant closes his eyes and pinches the root of his nose. "*Are* you?"
Zh: “真的吗？”警督闭上眼睛，捏住自己的鼻根。“你*真有*吗？”

En: I'm sorry, Kim. I just wanted to see what happens, if I say it.
Zh: “很抱歉，金。我只想说出来看看会发生什么。”

En: He said I'm his 'pig hooker' or something, whatever that is.
Zh: “他说我是他的‘猪猡’或者什么的，不管到底是什么意思。”

En: The fresh entrepreneur has forgotten your presence and is now trying to count a small number of coins in his hand.
Zh: 这位新创业家已经忘记了你的存在，开始在手里数起了硬币。

En: There's reál, fifty in his palm.
Zh: 他的手掌里放着50雷亚尔。

En: Good, good, good, he repeats, nodding enthusiastically. "So you want this shit or not?"
Zh: “很好，很好，很好，”他热情地点点头，重复着。“所以你到底想不想要这玩意了？”

En: Fine, he sighs.
Zh: “好吧，”他叹了口气。

En: ... and the lieutenant is annoyed.
Zh: ……而且警督有些生气。

En: I think you didn't hear me, when I said I'm a police officer.
Zh: “我说自己是警察的时候，你应该没听到吧。”

En: Sure did, buddy-boy, he taps the side of his nose. "That's why I said amphetamine. I mean speed. I mean amphetamine. I got both."
Zh: “当然听到了，伙计，”他拍了拍鼻翼。“所以我才会说安非他命。我的意思是思必得。安非他命。两种我都有。”

En: While the *grand bourgeois* in La Delta are so rich, they literally float above the law, this merry company crawls so far beneath, they too are rendered virtually untouchable. The beauty of extremities.
Zh: 德尔塔的*大资产阶级*非常富有，他们实际上是凌驾于法律之上的，而这个愉快的小企业家在距离很远的下面缓慢爬行，他们基本上也可以说是无法碰触的。这就是身处末端的美丽。

En: He has long forgotten what you were talking about.
Zh: 他早就忘记你在说什么了。

En: Snakes or intestinal worms?
Zh: 是蛇还是肠虫？

En: Oh... He gets a proud gleam in his eyes. "The system's been good to old Rosemary here and I'm milkin' 'er like a bitch goat in the backyard."
Zh: “哦……”他的眼底流露出一丝自豪的光芒。“系统对老罗斯马雷很好，我就像后院里的母羊一样给她挤奶。”

En: In the civilized world, it's a custom to tip the shopkeep, friend. But come back anyway. He waves you off.
Zh: “在一个文明的世界里，给店主小费是一种习俗，我的朋友。不过记得再回来看就好。”他挥手让你离开。

En: Good to see you, friend! Do I have *deals* set up for you, buddy-boy! He spreads his arms as if wanting to embrace you.
Zh: “很高兴见到你，朋友！我这里有些好*交易*，专门为你准备的，伙计！”他张开手臂，似乎想拥抱你。

En: Just step away from the old man and let's have a little word alone, just you and me. Soon we will enter the *Other-World*.
Zh: 远离这个老男人，我们单独聊几句，就你和我。很快我们就会进入*另一个世界*。

En: Who said anything about putting it up there? No, we're gonna put it in a *way* more special place. Soooo special. Now just step away from the old man and let's have a little word alone, just you and me.
Zh: 谁说要放到那里去了？不，我们要把它放在一个*更加*特别的地方。非常特殊。远离这个老男人，我们单独聊几句，就你和我。

En: Son, you will NOT kill yourself with this. Not today. So we're going to store this as a SELLABLE item. Go sell it at the pawnshop for a profit.
Zh: 孩子，你是不会因为这个死掉的。所以，我们会把它存放在可出售道具里。拿去当铺卖掉换点钱吧。

En: If you drink this, then you will die. That's a fact. And that's why we're going to save you from yourself and store this as a SELLABLE item. Go sell it at the pawnshop for a profit.
Zh: 如果喝下去，那你就会死。这就是事实。而且这就是我们为什么要拯救你的原因，把它存放在可出售道具里。拿去当铺卖掉换点钱吧。

En: Harry... we're just going to store this one as a sellable item, okay? We're not going to let you kill yourself by drinking it. That's just not an option. Sell it at the pawnshop for profit.
Zh: 哈里……我们把它存放在可出售道具里，好吗？我们不会让你喝掉它害死自己的。没有那种选项。拿去当铺卖掉换点钱吧。

En: Good to see you too, friend.
Zh: “我也很高兴见到你，朋友。”

En: What are you talking about?
Zh: “你在说什么呢？”

En: I'll just have the spirits and not the story today, thank you.
Zh: “今天我要烈酒就好，故事就免了，谢谢。”

En: I thought about it and you know what? I can pay 3 reál for the spirits.
Zh: “我想了一下，你猜怎么着？我打算出3雷亚尔买下你的烈酒。”

En: Nod -- let him speak.
Zh: 点点头——让他说。

En: You know, it's funny, actually... He bursts out laughing, then takes three gulps of his pilsner and stares at you intently.
Zh: “你知道的，其实挺有意思的……”他爆笑出声，猛灌了三大口比尔森啤酒，然后专注地盯着你。

En: Right... right... His tone cools considerably. "As I said, that'll be 300 reál."
Zh: “好吧……好吧……”他的口气非常冷静。“我说过了，要300雷亚尔。”

En: He's finding it difficult to focus his watery gaze.
Zh: 他发现很难让自己那双水汪汪的眼睛聚焦起来。

En: What is?
Zh: “怎么回事呢？”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: What do you mean *what*? What did you think was crazy earlier?
Zh: “你说*什么*是什么意思？刚才你说疯狂是什么意思？”

En: What do you mean *what*? What did you think was funny earlier?
Zh: “你说*什么*是什么意思？刚才你说有意思是什么意思？”

En: This guy, this guy... He says and shakes his index finger at you.
Zh: “这个家伙，这个家伙啊……”他一边说，一边对你摇晃着食指。

En: Forget about it, I just want the spirits!
Zh: “忘了吧，我只想要烈酒！”

En: Okay, can I just get the spirits if that's okay with you?
Zh: “好吧，如果你不介意的话，能把烈酒给我吗？”

En: You know what?! I don't even want the spirits anymore!
Zh: “你知道吗？！我根本不想要那个该死的烈酒了！”

En: So what do you want then?!
Zh: “那你是什么意思？！”

En: His tone is aggressive. You're getting on his bad side now.
Zh: 他的口气有些咄咄逼人。现在你站在他的对立面上了。

En: The alcohol boiling his blood causes the agitation. It burns!
Zh: 酒精让他的血液沸腾、激动不安。它在燃烧！

En: Where did you get the bottle of spirits from?
Zh: “你从哪里弄到烈酒的？”

En: Oh, this is medicinal spirits. The good stuff. Got it from the doctor's office.
Zh: “哦，这是医用酒精。好东西。从医生办公室拿到的。”

En: He ain't shitting you. Medicinal spirits are a blast, *bratan*. The flaming truth of this joke of a world.
Zh: 他没有骗你。医用酒精可猛了，*兄弟*。燃烧的真理，属于这个世界的笑话。

En: I got one of those scientific ampoules a few months ago. 'Torpedo' they call it. It's supposed to keep a man from takin' a drink. He spits a nasty yellow clot on the ground before you.
Zh: “几个月前，我弄到了那些科学安瓿。他们叫它‘鱼雷’。本来是用来阻止男人喝酒的。”他吐出一团恶心的黄色粘液，落在你面前的地上。

En: Didn't stop *me* for shit, that's for sure! His voice rings with pride. "Five lemons with half a pack of butter and you're good to go."
Zh: “反正没能阻止*我*，这是肯定的！”他的声音充满自豪。“5个柠檬再加上半包黄油就万事俱备了。”

En: That's a good tip, I should remember it.
Zh: “这是个好建议，我应该记下来。”

En: That sounds dangerous.
Zh: “听起来很危险的样子。”

En: It was, he croaks. "In a week the goddamned kidneys started giving me all kinds of hell. Finally the missus took me to a private doctor's office -- not a charity, the real thing..."
Zh: “是的，”他用嘶哑的声音说道。“一星期后，该死的肾脏疼得跟鬼一样。最后我老婆带我去了一家私人医生诊所——不是慈善机构，真的那种……”

En: Those assholes! He gets visibly angry. "Those assholes charged me *four reál* to remove the damn thing. But I came out on top after all!"
Zh: “那帮混球！”他明显很生气。“那帮混球收了我*4雷亚尔*把那个该死的玩意摘了。不过最终还是我赢了！”

En: But the idiots left me alone in there. Now, I used to teach high school biology. I *know* what doctors use to preserve dead thingies... He gets an excited gleam in his eyes.
Zh: “但是那帮白痴把我一个人留在那里。我以前是在中学教生物的。我*知道*医生会用什么来保存死掉的东西……”他的眼睛里闪烁着兴奋的光芒。

En: Have they no shame taking money for a service they provide!
Zh: “他们为自己提供的服务收钱，难道不觉得羞耻吗！”

En: Assholes...
Swiped three cans of this blue medicinal stuff from the back room. Threw the snakes out and voilà -- what's left is this beautiful blue stuff. He shakes the bottle. "98.7%, almost pure alcohol."
Zh: “我从里屋里偷走了三罐蓝色的药用东西。把蛇扔了出去，然后你看——剩下来的就是这种漂亮的蓝色液体了。”他晃了晃瓶子。“98.7%，基本上就是纯酒精了。”

En: Two I already sold to these fine gentlemen here, he nods at his companions. "But this last one is yours for three reál, if you want it?"
Zh: “其中两瓶我已经卖给这两位先生了，”他朝着同伴点点头。“如果你想要的话，最后这瓶就3雷亚尔卖给你？”

En: I think it will prove... useful. Yes.
Zh: “我觉得它能……挺有用的。没错。”

En: No, not really.
Zh: “不，不见得。”

En: Can I smell it first?
Zh: “我能先闻闻吗？”

En: Here... He uncorks the bottle and holds it under your nose. "Be careful, it's extremely flammable. One spark and the entire city of Revachol is wiped off the map."
Zh: “给你……”他打开瓶盖，放到你的鼻子底下。“小心点，这玩意极度易燃。只要一点火星，地图上就不会有瑞瓦肖城了。”

En: Feels like someone set a mustard field ablaze right inside your nose, then drenched it in tear gas. Your nose is a singular source of pain... but at the same time you don't remember the last time you felt so alive.
Zh: 感觉就像有人直接在你的鼻子里点着了一片芥菜田，然后又用催泪瓦斯把它浸透了。你的鼻子就是疼痛的来源……不过同时，你已经不记得上次感觉这么有活力是什么时候了。

En: Feels like someone's trying to jam an angry hedgehog down your nostrils, doesn't it, buddy-boy? Wait for the adrenaline-tsunami, it's *really* great!
Zh: “感觉就像有人试着把一只愤怒的刺猬塞进你的鼻孔里，不是吗，伙计？等待肾上腺素的大海啸吧，它*真的*很棒！”

En: Did I not tell you, *bratan*? This is it -- this is our party drink. This is what we're having, baby! Please get it for me! (The tie vibrates excitedly around your neck again.)
Zh: 我没告诉过你吗，*兄弟*？就是它了——这就是我们的派对饮料。我们要的就是这个，宝贝！我要的就是它！（你脖子上的领导又开始兴奋地颤抖。）

En: So, what's the deal, friend? Want the spirits or not?
Zh: “所以，怎么样，朋友？想不想要烈酒了？”

En: Three reál and it's yours, friend. The deal of a lifetime!
Zh: “只要3雷亚尔，它就是你的了，朋友。这可是一生都难得一见的好交易！”

En: Here's the money for the spirits.
Zh: “这是买烈酒的钱。”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: You see, friend, he raises his index finger, "man makes his own luck -- and I made mine real good. Got my hands on three bottles of *liqueur exquise*, sold two to the fellows around here and *immediately* invested the profit."
Zh: “你看，我的朋友，”他举起食指，“运气是自己创造的——而我创造的就相当不错。弄到了三瓶*高档利口酒*，卖了两瓶给附近的人，然后*立即*就把利润投资了。”

En: Bought cigarettes, bought beer, even bought a bit of speed. And look at me now... I got everyone on my hook. He spreads his arms and smiles a crooked toothless smile.
Zh: “买了香烟，啤酒，甚至还买了点思必得。现在，看看我……所有人都上了我的钩。”他张开双臂，咧嘴扭曲一笑，嘴里没有牙齿。

En: Looks like you're on your own hook, too.
Zh: “看起来你自己也上钩了。”

En: Impressive entrepreneurship. (Point to his vice-stand.) "I approve."
Zh: “真是令人印象深刻的创业精神。”（指着他的小货摊。）“我很赞同。”

En: Got a permit for this little shit-show?
Zh: “你这摊破玩意有许可证吗？”

En: Of course, of course, of course... The drunk raises his pilsner and nods. "It is what it is, you know? What it's always been. People, buddy-boy, it's the people."
Zh: “当然，当然，当然了……”醉汉举起比尔森啤酒，点点头。“你也知道是怎么回事。一直都是这么回事。民众，伙计，这就是民众。”

En: Sell me some speed.
Zh: “卖点思必得给我。”

En: I want a pack of smokes.
Zh: “我想要一包烟。”

En: Here's the money for a pilsner.
Zh: “来一瓶比尔森啤酒。”

En: The legend returns. You know the deal, legend-man. Smokes are smokey, pilsner... gets you drunk. You've already cleaned me out of spirits... and speed. He looks sad.
Zh: “传说回归了。你知道是怎么回事，传说中的男人。烟是烟，比尔森啤酒……让人醉酒。你已经把烈酒买走了……还有思必得。”他看起来很伤心。

En: The legend is here. You know the drill. Smoke up, pilsner up... buy the beautiful bottle of blue spirits. It's... he spreads his arms, "*magic*."
Zh: “传说来了。你知道怎么回事。烟没了，比尔森啤酒没了……买下这瓶美丽的蓝色烈酒吧。它……”他张开双臂，“是有*魔力*的。”

En: The legend! He's back. And thirsty. He nods. "I got smokes and piss and a little... speed to spice things up."
Zh: “传奇人物！他回来了。而且还很饥渴。”他点点头。“我这里有烟，啤酒，还有一点……思必得，来增加点气氛。”

En: The legend returns! You know the dance. Smokable smokes. Pissable pilsner -- drink it, then convert it to piss! And there's... spirits and speed too.
Zh: “传说回归了！你知道是怎么回事。可以抽的烟，可以尿出来的比尔森啤酒……喝下去，然后把它转化成尿液！还有……烈酒和思必得。”

En: So whadda'ya want? He tilts his head. "I got smokes. They're cheap. Very cheap. I got pilsner. Great deal. You won't get a better deal on that piss... Spirits I can let go for 300 reál. I also have speed. And by *speed* I mean amphetamine."
Zh: “所以，你想要什么呢？”他歪起脑袋。“我有烟。很便宜。非常便宜。我有比尔森啤酒。很划算。你是不可能找到比我这里更划算的价格了……烈酒的话只要300雷亚尔。我还有思必得。而且我说的*思必得*指的是安非他命。”

En: Quite the business venture you've set up here.
Zh: “你的生意好像挺不错啊。”

En: I'm off. [Leave.]
Zh: “我先走了。”[离开。]

En: Here you go, friend. He hands you a tiny bottle with a straw. The powder looks clotted and quite mouldy.
Zh: “给你，朋友。”他递给你一只带吸管的小瓶子。粉末看起来很粘稠，而且还发霉了。

En: Bottoms up, captain! He hands you an ugly brown bottle.
Zh: “干杯，警官！”他递给你一支丑陋的棕色瓶子。

En: My kinda guy, he mumbles, handing you a pack. "Here you go, friend."
Zh: “我喜欢你这种人，”他一边嘟囔，一边递给你一包。“给你，朋友。”

En: See, friend... He brings out a one-litre bottle with blue-ish liquid. The mouth is corked shut. "It's *real* valuable. Worth every reál, if you catch my drift. Got it from a bit of a business venture..."
Zh: “你看，我的朋友，”他拿出一个装着蓝色液体的一升装瓶子。瓶口被塞住了。“它*真的*非常贵重。如果你明白我的意思，花在它身上的每一分雷亚尔都是值得的。我是从一家企业弄到的……”

En: No one can buy spirits for 300 reál, that's crazy.
Zh: “没人会花300雷亚尔买烈酒的，太疯狂了。”

En: Here's the money for the spirits.
Zh: “这是买烈酒的钱。”

En: Whoaaa, he gasps as he hands you the bottle. "My investment hit *big bank* this time around... Look, Doom Spiral, look!"
Zh: “哇啊啊啊，”他喘息着把瓶子递给你。“这次我的投资真是命中*大银行*了……看啦，厄运漩涡，快看！”

En: He hands you the bottle. "Jus' make sure to *enjoy* that one, friend!"
Zh: 他把瓶子地给了你。“记得好好*享受*哦，朋友！”

En: What? The man in the dirty tracksuit turns his head. "Big bank?"
Zh: “什么？”穿着脏运动物的男人转过头来。“大银行？”

En: The fella 'ere just gave me 300 reál for a bottle of spirits! Rosemary's playin' in the big league now!
Zh: “这小伙子花300雷亚尔买走了一瓶烈酒！这次罗斯马雷真是赚的大了！”

En: High-concept business venture. The man nods. "Solid vision, stellar execution."
Zh: 男人点点头。“刺激的高风险生意经。可靠的愿景，一流的执行力。”

En: You know what's crazy, actually... He grins, then bursts out laughing, then takes three gulps of his pilsner and stares at you intently.
Zh: “其实，你知道疯狂的是什么吗……”他咧嘴一笑，接着爆笑出声，猛灌了三大口比尔森啤酒，然后专注地盯着你。

En: Ah, yes, yes... the spirits. His tone cools considerably. "That'll be 300 reál."
Zh: “啊，是的，是的……烈酒。”他的口气非常冷静。“那个要300雷亚尔。”

En: Keep him talking.
Zh: 让他继续说。

En: Sure-sure, the spirits! He rubs his palm against the back of his head. "Now, what did I say the price was again?"
Zh: “当然了，烈酒！”他用掌心摩擦着后脑勺。“我刚才说价格是多少来着？”

En: Here's your chance.
Zh: 你的机会来了。

En: Three-hundred reál.
Three reál.
Zh: “3雷亚尔。”

En: Aye, that was it. He gives you a thumbs up. "The spirits, real good stuff, you see!"
Zh: “啊，那就是了。”他朝你竖起大拇指。“烈酒，真正的好东西，你懂得！”

En: Good man! You didn't cheat the poor drunk out of his money. It's very *honourable* of you.
Zh: 好好家伙！你没有骗走那个可怜醉汉的钱。你还真是非常*正直*呢。

En: Oh, aye... exactly! I remember now, I remember what was so crazy. He waggles the blue bottle in his hand. "Three reál, that's a *real steal* for such good stuff!"
Zh: “哦，是啊……没错！现在我记起来了，我记得为什么那么疯狂了。”他摇晃着手中的蓝色瓶子。“3雷亚尔，这么好的东西，那不跟*白捡*一样！”

En: Oh, aye... exactly! I remember now... I remember what was so funny. He waggles the blue bottle in his hand. "Three reál, that's a *real steal* for such good stuff!"
Zh: “哦，是啊……没错！现在我记起来了……我记得为什么那么好笑了。”他摇晃着手中的蓝色瓶子。“3雷亚尔，这么好的东西，那不跟*白捡*一样。”

En: It's almost too easy, haggling with incoherent drunks.
Zh: 跟语无伦次的醉汉讨价还价似乎有点太容易了。

En: Well, it really isn't, he croaks. "In a week the goddamned kidneys started giving me all kinds of hell. Finally the missus took me to a private doctor's office -- not a charity, the real thing..."
Zh: “嗯，还真不是，”他用嘶哑的声音说道。“一星期后，该死的肾脏疼得跟鬼一样。最后我老婆带我去了一家私人医生诊所——不是慈善机构，真的那种……”

En: I thought it was 300 reál.
Zh: “我想是300雷亚尔。”

En: Yeah! You thought right! Don't even try to sneak anything past old Rosemary here. The man leans forward. "I got all senses locked on you, buddy-boy. So what will it be?"
Zh: “哦耶！你想的很对！居然没有试着从老罗斯马雷这里把东西偷走！”男人身体前倾。“我所有感觉都锁定在你身上了，伙计。所以，你打算怎么样呢？”

En: You said it's three reál just now, but at first you asked three-hundred.
Zh: “刚才你说3雷亚尔，不过一开始你要300雷亚尔。”

En: Never mind, I misheard. Three reál is a fair price.
Zh: “没关系，我听错了。3雷亚尔挺公道的。”

En: Aye, the mind... He pats his temple. "...plays tricks sometimes. Always double-check. So you want it or not?"
Zh: “啊，脑袋……”他拍了怕太阳穴。“……有时候会恶作剧。永远都要记得复查。所以，你想不想要？”

En: Aye, that's what I said just now... 300 reál. Don't you try to trick me, buddy-boy. He points his finger in your general direction. "Now you want it or not?!"
Zh: “啊，我刚才是这么说的……300雷亚尔。你可别想骗我，伙计。”他朝你的大致方向指了指，“现在，你到底想不想要？！”

En: Don't say it.
Zh: 别说。

En: Of course I do!
Zh: “当然想了！”

En: No, not really.
Zh: “不，不太想。”

En: Three-hundred reál and it's yours, as promised. He shakes the bottle. "Best shit around!"
Zh: “300雷亚尔，它就是你的了，就像之前承诺的。”他晃了晃瓶子。“附近最好的玩意了！”

En: Aye, by amphetamine I mean speed.
Zh: “嗨，我说的安非他命就是思必得。”

En: I thought by speed you meant amphetamine?
Zh: “我以为你说的思必得是指安非他命？”

En: Right. Got it.
Zh: “好吧。明白了。”

En: Good-good, my man. He takes a chug from his beer bottle. "Now what can I offer ya?"
Zh: “很好，伙计。”他从啤酒瓶里喝了一口。“现在，你需要点什么呢？”

En: Aye, 's'what I said.
Zh: “是啊，我之前说过了。”

En: Why does the bottle of spirits cost 300 reál?
Zh: “为什么这瓶烈酒要300雷亚尔？”

En: Amphetamine?
Zh: “安非他命？”

En: Does he actually know you or is he just shopkeep-friendly?
Zh: 他是真认识你，还是招待顾客装出来的那一套友好？

En: Friend? Do we know each other?
Zh: “朋友？我们认识吗？”

En: Sure, friend! You know it! He points a finger-pistol at you and squeezes the trigger. "*Chck-chck*."
Zh: “当然了，朋友！你知道的！”他用手指手枪指着你，然后扣动了扳机。“*咔擦-咔擦*。”

En: Don't mind ol' Rosemary here, he's a buffoon. The tracksuited man waves his hand at you. "Let's have a word after your high-concept transactions, I'll give you the lowdown."
Zh: 穿着运动服的男人朝你挥挥手。“别管老罗斯马雷，他就是个小丑。等你们刺激的交易完成之后，我们聊几句，我会告诉你真相的。”

En: Mhm -- hook 'em and cook 'em, huh? You wanna buy something? Let's keep it moving.
Zh: “让人们产生依赖再榨干他们，对吧？你想买点什么吗？我们继续吧。”

En: Permit? He's not really listening. "Who gives a shit? You wanna buy something? Grease the wheels, buddy-boy."
Zh: “许可？”他并没有真的在听。“谁在乎？你想买点什么吗？让事情顺利进行下去吧，伙计。”

En: *Bratan*, I am so proud. Now -- whatever you do, don't drink it. This deserves so much more than just regular *oral* consumption.
Zh: *兄弟*，我真的感到很骄傲。现在——不管你做什么，不要喝掉它。它值得更好的东西，而不是普通的*口头*消耗掉。

En: Just wait for my sign. Keep it with you. Keep it warm, keep it safe. Soon we will enter the *Other-World*.
Zh: 只要等待我的信号。把它带在身上。保持它的温暖，保证它的安全。很快我们就会进入*另一个世界*。

En: Okay! Wow! Bratushka! -- you feel your necktie *strangle* you with excitement -- the *spirit*! Let's buy the spirit! 300 reál is a lot, but this has to be done.
Zh: 好吧！哇哦！小兄弟！——你感觉领带兴奋地*勒紧*了你的脖子——*烈酒*！我们买下烈酒！300雷亚尔挺贵的，不过必须买下来。

En: It's our END GAME.
Zh: 这是我们的终局。

En: This is just another stupid drunk idea I'm having, that I'm attributing to my necktie.
Zh: 这只是我脑袋里又一个愚蠢的想法罢了，这要归因于我的领带。

En: This is the mystery and the truth and I need to buy that spirit.
Zh: 这就是神秘和真相，我必须买下那瓶烈酒。

En: Bratan, you don't understand. It's not just another drink. This is what our relationship has been building towards all these years. This is the climax. The mystery. The virginal sigh.
Zh: 兄弟，你不明白。它不仅仅是一瓶酒。这么多年来，我们的关系一直在朝这个方向发展。这就是高潮。这就是神秘。这就是处女的叹息。

En: Exactly! This is no ordinary drink. It's what our relationship has been building towards all these years. This is the climax. The mystery. The virginal sigh. That drink is *special*.
Zh: 没错！它不是普通的酒。这么多年来，我们的关系一直在朝这个方向发展。这就是高潮。这就是神秘。这就是处女的叹息。那瓶酒是*特别的*。

En: You *have* to buy it from him. Get it off him. Kill him, if you have to. Our ultimate fate depends on it. And the fate of *many worlds*.
Zh: 你*必须*从他手里买下来。从他那里夺过来。如果有必要的话，杀了他。我们最终的命运就取决于它。还有*很多世界*的命运。

En: The lieutenant looks at you looking at the bottle of spirits. Then at Rosemary, suspiciously.
Zh: 警督看着盯着烈酒瓶子的你。然后怀疑地看了看罗斯马雷。

En: He knows something is going on. In your *head*.
Zh: 他知道有什么事发生了。在你的*脑袋*里。

En: What if I don't want to listen to my necktie anymore?
Zh: 如果我不想再听这条领带的话呢？

En: Snap out of it, bratan. This is no time for melancholy introspection. It's time to BUY THAT SPIRIT.
Zh: 振作起来，兄弟。没时间让你做什么惆怅的反省了。

En: See, there it is, bratushka! -- you feel your necktie *strangle* you with excitement -- the *spirit*! Let's buy the spirit! 300 reál is a lot, but this has to be done.
Zh: 看见没，就是它了，小兄弟！——你感觉领带兴奋地*勒紧*了你的脖子——*烈酒*！我们买下烈酒！300雷亚尔挺贵的，不过必须买下来。

En: Huh...
But... I don't wanna put it up my bum.
Zh: 但是……我不想把它放在我的屁股上。

En: Okay, right. We're putting it in the bum!
Zh: 好吧，好吧。我们就把它放在屁股上！

En: Who said anything about putting it up there? No, we're gonna put it in a *way* more special place. Soooo special. Just hang on to it, keep it safe. Wait for my sign!
Zh: 谁说要放到那里去了？不，我们要把它放在一个*更加*特别的地方。非常特殊。先拿好了，保证它的安全。等待我的信号！

En: I'm getting a really dark vibe from this. This... won't be pretty.
Zh: 我感觉到一种特别黑暗的氛围。这下……可不太妙啊。

En: I don't know. Sounds like it's gonna turbo-boost my alcohol habit. My regular alcohol habit's gotten me enough trouble already.
Zh: 我不知道。听起来会让我的酒瘾更加严重。普通的酒瘾就已经给我惹了不少麻烦了。

En: No, no, no *bratan*! This is different. This is *SO GOOD* it's going to get you *out of* trouble! We're going to use it in the most *extravagant* manner!
Zh: 不不不，*兄弟*！它不一样。它*太好了*，只会帮你*摆脱*麻烦的！我们要用*最奢侈*的方法使用它！

En: Finally came to your senses, huh, buddy-boy? Ain't nobody else gonna give you a price like that. The man starts laughing to himself. "Had to let life squeeze you to get that, huh?"
Zh: “总算醒悟过来了，是不是，伙计？不会有其他人能给你这种价格了。”男人笑了起来。“必须被生活压榨过后才能搞明白，是吗？”

En: Cool. So can I get it for that?
Zh: “挺酷的。所以我能买到吗？”

En: Yes, yes. Life, squeezing, spirits. Let's just make a deal already.
Zh: “是的，是的。生活，压榨，烈酒。我们做笔交易吧。”

En: Sure, friend. A bottle of high-quality spirits comin' right up, that'll be 3 reál and you're practically robbing me at this price.
Zh: “当然，我的朋友。一瓶优质烈酒马上就来，只要3雷亚尔就行。这个价格你简直就是在抢劫。”

En: Sure-sure, friend. People are just so impatient these days. So, that'll be 3 reál from your pocket to mine. And at this price you're practically robbing me.
Zh: “当然了，朋友。现在的人真是没耐性。那就是3雷亚尔，从你的口袋进到我的口袋。而且，这个价格的话，你基本上就是抢劫。”

En: That's just life, friend.
Zh: “这就是生活，朋友。”

En: Are you drinking that?
Zh: “你在喝那个吗？”

En: What was I gonna say?
Zh: “我想说什么来着？”

En: I'm not running tabs. It's bad for business.
Zh: “我没在攒钱。这样对生意不好。”

En: Doctors are idiots, buddy-boy.
Zh: “医生都是白痴，伙计。”

En: Look, opening it with my eye-socket again... No problemo.
Zh: “看，又用我的眼窝把它打开了……完全没问题。”

En: The way she goes, buddy-boy...
Zh: “她的样子真是，伙计……”

En: The way she fucking goes...
Zh: “她的样子真是……”

En: I'll sleep here then. See you in the morning. (Keep turning the key.)
Zh: “那我就在这里休息。明早见。”（继续转动钥匙。）

En: Good night. He smiles: "And try not to break the case without me."
Zh: “晚安。”他笑了。“还有，不要尝试甩掉我自己破案。”

En: I'm afraid we don't have time for rest stops right now, officer. We should really get back to the Whirling.
Zh: “恐怕我们现在没时间休息了，警官。我们真应该返回飞旋旅社。”

En: Right. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧。”[离开。]

En: I'll wait outside to give you some time and privacy to check out your new living arrangement and look for any signs of Ruby. But just so you know...
Zh: “我在外面等着，给你一点时间和隐私处理新的生活安排，顺便找找有没有露比的痕迹。不过你也知道……”

En: I'll wait outside to give you some time and privacy to check out your new living arrangement. But just so you know...
Zh: “我在外面等着，给你一点时间和隐私处理新的生活安排。不过你也知道……”

En: After we're done with the day, I'll still be staying in the Whirling-in-Rags for the night. We'll meet in front of the shack in the morning.
Zh: “今天的工作结束之后，我会留在褴褛飞旋过夜。明天一早，我们在棚屋门口见。”

En: It's getting late and it's raining. Time to call it a day.
Zh: 现在很晚了，而且还在下雨。今天就到此为止吧。

En: It's getting late and it's snowing. Time to call it a day.
Zh: 现在很晚了，而且还在下雪。今天就到此为止吧。

En: It's getting cold this late in the night. Time to call it a day.
Zh: 很晚了，天越来越冷。今天就到此为止吧。

En: Good night, Kim. (Send him away for the night.)
Zh: “晚安，金。”（让他回去休息。）

En: Not yet. [Leave.]
Zh: 还不行。[离开。]

En: Good night, officer. We'll meet in front of the shack in the morning.
Zh: “晚安，警官。明早我们在棚屋门口见。”

En: Enter the shack.
Zh: 进入棚屋。

En: Enter the shack.
Zh: 进入棚屋。

En: The door has seen better days -- the layer of paint has started to peel off due to the salt and wind from the sea. Even the lock looks slightly rusted.
Zh: 这扇门曾经有过辉煌的日子——由于大海的盐分和海风，上面的油漆已经开始剥落。就连门锁看起来也有些锈蚀。

En: Unlock the shack door with the key.
Zh: 用钥匙打开棚屋门。
