En: Never mind. [Leave.]
Zh: 没关系。[离开。]

En: The key turns with a satisfying click. You can enter the shack now.
Zh: 伴随着一阵悦耳的咔哒声，钥匙转动了。现在你可以进入棚屋了。

En: It's getting late. I'll let you sweep this place on your own. I'm sure you can handle the washerwoman... I'll be sleeping in my room in the Whirling. We'll meet in front of the shack in the morning.
Zh: “现在很晚了。你自己打扫一下这个棚屋吧。我肯定你能处理好跟洗衣女工的关系……我回飞旋旅社的房间休息。明天早上在棚屋前面见。”

En: I'll be sleeping in my room in the Whirling. We'll meet in front of the shack in the morning.
Zh: “我回飞旋旅社的房间休息。明天早上在棚屋前面见。”

En: I don't know what I'm even doing here.
Zh: “我都不知道自己到底在这里干嘛。”

En: It's too late now.
What?
Forging the signatures, of course. Was that not obvious?
Zh: 当然是伪造签名了。这还不明显吗？

En: Talk to me, Itchy Fingers? What's the plan?
Zh: 跟我说说吧，发痒的手指？有什么计划？

En: Okay, forgery it is.
Zh: 好吧，那就伪造吧。

En: It will be good, I promise.
Zh: 我保证，肯定会很不错的。

En: Your call, chief.
Zh: 听你的，老大。

En: No, I don't wanna resort to forgery.
Zh: 不，我不想依靠伪造。

En: I said, 'You can start by signing these papers.' Where the squiggly line is. I've got a pen right here. (Hand her the documents.)
Zh: “我说过了，‘你可以从签署这些文件开始’。就在弯弯曲曲的这条线这里。我这里正好有支笔。”（把文件递给她。）

En: You can start by signing these papers. Where the squiggly line is. I've got a pen right here. (Hand her the documents.)
Zh: “你可以从签署这些文件开始。就在弯弯曲曲的这条线这里。我这里正好有支笔。”（把文件递给她。）

En: I said 'Will you sign the papers?' Right where the squiggly line is. For the children? (Hand her the documents.)
Zh: “我说‘你可以在文件上签字吗？’就在弯弯曲曲的这条线这里。为了孩子们着想？”（把文件递给她。）

En: Will you sign the papers? Right where the squiggly line is. For the children? (Hand her the documents.)
Zh: “你可以在文件上签字吗？就在弯弯曲曲的这条线这里。为了孩子们着想？”（把文件递给她。）

En: I think I also met the drunks...
Zh: “我想我也见到了那帮酒鬼……”

En: I haven't seen any drunks yet, though...
Zh: “不过，我还没有见过任何酒鬼……”

En: No. They've tried, but things just keep happening. Crime. *Accidents*. Other things. The place never stays open. She frowns. "It's a pity. It used to be such a nice church."
Zh: “不。他们试过了，不过还是状况频出。犯罪，*意外事故*，其他事情。这个地方永远开不了。”她皱起眉头。“真可惜。它曾经是一座特别好的教堂。”

En: So they don't hold services there anymore? The Ecclesiastes?
Zh: “所以他们不再提供服务了吗？那传道书呢？”

En: The Ecclesiastes have tried to get things going there, but things just keep happening. Crime. *Accidents*. Other things. The place never stays open. She frowns. "It's a pity. It used to be such a nice church."
Zh: “传道书试着让事情朝着那个方向发展，但总是会出事。犯罪。*意外事故*。其他事情。这个地方永远开不了。”她皱起眉头。“真可惜。它曾经是一座特别好的教堂。”

En: Nothing good ever comes from paperwork, in my experience. She nods.
Zh: 她点点头。“根据我的经验，文件从来都不会带来任何好处。”

En: She makes it sound like losing one's name was as common as a mid-life crisis.
Zh: 在她那里，失去自己的名字就像中年危机一样普通。

En: Then we'll be *ready* for them.
Zh: 那时我们已经*准备*好了。

En: I'm sorry I couldn't warn him in time.
Zh: “没能及时警告他，我很抱歉。”

En: I warned you, she nods solemnly. "I warned you men with guns would be coming after you. They always are with your kind..." Her features soften.
Zh: “我警告过你，”她严肃地点点头。“我警告说带枪的人会来追你。他们总是跟你的同类在一起……”

En: So be careful. And don't be a hero.
Zh: 她的表情柔和了许多。“所以要小心。别逞英雄。”

En: You rented the room to her?
Zh: “你租了一个房间给她吗？”

En: The 'Feld Electric' mural... you feel like you should go look at it again. Step *closer* this time.
Zh: ‘菲尔德电气’的壁画……你觉得自己应该再去看看。这次再靠*近*一点。

En: Under carts, boats, in little boxes. It's not hard to find.
Zh: 在手推車下面，小船，在小盒子里。要找到不难。

En: Then how does she know you're here?
Zh: 那她怎么会知道你在这里？

En: That's you alright. A black hound licking your own heels.
Zh: 那是你好吧。一只黑色猎犬在舔舐你自己的脚踝。

En: Finally you have those *lamos* of Martinaise off your back, bratan. This looks like a great place to bring chicks.
Zh: 你终于把马丁内斯的那些*笑话*抛诸脑后了，兄弟。这地方很棒，很适合带些小妞过来。

En: Between here and Jamrock, a dusty sea of old trees, all covered in industrial soot. Small houses under them, an overgrown park...
Zh: 在这里和加姆洛克之间，是一片灰色的古树海洋，上面满是工业煤烟。下面有些小房子，一个杂草蔓生的公园……

En: Perhaps the mysterious music is somehow connected to the case? A rusálka, or a half-demonic *apsara*, singing...
Zh: 也许这段神秘的音乐跟案子有关联呢？水仙女鲁沙卡，或者是半恶魔*阿普莎拉*，在歌唱……

En: Nothing is *impossible*, surely.
Zh: 诚然，没有什么是*不可能*的。

En: Ruby was here. You know it.
Zh: 露比来过这里。你知道。

En: In her mind, the Union is right wing because Evrart is fat. It's that simple and there's no changing it.
Zh: 在她看来，工会是右翼组织，因为艾弗拉特很胖。就是这么简单，而且没办法改变。

En: I'm gonna find another way. (Don't get her signature.)
Zh: 我会想个别的办法。（不用她的签名。）

En: The grandmother card *will* do the trick. Last chance to turn back.
Zh: 打出奶奶这张牌*就会*达到理想的效果。这是最后翻盘的机会。

En: Does this *feel* like a *good* thing though?
Zh: 不过，你*觉得*这是一件*好*事吗？

En: Damn, you almost had it. What was this -- a pang of conscience?
Zh: 该死，你差点就成了。这算什么——良心的谴责吗？

En: You didn't put it there, did you? *She* did.
Zh: ……然后转向洗衣女工。“不是你放在那里的，对吧。是*她*。”

En: The truth, sire.
Zh: 这是事实，大人。

En: She wanted to talk to her, as they usually do. But she was brooding.
Zh: 她想跟她说话，她们经常这样做。不过她在沉思。

En: This... (Spread your hands.)
Zh: “这个……”（摊开你的手。）

En: Not a quitter. Like you sometimes get, son.
Zh: 不是轻言放弃的人。你有时候也会这样，孩子。

En: No. You can do it. You still have plenty of juice in you before you drop.
Zh: 不。你可以的。在你不行之前，身体的弹药库里还是有不少存货的。

En: Behind the cinderblock houses, old pre-war ruins rise to the sky like dark palaces. The wind calls.
Zh: 在煤渣砖房屋背后，破旧的战前废墟拔地而起，宛如黑暗宫殿。风在召唤。

En: She really means it. It's an honest plea.
Zh: 她是认真的。这是一个诚恳的请求。

En: Test the waters here. Say that there's nothing to be worried about.
Zh: 试探一下说。说没什么好担心的。

En: She wanted to see if you would do it and if she would.
Zh: 她想看看，如果她愿意的话，你会不会这么做。

En: Evrart gave me five reál, he could easily give you fifteen. Or even fifty?
Zh: “艾弗拉特给了我5雷亚尔，他可以轻轻松松地给你15雷亚尔。或者甚至是50？”

En: The lieutenant gives you a look and sighs. "Let's go mail it."
Zh: 警督看了你一眼，叹了口气。“我们把它寄出去吧。”

En: He's not dissatisfied with you, but with himself.
Zh: 他没有对你不满，而是对他自己。

En: Lilienne is tough. She nods. "Tougher than the men here at least. If it wasn't for her and the kids, this place wouldn't have a spark of life left."
Zh: “莉莉恩很坚强。”她点点头。“比这里的大多数男人坚强。如果不是因为她和她的孩子们，这地方不会剩下任何一丝生机。”

En: She's right over there. She points towards the jetty. "The dark-haired woman, leaning over the railing."
Zh: “她就在那边。”她指向码头。“黑头发的女人，倚靠在栏杆上那个。”

En: About the document you signed... Evrart plans to muscle you out with construction noise.
Zh: “关于你签署的那份文件……艾弗拉特打算用建筑噪音把你们强行赶走。”

En: Heh... Not a muscle moves in the wrinkled face. "I'd like to see him try. If anything, *I* am gonna muscle *him* out with construction noise."
Zh: “呵……”那张满是皱纹的脸上没有任何动作。“我倒是想看他试试。如果有什么的话，*我*会用建筑噪音把*他*强行赶出去。”

En: She doesn't seem surprised by this sudden twist.
Zh: 她似乎对这种突然的转折并不惊讶。

En: Get out of here, trying to ruin my day! She raises her bony finger. "And that youth centre better be a good one or you'll have trouble from me."
Zh: “滚出去，你想毁了我一天的心情！”她竖起瘦骨嶙峋的手指。“还有，那家青少年和活动中心最好是个好地方，不然我会找你麻烦的。”

En: Trouble how?
Zh: “怎么个找麻烦？”

En: I wield an army of drunks. A clever smile passes her face. "Do not underestimate the effect of such a force if it's pointed to the right direction."
Zh: “我指挥着一支酒鬼大军。”她的脸上闪过一丝狡黠的微笑。“如果能指引出正确方向的话，不要低估了这股力量的影响。”

En: That's kind of grim for a children's song. Even if it is a lullaby.
Zh: 对于一首儿歌来说，它有点糟糕。即便它是一首摇篮曲。

En: It's weird. I don't like it.
Zh: “它很怪。我不喜欢。”

En: Like I said, her tone sharpens, "it's an old *children's* song. You don't have to like it."
Zh: “我说过了，”她的口气变得尖锐起来，“这是一首古老的*儿歌*。你不用喜欢它。”

En: It's a cold, cynical smile.
Zh: 这是一个冷冰冰的、愤世嫉俗的笑容。

En: Relax, you're not a 'new face'. She doesn't mean you. You are an outsider. And a cop, remember?
Zh: 放松，你不是个‘新面孔’了。她指的不是你。你是个局外人。一个警察，还记得吗？

En: Here? For you? She lets out a dry chortle. "No, officer. The only money we have here is some coins the drunks tried hiding from their women -- and then forgot about."
Zh: “这里？你吗？”她发出一阵干笑。“不，警官。我们这里只有一点硬币，还是那些醉鬼藏起来不想被他们的女人发现的——然后自己都忘记了。”

En: Around here it's probably even more common.
Zh: 在这附近甚至可能更普遍。

En: Your mind just flips back and forth, doesn't it... what are they about *exactly*?
Zh: “你的想法总是翻来覆去的，不是吗……*到底*是怎么一回事？”

En: Aye? What's this about? She takes out the documents and squints her eyes. "Come now, I can't read all this scribble. Tell me what it says."
Zh: “嗯？这是干什么？”她拿出文件，眯起眼睛。“过来，我看不懂这些乱七八糟的字。告诉我上面说的是什么。”

En: I'll go ask her first then.
Zh: “那我先去问问她。”

En: Wait, where can I find her?
Zh: “等等，我能在哪里找到她？”

En: Yes, she replies, slowly nodding. "You do that. Can't let fat Mr. Claire down now, can you?"
Zh: “是的，”她慢慢点点头，回复到。“你去吧。不能让那个胖胖的克莱尔先生失望，不是吗？”

En: She is not impressed by a cop running errands for a local big wig. Not at all.
Zh: 她并没有被一个给本地大人物跑腿的警察打动。完全没有。

En: Wait, you promised!
Zh: “等等，你答应过的！”

En: You need to check your facts, officer. Her bony finger is pointing at you, soapy water dripping on the skirt. "I said no such thing."
Zh: “你需要检查你的事实陈述，警官。”她用干瘪的手指指着你，肥皂水滴落在她的裙子上。“我没说过这种话。”

En: True. She only said she wouldn't even *consider* signing before Lilienne.
Zh: 这是真的。她只说过莉莉恩没签之前她是不会*考虑*的。

En: He's a little relieved you're no longer in that room.
Zh: 你不在那个房间了，他稍微有些宽慰。

En: Sire, she doesn't believe things will ever improve around here.
Zh: 大人，她不相信这里的情况还有好转的余地。

En: Should he? This environment encourages one thing and one thing only -- drinking.
Zh: 他会吗？这种环境只会激发一件事——喝酒。

En: Yum yum. It's not a shack nor a room nor a lodging -- it's a spiral staircase disappearing into bliss. Empty bottles on the floor, maybe some baggies...
Zh: 嗯嗯。这不是一间棚屋，也不是一个房间或者住所——它是一个螺旋形的楼梯，消失在极乐之中。地板上有些空瓶子，也许还有条喇叭裤……

En: And what do you think I'm doing right now, Mr. RCM?! The scrub brush flies into the bucket so furiously some splashes hit your face.
Zh: “你以为我现在在做什么，RCM的这位先生？！”硬毛刷猛地一下扎进水桶里，几滴水花飞溅到你脸上。

En: Tastes like soap. Unsurprisingly.
Zh: 不出所料。肥皂的味道。

En: We really should focus on some actual leads... The lieutenant looks around uncomfortably. "Negotiating real estate deals isn't our main thing."
Zh: “我们真的应该关注一些更实际的线索……”警督不自在地四下看了看。“房地产交易谈判不是我们的主要工作。”

En: I'm not forging anything. It's dishonourable. And illegal.
Zh: 我不会伪造任何东西。这样太可耻了。而且是违法的。

En: Oh yes... of course. And illegal too! Forget it!
Zh: 是啊……当然了。而且还违法呢！还是忘了吧！

En: The virtue you exhibit in even the most difficult of moral dilemmas, speaks of the purest of heart and soul. Soon it will be time for another Arch.
Zh: 即使面临最艰难的道德困境，你也展现出了自己的美德，这就是最纯粹的心灵和灵魂。很快就会轮到下一个荣耀之拱了。

En: You catch the lieutenant's side-glance, but he doesn't say anything.
Zh: 你注意到警督瞥了你一眼，但他什么也没说。

En: He did not like that.
Zh: 他不喜欢这样。

En: That doesn't make a whole lot of sense.
Zh: 这样根本说不通啊。

En: Hell yeah!
Zh: 哦耶！

En: No, we must run around ceaselessly. It would be torture to stay put.
Zh: 不，我们必须不停地跑来跑去。待在原地简直就是一种折磨。

En: I could use a breather before another *runny* day begins...
Zh: “在新的*跑步*日开始之前，我可以先喘口气……”

En: I could use a breather, it's been another *track and field day*, the lieutenant says, rubbing his thigh.
Zh: “我可以先喘口气，今天又会是个*田径赛日*，”警督一边说，一边搓了搓大腿。

En: Yeah, I'll wait.
Zh: “可以，我会等的。”

En: No, not right now.
Zh: “不，现在不行。”

En: Suit yourself, replies the old woman and shrugs. "Patience obviously isn't your strong suit."
Zh: “你请便吧，”老妇人耸耸肩，回复到。“耐性显然不是你的强项。”

En: Well, hand it over then and I'll see what I can do...
Zh: “好吧，那就交给我，我看看我能做些什么……”

En: Must say, I'm proud of this one, she nods, handing the jacket back to you. "It's pretty nice underneath all that filth. I hope it'll have an easier life in your hands."
Zh: “必须说，我对这个很骄傲，”她点点头，把夹克交还给你。“洗掉那些污渍的话，它还挺不错的。希望它在你手里能活得轻松一些。”

En: Must say, I'm proud of this one, she nods, handing the jacket back to you. "It's pretty nice underneath all that filth. I hope you take better care of it than its last owner."
Zh: “必须说，我对这个很骄傲，”她点点头，把夹克交还给你。“洗掉那些污渍的话，它还挺不错的。相比它的前任主人，希望你能把它照顾地更好一些。”

En: I have 30 minutes to spare, would you still wash the jacket for me?
Zh: “我可以抽出30分钟来，你能帮我清洗这件夹克吗？”

En: You know something. We're here to help.
Zh: “你知道些什么。我们是来帮忙的。”

En: You need to look into these shacks. Especially hers.
Zh: 你需要调查一下这些棚屋。特别是她的。

En: Ask her about lodgings...
Zh: 问问她出租房屋的事……

En: You should look around your shack -- maybe she's rented it out to others too?
Zh: 你应该去她的棚户周围转转——也许她也租给过其他人？

En: As I've done before when she's been in trouble, or just looking for solitude. I've made it clear -- we welcome all kinds of people here.
Zh: “之前她遇到麻烦，或者只想独自清静一下的时候，我也是这么做的。我说的很清楚——我们这里欢迎各种各样的人。”

En: When was this?
Zh: “这是什么时候的事？”

En: She came last Friday. Left on Monday, in a hurry... Her wrinkled hand kneads a blue rag in the water. "What has she gotten herself into, that girl?"
Zh: “她上周五来的。周一走的，很匆忙……”她用皱巴巴的手揉搓着水里的一件蓝色破布。“她到底惹上什么麻烦了，那个姑娘？”

En: You could ask her about your hunch. That it was a *desperate* measure. See if she thinks Ruby fits the bill.
Zh: 你不是有种直觉吗，可以问问她。这是一种*孤注一掷*的手段。看看她会不会觉得露比刚好符合要求。

En: The world.
Zh: “世界。”

En: She really believes that.
Zh: 她真的信了。

En: She may simply have kept the equipment elsewhere.
Zh: 她也许把设备保存在别的地方。

En: Further up the coast we go then.
Zh: “那我们就沿着海岸往上走吧。”

En: (Wipe your brow.) "Man, I was really hoping she'd be hiding in this village."
Zh: （擦拭你的眉头。）“天啦，我真心希望她就藏在这个村子里。”

En: But that place is huge. She's a needle in a haystack.
Zh: “但是那个地方太大了。找她就像是大海捞针。”

En: You're right, this place is huge. She's a needle in a haystack.
Zh: “你说得对，这地方太大了。找她就像是大海捞针。”

En: Are you sure she didn't go somewhere more pleasant... and less wet?
Zh: “你确定她没有去些更舒服……而且没那么潮湿的地方吗？”

En: Yes, it's going to be too much work for you to find her. She drops the rag into the bucket -- it's clean now. "Better for you to stay here, get a nice, cosy fire going in the heater..."
Zh: “是的，要找到她，你肯定要费不少功夫。”她把破布扔进水桶——现在它已经变干净了。“你最好还是待在这里，在加热器里点上一把温暖又舒适的火……”

En: A bullet? The lieutenant turns to you and gives you a little nod, then turns to the washerwoman:
Zh: “一枚子弹？”警督转向你，朝你微微点点头……

En: I'm guessing you didn't put it there -- Ruby did.
Zh: “我猜这个不是你放的——是露比。”

En: She seems genuinely worried about her previous tenant. She's seen her hiding out from trouble before, but this seems different.
Zh: 她似乎是真心为前任房客感到担忧。她之前也见过她躲避麻烦的时候，不过这次似乎不太一样。

En: You said she left on Monday?
Zh: “你说她是周一离开的？”

En: Yes, early, with the dogs -- around 8 o'clock, I think.
Zh: “是的，很早就离开了，还有狗叫声——我想大概8点左右。”

En: She probably heard the lieutenant's Kineema drive by.
Zh: 她可能听见了警督的锐影驶过的声音。

En: She probably heard the lieutenant's Kineema drive by and it woke her up, just like it did you.
Zh: 她可能听见了警督的锐影驶过的声音，把她吵醒了，就像把你吵醒一样。

En: Kim, she must have heard your Kineema.
Zh: “金，她肯定是听见你那台锐影的声音了。”

En: Yes. That is a downside of having a 130 kilowatt engine. His voice is tinged with pride. "It lets the bad guys know when you're coming."
Zh: “是的。这就是180千瓦发动机的一个缺点。”他的口气有点骄傲。“它能让那些坏人知道，你来了。”

En: Was there anything else there?
Zh: “还有别的什么吗？”

En: No.
Zh: “没有。”

En: This Ruby is an old-lady whisperer. She knows how to work the village elder -- unlike you.
Zh: 这位露比会跟老妇人闲聊。她知道怎么应付村子里的长者——不像你。

En: Did she talk to you much during her last stay?
Zh: “她最后一次待在这里的时候，有没有跟你说些什么？”

En: Did she talk to you much this time?
Zh: “她这次有没有跟你说些什么？”

En: No, she was mostly silent this time. Kept to herself...
Zh: “没有，她大多数时候很沉默。话都憋在心里……”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: She tried not to let it show, but I could tell she hadn't come to fish -- usually she likes to cast a few lines, but this time she mostly stayed in her room.
Zh: “她尽量不表现出来，不过我可以看出她不是来钓鱼的——通常她喜欢小钓那么几下，不过这次她基本只待在自己的房间里。”

En: Are you sure you wouldn't rather stay here, get a nice, cozy fire going in the heater? She drops the rag into the bucket -- it's clean now. "Seems like a better idea to me..."
Zh: “你确定不要待在这里，享受加热器里温暖又舒适的火光吗？”她把破布扔进水桶——现在它已经变干净了。“在我看来，这似乎是个更好的主意……”

En: Enough about Ruby, I had other questions...
Zh: “露比的事就说到这里吧，我还有别的问题……”

En: Yes, let's hear those other questions. She looks around as spring snow starts falling -- lightly on the fishing village.
Zh: “是的，那就听听别的问题吧。”她环顾四周，春雪缓缓飘落——轻轻地落在渔村上空。

En: Those would be the dance music enthusiasts in their tent.
Zh: 那应该是帐篷里的那群舞曲爱好者。

En: Interesting. You could look into this *ruckus*, if you have the time.
Zh: 有意思。如果有时间的话，你可以调查一下这个*骚动*。

En: Alright. Got it.
Zh: “好吧。明白了。”

En: As I said. It's a peninsula. There's no one there. Just ghosts and vagrants. And teenagers making out.
Zh: “我说过了。那是个半岛。上面没有人。只有幽灵和流浪汉。还有亲热的年轻人。”

En: This is like when that man locked himself in the woodshed. We just need to help her come out.
Zh: “这就跟那人把自己锁进柴房的时候一样。我们只是想帮她走出来。”

En: Aye, that you are, dark Omen -- help yourselves and your organization. Help the storm clouds gather on the horizon... She shakes her head.
Zh: “是啊，你是的，黑暗的预兆——只想帮你自己和你的组织。帮助暴风云聚集在地平线上……”她摇摇头。

En: No, it's *not* like that man. With him, we called you. Right now, you just arrived by yourself. Like a raven, plucking at the window glass.
Zh: “不，这跟那个男人*不一样*。如果是他，我们会找你。现在，你是一个人来的。就像一只乌鸦，啄着玻璃窗。”

En: Okay, I'll take it.  (Get keys to your new home.)
Zh: “好吧，那我就收下了。”（收下新家的钥匙。）

En: It's not that she misses the kids -- they've been gone too long for that -- but deep deep down the old woman is incurably lonely.
Zh: 并不是说她在想念自己的孩子——他们已经离开太久了——不过在内心深处，这个老妇人孤独的无可救药。

En: When Vaasan communist revolutionary Ignus Nilsen was in hiding, he stayed in a hut on the boreal plateau for ten months.
Zh: 当瓦萨裔康米主义者、革命家伊格纳斯·尼尔森藏匿的时候，他在北部高原的一间小屋里待了十个月。

En: The woman next to a bucket of clothes hums an odd melody. Her eyes are closed.
Zh: 坐在一桶衣服旁边的女人哼唱着一段奇怪的旋律。她的眼睛是闭起来的。

En: The old woman still hums her unknown song. She opens her eyes to acknowledge your presence.
Zh: 老妇人还在哼唱着那首未知的歌谣。她睁开眼睛，感觉到了你的存在。

En: Goodbye, I'm off. [Leave.]
Zh: “再见，我走了。”[离开。]

En: Aye? What's this about? She takes out the documents and squints her eyes. "Come now, I can't read all this scribble. Tell me what it says."
Zh: “嗯？这是干什么？”她拿出文件，眯起眼睛。“过来，我看不懂这些乱七八糟的字。告诉我上面说的是什么。”

En: I was asked to get your signature. (Hand her the envelope.)
Zh: “有人要我来找你要签名。”（把信封递给她。）

En: Have you heard a radio on the coast in the last week or so?
Zh: “你上周在海岸听到过广播吗？”

En: A radio? Hah -- no, we play no radio here. There is one in the shack, but it's never used.
Zh: “广播？哈——没有，我们这儿从来不听广播。小棚屋里有个收音机，但是从来没用过。”

En: Are you *sure* you didn't hear any radio playing?
Zh: “你*确定*没听到过广播的声音？”

En: We good, Kim?
Zh: “就这样吧，金？”

En: These ears know every sound that belongs to this coast. I would know if I heard anything different.
Zh: “我这对耳朵听到过所有海岸上的声音。如果有异响肯定会知道的。”

En: Any idea how the twins could have heard Speedfreaks FM?
Zh: “那你知道那对双胞胎是怎么听到狂飙怪人.FM的吗？”

En: I told you before! No, there has been no listening to the radio. Not last week, not in the weeks before.
Zh: “我不是才和你说过吗！没人听过广播，上周没有，以前也没有。”

En: Can you confirm for my partner Kim that no one listened to the radio recently?
Zh: “你能和我的搭档金确认一下最近没有人听到过广播吗？”

En: Was that the nonsense they were spouting? Pah! She shakes her head.
Zh: “他们就只会说这些废话吗？呸！”她摇着头说道。

En: Olden days were golden days. Life's lustre is lost on those who speed.
Zh: 往昔岁月便是黄金岁月。生命的光彩在那些匆匆赶路之人身上消失。

En: That's useful information. Thanks.
Zh: “这条信息很有用，谢谢。”

En: The lieutenant, elsewhere in Revachol, runs a finger over the rough mesh of a stereo. He is deep in thought. He needs to be here for this.
Zh: 在瑞瓦肖的警督用手指抚摸着立体声音响的粗糙网布。他陷入了沉思。他应该待在这儿。

En: If you say it is, she says, shrugging.
Zh: “你说是就是吧，”她耸耸肩说道。

En: No, I have no idea where they heard it. They've been in earshot all week, and no radio.
Zh: “不，我怎么道他们是怎么听到的。在没有收音机的情况下，他们已经听了一周了。”

En: Hm... The lieutenant furrows his brow. He looks back to the children and sighs.
Zh: 警督皱起眉头。“唔……”

En: Is there anything else I can help you with?
Zh: “还有什么我能帮你的吗？”

En: Thank you, Kim.
Zh: “谢谢你，金。”

En: Ready to meet your heroes, Kim?
Zh: “准备好迎接你的英雄了吗，金？”

En: Must be exciting for you, Kim. Calling the station you're *accidentally* tuned into.
Zh: “打电话给你*不小心*收听到的电台，你的心情一定很激动吧，金。”

En: I'm ready when you are. He turns his face away, toward nothing in particular.
Zh: 他茫然地转过头去。“你准备好了就行。”

En: I'm ready when you are. He turns to the old woman. "Thank you, ma'am."
Zh: “你准备好了就行。”他扭过头看向那名老妇人。“谢谢你，夫人。”

En: Let's just get it out of the way as fast as we can. Contact dispatch from my Kineema and ask them to connect you to the station. Maybe they can shed some light on this.
Zh: 他转头看向孩子们，然后叹了口气。“我们尽快把案子解决掉吧。用我的锐影联络调度台，然后叫他们帮你连接到电台。没准他们能帮点忙。”

En: Anything else? A cold breeze rushes by. The old woman smiles.
Zh: 一阵冷风刮过。老妇人在寒风中笑着。“还有什么事情吗？”

En: Let's just get it out of the way as fast as we can. Contact dispatch from my Kineema and ask them to connect you to the station. Maybe they can shed some light on this.
Zh: 他转头看向孩子们，然后叹了口气。“我们尽快把案子解决掉吧。用我的锐影联络调度台，然后让他们帮你连接到电台。也许他们能帮上我们的忙。”

En: Anything else, now? The old woman frowns. Her wrinkles frown with her.
Zh: 老妇人皱起眉毛，他也一同皱了皱眉。“那现在还有别的事情吗？”

En: I'm a cop, *hellraiser*. Click, click. Bang, bang! (Point your finger pistols at her.)
Zh: “我是警察，*滋事天王*。咔哒，咔哒，砰，砰！”（用你的手指手枪对准她。）

En: We're cops. We're hellraisers. Click, click. Bang, bang! (Point your finger pistols at her.)
Zh: “我们是警察。我们是滋事天王。咔哒，咔哒，砰，砰！”（用你的手指手枪对准她。）

En: Have you seen a red-haired woman named Ruby around the coast?
Zh: “你在附近见过一个叫露比的红发女人吗？”

En: Have you seen a woman named Ruby around here?
Zh: “你在附近见过一个叫露比的女人吗？”

En: Nay, I haven't *seen* anyone lately.
Zh: “没有，我最近没有*见过*任何人。”

En: Okay, but do you know who I'm talking about?
Zh: “好吧，不过你知道我在说谁吗？”

En: Because she's blind, which you seem to have forgotten.
Zh: 因为她是个瞎子，你似乎已经忘记了。

En: Where could someone stay around here?
Zh: “要是有人留在这里的话，会是哪里？”

En: What is in this fishing village?
Zh: “这个渔村里有什么？”

En: What's further down the coast?
Zh: “沿着海岸再往前是什么地方？”

En: Tell me about yourself. Who exactly are you here?
Zh: “跟我说说你自己。你到底是谁？”

En: Why was there a bullet under the floorboards of your shack?
Zh: “为什么你棚屋的地板下面有一枚子弹？”

En: As you please.
Zh: “你请便吧。”

En: There's a gap where the name of that song should be. You should ask her about it, right now!
Zh: 那首歌名字的地方有一个缺口。你应该问问她，就是现在！

En: That said, if lodgings is what you're looking for, I've got a free room in the shack. Her soapy thumb points to the building behind her.
Zh: “也就是说，如果你在找住处的话，我的棚屋里有一个免费的房间。”她用沾满肥皂泡沫的拇指指向身后的建筑。

En: Just us. She sounds tired. "It's barely a village anymore. We... *almost* don't exist."
Zh: “只有我们。”她听起来很疲惫。“这已经算不上村子了。我们……*几乎*是不存在的。”

En: Some things just don't *fly*, officer. She smiles a gap-toothed smile and smells the air. "Look around. Who'd go to church here? They built it three hundred years ago, must have been nicer then..."
Zh: “有些东西是不会*飞*的，警官。”她张嘴笑了笑，闻了闻空气里的味道。“看看周围。谁愿意来这里做礼拜？它是三百年前建成的，那时候肯定要好很多……”

En: This is my little cinderblock town. She nods with a wrinkled smile. "I know what goes on around here."
Zh: “这是我的煤渣小镇。”她点点头，微笑的脸上满是皱纹。“我知道附近发生了什么事。”

En: Me? No one. Just an old washerwoman. Mother called me Isobel, if that's what you're asking. And my married name is Sadie.
Zh: “我？谁也不是。只是个老洗衣妇。母亲叫我伊泽贝尔，如果你想问这个的话。我的婚后姓是莎蒂。”

En: Hah! She lifts the paper very close to her face and studies it intently. "I might be half blind, but it looks like part of the village is gonna be a street. The best part. The part we need to get out of our houses."
Zh: “哈！”她把纸举到离脸非常近的地方，一心一意地研究着。“我可能是个半瞎子，不过看起来村子的一部分要变成一条街道。最棒的是，我们需要离开自己的房子。”

En: The place is so pornographically poor it's not even funny.
Zh: 这地方穷的太生动了，这一点也不好笑。

En: Wait, hold on, you're just *giving* it to me?
Zh: “等等，等一下，你就这么把它*给*我了吗？”

En: Wait, why isn't anyone using the room?
Zh: “等等，为什么没人用那个房间呢？”

En: My kids grew up and left, like they do. The house is long empty now. I live in the small side attachment -- it's easier and cheaper to keep warm.
Zh: “我的孩子长大了，离开了，像他们一样。现在房子空了很久。我住在一个小隔间里——这样更简单，保暖也更便宜。”

En: I don't need much. This sounds just fine.
Zh: “我需要的不多。这听起来刚刚好。”

En: I'm not sure it's appropriate for the RCM to accept free accommodations.
Zh: “我不确定RCM接受免费住宿合不合适。”

En: Mhm, that's exactly how they get ya. That's why we built our own cinder block houses on the seaside. So we don't have to give money to those crooks... She looks around. "They might not look like much, but they're *ours*."
Zh: “嗯，他们就是这样抓到你的。所以我们才会把煤渣砖房子建在海边。这样就不用把钱交给那些恶棍了……”她四下看了看。“可能看起来不怎么样，不过至少是属于*我们*的。”

En: One more time: I can just *have* the room?
Zh: “再问一次：我能*直接住*这屋子吗？”

En: Aye. She nods and looks at the shack. "The room is pretty bare bones, but it's got a bed and roof over it. That's more than some folks have around here."
Zh: “是的。”她点点头，看着棚户。“房间很简陋，不过有一张床和一个屋顶。附近有些人连这个都没有呢。”

En: Don't get me wrong, but 'bare-bones' isn't usually my style. (Point at yourself.)
Zh: “别误解我的意思，不过‘简陋’一般不是我的风格。”（指向你自己。）

En: It *would* help me stay in touch with the proletariat.
Zh: “它*能够*帮助我与工人阶级保持联系。”

En: (Nod.) "This could be the first chapter of my rags-to-riches memoir."
Zh: （点点头。）“这可能会成为我白手起家自传的第一章。”

En: Now this is real Revacholian hospitality. That Garte must be half-kipt or something.
Zh: “这才叫真正好客的瑞瓦肖人。那个加尔特肯定有一半的吉皮特血统，或者别的之类的。”

En: Unfortunate that incremental development hasn't *elevated* this neighbourhood yet. I see potential here.
Zh: “不幸的是，增量开发还没有*提升*这片区域的地位。我在这里看到了潜力。”

En: She waves her hand southwest. "Over there you can find... more of the same. Shacks and trees growing wild. That's the Pox."
Zh: 她朝西南边挥挥手。“在那边，你可以找到……更多类似的东西。棚户和繁密茂盛的树木。那就是帕克斯。”

En: Who else lives in this... village?
Zh: “还有谁住在这个……村子里？”

En: Well, there's Lilienne and her kids. A few new folks live in the house to the east. She nods her head across the courtyard. "But they're away right now."
Zh: “呃，莉莉恩和她的孩子们。几个新来的人住在东边的房子里。”她朝着院子对面的方向点点头。“不过他们现在不在。”

En: Who, what drunks?
Zh: “谁，什么酒鬼？”

En: Oh yeah, I met a couple of drunks here.
Zh: “哦，是的，我在这里见过几个酒鬼。”

En: Sooner or later you'll see for yourself. She slowly shakes her head. "Don't have to look long to find these guys."
Zh: “你迟早会见到的。”她慢慢摇摇头。“不用很长时间就能找到这些家伙。”

En: Is there a way to make a little money around here?
Zh: “这附近有办法能赚点小钱吗？”

En: I get the feeling you're leaving stuff out. What else is going on?
Zh: “我感觉你隐瞒了什么东西。这里还发生了什么事？”

En: Well, there's that music. Music from across the sea. She scoffs. "It started a few days ago and now it's blasting, even through the nights."
Zh: “呃，还有音乐。从大海对面飘过来的音乐。”她嘲弄到。“几天前开始的，现在吵的不得了，就算晚上也是一样。”

En: She's not telling you all she knows. Keep her talking.
Zh: 她没有把自己知道的一切都告诉你。让她继续说下去。

En:
What else is on down coast?	“沿着海岸往下还有什么？”
0x0100005A0000E335	Dialogue Text	VILLAGE / WASHERWOMAN	Washerwoman	Before you get to the church, there's some ruins -- an apartment complex, or some kind of electrical plant... Run down bunch of houses, empty."
Zh: “去教堂的路上有一些废墟——一栋公寓大楼，或者是某种电气厂……有一堆倒塌的房子，都是空的。”

En: Which is it then? Apartments or electrical plant?
Zh: “到底是什么？公寓还是电气厂？”

En: Anything else of note?
Zh: “还有什么值得注意的吗？”

En: Of note? The old fishmarket up on the boardwalk. But it's closed.
Zh: “值得注意？木板道上边有个旧鱼市。不过已经关门了。”

En: No one, that's why it's closed. After a long pause, she adds, "It was once a bustling place, back when I was young and so was everyone else..."
Zh: “没有人，所以关门了。”停顿了很长一段时间之后，她补充了一句，“那里曾经是个热闹又繁忙的地方，那时的我还很年轻，其他人也一样……”

En: That's it? There's got to be more along the coast.
Zh: “就这些？海岸上肯定不止这些吧。”

En: And now you know everything there is to know about this coast. She drops a bar of soap into the bucket with a splash.
Zh: 扑通一声，一块肥皂掉进了水桶里。“现在，这片海岸的一切你都知道了。”

En: There was a murder in Martinaise. He points east. "She might be a suspect. We would appreciate your help."
Zh: “马丁内斯发生了一起凶杀案。”他指向东边。“她可能是个嫌疑人。如果你能帮忙的话，我们会很感激的。”

En: She's being evasive. She knows something.
Zh: 她在闪烁其词。她知道一些事。

En: This isn't about the Union, you know. You don't have to worry about retaliation.
Zh: “你知道的，这跟工会无关。你不用担心报复问题。”

En: Ah, I should have known... She shakes her head. "This is yet another Union mess. I'm not afraid of them, you know. We're not in the habit of being afraid around here."
Zh: “啊，我早该知道的……”她摇摇头。“这又是工会惹出来的烂摊子。你知道吗，我不怕他们。我们这里可没有害怕的习惯。”

En: Enough of this! She better start talking RIGHT NOW!
Zh: 够了！她最好赶紧开口！

En: God dammit, I know you know something. This shit is important!
Zh: “该死，我就知道你在隐瞒什么。这件屁事很重要！”

En: 'This *shit*? Her eyes with their cataracts look at you. "I'm not going to do your work for you, just because you've got a potty mouth."
Zh: “‘这件屁事？’”她用那双有白内障的眼睛看着你，“那是你自己的事，我不会帮你的，因为那张嘴实在是太脏了。”

En: No one? There's something about her... She's definitely not a 'no one'.
Zh: 谁也不是？她有些不对劲……绝对不是什么‘无名小卒’。

En: Isobel, you must understand, this place *needs* that construction project.
Zh: “伊泽贝尔，你必须理解，这个地方*需要*那个建设项目。”

En: She's good company. Knows how to talk to an old woman. She rubs her cold hands together. "At my age, you don't get a lot of quality conversation, so I really appreciate that about her."
Zh: “她是个很好的伙伴。知道该怎么跟一个老妇人说话。”她摩擦着冰凉的双手。“在我这个年纪看来，你谈话的水平实在不高，所以我真的很欣赏她这一点。”

En: Where did she go? (Move on.)
Zh: “她去哪了？”（继续。）

En: Why do you think she left the bullet there?
Zh: “你觉得她为什么会在那里留下一枚子弹？”

En: Did she have any technical equipment with her, like radio stuff?
Zh: “她有带什么技术设备吗，比如无线电之类的？”

En: I don't know... further up the coast. She tried to leave quietly but the hinges on that door screech like a cat in heat -- woke me up. I heard her rushing in those heavy boots, heading up north.
Zh: “我不知道……海岸上边吧。她想要安静地离开，不过门上铰链的声音很刺耳，像发情的猫一样——把我吵醒了。我听见她穿着那双重重的靴子匆匆跑了出去，往北边去了。”

En: It's a peninsula. She might be *trapped*.
Zh: 那边是一个半岛。她可能是*陷入困境*了。

En: The old woman still sits in her chair, continuing with her chores. As she does so, she quietly hums to herself.
Zh: 老妇人依然坐在椅子里，继续做着杂务。她一边做，一边自己轻轻哼着歌。

En: You're still up?
Zh: “你还没睡？”

En: Yes. I can't really sleep anymore. Only a few hours a night. It happens when you grow older... She sloshes the water in the bucket around for a bit.
Zh: “是啊。我真不怎么睡觉了。一晚上大概只睡几小时。人老之后就会这样……”她稍微搅了搅桶里的水。

En: She nods, rinsing another piece of cloth.
Zh: 她点点头，又开始冲洗衣服。

En: I won't charge you for it. Take it as a gesture of goodwill, from the village to the RCM.
Zh: “我不收你的钱。把这当作是村子向RCM表达的一种善意吧。”

En: This place is pornographically poor.
Zh: “这地方穷的太生动了。”

En: Later. [Leave.]
Zh: “回见。”[离开。]

En: I heard she was a heavy drinker with anger issues. You ever witness that kind of behaviour?
Zh: “我听说她是个没法控制愤怒的老酒鬼，你见过她那样吗？”

En: Nothing of the sort. She shakes her head. "Sure, she was no stranger to the bottle... she fit in, that way. But I only knew her to have a beer on the beach while watching the sunset..."
Zh: “完全没有。”她摇摇头。“当然了，她对那些瓶瓶罐罐很熟悉……她挺合群的，你懂的。不过，我只知道她会在海滩看日落的时候喝上一罐啤酒……”

En: She isn't what you'd call a *drunk*. Even offered me some, from time to time. Said it was part of the camionneur life. But I never saw her lose control of herself, the way some people do.
Zh: “她并不是你说的那种*老酒鬼*。有时候甚至还会分给我一些。说这是货車司机生活的一部分。不过我从没见过她失控的样子，就像某些人那样。”

En: How would I know? She's a gruff one, but not violent. She doesn't go around toting a gun. She looks back toward her shack, thinking.
Zh: “我怎么会知道？她脾气是很坏，但是并不暴力。她也没有带着枪到处跑。”她若有所思地回头看了看她的棚屋。

En: Not that I know of, though she was into nice music. She once showed me a few mixtape-milieus she'd made. She brushes her forehead with the back of her hand. Water drips to the ground.
Zh: “据我所知没有，不过她喜欢听音乐。曾经还给我听过她自己做的混音带。”她用手背擦了擦额头。水滴落在地面上。

En: Welcome to the fishing village. She opens her eyes. "Please lean in closer. I have cataracts."
Zh: “欢迎来到渔村。”她睁开双眼。“请靠近一些。我有白内障。”

En: Lean forward.
Zh: 探身过去。

En: Uh, what if I don't want to?
Zh: “呃，如果我不想呢？”

En: Oh. A shadow passes over her face. "Welcome, police officer. We don't cause any trouble around here -- and we don't *want* any trouble either."
Zh: “哦。”一阵阴影掠过她的脸庞。“欢迎，警官。我们这里不会惹麻烦的——而且我们也*不想*要任何麻烦。”

En: Tut, tut. Don't be shy now. It's going to be alright.
Zh: “啧啧。别害羞。一切都会好起来的。”

En: I won't be going anywhere. She side-eyes the bucket full of clothes.
Zh: “我哪里也不去。”她瞥了一眼那满满一桶衣服。

En: If you can't see, then how did you know I was here?
Zh: “如果你看不见的话，怎么会知道我来了呢？”

En: I still have a golden ear. Come-come. She beckons you toward her.
Zh: “我的耳朵很灵敏啊。过来吧。”她向你招手。

En: The buzz of electric lights blends together with the slow rumble of ocean waves at night.
Zh: 夜晚，电灯的嗡嗡声和海浪缓慢的隆隆声混杂在了一起。

En: Don't bother her. [Leave.]
Zh: 不要打扰她。[离开。]

En: She keeps humming, nodding her head back and forth to her unknown melody.
Zh: 她继续哼唱着，伴随着那段未知的旋律前后点着头。

En: My suggestion is -- don't. Don't grow any older than you already are. That's old enough. She smiles. "What's troubling your mind?"
Zh: “我的建议是——不要。不要变得比你现在更老。已经够老的了。”她笑了。“你有什么烦心事吗？”

En: Now it's your turn, mister...?
Zh: “现在轮到你了，先生……？”

En: Uh... call me Harry.
Zh: “呃……叫我哈里吧。”

En: My name is... Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau. (Sigh.)
Zh: “我的名字叫……拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托。”（叹气。）

En: You can call me Tequila Sunset.
Zh: “你可以叫我龙舌兰日落。”

En: I lost my name.
Zh: “我忘了自己的名字。”

En: These things happen occasionally. Don't worry, you'll find yourself once more.
Zh: “这种事偶尔是会发生的。别担心，你还会找回自己的。”

En: Why would I do that? That's a fool's name. Are you a fool? Her bony finger raises before you can speak. "Don't ridicule yourself before an old woman, officer. It's undignified."
Zh: “我为什么要那么做？那是个傻瓜的名字。你是个傻瓜吗？”你还没来得及开口，她就举起了干瘦的手指。“不要在一个老太太面前嘲笑自己，警官。这样做很不体面。”

En: Your parents must have had big plans for you. She pauses, measuring you up and down. "Wonder if you measured up?"
Zh: “你的父母肯定为你制定了很宏大的计划。”她停顿下来，上下打量着你。“不知道你合格了吗？”

En: Quite a handle you got there. The old woman nods, with evident respect. "So many titles. One of them *double*."
Zh: “你挺厉害的。”老妇人点点头，能明显看出尊重的意味。“这么多头衔。其中有一个还挺*麻烦*的。”

En: Why, I guess I will, Harry. Aye, it just rolls off the tongue. I used to know a Harry. Strong lad, but dumb as a rock.
Zh: “我想我会的，哈里。哎呀，就这么脱口而出了。我曾经认识一个叫哈里的人。他是个很强壮的小伙子，不过像块石头一样闷不做声。”

En: How much is it?
Zh: “多少钱？”

En: Don't know if I can afford another place to stay.
Zh: “不知道我能不能住得起。”

En: There's this guy -- Garte -- who makes me give him money *every night* just so I don't die out in the cold.
Zh: “有一个家伙——加尔特——让我*每晚*都要付钱给他，这样我就不会被冻死在外面。”

En: No one is using it and god knows it's not much anyway. She tilts her head on the side, pondering over something. "You can stay there."
Zh: “那里没人用，天知道里面也没太多东西。”她把头歪向一边，考虑着什么。“你可以待在那里。”

En: The old woman shrugs. "Or don't. It's your choice. No skin off my teeth."
Zh: 老妇人耸耸肩。“或者不要。选择权在你。跟我也没什么关系。”

En: You got yourself a tenant! (Get keys to your new home.)
Zh: “你有新房客啦！”（拿到新家的钥匙。）

En: No, I don't want a free room, it's not proper. (Stay at Whirling-in-Rags.)
Zh: “不，我不想要免费的房间，这样不太合适。”（留在褴褛飞旋。）

En: Don't make an old woman regret opening her house to the police. A key appears from under her apron. She hands it to you.
Zh: “不要让一个老妇人后悔为警察开了门。”她从围裙下面拿出一把钥匙，递给了你。

En: Suit yourself, she says, tucking some loose hair back under her shawl. "But should you ever need shelter, remember old Isobel and her shack by the sea."
Zh: “随你便吧，”她一边说，一边把散下来的头发塞到披肩下面，“不过如果你什么时候需要庇护，记得来找老伊泽贝尔和她的海边棚屋。”

En: Don't make this old women regret giving the police the key to her house. She takes out a key from under her apron and hands it to you. "Here."
Zh: “别让老太太我后悔把房间钥匙交给警察。”她从围裙下面拿出一把钥匙，递给了你。“给你。”

En: Actually, is that free room still available?
Zh: “其实吧，那个免费的房间还在吗？”

En: An uncomplicated man, she nods approvingly. "I take it that means you're interested?"
Zh: “你是个很简单的男人，”她赞许地点点头。“我就当你是有兴趣了？”

En: I can see that, she nods, a clever flicker in her eyes. "Everyone can see you think well of yourself. Now are you interested or not?"
Zh: “我能看出来，”她点点头，眼睛里闪烁着聪慧的光芒。“大家都能看出来你自我感觉良好。所以你到底感不感兴趣呢？”

En: You best not be plotting another revolution under my roof, she mumbles. "We barely got through the last one... But the room -- do you want it?"
Zh: “你最好别在我家屋檐下密谋一场大革命，”她嘟囔着。“我们好不容易才挺过上一次……不过，那个房间——你想要吗？”

En: Surely not the first one... She looks you over, a clever smile on her lips. "So are you interested or not?"
Zh: “肯定不是第一章了……”她看着你，嘴角带着一丝聪慧的微笑。“所以你到底感不感兴趣？”

En: Go ahead, she remarks sharply, "call people rude things. We've heard worse here. Now are you interested or not?"
Zh: “继续吧，”她厉声说道，“无礼的称呼别人。我们听过比这更糟糕的。你到底感不感兴趣？”

En: Any day now. Her eyes turn to the sky. "I'm sure of it. Are you interested or not?"
Zh: “随便哪天吧。”她的眼睛望向天空。“我很肯定。你到底感不感兴趣？”

En: Well, if you're not in the hostel in the morning, I'll know where to find you. He looks around and adds: "Here. In a shack."
Zh: “呃，如果早上你不在旅社的话，还能知道去哪里找你。”他看了看周围，补充了一句：“在这里。一间棚屋。”

En: Oh, because you're blind, right.
Zh: “哦，因为你瞎了，好吧。”

En: He's a sharp one, she says to herself and runs her hand across the washboard.
Zh: “他是个尖锐的家伙，”她自言自语，用手拂过洗衣板。

En: Would you now? She turns to him. "I know how this world works, and it doesn't work when people tell on each other."
Zh: “那你呢？”她转向他。“我知道这个世界是怎么运转的，而且当人们互相告发的时候，它就不灵了。”

En: I'm not going anywhere. Her features soften. "Perhaps there's something else the village can do for you, huh? Though we don't have much here -- just garbage. And a skiff or two..."
Zh: “我哪里也不去。”她的表情柔和了许多。“也许村子还能为你做点别的什么？不过我们这里的东西不多——只有垃圾。还有一两支小艇……”

En: Okay, but we'll be back later if we find anything *suspicious*.\n
Zh: “好吧，如果之后我们发现什么*可疑*的东西，还会再回来的。”

En: Okay, but I'll be back later if I find anything *suspicious*.
Zh: “好吧，如果之后我发现什么*可疑*的东西，还会再回来的。”

En: 'I see. You know something, but you've decided not to tell us.
Zh: “我明白了。你知道些什么，但是你决定不告诉我们。”

En: There's not much *to* tell. People come and go. Now, was there something else?
Zh: “没什么*好说*的。这里只有匆匆过客。现在，你还有别的事吗？”

En: There's got to be *something* here. Tell me.
Zh: “肯定有些*什么*的吧。告诉我。”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: This is pretty much a non-place. She grins. "A gap. A blank spot on the map, just a cluster of nameless shacks on a nameless street."
Zh: “这里基本什么也算不上。”她咧嘴一笑。“一个缺口。地图上的一个空白点，只是一条无名街道上的一堆无名棚屋。”

En: The lack of wealth is the one thing we've got in abundance. The woman smiles cryptically.
Zh: “我们拥有最多的东西就是缺少财富。”她神秘的笑了。

En: That's what I thought.
Zh: “我也是这么想的。”

En: Riddles, the lieutenant marks dryly and looks around. "Fits with the general *ambience* here."
Zh: “谜语，”警督四下看了看，干巴巴地评论到。“很符合这里的*氛围*。”

En: That doesn't make any sense.
Zh: “这样根本说不通啊。”

En: Aye. Her eyes grow wide with glee. "Sometimes it's as though I've also gotten lost inside this nameless nothing."
Zh: “是啊，”她莞尔一笑。“有时候我好像也迷失在了这片虚无之中。”

En: Then all's not lost, she replies, smoothing a wrinkle in her skirt. "As you can see, even in 'pornographic' poverty people make do..."
Zh: “那还不算失去一切，”她抚平了裙子上的褶皱，回复到。“你也看到了，就算是在‘贫穷真人秀’里，大家该怎么做还是怎么做……”

En: It's okay, she calms you. "What matters is we are still around. Still living our lives. Even in 'pornographic' poverty we make do..."
Zh: “没事的，”她安慰着你。“重要的是我们还活着。仍然过着自己的生活。就算在‘贫穷真人秀’里，大家该怎么做还是怎么做……”

En: An old military hospital and its surroundings. She looks toward the buildings to the south. "Or it used to be, during the time of the Suzerain."
Zh: “一间旧军事医院还有周围的东西。”她看向南边的建筑。“或者过去是的，在宗主国时期。”

En: The Pox? What's that?
Zh: “帕克斯？那是什么？”

En: It feels like I've heard the name somewhere, but tell me more.
Zh: “我好像在什么地方听过这个名字，不过跟我多说一点吧。”

En: Alright, there's another topic I'd like to address.
Zh: “好吧，我还想谈谈另外一个话题。”

En: Now the area is criss-crossed with nameless streets and makeshift cinderblock houses. Shacks as far as the eye can see.
Zh: “现在这片区域里都是纵横交错的无名街道和临时的焦渣砖房。

En: After the war it was turned into a goodwill hospital for shell-shocked veterans and folks looking for some quiet in the old sanatorium gardens.
Zh: “战争结束之后，它变成了一家慈善医院，专门收纳那些患上弹震症的老兵，还有那些想在旧疗养院的花园里寻找安宁的人。”

En: What happened to the hospital?
Zh: “医院怎么了？”

En: The good will ran out. She tightens the scarf around her neck. "The staff left and the place was shut down. It's long gone by now."
Zh: “善意终究会耗尽。”她拉紧了脖子上的围巾。“员工离开，那个地方也被关闭了。现在早就已经没了。”

En: I'm lost too.
Zh: “我也迷失了。”

En: I'd really rather go back to talking about the village.
Zh: “我觉得还是说回村子的话题比较好。”

En: Oh? Her expression betrays curiosity. "It seems to be a common theme these days."
Zh: “哦？”她的表情有些好奇。“最近这似乎变成了一个常见的话题。”

En: At least those who remain... She falls silent and turns to look over her shoulder, as though looking for someone.
Zh: “至少那些留下来的人……”她陷入沉默，回过头去，似乎在寻找某人。

En: I think I'm in the process of finding myself again.
Zh: “我想我正处在重新找回自我的过程中。”

En: Maybe I should stay lost. Just move here and... disappear completely.
Zh: “也许我应该保持迷失的状态。搬到这里来……完全消失。”

En: Isn't that what people always think when they're lost? A fleeting smirk runs across her lips. "Something *I* can do to help you find your way, officer?"
Zh: “大家迷失的时候不都是这么想的吗？”她的唇边掠过一抹幸灾乐祸的笑容。“在你寻找自我的道路上，*我*有什么可以帮你的吗，警官？”

En: Maybe, she says with unnerving calm. "It would be easy. But right now you want something from me, right?"
Zh: “也许吧，”她的口气平静地让人焦躁。“那挺容易的。不过现在你想从我这里得到些什么，对吗？”

En: And then there's the drunks... She sighs. "Not a pretty sight, but there's little we can do about it. Home is home, even for them."
Zh: “然后还有那些酒鬼……”她叹了口气。“不是什么美妙的风景，不过我们也没什么能做的。家就是家，即便对于他们来说也是。”

En: I'm sure you did, she says with a peculiar smile and nods. "Nature keeps them in rotation. A new face pops up every now and then and an old one disappears and is forgotten. This is who we are."
Zh: “我肯定你见过，”她点头说道，脸上还带着一种奇怪的笑容。“大自然保持着循环往复。新面孔时不时地出现，旧面孔会消失，被人遗忘。这就是我们。”

En: I'm not really interested in town drunks, but thanks.
Zh: “我对城里的那些酒鬼真的不感兴趣，不过还是谢谢你。”

En: She nods and raises her unseeing eyes. "What are you interested in then?"
Zh: 她点点头，抬起朦胧的双眼。“那你对什么感兴趣呢？”

En: Not much... She replies and wipes her forehead. "There's the abandoned church -- the Dolorian Church of Humanity. It's been there since before my time even."
Zh: “没什么……”她一边回复着，一边擦了擦额头。“有一座废弃的教堂，德洛莉丝人道主义教堂。甚至在我来这里之前，它就已经在那里了。”

En: Why is it abandoned?
Zh: “它为什么会废弃呢？”

En: Okay, thanks, I've heard enough.
Zh: “好吧，谢谢，我已经听够了。”

En: And now *suspicious*-looking people are sneaking around the church. I don't like that.
Zh: “现在看起来很*可疑*的家伙会在教堂附近鬼鬼祟祟地走动。我不喜欢那样。”

En: I don't know exactly. A pre-war place. It used to be something. She shrugs. "Before the war. I wasn't here then, you know. Was born in Samara."
Zh: “我也不太清楚。那是战前的一个地方。曾经挺重要的。”她耸耸肩。“不过那是战前的事。你知道的，那时我还不在这里呢。我出生在萨马拉。”

En: Who'd want to come to a fishmarket here?
Zh: “谁会想跑到这里的鱼市来呢？”

En: Now what catch we *do* bring in goes straight into a lorry for the Delta or somewhere else.
Zh: “现在我们捕获的东西会*直接*送上开往德尔塔或者其它地方的货車。”

En: What, you're one of those real-estate people with big plans? If you want a development opportunity, you can check out the abandoned building over at Land's End.
Zh: “怎么，你是那种到处宣扬大计划的房地产商吗？如果你想寻找开发机会，可以去看看地之角那栋废弃的建筑。”

En: Used to be a supply depot... we think. Sending goods and ammo across the bay. It's jammed shut, though. We tried to get in, see if there was anything to sell or scavenge, but it's impossible.
Zh: “那里曾经是个补给站……我们是这么认为的。把货物和弹药运过海湾。不过那里被卡死了。我们想进去看看，有没有什么可以卖或者是回收的东西，但是不可能。”

En: And without anger. A long and harsh life has taught her not to buckle under pressure.
Zh: 没有愤怒。漫长而艰苦的生活已经教会她不要在压力下屈服。

En: “Yes, I let my room to that Ruby girl…” She speaks slowly, wringing out a rag, after a long silence. Her hands move into the water bucket. Some water sloshes over the edge.
Zh: 老妇人沉默地坐着，把双手伸到水桶里。水滴从边缘溅了出来。她拧动一块破布，慢慢说道：“是啊，我把房间让给了那个叫露比的姑娘……”

En: What is she like, Ruby?
Zh: “她怎么样，那个露比？”

En: Is the room exactly as she left it?
Zh: “房间跟她离开的时候一样吗？”

En: That's for the police to find out. The lieutenant takes out his familiar blue notebook. "Right then, please answer each question to the best of your ability. I'm sure we have a few."
Zh: “那就要靠警察去发现了。”警督掏出熟悉的蓝色笔记本。“好吧，请尽你所能地回答每一个问题。我肯定我们有些问题要问。”

En: I cleaned it, like I always do.
Zh: “我清理过了，像往常一样。”

En: One thing. Officer, if you do find her, please go easy on her... She looks around. The air is getting colder.
Zh: “有一件事。警官，如果你找到她，请对她宽容一些……”她四下看了看。空气变得更加寒冷了。

En: She planned on fuckin' shooting herself in the goddamned head.
Zh: 他妈的，她打算一枪打爆自己的脑袋。

En: I have a possible explanation in my mind.
Zh: “我脑子里有一种可能的解释。”

En: Your hand twitches. A single bullet hidden from sight. Remember the way it slides into the barrel. The muzzle of the loaded weapon pointed at your heart -- it feels familiar, doesn't it?
Zh: 你的手在抽搐。一枚子弹隐藏在视野范围之外。还记得它滑入枪管的方式吗。上膛的枪口对准了你的心脏——这种感觉很熟悉，不是吗？

En: Aye? Do tell. A seagull flies overhead -- obviously a bad omen.
Zh: “是吗？那就说吧。”一只海鸥飞过头顶——显然是个不祥之兆。

En: It's an exit plan.
Zh: “这是一个逃离计划。”

En: On second thought, I'd rather not say. It's not something I want to think about.
Zh: “重新考虑之后，我还是不说的好。这不是我想去考虑的事情。”

En: Exit from what?
Zh: “逃离什么？”

En: That bad, is it?
Zh: “挺糟糕的，不是吗？”

En: Her life.
Zh: “她的生活。”

En: No... She tilts her head to the side, looking up at you, deep in thought. "She's a fighter."
Zh: 她把头歪向一边，看着你，陷入深思。然后，她下定了决心。“不，她是个战士。”

En: She's a good girl, whatever she's gotten herself mixed up in.
Zh: “她是个好女孩，不管她到底卷入了什么样的麻烦。”

En: Although I guess she was pretty handy with the mechanical and technical stuff. Even fixed the heater in the shack. You should be thankful for that.
Zh: “我猜她对机械和技术方面的东西很在行。甚至还修好了棚屋里的加热器，你该好好谢谢她。

En: The sun sets for everyone.
Zh: 太阳会为每一个人而落下。

En: I had a few more questions about Ruby...
Zh: “我还有一些关于露比的问题……”

En: What more do you want to know about that poor girl?
Zh: “你还想知道那个可怜女孩的什么事？”

En: You'll never find her, you know. Her tone is without malice. "She knows the coast like the back of her hand. *You* only just arrived."
Zh: 沉默了一阵子之后，她说，“你知道吗，你永远也找不到她的。”她的语气没有恶意。“她对这片海岸了如指掌。而*你*才刚刚来到这里。”

En: The lieutenant stops writing for a moment. He looks at you, then at the old woman.
Zh: 警督停下了手中的笔。他看了看你，又看了看那个老妇人。

En: I wouldn't worry about that, ma'am. We're persistent.
Zh: “那我倒不担心，夫人。我们很执着。”

En: Harrier Du Bois, but call me Harry.
Zh: “哈里尔·杜博阿，叫我哈里就行。”

En: Lieutenant double-yefreitor Harrier Du Bois.
Zh: “双重荣誉警督哈里尔·杜博阿。”

En: You're not sure about the melody, but it might be South Samaran, possibly Siigayan (also known as the Apricot Suzerainty).
Zh: 你不确定那段旋律是什么，也许来源于萨马拉南部，可能是希依盖（也称为杏味附属国。）

En: I'm a cop, I don't cause trouble, I take care of trouble.
Zh: “我是个警察，我不会惹麻烦，我只会处理麻烦。”

En: What he said. We're cops: We don't cause trouble, we take care of trouble.
Zh: “他说的没错。我们是警察：不会惹麻烦，只会处理麻烦。”

En: We're not here to cause any trouble, madam.
Zh: “我们不是来这里找麻烦的，夫人。”

En: We're cops. We're hellraisers. Click, click. Bang, bang!
Zh: “我们是警察，我们是来搞事情的。咔哒，咔哒，砰，砰！”

En: Trouble? Say the second thing, *bratan*! Shows you got style.
Zh: 麻烦？说出第二件事，*兄弟*！展示一下你的风格。

En: Oh, of course. Last time we saw you around here was... twelve years ago. You also came to take care of trouble then. Which you *did*... but, still, in Martinaise you're considered an ill omen.
Zh: “哦，当然了。我们上一次在附近见到你……是12年前。那时的你也是来处理麻烦的。而你也是这么*做*的……不过，你在马丁内斯依然被认为是个不祥之兆。”

En: No, you're not. I've seen you around here before. Twelve years ago. You didn't raise any hell -- you were quite helpful, actually. But, still, in Martinaise you're considered an ill omen.
Zh: “不，你不是。我之前在附近见过你。12年前。你没惹什么麻烦——事实上你挺乐于助人的。不过，你在马丁内斯依然被认为是个不祥之兆。”

En: Wait, I've been here before?
Zh: “等等，我之前来过这里吗？”

En: No, not you personally. I meant the RCM. Some of the men got into a fight, one of them killed another. Locked himself in that woodshed over there, she points to the building behind her.
Zh: “不，我不是说你个人。而是RCM。有些人卷入了一场争斗，其中一个把另一个人杀了。然后把自己锁进了那边的柴房里，”她指向身后的建筑。

En: I am *totally* an ill omen. It's exactly what I've been telling everyone. The end is here. (Proceed.)
Zh: “我就是*彻头彻尾*的不祥之兆。一直以来我都是这么跟大家说的。末日已经到来。”（继续。）

En: I *am* an ill omen, alright. (Proceed.)
Zh: “我*就是*不祥之兆，没错。”（继续。）

En: He was brooding, needed some help opening the door. You got it open for him and took him to think about what he'd done in a more secluded place. Somewhere more quiet.
Zh: “他在沉思，需要人帮忙开门。你帮他打开门，带他去了一个更隐蔽的地方，让他想想自己到底做了什么。一个更安静的地方。”

En: She says it as if he was on some kind of spiritual retreat.
Zh: 她说的好像他在进行某种心灵上的静修。

En: If I'm considered an ill omen, why hasn't anyone told me that? (Proceed.)
Zh: “如果我是不祥之兆，为什么没人告诉我呢？”（继续。）

En: Maybe they're afraid.
Zh: “也许他们是害怕了。”

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: Because you're an *ill omen*. But you're still welcome here as long as men with guns aren't chasing you. And maybe even then, because that's the kind of fishing village we've built.
Zh: “因为你是个*不祥之兆*。但是只要没有带枪的人追你，你在这里还是受欢迎的。甚至在那种情况下也是一样，因为我们渔村从来就是如此。”

En: I'm sorry there's not a lot of room to park your motor carriage. And not a lot of houses. Or a lot of people. My kids are long gone... searching for treasure. So are others'.
Zh: “很抱歉，这里没太多地方给你停車。房子也不多。人也不多。我的孩子早就走了……赚钱去了，跟其他人一起。”

En: You're not. No one around here considers us an *ill omen*. People would have told us.
Zh: “你不是的。这里没人把我们当成是*不祥之兆*。不然大家会告诉我们的。”

En: You're still welcome here. She nods. "As long as men with guns aren't chasing you. And maybe even then, because that's the kind of fishing village we've built."
Zh: 她点点头。“只要没有带枪的人追你，你在这里还是受欢迎的。甚至在那种情况下也是一样，因为我们建造的就是这样一座渔村。”

En: What kind of an ill omen are we talking about?
Zh: “我们说的到底是哪种不祥之兆？”

En: Oh, the usual. Dark tidings. Black hound.
Zh: “哦，普通的那种。暗潮，黑色猎犬。”

En: The policeman. She squints at you. "What can our village do for you?"
Zh: “警察。”她眯起眼睛看着你。“我们这个村子能为你做些什么？”

En: Our tenant, the policeman. I hope the waves don't keep you up at night. What can I help you with?
Zh: “我们的房客，警察先生。希望海浪没有让你彻夜难眠。我有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: I got shot!
Zh: “我中枪了！”

En: This is no time for jokes. Can't you see I'm in pain?
Zh: “没时间开玩笑。你没看见我很痛吗？”

En: What do you expect? Police officers get put into dangerous situations all the time. Sometimes they get shot. It's a rough trade you're in.
Zh: “你在期待些什么呢？警察总是会陷入危险的境地。有时候也会中枪。你这一行可不容易。”

En: What is the song you're humming?
Zh: “你哼的是什么歌？”

En: A lullaby my mother used to sing. I sang it to my kids too. It's an old Samaran children's song.
Zh: “母亲曾经唱的一首摇篮曲。我也给自己的孩子唱过。这是一首古老的萨马拉儿童歌曲。”

En: I guess you were right. The men with guns were coming for me after all.
Zh: “我猜你是对的。毕竟那些带枪的人是冲着我来的。”

En: I'm not sure those were the last of the men with guns, either. She nods. "There are always more coming for your kind, officer."
Zh: 她点点头。“我不确定他们是不是最后一批带枪的人。总会有更多人找上你们的，警官。”

En: Convince her to sign Evrart's document.
Zh: 说服她签署艾弗拉特的文件。

En: Your name is ridiculous.
Zh: 你的名字太可笑了。

En: Harrier's a good name.
Zh: 哈里尔是个好名字。

En: Trusts the tenant.
Zh: 相信房客

En: Dresscode: Interisolar business class.
Zh: 着装要求：跨岛商务舱。

En: There's a gap where the name of that song should be. You should ask her about it, right now!
Zh: 那首歌名字的地方有一个缺口。你应该问问她，就是现在！

En: Ah, look at me ramble on. She waves her hand. "What brings you to us?"
Zh: “啊，看看我，一直喋喋不休的。”她挥了挥手。“是什么风把你给吹来了？”

En: Logic and reason won't work on this old bat. Better go for shameless emotional manipulation. What's a grandmother's deepest vulnerability?
Zh: 逻辑和理性对这个傻老帽没用。最好使用无耻的情感操纵。一个老奶奶最大的弱点会是什么呢？

En: It's really in her best interest to listen to you. You *know* how the world works.
Zh: 听你说话对她来说才是最有力的。你*知道*这个世界是怎么运转的。

En: You know, there could be another way. You'll need to go some place *private* and *discreet*, though... like your shack.
Zh: 你知道的，可能还有别的办法。不过，你需要找一个比较*私密*和*谨慎*的地方……比如你的棚屋。

En: You know, there could be another way. You'll need to go some place *private* and *discreet*, though. Your room in the Whirling would do...
Zh: 你知道的，可能还有别的办法。不过，你需要找一个比较*私密*和*谨慎*的地方。你在飞旋旅社的房间应该可以……

En: She is headstrong, but there's a *slight* hesitation in there... You may be able to convince her.
Zh: 她很顽固，不过你感觉到*一丝*犹豫……你还是有可能说服她的。

En: I see Lilienne's name is already here. Tsk, tsk. The girl is too trusting. Either way, I won't sign Union papers. She hands the envelope back to you.
Zh: “我看见上面已经有莉莉恩的名字了。啧啧啧。这个女孩太容易轻信别人。不管怎么说，我是不会在工会文件上签名的。”她把信封还给了你。

En: Have you asked Lilienne about this? I won't even consider signing till I know she's on board. She hands the envelope back.
Zh: “你问过莉莉恩了吗？在她同意之前，我根本不会考虑签名的事。”她把信封还了回来。

En: I said, 'You can start by signing these papers.' I've got a pen right here. (Hand her the documents.)
Zh: “我说过了，‘你可以从签署这些文件开始’。我这里有支笔。”（把文件递给她。）

En: You can start by signing these papers. I've got a pen right here. (Hand her the documents.)
Zh: “你可以从签署这些文件开始。我这里有支笔。”（把文件递给她。）

En: I said 'Will you sign the papers?' For the children? (Hand her the documents.)
Zh: “我说‘你可以在文件上签字吗？’为了孩子们着想？”（把文件递给她。）

En: Will you sign the papers? For the children? (Hand her the documents.)
Zh: “你可以在文件上签字吗？为了孩子们着想？”（把文件递给她。）

En: Fine, she mutters. Grabbing a pen from inside her apron pocket, she scribbles her name on the dotted line and hands the document back to you.
Zh: 老妇人默默煎熬着，然后低声说道：“好吧。”她从围裙口袋里掏出一支笔，在虚线上签下自己的名字，然后把文件交还给你。

En: Reluctantly, she takes the pen from your hand, scribbles her name on the line, and hands everything back to you.
Zh: 她不情愿地从你手中接过钢笔，签下自己的名字，然后把东西都还给了你。

En: See that you didn't just get us all screwed. I might be half-blind, but I have a good memory for treachery.
Zh: “千万不要欺骗我们。我可能是个半瞎子，不过在背叛这方面，我的记性很好。”

En: I got Lilienne's signature. Will you sign the papers now?
Zh: “我拿到了莉莉恩的签名。现在你可以签字了吗？”

En: It's just a real estate deal. Nothing ominous.
Zh: “这只是个小房地产交易。没什么不吉利的。”

En: Evrart wants to turn part of the village into a little youth centre.
Zh: “艾弗拉特想把村子的一部分变成一家小型青少年活动中心。”

En: A deal with who? She narrows her eyes, then scoffs. "It's the Débardeurs Union, isn't it?"
Zh: “和谁交易？”她眯起眼睛，然后嘲笑到。“装卸工会，对吗？”

En: Yes, it's the Union.
Zh: “没错，是工会。”

En: Uh, no. It's... someone else altogether.
Zh: “呃，不是。是……别人。”

En: I don't believe that. Police showing up here, collecting signatures -- this has Union written all over it.
Zh: “我不相信。警察出现在这里，收集签名——绝对跟工会脱不了干系。”

En: Thought so. The old woman inhales sharply. "So what do the Claire brothers want this time?"
Zh: “我也是这么想的。”老妇人深深地吸了一口气。“这次克莱尔兄弟想干什么？”

En: Uh, actually, never mind. I won't bother you with this.
Zh: “呃，其实吧，没关系。我还是不打扰你了。”

En: Nothing good ever comes from paperwork, in my experience. She nods.
Zh: 她点点头。“根据我的经验，文件从来都不会带来任何好处。”

En: So it was a test?
Zh: “所以这是个测试？”

En: Aye. Lilienne's not the only one who's too trusting.
Zh: “是的。莉莉恩不是唯一太容易轻信别人的人。”

En: I changed my mind, I want you to sign the documents. (Give her the envelope.)
Zh: “我改变主意了，我想让你签署这些文件。”（把信封给她。）

En: Your mind just flips back and forth, doesn't it... what are they about *exactly*?
Zh: “你的想法总是翻来覆去的，不是吗……*到底*是怎么一回事？”

En: Lilienne should let her sword make her decisions. The girl just doesn't have the head for it. She lets out a chortle, coarse like a raven's caw. "Either way, I won't sign."
Zh: “莉莉恩应该让她的剑来做决定。那姑娘在这方面真的没什么头脑。”她大笑起来，粗哑地就像乌鸦在鸣叫。“不管怎么说，我是不会签的。”

En: What do you have against the Union?
Zh: “你对工会有什么不满吗？”

En: I've seen it all before. You think they've got *our* interests at heart? Rich men are always selling poor men promises they never plan to keep... She pulls a dark red rag out of the bucket and puts it back.
Zh: “我之前就见识过了。你以为他们会把*我们的*利益放在心上？有钱人总是在向穷人兜售那些永远没打算信守的诺言……”她从水桶中拉出一件深红色的破布，然后又放了回去。

En: Then the poor get pushed out of their homes and the rich get a little richer. That's the way it goes. So no, I don't trust the fat man, and neither should you.
Zh: “然后穷人被赶出家园，富人变的越来越富有。现实就是这样。所以不行，我不相信那个胖男人，你也不该相信他。”

En: She speaks with the authority of a leader. Hers is the final word around here.
Zh: 她拥有领导者的最高权利。在这里，她的话就能一锤定音。

En: Perhaps some... 'financial compensation' would change your mind? (Wink.)
Zh: “也许有些……‘经济补偿’可以改变你的主意？”（眨眨眼。）

En: Cops bribing citizens? Doesn't it usually go the other way around? She doesn't wait for your reply.
Zh: “警察贿赂民众？一般不都是反着来的吗？”她没有等待你的回应。

En: Sign it or I'll have the RCM turn this place upside down. We're gonna find *something*...
Zh: “要么签，要么我就让RCM把这里搅个天翻地覆。我们总会找到些*什么*的……”

En: What you'll find is poverty and abandon. She grins. "Not much of a threat you've got there."
Zh: “你只会找到贫穷和遗忘。”她咧嘴一笑。“你的威胁没什么用。”

En: I've heard that pitch before plenty. 'Building this, building that, new jobs, new blood...' She shakes her head in disdain. "Somehow the people here always end up holding the short end of the stick."
Zh: “这种论调我之前已经听过很多了。‘建这个，盖那个，新的工作机会，新鲜血液……’”她不屑地摇摇头。“不知道为什么，最后总是这里的人吃亏。”

En: I could try to ask Evrart to pay you some money...
Zh: “我可以试试让艾弗拉特给你一点钱……”

En: Were you dropped on the head as a kid? You can't live off a pittance for long...
Zh: “你小时候摔过脑袋吗？靠着那么一点施舍是撑不了太久的……”

En: Do you know who takes the cream off these deals? Real estate developers, construction companies, restaurant owners, Claire's accountants in La Delta...
Zh: “你知道是谁从交易里拿走了最多利益吗？房地产开放商，建筑公司，餐厅老板，还有克莱尔在德尔塔的会计师……”

En: Have some integrity. You're an officer of the law, not some fat slug's corrupt little crony.
Zh: “正直一些吧。你是一名执法人员，不是什么胖鼻涕虫手下腐败的小亲信。”

En: You got to be smart, read the fine print, think ahead, look for opportunities...
Zh: “你得聪明点，阅读细则，深谋远虑，寻找机会……”

En: Like ocean waves shaping the coastline, you will wear her down. Pebble by pebble. Just be persistent.
Zh: 就像雕塑着海岸线的海浪，你会说服她的。一点一滴。只要坚持不懈就好。

En: Uh, I can't come up with anything else to say.
Zh: 呃，我想不出还能说什么。

En: Maybe just look at her with big pleading eyes?
Zh: 也许只要用恳求的眼神看着她就好？

En: It's okay. She's emotional right now. Keep at it, wear her out.
Zh: 没关系的。她现在很情绪化。继续下去，让她精疲力尽。

En: Save your breath, officer. This deal isn't going anywhere. Now if you'll excuse me, I've got this whole bucket-full of rags to finish. That's my job. Maybe you should do yours?
Zh: “省省吧，警官。这笔交易不会有结果的。现在，如果你不介意的话，我还有满满一桶衣服要洗。这是我的工作。也许你应该去做你的工作？”

En: I won't be around much longer, anyway. Not with the dampness in my bones. She looks at her knees. "If they want to take a chance, who am I to stop them?"
Zh: “骨头里全是潮气，我也活不了太久。”她看着自己的膝盖。“如果他们想冒险一试，我又怎么能阻止得了他们呢？”

En: You've got to put the kids first.
Zh: “你应该把孩子放在第一位。”

En: Nod.
Zh: 点头。

En: Shake your head.
Zh: 摇头。

En: Did you say something? She squints at you.
Zh: “你说什么了吗？”她眯起眼睛看着你。

En: Aye, we do... we do... A hint of sadness passes through her face. "But how do we do that?"
Zh: “啊，我们有……有的……”她的脸上掠过一阵忧伤。“但是，我们怎么能做得到呢？”

En: What's it called?
Zh: “它叫什么？”

En: Sounds nice.
Zh: “听起来很不错。”

En: 'Surrender to the Night', she replies, slowly rocking back and forth.
Zh: “‘臣服黑夜’，”她一边回复，一边慢慢地前后摇晃。

En: Yes. She nods. "It does."
Zh: “是的。”她点点头。“的确如此。”

En: Aye, even *I* can see that. I told you not to bring your trouble with you, policeman. We've got troubles of our own here...
Zh: “是啊，就连*我*也看得出来。我告诉过你不要自找麻烦，警察先生。我们这里也有自己的麻烦……”

En: Though I suppose you took the worst of it. Turns out you were your *own* ill omen. The woman chuckles to herself.
Zh: “尽管我猜你已经做好了最坏的打算。原来你是*自己的*不祥之兆。”老妇人暗自发笑。

En: My partner got shot, too. He's still in the hospital.
Zh: “我的搭档也中枪了。他还在医院里。”

En: What do you expect? Police officers get put into dangerous situations all the time. Sometimes they get shot. It's a rough trade you're in.
Zh: “你在期待些什么呢？警察总是会陷入危险的境地。有时候也会中枪。你这一行可不容易。”

En: Still, I'm sorry to hear about your partner. He seemed to be a decent enough sort.
Zh: “不过，听到你搭档的消息，我很抱歉。他似乎是个很正派的人。”

En: He was my half-brother.
Zh: “他是我的好哥们。”

En: He was alright.
Zh: “他没事。”

En: He was a prig who wouldn't dance with me.
Zh: “他是个一本正经的家伙，不肯跟我一起共舞。”

En: Wouldn't *dance* with you? Is that what you look for in a partner?
Zh: “不肯跟你*共舞*？这就是你找搭档的标准吗？”

En: You did call him a monkey fucker. That had something to do with it.
Zh: 你确实叫他混蛋。这里面肯定有些关系。

En: That the best you can say about your *partner*? Sounds like it wasn't working out.
Zh: “这就是你对自己的*搭档*最好的评价吗？听起来你们的关系经营的并不成功。”

En: Losing a family member's tough, she nods solemnly. "But we've all lost someone we're close to, some more than once."
Zh: “失去家人是很困难的，”她严肃地点点头。“不过我们都失去过很亲近的人，有些人还不止一次。”

En: You could follow up on that, but suspect you're not going to pry much out of her.
Zh: 你可以继续追问下去，不过我怀疑你不会从她那里打听到太多东西。

En: Guy was cramping my style. Every superstar goes solo in the end.
Zh: “他们都在限制我的风格。每一个超级明星最后都会单飞。”

En: What kind of foolishness is that? A cop's got to have a partner. That's in your nature, however you may deny it.
Zh: “这说的是什么蠢话？每个警察都会有搭档。不论你怎么否认，这就是你的天性。”

En: Should you try *THE EXPRESSION* on her?
Zh: 要不要对她试试那个*表情*？

En: Absolutely not.
Zh: 绝对不要。

En: And risk fleshly contact with this old crone? Spare yourself!
Zh: 冒着跟这个丑老太婆产生肉体接触的风险？饶了你自己吧。

En: *Stay*? Most people here are trying to *leave*...
Zh: “*留下来*？这里大部分人都在想怎么*离开*……”

En: Change your mind, did you? I thought you might. Won't find a better deal than free.
Zh: “改变主意了，是吗？我想你会的。没有比免费更好的交易了。”

En: There's nobody there.
Zh: 那里没有人。

En: I met Lilienne and her kids already.
Zh: “我已经见过莉莉恩和她的孩子们了。”

En: I met Lilienne and her daughter already.
Zh: “我已经见过莉莉恩和她的女儿了。”

En: I met Lilienne and her twins already.
Zh: “我已经见过莉莉恩和那对双胞胎了。”

En: I met Lilienne and her twins already.
Zh: “我已经见过莉莉恩和那对双胞胎了。”

En: I met Lilienne already.
Zh: “我已经见过莉莉恩了。”

En: What happened to him?
Zh: “他怎么了？”

En: He did too many narcotics. So many he fell off his boat and split his skull on a buoy. She rubs soap off her hands. "Folks who saw it, say his head cracked open like a melon. Nasty-nasty."
Zh: “他服用了太多的麻醉剂。多到让他从船上掉了下去，脑袋被一个浮标给切开了。”她擦掉了手上的肥皂。“看见的人说，他的脑袋就像个开了瓢的西瓜。惨不忍睹。”

En: She acknowledges you as her equal.
Zh: 她承认你跟她是平等的。

En: The question must be prompted by your sloppy posture. Straighten you back, man.
Zh: 这个问题肯定是因为你马虎的姿态引起的。把背挺直了，老兄。

En: Like the hag can tell the difference between fool and cool? C'mon... As far as names go, Tequila Sunset is *dangerously* cool.
Zh: 说的好像这个丑老太婆懂得分辨什么是愚蠢，什么是酷吗？拜托……就名字而言，龙舌兰日落是那种很*危险*的酷。

En: She's got a couple of ranks herself. Honoured chief and so on.
Zh: 她自己也有几个头衔。荣誉领袖之类的。

En: What's going to happen to Lilienne's kids? The twins and little Lily?
Zh: “那莉莉恩的孩子会怎么样？那对双胞胎，还有小莉莉？”

En: What's going to happen to little Lily?
Zh: “那小莉莉会怎么样？”

En: What's going to happen to Lilienne's twins?
Zh: “那莉莉恩的双胞胎会怎么样？”

En: What's going to happen to the children?
Zh: “那孩子们会怎么样？”

En: What do you mean, 'what's going to happen'? They're gonna grow up, of course...
Zh: “你说‘会怎么样’是什么意思？当然了，她们会长大……”

En: I raised my own kids in this village and they're doing fine for themselves.
Zh: “我在这个村子里养大了自己的孩子，他们自己挺好的。”

En: Are they here now?
Zh: “他们现在在这里吗？”

En: They didn't end up like the moribund alcoholics? (Point to the drunks.)
Zh: “他们没有变得像那些垂死的酒鬼一样吗？”（指向那群酒鬼。）

En: You must be so proud.
Zh: “你肯定很骄傲吧。”

En: No... the old woman says, her cloudy eyes darting sideways. "They moved to Faubourg for work."
Zh: “没有……”老妇人说道，阴郁的眼睛朝旁边瞥了一眼。“他们搬到市郊工作去了。”

En: No! They're good kids. I brought them up the right way. There's a note of defiance in her voice.
Zh: “没有！他们是好孩子。我引导他们走上了正确的道路。”她的声音里有一丝反抗的意味。

En: Aye, they're good kids. I brought them up the right way. There's a touch of pride in her voice.
Zh: “是啊，他们是好孩子。我引导他们走上了正确的道路。”她的声音里有种骄傲的意味。

En: And what do you think Lilienne's kids are going to do when they grow up?
Zh: “你觉得莉莉恩的孩子们长大以后会做什么呢？”

En: Well, I imagine they'll grow up and leave, too.
Zh: “呃，我想她们会长大，然后也会离开这里吧。”

En: You've got her. Now just reel 'er in.
Zh: 你已经让她上钩了。现在收线吧。

En: And *then* what?
Zh: “那*然后*呢？”

En: Then... the old woman exhales. She stares at the ground, unable to finish the thought.
Zh: “然后……”老妇人呼出一口气。她凝视着地面，无法完成这个想法。

En: Wait -- Let her come to it herself.
Zh: 等等——让她想明白吧。

En: You have her! Now twist the knife...
Zh: 你说动她了！现在只要落井下石就好……

En: Look, this village isn't what it used to be. It's a dilapidated shit hole.
Zh: “看，这个村子已经不是之前的样子了。它就是个荒废的垃圾堆。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: You watch your tongue, boy. This village has held up much better than *some* people. Even with her cataracts she can pierce right through you.
Zh: “注意点你说话的口气，小子。这个村子比*某些人*要好很多。”即使有白内障，她的眼神还是能直接将你穿透。

En: Damn, that's cold.
Zh: 该死的，好冷。

En: That doesn't mean you're wrong though, she sighs. "This place ain't what it used to be, even I can see that..."
Zh: “不过，这并不代表你错了，”她叹了口气。“这个地方不是之前的样子了，就连我也看得出来……”

En: I don't believe the fat man's *youth centre* is gonna change anything. But what other choice do we have? It's not like the Coalition Government is coming to save us...
Zh: “我不相信那个胖男人的*青少年活动中心*能改变什么。不过我们还有别的选择吗？又不是说联盟政府会来拯救我们……”

En: Oh. Well, let us know if that changes. We're looking for a fugitive.\n
Zh: “哦。好吧，如果改变主意的话，记得告诉我。我们在寻找一个逃犯。”

En: Oh. Well, let me know if that changes. Let's talk about something else.
Zh: “哦。好吧，如果改变主意的话，记得告诉我。我们先聊点别的吧。”

En: Aye, officer. I certainly will.
Zh: “好的，警官。我一定会的。”

En: I see, ma'am. The lieutenant turns to you. "I hope you don't mind if we look around anyway."
Zh: “我明白了，夫人。”警督转向你。“我希望你不介意我们四处看看。”

En: Do you know anything about a lost jacket?
Zh: “你听说过一件丢失的夹克吗？”

En: He probably pawned it for booze and then forgot about it. You know how drunk men are...
Zh: “他可能把它拿去当掉换酒，然后忘记了。你知道酒鬼是怎么回事……”

En: What do you want with a lost jacket?
Zh: “你要一件丢失的夹克做什么？”

En: One of the drunks lost his and I agreed to look for it.
Zh: “有一个酒鬼弄丢了自己的夹克，我答应把他找回来。”

En: I'm afraid I can't comment -- Official police business.
Zh: “恐怕我不能发表任何评论——这是警察的官方事务。”

En: It's not for me. I'm on a *philosophical* journey to fetch it for someone.
Zh: “不是给我的。我正在进行一场*哲学*之旅，要为某个人把它取回来。”

En: Oh, aye, must be *real* important from the sound of it...
Zh: “哦，好吧，听起来好像*真的*很重要……”

En: Anyhow, you might ask Lilienne if she's seen anything lately -- that girl's got a way of attracting lost and broken things.
Zh: “总之，你可以问问莉莉恩最近有没有看见什么——那个姑娘有办法吸引丢失和破损的东西。”

En: Are you getting a reward for it at least?
Zh: “至少你会得到一份回报吧？”

En: No.
Zh: “不会。”

En: (Lie.) "Yes."
Zh: （撒谎。）“会的。”

En: What? You're fetching someone's missing laundry *free of charge*? Do you not have anything better to do?
Zh: “什么？你*免费*帮人找回丢失的衣物吗？你没有更好的事可以做了吗？”

En: Good for you. Can't expect to receive help if you're not helpful in return. A fine arrangement, that.
Zh: “对你有好处。如果不帮助别人的话，也别指望别人会帮助你作为回报。这是一个很好的安排。”

En: In fact, he does. The lieutenant glances at his watch.
Zh: “其实吧，他有的。”警督瞥了一眼手表。

En: I just need to be needed.
Zh: “我只是需要被人需要的感觉。”

En: I just forgot to ask for a reward.
Zh: “我只是忘记要奖励了。”

En: None of my business, either way. How you spend your hours is up to you...
Zh: “不管怎么说，都跟我无关。怎么分配你的时间是你自己的事……”

En: I found that jacket I was looking for, but it's filthy. Could you wash it for me?
Zh: “我找到那件夹克了，不过很脏。你能帮我洗干净吗？”

En: I found this jacket, but it's filthy. Could you wash it for me?
Zh: “我找到他的夹克了，不过很脏。你能帮我洗干净吗？”

En: I *can* wash it for you, she says after looking the jacket over, "but it's going to take about a half an hour. Think you can stay put for that long?"
Zh: “我*可以*帮你洗干净，”看了一眼夹克之后，她回复到，“不过大概需要半个小时。你能等那么久吗？”

En: That's an *excellent* question, ma'am.
Zh: “这是个*非常棒*的问题，夫人。”

En: Like a smart dog, yes?
Zh: “就像一条聪明的狗，不是吗？”

En: Like a dog, yes! Loyal and dependable.
Zh: 就像一条狗，没错！忠诚又可靠。

En: More like a slave to any master who tosses a bone your way.
Zh: 更像是一个奴隶，谁朝你扔骨头就跟谁跑。

En: There's honour in retrieving lost things.
Zh: “找回丢失的东西是一种荣耀。”

En: Aye, laundry fetchers are some of the most *honourable* people I know. Her smile gleams like a freshly honed knife.
Zh: “是啊，取衣服的人是我知道的最*光荣*的人。”她的笑容就像一把刚刚磨好的尖刀。

En: That's for *me* to know, and *you* to find out. (Wink at her.)
Zh: “那就是*我*知道，而*你*需要去搞明白了。”（朝她眨眨眼。）

En: You're trying to wink at me, aren't you? You seem to have trouble remembering that I don't see so well these days.\n
Zh: “你是想朝我眨眼，不是吗？你的记性是不是出了问题，最近我已经看不太清楚了。”

En: You're trying to wink at me, aren't you? I can't see, or did you forget that part?
Zh: “你是想朝我眨眼，不是吗？我看不见的，还是你忘记这码事了？”

En: Gone and hid things in there... She shakes her head. "She's usually a good tenant. And not a *stupid* one either."
Zh: “走了，还在那里藏了点东西……”她摇摇头。“平时她是个好房客。而且也不是个*愚蠢*的家伙。”

En: God damn that girl, she murmurs softly.
Zh: “该死，那姑娘真是的，”她轻声低语到。

En: What?
What did you say. The lieutenant gives you a puzzled look.
The old woman shakes her head slowly. She drops the rag into the bucket -- it's clean now.
You're still not saying it right.
Nothing. What did you say?
Okay -- let's rock with our cock*s* out!
This is a cool moment. It needs a cool thing to be said.
I meant: let's rock with our cock out.
The old woman shakes her head slowly.
(Say the cool thing.) "Let's rock with our cock."
Nevermind, he says, shaking his head. "Let's just continue."
Suliram, suliram, ram, ram...
Zh: “睡吧，睡吧，乖乖睡去……”

En: Suliram yang manis...
Zh: “睡去吧，可爱的宝贝……”

En: Adu hai indung suhoorang...
Zh: “在妈妈的怀抱里……”

En: Bidjakla sana dipandang manis...
Zh: “进入甜美的梦乡……”
